Description: At 7:30 pm, an officer responded to a call of a robbery and learned a juvenile had grabbed a laptop computer from a patron inside the business and ran from the store. The juvenile was tackled by several citizens and held until police arrived. The juvenile also had a warrant for his arrest and was transported to juvenile hall in Martinez.
At 4:30 am, an officer responded to a report that an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and rummaged through its interior, but no loss was reported.
At 8:43 pm, an officer responded to a report that an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and stole a Fastrak transponder and a pair of shoes.

At 8:30 pm, an officer was dispatched to the BART Station to meet with a BART PD officer who had located a 28-year-old woman with an El Cerrito PD warrant for her arrest. The El Cerrito PD officer transported the woman to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 6:54 pm, an officer responded to a call of a robbery and learned a pizza delivery person had been approached by two suspects and robbed. The suspects took the pizza and cash from the man before fleeing.
At 4:48 am, an officer responded to a report that an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and stole a small knife.

CrimeType: Failure to Register, Probation Violation, Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 7:18 pm, an officer responded to a call of a suspicious male inside a local business. Upon arrival the officer located a 29-year-old transient man who was acting erratically. The man was found to be a registered sex offender who had failed to comply with his registration requirements. The man was also found to be on probation and was in possession of drug paraphernalia. The man was arrested and later released from the El Cerrito Police Department with a citation.

At 8:25 pm, an officer responded to a call of a hit and run collision where the at-fault party had driven off without providing all of the required information. Witnesses at the scene of the crash reported the at-fault driver appeared to be intoxicated. Officers located the 54-year-old Richmond man driving by the scene of the accident and identified him as the responsible party. At the conclusion of several field sobriety tests, the man was arrested for DUI and later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 11:02 pm, an officer responded to a call of an auto burglary and learned an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and rummaged through its interior, but no loss was reported.
At 6:36 pm, an officer responded to a call of an auto burglary and learned an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and stole a laptop computer.

At 6:19 pm, an officer responded to a call of a robbery and learned a juvenile victim was confronted by two juvenile suspects and challenged to a fight. The victim traded punches with the suspects who at the end of the confrontation stole the victim’s backpack.
At 9:00 pm, an officer responded to a call of a residential burglary and learned an unknown suspect entered an apartment by unknown means and stole electronics.
At 8:16 pm, an officer responded to a call of an auto burglary and learned an unknown suspect broke a window on the victim’s vehicle and stole a backpack which contained a computer.

At 7:40 pm, an officer responded to the call of an auto burglary that just occurred.  When the officer arrived, he found the suspect had shattered the window of a van and stole one backpack from inside.

Location: San Pablo Avenue at Potrero Avenue
Description: An officer pulled a vehicle over for a vehicle code violation and found the driver had a warrant for their arrest from the Berkeley PD.  The driver was issued a citation for the warrant and was released from the scene.

At 2:36 pm, officers responded to the report of a two-year-old child abandoned at Churches Chicken.  A male was pushing the baby in a stroller and came in and asked for water.  He then sat on the bus bench.  Community members noticed the man had departed, leaving behind the stroller.  About fifteen minutes later, they checked on the child in the stroller and couldn’t find the man anywhere.  Officers summoned an ambulance, and the child was transported to the County Hospital.  Child Protective Services responded to take temporary custody of the baby girl.  The male, the child’s father, a 23-year-old man was found a few blocks away. He was arrested, booked and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 7:40 pm, an officer responded to the call of an auto burglary that just occurred.  When the officer arrived, he found the suspect had shattered the window of a van and stole two backpacks from inside.
At 8:20 am, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary.  In between 8:20 and 11:20 am, an unidentified suspect smashed a window and stole cash.

Location: Bart Path to the rear of El Cerrito Plaza
Description: At 9:19 pm, officers responded to the report of a robbery that just occurred.  The victim reported that as he rode his bicycle along the BART path, a group of five teenagers knocked him off his bicycle and took his laptop and money out of his pocket.  After they stole his property, the suspects ran away.

At 7:03 pm, an officer saw a suspicious looking vehicle parked at this location with nobody inside and found it to be reported stolen to the Richmond Police Department.  The owner of the vehicle was called, and they came and picked up their vehicle.
An officer contacted an individual who had a warrant for their arrest.  The individual was arrested, booked at the ECPD, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

An officer responded to the report of an auto burglary that had just been discovered.  The officer learned that between 5:00 PM and 10:40 PM someone had cut a lock on a toolbox mounted in the bed of a pickup truck and stole power tools.

At 12:48 pm, officers were dispatched to the report of a man with a gun at the EC Plaza.  The subject was reported to be in a purple Cadillac Escalade with a black handgun.  A 41-year-old Richmond man was contacted and found to be in possession of a variety of replica firearms and knives secreted inside his vehicle; in violation of his probation. Grand Theft (Person) Peet’s Coffee, 9895 San Pablo Ave.: At 1:35 pm, officers responded to the report of a theft from a person.  A patron was working on their laptop when the suspect stole the computer from them and fled south on San Pablo Ave.  A good Samaritan chased the suspect who dropped the MacBook Pro laptop near Wells Fargo.
A CSO reported that in between 10:40-10:50 am, an unidentified suspect broke the window of a Nissan Maxima and stole the victim’s purse from the trunk.

An officer responded to a call of an auto burglary that occurred between 5:40 PM and 6:20 PM.  The officer learned that someone had shattered two windows on the vehicle and stole a backpack and a sweater.

At 2:43 pm, officers responded to the report of a subject stealing a large number of purses from the store. An officer located the suspect vehicle entering W/B I-80 at Potrero Ave.  and contacted a 42-year-old Richmond woman.  The female was arrested and booked into the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 11:19 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle but did not remove any items.
At 3:24 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 51-year-old female after she attempted to cash a stolen check. The female was booked and then released with a citation.
At 9:36 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 25-year-old male after he was found to have a warrant for his arrest. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:28 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s residence which is under construction and took over 10K in tools belonging to the contractor.

At 4:36 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 30-year-old male after the officer responded to a report of an open door to a residence. The officer arrived and found the male inside. The male is a resident there but his roommate is in the process of evicting him. The male was cited out in the field.
At 6:37 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the business, selected merchandise and fled without paying.
At 7:35 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
At 5:48 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed a backpack.
At 4:05 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed a laptop bag containing various items.
At 11:55 am, an officer reports a UPS employee was tackled by an unknown suspect during a delivery. The suspect picked up two boxes being carried by the UPS employee and fled to an awaiting vehicle.
At 2:51 pm, an officer reports an employee of a car sales lot left a vehicle running  in front of the business. An unknown suspect got into the vehicle and left the area.
At 10:48 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed a window to the victim’s vehicle and ransacked the interior. There was no reported loss at the time of the report.
At 2:19 am, an officer reports the victim heard their car alarm activate and learned an unknown suspect had broken the vehicle’s window and removed property.
At 8:17 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed a bag containing tennis items.

At 6:41 am, officers received a call of an unknown suspect trying to cut open an ATM with a chain saw. The reporting party advised the suspect got into a vehicle and left the area. An officer located the vehicle, but the vehicle got onto the freeway and fled at high rate of speed.  Officers went to the scene and saw the suspect attempted to open the ATM  with a power tool.

At 6:52 pm, an officer reports the victim located their vehicle which had previously been reported stolen to the El Cerrito Police Department. The victim drove the vehicle home before reporting the incident to the police.
At 7:27 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed a bag containing emergency equipment.
At 4:58 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the rear driver side window to the victim’s vehicle but no loss was reported.
At 11:11 am, an officer reports a suspect stole the license plates off the victim’s 2017 Jeep Cherokee.
At 7:27 am, a Community Service Officer reported an unknown suspect entered the victim’s vehicle, and stole a backpack which included a laptop.

At 11:50 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 60-year-old woman, a 34-year-old woman, and a 38-year-old woman for shoplifting. One of the women also had several warrants for her arrest and was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. The other two women were issued citations and released at the scene.
At 9:09 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property.
At 8:08 am, a Community Service Officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle, rummaged through the interior, but did not steal anything.

At 4:28 pm, an officer responded to several reports of juveniles throwing packages in the 400 block of Albemarle St. They were last seen walking towards Fairmount Ave.  The police officer located the empty packages, determined they were stolen and contacted the victim.

At 12:16 pm, officers responded to the report of a subject hitting a woman.  A 49-year-old Palo Alto man was located nearby and identified as the suspect.  The man was placed under citizen’s arrest and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility after booking. Resisting Arrest, Public Intoxication, San Pablo Ave./Fairmount Ave: At 2:31 pm, an officer responded to several calls of a subject touching the windows of vehicles, blocking traffic and yelling at people.  Officers contacted a 33-year-old man who was found to be heavily intoxicated.  He attempted to access his bottle of vodka and grab onto a pole causing the officer to physically restrain the man. The man was arrested, booked and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. Auto Burglary, 160 El Cerrito Plaza: At 4:10 pm, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary.  An unknown suspect smashed the front right window of the victim’s Tesla and stole a grey backpack full of fifth grade homework. Grand Theft, Resisting Arrest, 10650 San Pablo Ave. At 5:20 pm, an officer was dispatched to a suspicious person at CVS, who was seen concealing items in a large Macy’s bag.  The officer contacted the manager who confirmed the subject was concealing items in the bag.  The officer made contact with the subject and asked him to drop the items.  Once outside the store and setting off the security sensors, the subject, a 31-year-old Richmond began to flee and was arrested a short time later.   It was determined he stole approx. $1050 worth of merchandise.  The man was booked and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 2:04 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 19-year-old male after he was involved in an argument with his girlfriend that became physical. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:48 am, a Community Service Officer reported that an unknown suspect attempted to make entry into several offices located in the mezzanine of the El Cerrito Plaza. The suspect also sprayed bear repellent under the doors to those offices. Officers contacted a suspicious male wandering in the area, but no arrest was made.
At 8:55 am, the victim reported that an unknown suspect stole their vehicle. The theft occurred sometime between 8:00 pm on February 3rd to 8:00 am on February 4th.
At 7:45 pm, an officer reports the recovery of a vehicle previously reported stolen to the El Cerrito Police Department earlier that same morning.
At 8:06 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and ransacked the interior. No loss was reported by the victim.
At 8:51 am, an officer reports the recovery of vehicle which was reported stolen to the Oakland Police Department on February 3, 2019.

At 9:25 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle before being scared off by a passerby. The suspect then entered a dark colored Ford Edge SUV and drove off on San Pablo Avenue.
At 1:28 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect tried the door handles of several vehicles parked in the area and entered one unlocked vehicle.

At 3:44 am, an officer located a vehicle parked on the street that appeared to have been burglarized. The officer made contact with the owner and learned an unknown suspect had broken a window on the vehicle and rummaged through the interior. No loss was reported.
At 8:47 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed tools from a shed and then attempted to use the tools to break into the home.

At 9:59 am, an officer reported that an unknown suspect entered an apartment, ransacked the interior, and took several identifying documents belonging to the victim and their family members.  The burglary occurred between 8:00 am to 8:45 am.

At 5:07 pm, an officer reported that the victim was walking down San Pablo Avenue when he was approached by two males who punched and pushed him, knocking the victim to the ground. The suspects ripped off the victim’s backpack and took his shoes. The suspects got into an awaiting white, older model Mercedes SUV with paper plates and fled southbound on San Pablo Avenue.

At 2:27 pm, an officer reports that a Toyota Camry, driven by an unknown male, parked next to the victim’s vehicle, and an unknown suspect got out and went into the victim’s vehicle. The victim’s car alarm sounded, scaring off the suspect.
At 2:47 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property some time during the evening, however, no loss was reported.

CrimeType: Arrest for Possession of a Controlled Substance and Drug Paraphernalia
Location: Parking Lot of 1000 El Cerrito Plaza
Description: At 4:05 pm, an officer contacted the occupants of a suspicious vehicle.  During a search, the officer arrested a 42-year-old male from Richmond for being in possession of methamphetamine and drug paraphernalia.  The male was released at the scene with a citation.
At 11:39 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property some time during the evening.
At 11:29 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle some time during the evening, however, no loss was reported.
At 10:47 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property some time during the evening.

At 10:12 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property while the victim was seated inside his vehicle. The suspect then fled in a silver vehicle.
At 10:36 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property sometime during the evening.
At 9:46 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property sometime during the evening.
At 8:44 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property sometime during the evening.
At 2:07 pm, a Community Service Officer reports that an unknown suspect shattered a window of the victim’s vehicle, rummaged through the interior, but did not steal anything.
At 11:53 pm, an officer reports a woman mistakenly left her backpack inside the McDonald’s restaurant where it was stolen by another patron.
At 6:27 am, an officer reports that an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.  The theft occurred between 6:30 pm on February 6th to 6:15 am on February 7th.
At 2:07 pm, an officer reports that an unknown suspect shattered a window to the victim’s vehicle and stole a bag containing miscellaneous clothing.
At 2:07 pm, a officer reports that an unknown suspect shattered a window to the victim’s vehicle and stole a bag containing miscellaneous gym clothing.

At 7:53 pm, an officer reports a 51-year-old male transient was confronted by uniformed security for theft. The male physically attacked the security officer causing injury. The male was arrested for robbery, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:50 pm, an officer contacted a 44-year-old male from Richmond who was wanted on two San Francisco County warrants.  He was arrested, booked, and released on a citation.
An officer reported an unknown suspect smashed the right front window of the victim’s parked vehicle and stole property.  The burglary occurred between 9:45 pm and 8:00 am.
An officer contacted a subject at this location and found they had a warrant for their arrest.  They were arrested, booked at the ECPD, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Location: arm Robbery  – BART Path near Potrero Ave
Description: At 3:20 pm, officers responded to the report of a robbery.  The victim was attacked and knocked to the ground and beaten by three suspects.  They stole his backpack containing his computer, phone, and  school supplies.  They also stole his shoes off his feet.  The suspects fled in a gray vehicle.

Location: Madison Ave / San Pablo Ave; Eastshore Blvd / San Pablo Ave
Description: At 3:49 am, officers located vehicles in two different locations on surface streets which had been involved in a collision on the freeway. One of the drivers exhibited signs of alcoholic beverage intoxication. The California Highway Patrol responded and took over the investigation due to the collision occurring on the freeway.

An officer responded for the report of an auto burglary that had occurred between 7:00 PM and 8:00 PM.  Someone had shattered a window on the vehicle and had ransacked the interior but did not steal anything.
An officer reports that an unknown suspect shattered the right front window of the victim’s vehicle and ransacked the interior.  Nothing of value was left in the vehicle so there is no known loss.
An officer responded for the report of an auto burglary that had occurred between 9:43 PM and 10:09 PM.  Someone had shatterd a window on the vehicle and stole items from inside.
An officer reported that an unknown suspect stole a package from the porch of a residence sometime during the day on January 28th.  The package contained a Santa Claus doll worth $40.
At 5:23 am, an  officer reports the victim’s right front passenger window was smashed and property was removed.

An officer responded for the report of a shoplifting that just occurred.  The suspect was detained a block away and was found to have three warrants for his arrest. He also had narcotics in his pocket.  He was arrested, booked, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer responded for the report of an auto burglary that had occurred between 8:00 PM and 8:30 PM.  Someone pried open the rear doors of a cargo van and stole tools from inside.
An officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and found the driver had a warrant for their arrest.  The officer issued the person a citation and they were released from the scene.
An officer arrested a 47-year-old male from San Pablo for DUI during a traffic stop.  He was arrested, booked, and released to a friend.

An officer conducted a suspicious vehicle check and found the two people inside had multiple arrest warrants between the two of them. They were both arrested and booked at ECPD.  One of the subjects was issued a citation at the ECPD and released, and the other was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 4:08 pm, an officer responded to the report of a theft at Sprint. Three male suspects stole phones and fled on foot.

An officer responded for the report of someone threatening a security guard telling him he was “going to kill him”.  The security guard was scared of the subject and subsequently placed the suspect under citizen’s arrest.  The subject was also heavily intoxicated and found to be in violation of his probation status.  He was booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer located a vehicle that had been reported stolen to the Richmond Police Department.  The officer contacted the owner and released their vehicle to them at the scene.

At 7:49 pm, officers responded to a reported home invasion robbery which had just occurred. Three unknown males entered the victim’s home and demanded money. The three males fled the area on foot. No injuries were reported.
At 12:20 pm, an officer responded to the report of a theft of a catalytic converter from a Toyota Prius. The theft occurred between January 2nd and 3rd.

At 7:05 pm, an officer responded to a reported residential burglary. The victim had been away from home for approximately two weeks. Upon return, he located a broken front window and the front door unsecured.
At 4:25 pm, an officer responded to the theft of a catalytic converter. An unknown suspect stole the victim’s catalytic converter from their Toyota Prius between January 4th and 5th.
At 9:03 pm, an officer reports the victim’s SUV was broken into while parked in front of Barnes & Noble. The loss was a backpack containing electronics and identification.

At 1:11 pm, officers responded to the report of a subject hitting a woman while holding a child. Officers located a 22-year-old male who was holding his two-year-old son at Del Norte BART. When contacted, the subject abandoned the child in the bus zone and fled on foot due to him having a small amount of meth. The man was taken in custody a short distance away by a responding officer. The female said she was not assaulted and took custody of the child. The male was booked and released with a citation.
At 9:59 pm, an officer reports the victim’s vehicle was struck by a metal object thrown by a homeless male who was last seen walking west on Potrero Ave.

At 10:48 am, an officer responded to the report of a subject smoking narcotics on the patio. The officer observed a 23-year-old transient male smoking methamphetamine. The man was booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 6:19 pm, an officer reports the victim’s home was broken into and ransacked sometime between 1:30 – 5:30 pm. The loss was electronics.
At 6:12 pm, an officer responded to a reported theft of a catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle. The theft occurred in the driveway sometime between January 5th and January 7th.

At 6:52 pm, officers responded to a reported hit-and-run collision involving a bicyclist. Upon arrival, it was determined a male was riding his bicycle south on Richmond St when he collided with a blue recycling bin. The male admitted to consuming two large alcoholic beverages before riding. The male was transported to the hospital for potential head injuries and released with a citation.

At 8:03 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s vehicle and stole a backpack containing clothes and books. The property, valued at approximately $580, was recovered by store security about 50 feet away in a bush.

At 8:53 pm, an officer reports three suspects entered the store with bags and walked to the linen section. The suspects filled their bags, along with another bag stolen from the store. All three suspects fled the store with approximately $270 in merchandise.

At 1:36 am, an officer reports the victim’s SUV was broken into while parked in the McDonald’s parking lot. The left rear window was shattered, and a black computer bag containing a laptop was stolen.

At 1:12 am, officers responded to the area for a report of vandalism at the residence. The victim stated a known male had smashed out windows to their home and fled in a vehicle. An officer located the vehicle, and the male was subsequently identified and arrested. The male was suspected in 5 previous vandalism cases at the same residence. The male was booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 11:26 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect cut the lock to a shed and removed miscellaneous tools. Some of the victim’s property was located in an empty lot several blocks away.
At 2:27 pm, an officer located the victim’s vehicle, which had been reported stolen on 12-28-19. The victim met with the officer and took possession of the vehicle.

At 4:19 pm, an officer reports the theft of merchandise which occurred last year in December. The theft occurred as part of a larger string of organized retail theft, and a suspect has been identified.
At 11:55 am, an officer reports a 27-year-old male entered the bank and attempted to cash a fraudulent check. The man was arrested and later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:48 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from underneath the victim’s Toyota Prius sometime during the night.
At 3:33 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from underneath the victim’s Toyota Prius sometime during the night.
At 9:54 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect cut the screen to the rear sliding glass door and a window at the residence, but no entry was made into the home.
At 2:47 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole an inventory scanner device from the store from the photo booth counter.

At 12:33 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect knocked on the front door of the residence and then entered the rear yard. Once in the backyard, the suspect shattered a bedroom window of the occupied home. The resident screamed, and the suspect ran off without any property.
At 3:10 pm, officer reports three suspects entered the residence by breaking a sliding glass door and removed property from inside the home.
At 4:01 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from underneath the victim’s Toyota Prius sometime during the night.
At 4:40 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and rummaged through the interior; however, nothing was taken.

At 9:27 pm, as the victim was walking along San Pablo Avenue, a male jumped out from bushes and snatched the purse from the victim’s shoulder. The suspect then pushed the victim to the ground and punched her before fleeing.
At 6:40 pm, an officer reports a group entered the victim’s residence via an unlocked kitchen window and ransacked the interior. The suspects left the residence with electronics.
At 11:15 am, an officer reported the catalytic converter was stolen from the victim’s 2006 Toyota Prius some time overnight.
At 5:05 pm, an officer responded to the report of a home being burglarized and ransacked. The victim returned home to find the house ransacked.
At 9:52 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the front driver side window of the victim’s vehicle.
At 9:52 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the rear passenger window to the victim’s vehicle, ransacked the interior, but nothing was removed from the vehicle.

At 3:30 pm, an officer responded to the report of three suspects in their teens, smashing a rear sliding glass door of the occupied residence and entered the family room. The resident was walking down the hall toward the kitchen and heard the glass break. She then came face to face with the suspects in her dining room. Upon seeing the resident, all three suspects fled through the broken door empty-handed and left in a black car.
At 8:58 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle sometime during the day.
At 6:18 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s Toyota Prius.

At 6:21 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle. Detective Update: Detectives were able to identify three suspects who were responsible for the numerous burglaries that occurred over the weekend of the 11th. The suspects, all adult males, were arrested and are currently in custody.
At 6:23 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the front passenger window to the victim’s vehicle.
An officer went to a report of someone stealing items from an unlocked locker in the women’s locker room earlier in the day.
Officers were dispatched to a house for a burglar alarm activation. When the officers arrived, they found the home had been burglarized.
At 10:16 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and rummaged through the interior, but no loss was reported.
At 10:10 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and rummaged through the interior, but no loss was reported.

At 8:36 pm, an officer reports the victim was approached by two unknown suspects who demanded the victim’s wallet and cell phone. The suspects punched the victim several times before running off with his property.

At 9:22 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect approached the victim’s vehicle while the victim was seated inside. The suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and stole a purse before running off.
An officer went to a report of someone breaking a window of the victim’s vehicle and entering the vehicle; however, nothing was stolen.
At 9:09 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and rummaged through the interior, but no loss was reported.

Officers responded to a Lucky's where an employee was assaulted by a suspect who was stealing. The suspect picked up several canned food items and hit the employee in the head several times, causing several cuts. Store employees held the suspect down until officers arrived and arrested him. The suspect was found to be on parole for assault with a deadly weapon. He was arrested for assault with a deadly weapon for today’s incident, and he was placed on a parole hold. The suspect was booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer went to a report that someone had stolen one of the license plates from the victim’s vehicle sometime during the last two weeks.

An officer contacted a subject who was sleeping in his vehicle and discovered he had a warrant for his arrest. The subject was arrested, booked at the ECPD, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 5:46 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect damaged the mailbox to an apartment complex.

CrimeType: DUI and Possession of Controlled Substance
Description: At 12:21 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 43-year-old male who was found to be driving while impaired by alcohol. The man was also in possession of a controlled substance and later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 6:35 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the back window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property.

At 1:09 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 38-year-old male who, during a verbal argument with his girlfriend, struck her in the face, causing a bloody lip. The male was taken into custody, booked at the ECPD, and then taken to the county jail.

At 8:56 pm, officers responded to a report of battery and the brandishing of a weapon. Officers arrived and contacted the victim who stated a male entered his home and began to pistol-whip him. The victim was able to wrestle away the firearm but was being choked by the suspect, so he returned it. The suspect then left the scene. The victim suffered multiple injuries and was evaluated by medical personnel but chose to drive himself to the hospital. Several days after the incident, ECPD detectives arrested the suspect who was a friend of one of the residents. The suspect is currently in custody.
At 9:22 am, an officer responded to the report of a stolen red 1999 Honda CRV. The vehicle was stolen sometime during the previous night by an unknown suspect.

At 5:40 pm, officers responded to the report of a woman lying on the ground, possibly suffering from a medical issue. Officers located a 44-year-old woman who was found to have just been sleeping; however, she had two warrants for her arrest. As officers took her into custody, she bit an officer and then tried to spit on them. She was booked and transported to the county jail.
At 1:22 am, an officer conducted a traffic stop and subsequently arrested the driver, a 31-year-old male, for DUI. The male was booked then released with a citation to his girlfriend.

At 2:19 pm, an officer responded to the report of three suspects stealing facial cream and beef jerky products. They walked in and loaded several bags full of products and then fled on foot. The total loss was over $1000.00.
At 1:44 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the rear windshield to the victim’s vehicle and removed a backpack.
At 12:36 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the front and rear passenger side windows to the victim’s vehicle but did not remove any property.
At 11:31 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the front passenger window of the victim’s vehicle but did not remove any property.
At 10:58 am, officers responded to the report of an alarm and discovered the front door had been kicked in. An unknown suspect entered the residence and left quickly.
At 9:08 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s laptop from a table inside the business and ran to a waiting vehicle.

Location: Safeway parking lot 11450 San Pablo Avenue
Description: Between 4:00 pm and 4:16 pm, an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and stole a backpack.

At 6:15 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the rear license plate from the victim’s vehicle.

An officer contacted an individual while he was walking on the BART path and discovered he had two warrants for his arrest. The man was booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 12:37 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 53-year-old female from Vallejo for driving under the influence. The female was taken into custody, booked, and then released with a citation to a friend.

At approx. 4:30 am, the victim was robbed at gun point by two subjects. One produced a handgun and demanded the victim’s property, while the second suspect approached from behind and pointed a gun at the victim. The suspects then fled east on Knott Ave.

Location: San Pablo Avenue and Portola Dr
Description: At 3:19 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 25-year-old male from Richmond. The male was heavily intoxicated and unable to care for himself. The male was taken into custody and transported to the county jail.

At 1:51 pm, and officer saw two subjects fighting. One of the suspects grabbed a large landscape tree pole and swung it at the victim’s head but missed. The officers intervened and convinced the subject to drop the pole. The man was arrested, booked, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 12:41 pm, an officer responded to the report of a theft. The victim interrupted the suspect, who was in the process of lifting her wallet from her purse. The suspect pulled the wallet out, removed cash, handed it back, and then fled on foot. Battery – 5920 Cutting Boulevard: At 10:24 pm, an officer reports an employee of a business was leaving and getting into her vehicle when she was approached by a female. The female knocked on the employee’s door. When the employee opened her door, the female began to assault the employee. After striking the employee several times, the female fled to an awaiting car. The employee suffered a bloody nose.
At 10:46 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the rear passenger window to the victim’s vehicle.

At 5:58 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 49-year-old male who entered an unlocked and closed business and removed money from the cash register. The owner arrived on scene and followed the male to a nearby business where officers arrived and took the male into custody. The male was booked then taken to the county jail.

At 2:16 pm, officers responded to the report of a woman being hit in the face with an umbrella. The woman was waiting for the bus when a 41-year-old Oakland man approached her and asked what she was staring at. He then struck her several times with an umbrella, laying open a laceration on her cheek. Officers located the suspect nearby and took him into custody. He was booked and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 1:46 pm, an officer responded to the report of a catalytic converter theft. An unknown suspect cut and removed the exhaust component from a Toyota Prius sometime in between 1:30 am and 1:15 pm. (Est. loss $3000).
At 5:16 am, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop and subsequently arrested a 49-year-old male for an outstanding warrant. The male was booked and then taken to the county jail.

While looking for a missing person, officers located a male who was wanted for an assault with a deadly weapon case. The male, a 61-year-old from Richmond, was taken into custody without incident. The male was booked and then taken to the county jail.

At 9:13 am, an officer reports two unknown suspects entered the victim’s apartment via a kitchen door. Once inside, the suspect took a gaming console and games. The time of occurrence was 5:30 to 6 pm. A neighbor saw the suspects leaving the area but decided not to call police.

At 1250 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 43-year-old male from El Cerrito. The male drove past another officer at a high rate of speed before colliding into a brick flower bed and two parked cars. The male was heavily intoxicated, and at the conclusion of testing, he was taken into custody. The male was booked then released to his father.
An officer saw an individual that he knew had a warrant for their arrest. The officer contacted the subject and issued them a citation for the warrant.
An officer stopped a vehicle and found the driver had a warrant for their arrest. The subject was arrested, booked at the ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
Officers went to a call that two people had started a small fire on the school campus the night before. They found that someone had lit some small pieces of cardboard on fire.

Officers went to the report that the bank had just been robbed. When they arrived, they found the suspect had fled the business.  The suspect was arrested several days later and charged for the robbery.
An officer went to a call that someone stole the catalytic converter from victim's car the night before.

An officer responded to the report that someone spit on an employee at the store. Officers found the subject and detained him in the field. The employee identified the subject, and the subject was placed under citizen’s arrest. A parole violation was also added by the subject’s parole officer.

At 1:03 pm, officers were dispatched to the report of an unruly subject at Safeway. Officers contacted a 39-year-old Richmond man who was found to be heavily intoxicated. He was taken into custody and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:51 am, officers were dispatched to the report of a suspicious subject. They contacted a 25-year-old woman who was wanted on two Contra Costa County warrants. The woman was arrested, booked, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 11:51 pm, an officer reports a 24-year-old Berkeley man refused to leave the bar after being asked to leave several times. The man was determined to be too intoxicated to care for himself. The man was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:17 pm, an officer reports the right rear window to the victim’s Toyota Prius was shattered, but no property was stolen. The vandalism occurred between 5-10 pm.

At 8:41 pm, an officer responded to the area for the report of an intoxicated driver that hit the curb with his vehicle, causing his tires to deflate. Officers contacted the 52-year-old Richmond man and arrested him for driving under the influence. The man was arrested, booked at the ECPD, and released with a citation to a sober friend. Residential Burglary – 5600 block of Poinsett: At 9:13 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victims’ residence through an unlocked window and stole jewelry. The burglary occurred between 7-9 pm.
At 8:53 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect struck the victim’s vehicle, causing moderate damage.
At 8:00 am, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary. In between 5:00 pm and 8:10 am, an unknown suspect punched the door lock on a van and stole three packages.

At 1:51 am, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on a vehicle and determined the driver was under the influence of alcohol. The driver, a 39-year-old Richmond man, was arrested, booked at the ECPD, and released with a citation to a sober friend.

At 11:26 pm, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on a vehicle and determined the driver was under the influence of alcohol. The driver, a 36-year-old San Pablo woman, was arrested, booked at the ECPD, and released with a citation to a sober friend.

At 4:00 pm, an officer was dispatched to the report of a stolen 1996 Ram 1500 truck which occurred at 4:27 am. Tampering with A Vehicle – 1900 block of Downey Place: At 7:58 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect damaged the locks to the victim’s vehicle. The crime occurred between 01/30/20 and 02/02/20.
At 4:56 pm, an officer arrested a 39-year-old male for a Contra Costa County warrant. The male was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation.
On Friday, January 31st, between 12:00 pm and 12:30 pm, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.
At 7:12 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s catalytic converter and fog lights from their vehicle sometime over the past few days.
At 7:11 am, an officer located the victim’s unoccupied Dodge truck, which was reported stolen to the El Cerrito PD on February 2nd.
Sometime between 8:30 pm and 9:30am, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
Sometime between 7:00 pm –and 7:30 am, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
At 7:10 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect attempted to force entry through a rear bedroom window, but no entry was made into the residence.
At 7:10 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect attempted to force entry through a rear sliding glass window; however, no entry was made.
At 5:50 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from underneath the victim’s vehicle sometime over the past three months.

At 8:01 pm, an officer reports the victim returned home and interrupted the burglary of his residence. The three juvenile suspects ran out of the front door and east on Fairmount Avenue towards Ashbury Avenue. The point of entry was a window located to the rear of the home.
Sometime between Jan 30th and Feb 2nd, a suspect entered a locked office and took a checkbook. One of the checks was cashed at a check-cashing business in El Cerrito.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence and Hit and Run Arrest
Description: A 54-year-old male from Albany hit a parked vehicle and then fled the scene on foot, leaving his rental car behind. During the investigation, the subject was seen walking nearby, into traffic, and appeared to be intoxicated. The male was detained by officers and was heavily intoxicated. The male was arrested, booked at the ECPD and released with a citation to a responsible family member.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence and Collision
Description: At 9:46 pm, officers responded to a report of a non-injury vehicle collision and contacted a woman who was under the influence of an alcoholic beverage. The woman admitted to driving and hitting a parked car. She was arrested, booked, and later released to a sober family member.

At 10:52 pm, officers responded to a report of an armed robbery which had just occurred behind the Ross Department Store in the El Cerrito Plaza. A short time later, an officer spotted two suspects attempting to climb the fence out of the Albany Middle School. The officer detained a woman in the 100 block of Behrens who was in possession of the victim’s stolen property. A continued search of the area by neighboring police agencies was unsuccessful. The woman was arrested and later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 8:54 pm, an officer reports the victim was struck in the ankle by a paintball, which was possibly fired from a vehicle traveling east on Hill Street. No medical attention was needed for the victim.

At 11:58 am, an officer arrested a 43-year-old male transient who had a Contra Costa County warrant. The male was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 10:44 am, officers responded to a public assist call. Officers contacted a 32-year-old transient woman who was wanted for an out of county warrant. She was arrested, booked, and released with a citation.

CrimeType: DUI and Driving with a Suspended License
Location: San Pablo Ave and Hill St
Description: At 12:02 am, an officer located a vehicle in the roadway with two flat tires. Upon contacting the driver, a 56-year-old female from Oakland, she appeared to be under the influence. After an investigation, the female was taken into custody, booked, and transported to the county jail.

At approx. 11:37 am, officers responded to the report of a fraudulent transaction. They contacted a 54-year-old Vallejo woman who was impersonating another while attempting to withdraw a significant amount of money. She was arrested, booked, and released pending further investigation. Residential Burglary 600 block of Norvell Ave: At 3:54 pm, an officer responded to the report of a burglary. An unknown subject forced entry into the home and stole jewelry and personal documents belonging to the victim.

The other victim gave the second suspect his wallet and cell phone. The suspect looked in the wallet and saw it was empty and returned the phone and wallet to victim. The suspects then fled on foot.

At 10:11 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered a living room window and then opened it. The home alarm was triggered, likely scaring off the suspects. No entry was made into the home, and there was nothing stolen.

During the previous evening, someone shattered a window to a parked vehicle.

An officer conducted a traffic stop and contacted the driver who appeared to be under the influence of alcohol. After a series of field sobriety tests, the man was arrested and later released from the El Cerrito Police Department.

At 9:14 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the front passenger side window to the victim’s vehicle. The suspect ransacked the interior but did not take any items.

Location: San Pablo Avenue and Portola Dr

An officer located the vehicles on San Pablo Ave. The officer watched as a driver of one vehicle approached the other and challenged the driver to fight. After an investigation, it appeared to be a love triangle. CHP arrived to take over the investigation. Saturday, February 15th
An officer responded to a residential alarm activation at a residence and found that an unknown suspect had made entry into the home. No loss was reported. Friday, February 14th

An officer reports a customer inside the business left his coat behind while shopping. When the customer returned to retrieve the jacket, it was determined property from the jacket had been stolen.

Location: San Diego St / San Pablo Ave
Description: An officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and rummaged through the interior.

A 42-year-old female from Washington attempted to cash a fraudulent check. The female was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer located a 38-year-old female inside a vacant residence who had a warrant for her arrest. The female was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation.

At 7:58 pm, an officer reports the victim was jogging when an unknown suspect on a bike rode by and slapped the victim’s buttocks. The victim chased the suspect, but the suspect was able to getaway. Sunday, February 23rd Residential Burglary 800 block of Galvin Dr: An officer responded to the report of a garage burglary. An unidentified suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and stole the victim’s garage door opener. The suspect then accessed the garage at 2:07 am and stole a $3000 blue Specialized mountain bike. Brandishing a Weapon - 10680 San Pablo Ave. An officer reported that a 24-year-old Oakland man brandished a hammer and a knife at the victim when they refused to get out of his vehicle during an argument. The victim refused to prosecute, and all parties were released.

Location: Check into Cash - 11299 San Pablo Ave
Description: At 12:01 pm, officers responded to the report of an attempted robbery that just occurred. Two masked subjects entered the Check Cashing Center. One of the subjects attempted to scale the plexiglass barrier but was unsuccessful and fell. Both subjects fled from the business.
At 6:00 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s locked vehicle and took the victim’s dog. The dog was found the following day.
At 7:48 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the rear windshield to the victim’s vehicle.

At 1:10 pm, an officer responded to the report of a robbery that just occurred. The suspect selected numerous lobster tails and then loitered in the store, pretending to shop. He then walked to the exit with lobster tails falling out from under his shirt. When store loss prevention tried to recover the seafood items, the suspect punched an employee on the chin. 000 block of Ramona Ave: At 1:48 pm, an officer responded to the report of a burglary. An unidentified suspect entered a house under construction and stole tools sometime during the night.

At 6:36 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect, possibly transient, asked the victim for money. When the victim told the suspect, she did not have any money, the suspect pulled down his pants, exposing his genitals and threatened to sexually assault the victim. The suspect then fled the area on foot. Saturday, February 22nd

At 7:24 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the front passenger window to the victim’s rental car. The suspect removed two suitcases, which contained the victim’s passport, clothing, and a MacBook.

An officer reports the victim’s vehicle had been stolen sometime during the night.

Description: An officer located a vehicle that was reported stolen to the Richmond Police Department. The officer contacted the owner, who came to the scene and picked up their vehicle.

An officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property.
An officer reports an unknown suspect forced entry into the victim’s vehicle and removed property.

Location: San Pablo Avenue at Jefferson Avenue
Description: An officer pulled over a vehicle and discovered the driver had four separate warrants for her arrest from three counties. She was arrested, booked at the ECPD, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

An officer stopped a subject and found he had an outstanding arrest warrant issued by the ECPD. The man was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. An officer reports a physical battery occurred on the school campus between two students.
An officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property.

Location: San Pablo Avenue and Potrero Avenue
Description: At 1:59 am, an officer conducted a traffic stop and subsequently arrested the driver, a 27-year-old male from San Pablo, for DUI. The male was taken into custody, booked, and then given a courtesy transport home.
At 7:24 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the rear windshield to the victim’s vehicle. The time of occurrence was midnight to 7:15 am.

An officer went to the report that the victim's catalytic converter was  stolen from their vehicle.
An officer went to the report that someone had broken into the home sometime in the morning and stole items from inside.

Officers conducted a security check of the vacant lot and found four heavily intoxicated subjects. The four subjects were all placed under arrest, booked at the ECPD, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer arrested a 30-year-old man after he was found to be impaired by alcohol while operating a motor vehicle. The man was later released from the police department with a citation.
An officer recovered a stolen vehicle which was left parked and running.

An officer arrested a 22-year-old man after he was found to be impaired by alcohol while operating a motor vehicle. The man was later released from the police department with a citation.

At 9:29 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect pried open a newspaper stand in front of a business and stole coins.

CrimeType: Parole Violation and Possession of a Controlled Substance
Location: San Pablo Ave and Stockton Ave
Description: At 5:08 am, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop and arrested a 26-year-old male for a parole violation warrant. The male was also in possession of a controlled substance.

At 4:34 pm, officers responded to the report of a suspect throwing a large river rock through a porch window of the house. The homeowner screamed, scaring the suspect off.

At 3:41 pm, an officer responded to the report of a stolen catalytic converter. In between 12:30 am on 03/07/2020 and 9:30 am on 03/08/2020, an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from a 2007 Toyota Prius.

At 8:52 am, an officer observed a 34-year-old Richmond man holding a bottle of alcohol in one hand and defecating on the sidewalk in front of Walgreens. In between 5:46 pm yesterday and 3:08 pm today, an unknown subject threw a rock through the garage door window. The suspect then entered the garage. There was no apparent loss.
An officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and stole property.

Location: San Pablo Ave / Van Fleet Ave
Description: An officer reports a 47-year-old man was contacted during a collision investigation and determined to have been driving while impaired by alcohol. The man was arrested and later released from the ECPD.

An officer reports an unknown suspect cut a hole in the fence to a construction yard and stole copper wire. The suspect discarded the wire just outside the fence, which was recovered and returned to the victim.

An officer reports an unknown suspect approached the victim asking for money.  Not being satisfied with the amount of money he received; the suspect broke the taillight to the victim’s vehicle with a hammer.

At 4:06 pm, officers responded to the report of a subject fighting with Safeway Loss Prevention. The loss prevention officers detained a subject outside the store, thinking he had stolen items and brought him back to the office where they discovered he didn’t have any stolen property. The subject, a 20-year-old transient, punched one of the store security staff and fled the store. Officers located the subject at the Del Norte BART station. He was placed under citizen’s arrest and released with a citation.

At 1:16 pm, officers responded to the report of a residential burglary that just occurred. The resident heard their doorbell but didn’t answer. A few minutes later, she heard someone in the backyard. The suspect broke the back window to the residence but didn’t enter the house. The suspect subsequently fled the area.
At 2:18 pm, an officer responded to the report of a stolen vehicle where an unknown suspect stole a blue 1991Acura Integra sometime overnight.
At 10:09 am, an officer responded to the report of a stolen vehicle. An unknown suspect stole a silver 1997 Acura Integra sometime overnight.
An unknown suspect stole the victim's white Ford F-250 between 3:00 pm yesterday and 9:40 am this morning.
An unknown suspect refused to pay for food that he ate, hit one of the employees, and walked out of the business.
An officer located a stolen Honda CRV which was reported stolen to the ECPD on February 25, 2020.
Two unknown suspects stole frozen food and drinks from the store. The security guard stood in front of the doorway to prevent them from exiting., but the suspects pushed past him.
An unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and stole the ignition key.
An unknown group of suspects broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and fled the area.
An officer located a BMW M6 which was reported stolen to the San Francisco Police Department on March 12th.
An unknown suspect broke out a window to the victim’s vehicle on the 17th between 11:00 am and 4:50 pm.
An unknown suspect stole the victim's white Ford F-250 sometime between 11:30 pm last night and 2:30 am this morning.

A 24-year-old male from San Francisco was arrested after he attempted to steal chicken from the store. A Lucky employee attempted to stop him, but the suspect pushed past the employee to exit the store. Lucky employees tackled the suspect and held him down until police arrived. The male was released with a citation.

At 3:18 am, officers responded to a burglary in progress. A male subject was on the roof of a building and kicked in a window. With the assistance of a Richmond PD K9, officers detained a 38-year-old male from Richmond. The male stated he thought he had been shot at near the intersections of Lexington Ave. at Blake St., but with further investigation, his story could not be substantiated. The property owner asked that the suspect be told not to return. The suspect was released from the scene.
At 9:59 pm, three parked vehicles were hit, causing significant damage. The suspect vehicle was located unoccupied and parked on McLaughlin Ave. at Roosevelt St. in the City of Richmond.
An unknown suspect threw a rock through a front window of a residence.
At 7:41 pm, a suspect stole approximately $80.00 from the till when an employee turned away from the open cash register.
An unknown suspect stole the victim’s catalytic converter from their Toyota Prius between 6:00 pm yesterday to 7:30 am this morning.
An officer located the victim’s unoccupied Honda Civic, which was reported stolen to the Oakland Police Department on February 29th.

CrimeType: Trespassing & False Identity to a Police Office
Description: At 4:22 pm, officers responded to the report of a trespasser within the construction site at the listed address. The suspect, a 30-year-old San Francisco man, was found to be wandering the premises. He provided several false names. After the man was identified, he was placed under citizen’s arrest and then released with a citation.
At 12:57 pm, an officer responded to the report of a residential burglary. An unidentified suspect entered the residence via an unknown means and stole property.
At 12:51 pm, an officer responded to the report of a stolen van. In between 12:20 pm and 12:50 pm, an unidentified suspect stole a white 2009 Ford E250 van.

An organized retail theft group of approx. ten suspects entered Walgreens to commit a theft with empty bags. The suspects broke open a cabinet and stole all of the perfume which was inside. The estimated loss is approx. $3,000, and the damage to the cabinet is $1,000.

At 6:38 am, an officer responded to the report of a stolen vehicle. The victim left their garage door open overnight, and an unknown suspect entered and stole a gray 2019 VW Tiguan. The keys were left in the vehicle.

Officers responded to a robbery that just occurred. Four suspects entered the store and concealed a large amount of property in bags within minutes. The four suspects were confronted by security and then forced their way out of the store. A male was located during a check of the area, and he was later identified as one of the suspects. The male, who was on probation, was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer reports that sometime over the weekend an unknown suspect stole a catalytic converter from a Toyota Prius.

Officers were dispatched to an empty apartment and located a transient male and female inside. A representative of the property placed both of them under citizen’s arrest. The male and female were each issued a citation and released from the scene.
An officer located two unoccupied parked vehicles and discovered they were stolen.
An officer reports the driver’s side front window to the victim’s vehicle was shattered. The interior was ransacked, but nothing was reported stolen.

An officer observed a male matching the description of a Hit & Run from a be on the lookout issued by the California Highway Patrol. The male was detained and positively identified as the responsible party. The CHP arrived and placed the male under arrest.

At 12:22 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 32-year-old female from Fairfield. The female got into an argument with a Lyft driver about a ride. The female was highly intoxicated and was unable to care for herself. The female was taken into custody. After booking, she was released to a sober friend.
At 12:50 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s vehicle and rummaged through the interior. However, nothing was taken.

At 10:57 am, officers responded to the report of a robbery in progress at CVS involving a subject with a knife assaulting security. When officers arrived, they contacted a 31-year-old Oakland man. It was determined there was no knife involved, but the suspect punched the security guard after being told to leave the property. He was placed under citizen’s arrest, issued a citation and released.

At 4:21 pm, an officer was flagged down by the manager of CVS regarding a theft. The suspect, a 31-year-old Richmond man, was contacted on his bike in the parking lot. It was determined that he stole alcohol and hair products. He was placed under citizen’s arrest, issued a citation and released.

At 1:32 pm, an officer responded to the report of two auto burglaries. At approx. 1:15 pm, the victim noticed the alarm sounding and discovered two of his vehicles had broken windows and property removed by an unidentified suspect.
At 6:10 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s 2009 Toyota Prius. The time of occurrence was between 3:00 pm on 3/27/2020 to 4:30 pm on 3/29/2020.
At 10:29 am, an officer responded to the report of a theft. In between 6:00 pm on 03/27/2020 and 9:55 am on 03/29/2020, an unknown suspect cut out the catalytic converter from a 2008 Prius.
At 2:50 am, an officer contacted a 43-year-old man who had methamphetamine. The man was issued a citation and released at the scene.

At 4:39 am, an officer observed a suspicious vehicle and attempted to contact the subject inside the vehicle. The subject ran off, and the vehicle was later determined to have been stolen from Oakland earlier that same day.

At 8:46 pm, a 70-year-old woman confronted her roommates regarding their unwanted guests. The woman threatened her roommates and guests and subsequently placed under citizen’s arrest. The woman was issued a citation and released at the scene.

At 1:19 am, an officer reports two unknown suspects pried open a small storage room but did not steal anything. Vandalism – Key Boulevard / Cutting Boulevard: At 3:55 am, an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle.
An officer went to a call where someone burglarized a commercial building sometime last week.

At 11:42 pm, officers responded to a report of a hit-and-run collision with a possible DUI driver. Officers located the vehicle and arrested the driver for DUI. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. Vandalism – 11060 San Pablo Avenue, Big 5 Sporting Goods: Officers went to a call where a bicyclist broke a window to the business. While responding to the call, an officer saw the suspect riding his bicycle a few blocks away. He was stopped and arrested after an employee at Big 5 placed him under citizen’s arrest. The subject was issued a citation for the crime and released.
At 6:17 pm, an officer was contacted by an elderly victim’s daughter, who reported a large sum of cash missing from the victim’s home.

At 11:30 pm, an officer reports a known suspect assaulted the victim after the pair disagreed about money. Possession of a forged check, Identity Theft, False Impersonation, and drug possession – 9996 San Pablo Avenue, Mechanic’s Bank: Officers went to a call where a subject was attempting to cash a fraudulent check. The suspect was contacted, lied about his name, and provided officers a driver's license that was not his. When the suspect was searched, officers located heroin in his pocket. The suspect was arrested, questioned about the crime, and then issued a citation and released.
At 11:13 pm, an officer located a vehicle that had been reported stolen to the Richmond Police Department earlier in the day. The vehicle was towed, and the owner was notified.
An officer went to a call where the victim’s Green Ford pick-up truck had been stolen within the past day.
Officers went to a call where someone was reporting that their brother had assaulted them several days before. The suspect was not at the home when the officers went there.
At 12:52 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole a package from the victim’s porch.

Location: arm robbery, obstruction, and conspiracy – Ohlone Greenway and Potrero Ave
Description: At 7:50 pm, an officer reports three subjects approached a victim and demanded his cellphone. When the victim refused, the suspects forcefully took the victim’s shoes and phone. A witness followed the suspects and reported the incident to the ECPD. One of the suspects, a 15-year-old, was located at the Del Norte BART station by officers. The juvenile tried to flee but was taken into custody without incident. The juvenile was booked at the ECPD and then transported to juvenile hall.
At 3:04 am, an officer located an unoccupied 2015 VW Jetta parked in the rear parking lot of Sketchers. The vehicle was reported stolen in Oakland on 5/8/2020.

At 11:49 am, an officer responded to the report of an auto theft that just occurred. The victim left her 2018 Ford Fusion running while delivering packages. The vehicle was found abandoned by officers at Clinton Ave./Amador Ave, with most of the victim’s property still inside.
In between 1:50 and 2:00 am, an unidentified male entered an apartment complex and went to the mailroom. The man collected several packages and used a small pry tool to open mailboxes and steal mail.
At 1:01 pm, an officer responded to the report of a disturbance. On May 12th, a 52-year-old male, threatened the victim with a knife. The victim refused to prosecute.

At 10:09 am, an officer conducted a traffic stop and contacted the driver, a 46-year-old male. He was determined to be on DUI probation and operating the vehicle with a BAC of .07. The man was issued a citation and released at the scene.
At 6:35 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s bicycle from their balcony. The theft occurred between 6:00 pm on 5/12/2020 to 10:00 am on 5/13/2020.

At 3:02 pm, officers responded to the report of a shoplifting that just occurred. A 35-year-old suspect and a 44-year-old suspect were located a few blocks away drinking alcoholic beverages. They were issued citations for the theft and for being intoxicated.
At 2:26 pm, officers contacted a 48-year-old subject during a traffic stop who had a warrant. The man was released at the scene with a citation.

At 2:13 pm, officers responded to the report of a robbery that occurred at 1:48 pm. An unknown female suspect approached an elderly male and attempted to talk to him. The woman put a chain on his neck and removed the victim’s chain in the process. After a brief struggle, the victim was able to get his property back.

At 8:58 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 38-year-old female from San Rafael. The female entered a business, concealed merchandise, and left without paying. The woman was placed under a citizen’s arrest and released with a citation.

At 3:48 pm, officers responded to the report of a gunshot heard. Upon arrival, officers contacted the victim who reported that his tire had been slashed. The suspect, a 48-year-old parolee from Livermore, was located at the EC Plaza BART station. He was placed under citizen’s arrest and subsequently released with a citation.

At 12:52 pm, an officer responded to the report of a robbery. An unknown suspect selected items and tried to take them into the bathroom. When a clerk told him not to go into the bathroom with the items, he shoved her into the wall. Several other employees intervened, grabbed the man, and escorted him out of the store.

At 10:10 pm, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary. In between 9:30 pm on May 14th and 10:00 am on May 15th, an unidentified suspect smashed the window of a Mini Cooper and stole property.
At 10:17 am, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary. In between 10:00 pm on May 14th and 10:00 am on May 15th, an unidentified suspect shattered the window of a Toyota Camry.

At 10:38 pm, an officer responded to a video alarm at a construction site. Upon arriving, he found a 46-year-old male walking away from the site. The male matched the description given by the alarm company. The male attempted to flee from officers on foot but was apprehended a short distance away. The male was identified and released with a citation.
At 12:00 pm, the victim’s 1995 Dodge Ram 2500 pickup was stolen.

CrimeType: Provoking a fight, Obstructing / Resist an officer
Description: Officers responded to the block for a verbal argument between two females. One party had fled prior to officers’ arrival. The remaining party, a 27-year-old female from Oakland, was instructed to return to her residence. She walked away but quickly returned. She began yelling at a female inside the residence, challenging her to a fight. Officers intervened, and she was arrested. After the arrest, the woman was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:04 pm, an acquaintance of the victim arrived unannounced to collect property. A physical fight began near the vehicle, and the suspect used a baseball bat to break several windows to the victim’s vehicle. The suspect fled before officers arrived.
Between 1:00 pm and 11:00 pm, an unknown suspect entered the victim’s vehicle and stole clothing, electronics, and a wallet.
Between 5:30 pm on the 17th and 10:00 am on the 18th, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 1997 Ford Ranger.

At 9:30 pm, the clerk at Chevron was in a back room when she heard popping sounds coming from the front door. The front doors were closed and locked at the time. Video determined the male suspect used what was likely a glass punch and attempted to break the front window.

The victim stated he woke up on the morning of the 15th, and his 2003 Acura TL was missing. He located a note from a female friend stating that she had a family emergency and took his vehicle. However, after several days and numerous attempts to reach the friend by phone, it was clear she was not going to return the vehicle. The vehicle was located approximately one hour after taking the report.
An officer located a Ford F-150 that had been reported stolen to San Francisco PD earlier that morning.
At 12:14 am, a witness called and reported he saw two unknown race males enter his neighbor’s garage and leave with property in their hands. The suspects fled in a vehicle.

At approximately 1:00 am, two suspects entered the parking garage of an apartment complex and shattered the windows of two vehicles. The suspects rummaged through the interior of one vehicle but were ultimately scared off before entering the second vehicle.
At 12:01 am, an unknown suspect threw a rock through the front window of McDonald's.

At approximately 8:00 pm, the security guard escorted two theft suspects out of the store. They got into a vehicle and began to drive out of the parking lot towards San Pablo Avenue. As they drove away, the front passenger waved a gun out the window and pointed it in the air.
At 9:26 pm, the victim heard his truck start and drive off southbound on San Pablo Ave. The victim likely left his keys in the truck.
At 1:31 am, an unknown suspect threw a rock through the front window of Jack-in-the-Box.
While seated in her vehicle, an unknown suspect opened the door and grabbed the victim’s purse. The victim held onto her purse, but the suspect overpowered her.
An unknown suspect entered the dental office and stole several dental machines, tools, and supplies between 1:00 am, and 5:00 am.
At 7:29 pm, two customers got in an argument because the victim was not wearing a mask. When the victim got too close, the suspect kicked the victim several times.
At 12:01 am, an unknown suspect threw a rock through a window of the victim’s house.
At 5:11 am, an unknown suspect threw a rock through a window of the victim’s house.

At 5:39 am, officers responded to a burglary in progress. The alarm company provided a description of the suspect. A responding officer observed a 29-year-old man getting on a bus at the El Cerrito Plaza BART Station. The bus was stopped, and the man was detained. The man was on parole and in possession of the stolen property from Sprint. He was arrested, booked,  then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer contacted a subject that had an outstanding arrest warrant. The subject was issued a citation with a new court date and released from the scene.
At 2:24 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle.
At 8:33 pm, an officer located a stolen vehicle and arrested the driver and passenger of the vehicle. Both individuals were later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:02 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect attempted to steal a catalytic converter from underneath a vehicle. The suspects fled the scene upon being confronted.
An officer went to a call where the victim’s catalytic converter was stolen from their vehicle sometime in the last three days.

Officers went to a call where a suspect had just stolen items from the store and threatened the security guard. An officer located the subject a few blocks away, and he was placed under arrest. The stolen property was returned to the store, and the man was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:19 am, an officer reports a man was hit by a woman inside the store following a disagreement over social distancing.
At 2:31 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke several windows to the victim’s vehicle.
An officer went to a call where the victim’s catalytic converter was stolen from their vehicle sometime in the last two days.

An officer went to a call where someone had stolen the victim’s license plate from their vehicle sometime in the last week. Domestic Battery – 900 block of Arlington Boulevard: At 1:13 am, an officer reports the arrest of a man after it was determined he assaulted his wife and threatened another family member. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:17 pm, an officer responded to a video alarm at a construction site. Upon arriving, a 34-year-old male was detained after jumping the fence from the property. The male was placed under citizen’s arrest by a representative of the construction company and released with a citation.

CrimeType: Arson, Assault on an Officer and Disorderly Conduc
Description: At 2:18 pm, an officer observed a small fire by the electrical box in front of the laundromat. A 32-year-old transient male was seen holding a burning phonebook and lighter. The man was found to be heavily intoxicated and recklessly endangering the lives of others. While being taken into custody, he tried to spit on officers. He was arrested, booked, and transported to the County Jail.

Location: Disorderly Conduct – Shoplifting – Church’s Chicken - 11575 San Pablo Ave
Description: At 11:57 am, an officer responded to the report of threats at Church’s Famous Fried Chicken restaurant. A highly intoxicated and unmasked 46-year-old transient male confronted the employees and said, “give me food, or I’ll kill you.” A customer intervened and shoved him out of the store and locked the door. When officers arrived, the subject was banging on the restaurant's door, trying to get back in. The suspect was found to be in a large quantity of beef jerky, and assorted nut products which were stolen from Walgreens. The subject was extremely intoxicated and subsequently transported to the hospital for alcohol detoxification.

At 8:24 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect asked the victim for a cigarette. Before the victim could retrieve the cigarette, the suspect punched him in the face. The suspect fled the scene before police arrived.
At 9:57 pm, an officer reports that the victim’s brown 1999 Ford F-250 pickup truck was stolen sometime during the day.
At 3:20 pm, an officer responded to the report of an auto theft. Sometime over the last ten days, someone tried to steal a white 2009 Ford E250 van.
