At 12:21 am, an officer contacted a 50-year-old Richmond male that had a narcotic drug, drug paraphernalia, and an outstanding parolee-at-large warrant. The male was placed under arrest and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 11:11 am, an officer was conducting a security check of the Walgreens store when staff notified her of a shoplifting that just occurred. The officer detained a 48-year-old San Francisco male at Del Norte BART. The man was placed under citizen’s arrest, and field cited.
At 9:59 pm, an officer reports two unknown male suspects shattered the front window to this business and attempted to use bolt cutters to cut through the security gates.
While responding to the above call, officers noticed windows to this business were also shattered. There are security gates that prevent entry into the business.
At 3:48 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect forced entry into the business and stole property. The incident occurred at 3:34 am.

At 4:05 am, an officer reports a group of unknown suspects in 10-15 cars arrived at the business and shattered several windows. The suspects entered the store and kicked open an office door. Most of the merchandise was locked up, and there did not appear to be anything missing.

Location: Old West Gun Room 3509 Carlson Blvd
Description: At 12:15 am, an officer responded to the report of a burglary. An unknown suspect forced entry into the business during the early morning hours. The suspects were unable to access the store's area where firearms are stored due to the heavy interior fortification. There was no loss.
At 6:41 am, an officer responded to the report of a stolen black 1999 Honda CRV. The theft occurred in between noon on May 31st, and 6:40 am on the 1st.
At 11:04 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 36-year old male from Richmond for having drug paraphernalia. The male was arrested and released with a citation.
A victim agreed to exchange U.S. currency to Chinese Yen. The victim used an online app through her bank but never received the Yen.
Sometime between May 29th to June 2nd, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s green Honda Civic.
Sometime between May 28th and May 29th, the victim’s catalytic converter was stolen from their Toyota Prius.
An unknown suspect stole the victim’s red 2007 Yamaha motorcycle. The theft occurred between May 31st and June 2nd.
The victim’s catalytic converter was stolen from their Toyota Prius. The theft occurred between May 25th and May 28th.

Location: Carlos Ave at San Pablo Ave
Description: The victim’s Honda CRV was stolen sometime last night and was found to have been towed from a private parking lot in San Rafael.
An officer located a stolen 2007 Yamaha SVX1300 motorcycle, parked to the rear of the business.
At 6:31 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect painted politically themed graffiti on one of the BART path pillars.
At 11:15 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle sometime during the night.

At 12:13 pm, officers were dispatched to CVS for an unwanted habitual shoplifter. Before police arrival, a 49-year-old male left the store with unpaid items. When an officer attempted to contact the male, he ran away. Officers circulated the area for the male but were unable to locate him.
At 7:17 am, an officer located an unoccupied Honda CRV, which was reported to the San Rafael PD on June 2nd.

CrimeType: Arrest for Terrorists Threats and Probation Violation
Description: At 9:07 am, officers were dispatched to a male causing a disturbance inside the restaurant. The suspect, a 40-year-old male, walked behind the counter and attempted to take food and drinks. The male attempted to fight and threatened to harm the employees but was deterred by pepper spray. The male fled from police officers into oncoming traffic but was safely detained on Cutting Blvd west of San Pablo Ave. After an identification from an employee at McDonald's, the male was arrested and transported to the County Jail in Martinez.
At 8:21 am, an officer responded to the report of a stolen red 2000 Chevrolet Tahoe. The theft occurred at 4:15 am.
At 11:47 am, an officer responded to the report of a catalytic converter theft. In between June 1st at 1:00 pm and June 6th, 9:30 am, an unidentified suspect cut the catalytic converter off a Prius.

At 10:40 am, an officer responded to the report of a theft. In between 06/05/2020 at 8:00 pm and 10:00 am, an unidentified suspect broke into a toolbox in the rear of a Ford F150 and stole tools ($2300 loss.)
At 12:32 pm, an officer responded to the report of a stolen vehicle. The investigation revealed the victim’s 1997 Honda CRV was taken at 1:54 am.

At 12:37 pm, an officer responded to the report of a person fighting with security at Walgreens. A 38-year-old Richmond man punched the manager and the security guard after being asked to wear a mask. The suspect then threatened to kill the security guard and fled from the scene.
At 12:02 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle, but no loss was reported.
An officer responded to the report that the victim’s vehicle had been stolen from in front of their house during the previous night.
At 11:51 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s motorcycle sometime over the past few days.
At 5:30 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle sometime during the evening.

An officer was flagged down by a man who reported his girlfriend had stabbed him. Officers went to the motel and met the girlfriend who admitted to stabbing her boyfriend during an argument. The woman told officers that she stabbed her boyfriend after he choked her and threatened to hit her with a hammer. The male was arrested after he was treated for his stab wound, and his parole agent placed a parole hold on him. The man was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Officers were sent to a call where someone was breaking into a vehicle. Officers arrived and located a subject still inside the car. The man was taken into custody, and was found to have broken into several other vehicles in the lot. During a search of the suspect, methamphetamine was found in his pocket. The man was booked into the Martinez Detention Facility for the above crimes and for violating his probation.
At 7:07 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle from an apartment complex parking lot sometime during the evening.
Officers responded for the report that the business had been burglarized. The unknown suspects had forced their way into the business , stole property, and fled.

A subject went into Walgreens, stole property, and walked out. When security attempted to stop him, he held up two wine bottles and threatened to hit the security guard. Several hours later, an officer located the subject who he recognized from video surveillance footage of the theft. The subject was taken into custody, and some of the stolen items were recovered from his backpack.   The man was booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer located a stolen vehicle that had been reported to the El Cerrito PD on the 9th. The vehicle was returned to its owner.
At 1:55 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle from in front of a residence sometime during the evening.
An unknown suspect went into the rear yard of a house and stole tools during the early morning hours.
At 00:12 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect used an object to break several windows to the business. No loss was reported.
At 8:23 pm, an officer reports two unknown suspects removed a package from the side of the victim’s home.
At 9:52 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect set a mattress on fire behind the business, but the business was not damaged.
At 8:20 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s motorcycle approximately one hour earlier.
At 11:22 am, an officer responded to the report of a motorcycle theft. In between 5:00 pm on June 11th and 11:00 am on June 12th, an unidentified suspect stole a white 2012 Honda CBR 250.
In between 6:00 pm on June 11th and noon on June 12th, an unknown suspect shattered the front window to a vacant business.

At 4:43 am, an officer responded to the report of a grand theft. Last night, in between 2:30 am and 3:05 am,  an unknown suspect cut the catalytic converters from two vehicles. An additional vehicle had its catalytic converter stolen on 06/08/2020.
At 7:18 am, an officer responded to the report of an auto theft. In between midnight on June 11th and 7:00 am on June 12th, an unidentified suspect stole a green 1997 Saturn SL1.

At 2:59 pm, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary. In between 11:00 pm, on June 12th and 2:00 pm on June 13th, an unknown suspect smashed the window to a truck and stole $900 in power tools.

At 11:25 am, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary. In between 7:00 pm and 11:20 am on June 13th, an unidentified suspect shattered the window to a truck window and stole a toolbox containing $500 in hand tools. Auto Burglary 1600 block of Roger Ct. At 11:50 am, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary. At approximately 1:58 am, an unknown suspect shattered the window to a Nissan and stole several items. Two additional vehicles were rifled through on Roger Ct as well.

At 12:31 pm, officers responded to the report of a theft that just occurred. An unknown suspect entered Walgreens and immediately started knocking products off the shelf and damaging property. Frightened employees ran to the rear of the store. As the suspect walked through the store, he squared up on customers, flinching like he was going to hit them. The man then went behind the counter, grabbed handfuls of cigarettes, and then jumped the counter, destroying the COVID-19 barrier in the process.
At 5:21 am, an unknown suspect shattered a window to a garage. The suspect fled in a 90’s Ford Mustang eastbound on Potrero Ave.

CrimeType: Recovered Stolen Vehicle and Hit & Run
Description: At 7:35 pm, officers responded to a non-injury hit & run collision. The driver of the suspect vehicle fled the area before police arrival. The abandoned suspect vehicle was found to be reported stolen.
At 7:15 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke into the victim’s home and stole property between 10:00 am and 7:00 pm.

At 4:01 pm, officers were dispatched to a robbery that just occurred. The suspect entered the business and threatened to harm the cashier if she did not give him cigarettes. The suspect walked behind the counter, grabbed two packs of cigarettes from the cashier’s hands, and two additional packs from a nearby display. The suspect fled the business before police arrival.
On June 16th, sometime during the day, the suspect pried open a side door to the garage of the residence and stole keys to a vehicle parked in the garage.
At 8:16 pm, an officer reports a former tenant of an apartment complex threatened violence against one of the tenants.

At 9:30 am, a suspect entered the business and requested two packs of cigarettes. The clerk told the suspect he had to pay before receiving the cigarettes. The suspect threatened to choke the clerk. The suspect walked behind the counter, grabbed two packs of cigarettes from the clerk’s hand, took four more packs off the shelf, and walked out.
The victim's white Toyota Avalon was stolen sometime between June 16th and 17th.

At 1:27 pm, contractors came to work inside the theater when a suspect exited the business through the front doors. After reviewing the theater's video surveillance footage, an officer recognized the suspect as a local 36-year-old transient male. Video surveillance footage showed that on Sunday, June 14th, the male forced entry into the business via the theater's front doors. The male consumed alcoholic beverages and food while sleeping in the lobby area.  Today at 4:04 pm, an officer located the male suspect near Moeser Ln. and San Pablo Ave. The male was wearing the same clothing depicted on the video surveillance footage from the theater. The male was found to have two warrants. The male was placed under arrest, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the county jail in Martinez.
At 4:06 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect pointed a firearm at the victim and demanded the contents of his wallet before fleeing.
At 6:05 pm, an officer reports two victims were approached by a suspect who pulled out a knife and confronted them.

Location: Del Norte Liquors 11299 San Pablo Ave
Description: At 6:37 am, an officer responded to the report of a commercial burglary. Sometime during the early morning hours, an unidentified suspect forced entry into the store and stole property.
At 4:01 am, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop and subsequently arrested a 26-year-old male from Richmond for an outstanding warrant. The male was booked and then taken to the county jail.

CrimeType: Possession of Burglary Tools, Possession of Burglary Tools, and Probation Violation
Location: San Pablo Ave and Stockton Ave
Description: At 9:21 pm, an officer conducted a traffic stop and subsequently arrested the occupants, a 29-year-old male, and a 40-year-old male. The males had illegal drugs, burglary tools, and both were on probation. The males were booked and then taken to the county jail.
An unknown suspect forced open the rear door to the residence that was under construction and stole tools.  The incident occurred from Saturday June 20th at 7 pm to Sunday June 21st at 12 pm.

An unknown suspect shattered the rear passenger side window to the victim’s vehicle and stole over $2000.00 worth of medical gear and power tools.  The incident occurred sometime between 10 pm last night to 7 am this morning.

At 11:26 AM, an officer reported that over the past few days an unknown suspect used the businesses bank account number and cashed fraudulent checks totaling over $2400.00.  The transactions did not occur in El Cerrito.
At 12:33 pm, the victim’s wallet was stolen out of her purse while she was shopping inside the store.  Later in the day, the suspect used the victim’s debit card at the Target in Richmond.

At 1:20 pm, while the victim was outside his home, a 51-year-old transient male made threats to harm the victim and made a hand gesture like a gun pretending to shoot the victim.  The victim felt scared and stated that the same male had been harassing him this whole week.  The suspect fled prior to officer’s arrival.

Last night at approximately 8:19 pm, an unknown suspect used a bolt cutter to cut the lock off the exterior propane storage cage and stole twelve propane tanks.  The suspect fled in a white Ford F-150, which was driven by another suspect.   The suspect driver was wearing a yellow reflective vest and the other suspect was wearing a blue baseball cap, a black long sleeve shirt and black pants.

An unknown suspect stole auto parts and power tools from the interior of the victim’s unlocked parked vehicles.  The incident occurred sometime between Monday, June 22nd at 4 pm to Wednesday, June 24th at 8:21 pm.

CrimeType: Attempted Robbery, Warrant Arrest, Possession of Controlled Substances and Probation Violation
Description: At approximately 10:24 am hours, El Cerrito police officers were dispatched to the area because BART PD had a suspect detained for a possible battery.   A 30-year-old male from Richmond, followed the victim off the AC Transit bus in front of Churches Chicken. Once off the bus, the male attempted to grab money out of the victim’s hands and punched the victim repeatedly on the head.  A citizen flagged down a BART police officer in the area and BART PD detained the male.  The male had a warrant for his arrest out of Contra Costa County and was on probation for assault with a deadly weapon.  The male was also in possession of suspected methamphetamine. The male was transported directly to the Martinez Detention Facility.
The victims Chevrolet Van was stolen sometime between 2 pm yesterday to 5 pm this evening.

An unknown suspect shattered the rear windshield to the victim’s vehicle and stole miscellaneous items and personal bank checks.  The incident occurred sometime from 9:30 pm, last night to 9:00 am, this morning.
An unknown suspect shattered the west window to the business with an unknown object.   The incident occurred sometime last night at 6:30 pm to 7:10 am this morning.
At 9:54 pm, an officer met with the victim who located their own stolen vehicle which had been stolen earlier that same day. The vehicle was released to the owner who drove the vehicle home.
At 11:59 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect attempted to steal the victim’s vehicle but was unsuccessful.
At 2:43 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect picked up packages from the front porch of a residence and drove off.
At 9:51 pm, an officer responded to an alarm call and located a broken window to a business.
At 7:08 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the license plates of the victim’s vehicle.

At 10:45 pm, officers responded to the report of an alarm at a construction site.  Two individuals were located inside a fenced off construction site.  The person in charge of the property did not wish to pursue charges, so the subjects were released.
At 7:17 pm, an officer reports two individuals damaged each other’s vehicle following an argument over a parking issue.
At 11:15 pm, an officer report an unknown suspect stole property from the passenger compartment of the victim’s unlocked vehicle.

At 9:38 am, an officer reported that a subject, described as a white male, approx. 60 years old had set up an encampment on the victim’s property.  When the victim asked him to leave, the subject became angry and kicked pipes on the building which subsequently caused approximately $1000.00 in damages when the building flooded.
At 12:21 pm, an officer reports a bank employee was spit on by a customer following a dispute over the customer’s account.
At 12:51 pm, an officer report an unknown suspect entered the business and stole several personal care items from the store.
At 12:19 pm, an officer reports a suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and stole a purse.
At 4:31 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect spit on the victim after the pair argued over parking.

At 6:47 pm officers were dispatched to a residence for the report that a suspect just took a screen off a window and crawled into the apartment.  That suspect opened the door and let another suspect inside.  As officers arrived, one of the suspects was walking out of the apartment with two bags of property that had been stolen from inside and the other suspect was found sitting in a car on the street in front of the apartment.  The homeowner arrived and said he did not know these people and they did not have permission to be inside of his apartment.  Both suspects provided false information regarding their identities to the officers but once fingerprinted, their true identities were revealed.  One was found to be on parole for burglary.  Stolen property that was found on one of suspects was linked to a burglary that had occurred in Marin County the night before.  Both suspects were transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer went to a call where the catalytic converter had been stolen from a vehicle sometime in the last four days.
At 12:52 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole several packages from the front porch of a residence.

While the burglary above was occurring, a witness yelled at the suspect to get them to stop.  One of the suspects ran over to the car where this person was sitting, displayed a handgun and demanded his property.  Property was stolen from the victim and then the suspects then fled the area.
An officer went to the call of an armed robbery that had occurred in the parking lot.  When they arrived they found that someone had broken a window of a vehicle and stolen a wallet from inside.
An officer reports an unknown suspect stole a Leica total station survey device from that area where a utility company was working. The theft took place at 8: 25 am.
An officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s between 6:00 pm on 6/29/2020 to 11:00 am on 6/30/2020.
At 2:30 pm, an unknown suspect shattered the window to a mail truck and stole several trays of mail.
An officer reports that sometime during the evening of the 30th and morning of the 1st, an unknown suspect stole their Toyota Sienna van from in front of their residence.

At 2:16 am, an officer recovered a stolen vehicle which was reported stolen to the Oakland Police Department on 5/14/2020. The owner arrived to pick up his vehicle after it was processed for evidence.

At 6:18 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the front passenger side window of the victim’s vehicle. DUI – Cutting Blvd and I-80: At 1:59 am, an officer conducted a traffic stop and subsequently arrested the driver, a 45 year old male from Las Vegas after he was  determined to be driving while under the influence of an alcoholic beverage.  After booking the male was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.
At 3:03 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the victim’s bicycle from an apartment complex carport sometime during the evening.

An officer saw a subject who was looking into cars in a carport.  The subject was found to be on probation for possession of burglary tools.  During a search of the subject, methamphetamine and a smoking pipe were located.  The subject was arrested, booked at El Cerrito PD, and released with a citation.

Location: San Pablo Avenue and Central Avenue
Description: An officer stopped a vehicle for two vehicle code violations and discovered the driver was intoxicated.  He was arrested, booked at El Cerrito PD, and released to a sober family member.

An officer saw a subject who was attempting to open a locked metal clothes donation container with a crow bar.  The subject damaged the door to the container.  After the subject was placed under arrest, suspected methamphetamine was found in his pocket.  He was booked at El Cerrito PD, and issued a citation.
Officers were sent to a business on the report of a burglary alarm.  When they arrived, they found someone had opened an unsecured window, went into the business, and stole property from inside.
At 8:17 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the rear license plate from the victim’s vehicle sometime during the evening.
At 1:15 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed tools from the victim’s work truck while he was working on a job site.
At 11:00 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the front wheel from the victim’s bicycle which was locked at the time.
At 1300 hours, an officer responded to the report of an unknown subject breaking the windows of the victim’s vehicle and then apparently ramming it with another vehicle.

An officer responded to the report of a stolen vehicle.  The victim reported that sometime overnight, a subject entered their unlocked apartment.  Once inside, the suspect stole the keys to the Ford Explorer and then stole the vehicle.  The Ford was recovered in Alameda County after a vehicle pursuit.

An officer responded to this vacant property to assist the Fire Department with an inspection.  The officer discovered a motorcycle which was reported stolen to the CHP back in August. The vehicle was stored and the owner was contacted.

At 9:23pm, an officer reports a victim’s vehicle was vandalized by a male while parked in the parking lot. The male broke all of the windows out of the vehicle using a bicycle and a pair of bolt cutters. The male fled the scene prior to the arrival of the police.

An unknown suspect broke into a Toyota Corolla and stole cell phone charger. The theft took place between the 11th and the 15th of July. Recovered Stolen Vehicle- Barrett Avenue @ Key Route: At 5:45 am, an investigated a suspicious vehicle and arrested the occupant, and a 32-year-old San Jose man, for being in possession of a stolen vehicle and property from nearby vehicles. The man was also on probation in Santa Clara County for a felonious assault. The man was booked at the E.C.P.D. and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:17 pm, a known suspect entered Luckys and stole a bottle of alcohol. Officers authored a report which will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office for review.
An unknown suspect broke into a vehicle and stole several digital storage devices. The theft took place between the evening of the 12th and 10:00 pm Wednesday morning.

At 8:30 am, the victim and her boyfriend were stopped at a red light on San Pablo Avenue talking about their vehicle. When the victim said she was going to give the vehicle to her mother, the boyfriend punched the victim in the face and ran off with the keys. Officers searched the area for the boyfriend but were unable to locate him.
At 12:59 pm, an officer contacted a 42-year-old man during a pedestrian stop and learned the man had a warrant for his arrest.  The man was issued a citation and released at the scene.

At 10:47 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect assaulted a store employee who attempted to confront the suspect during a theft. The suspect exited the store with the stolen merchandise and entered a waiting vehicle.

At 8:59 am, an officer located a white pickup truck which had been reported stolen to the Richmond Police Department several days earlier. The owner arrived at the scene and took possession of their vehicle.

At 4:20 am, an officer contacted the driver of a vehicle associated with a crime from earlier in the evening. The officer arrested the 46-year-old man after he was found in possession of a controlled substance. The man was later released from the El Cerrito Police Department with a citation.

At 3:51 am, while investigating a suspicious vehicle, an officer located a 45-year-old and a 32-year-old male running from the vehicle. Both men were detained and were later found to have been trying to cut the catalytic converter out from underneath the victim’s vehicle. Both men were arrested and later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:34 pm, an officer reports the victim was contacted by her ex-boyfriend at her home where a confrontation took place. The ex-boyfriend fled the area prior to police arriving on scene.
At 12:50 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect used the victim’s personal identifying information to obtain a loan to purchase a vehicle.

At 9:05 pm, an officer contacted a 22-year-old man during a traffic stop and determined the man had been driving under the influence of alcohol. The man was later released from the El Cerrito Police Department with a citation.
At 7:37 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect damaged a motorized shopping cart from a nearby business by setting the object on fire.
At 3 am, several unidentified suspects removed wood paneling from a boarded-up window to the business. While removing the panel, the suspects were frightened and fled without gaining access.
An officer reported a suspect attempted to break into the business but were unable to break the glass door. The burglary occurred at 2: 58 am in the morning.
At 3:33 pm, an officer stopped a vehicle and learned the driver had a warrant for their arrest. The driver received a citation for the warrant and for driving without a license.
At 12:30 pm, an unknown suspect walked behind the cash register area and was able to get the cash drawer open. The employee, in fear for her safety, walked away during the theft.
At 7:11 am, officers responded to an audible alarm at CVS. Upon arrival, it was discovered that an unknown suspect tried to pry open the rear receiving rollup door. No entry was made.

CrimeType: Possession of a Controlled Substance for Transportation and Sales
Description: At 9:25 pm, an officer contacted a male in the parking lot of the Valero Gas station. The male was on felony probation for sales and transportation of a controlled substance out of a neighboring county. The male was found to be in possession of a large amount of a controlled substance and other contraband related to drug sales. The male was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility for booking.

At 11:35 pm, officers responded to assist the El Cerrito and Richmond Fire Departments with a reported fire inside the construction site. Information from the security surveillance company indicates a male intentionally set two portable toilets and a dumpster on fire. The fires were extinguished by the fire department before the main structure was affected.
At 6:08 pm, an officer reports the victim’s vehicle was stolen from in between 7/22 and 7/23/2020.
At 1:56 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect was chased from the business by employees while wearing a stolen pair of shoes.
At 2:53 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect assaulted the victim while inside the restaurant.
At 2:32 pm, an officer reports a group of unknown suspects entered the business and selected numerous pieces of merchandise before exiting the store without paying.
At 12:25 pm, an officer located the victim’s vehicle which had reportedly been stolen the night before.
At 10:59 am, an officer located the victim’s vehicle which had been reported stolen to the Berkeley Police Department several days earlier.

An officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on a vehicle and after an investigation determined the driver was intoxicated.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then released to a sober friend.
At 11:36 am, an officer reports a victim’s vehicle was stolen from in front of their residence by an unknown suspect sometime during the evening.
Sometime between 3:00 am on July 26th to 8:15 pm on July 27th, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s white Ford Ranger.
At 11:36 pm, officers responded to a report of two subjects setting a trash can fire near a business. Another trash can fire was reported in the 10700 block of San Pablo Ave. as well.
At 2:57 am, officers responded to a video activation alarm at a business.  The suspect forced entry into the building and took approximately $150 in coins and fled the business.
Sometime during the night, an unknown suspect pried open the side door to the business and stole a safe containing cash.

At 2:35 pm, an officer was dispatched to recover a stolen vehicle which was tracked to the location via GPS by the owner. The officer located an Isuzu work truck which was stolen in Oakland within the past thirty minutes by an unknown suspect.

Location: San Pablo Avenue and Central Avenue
Description: At 12:23 am, an officer was flagged down regarding a garbage can fire at the listed location. ECFD arrived and put out the flame. There was no damage to a nearby building.
Sometime during the day of July 28th, an unknown suspect stole an Isuzu Oasis from the street. The vehicle was located later in the day in the Richmond Annex.
At 7:13 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s home and stole art, clothing, cash, and jewelry.

At 12:00 pm, a detective followed up on a possible arson event which had occurred several days earlier. The detective learned an unknown suspect had set fire to an unknown object while near a local business.
At 1:35 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the store and then exited the store carrying a box containing merchandise worth several thousand dollars.

At 3:08 pm, an officer contacted a 47-year-old male during a security check of a nearby commercial property. The officer learned the man had a warrant for his arrest and later released the man with a citation.
At 11:56 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the business and walked out with multiple items of merchandise which had not been paid for.
At 10:56 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window at the business after kicking the window several times.
At 10:18 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s Toyota Prius sometime during the night.
At 11:06 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the business yesterday and stole a cell phone valued at $1K+. The theft was not discovered until the following morning.

At 2157 hours, an officer responded to the report of a theft.  Two male suspects entered the business at approx. 2155 hours.  One suspect went to the perfume display and broke the glass case, removing approx. $1000 in merchandise.  The second suspect held the door open and blocked the security guard from intervening.  Both suspects fled on foot.
Sometime during the night, an unknown suspect shattered the front door glass to the business and stole two electronic tablets for food delivery services.

At 10:46 pm, an officer attempted to conduct a traffic stop on a vehicle which was traveling the wrong way on the roadway. When the officer attempted to stop the vehicle it failed to yield and drove into a parking lot. The driver got out the vehicle and jumped a wall. The vehicle rolled to a stop. During a search, a replica pistol was located on the ground next to the vehicle. The passenger of the vehicle was identified and released.

At 11:27 AM, an officer was dispatched to a stolen vehicle parked in the parking lot of the apartment complex.  The officer recovered a Honda SUV reported stolen to Richmond PD on 7/21/2020.  The vehicle was released to its owner at the scene.
At 3:32 AM on August 1st, a Hispanic male suspect entered the business through an unlocked side door.  He was scared off by the alarm and took a windbreaker jacket as he fled.

At 11:10 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect set a pile of trash on fire behind a vacant business. The fire passed through a chain link fence and set some vegetation on fire. ECFD arrived and extinguished the fire.

Location: San Pablo Ave and Barrett Ave
Description: At 11:18 pm, an officer conducted a traffic stop and subsequently arrested a 34 year old male. The male was in possession of a controlled substance and smoking pipe. The male was released on a citation at the scene.

CrimeType: Possession of Paraphernalia and Burglary Tools
Description: At 2:39 am, an officer conducted a suspicious vehicle investigation and subsequently arrested the driver, a 49 year old male. The male was on probation and found to be in possession of a pipe commonly used for smoking a controlled substance and a shaved key. The male was released at the scene on a citation.

Location: San Pablo Avenue at Carlson Blvd
Description: An officer conducted a traffic stop and after an investigation, determined the driver was under the influence of alcohol.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 4:40 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle and rummaged through the interior, however no loss was reported. While investigating this crime, the officer found two other vehicles which had also been damaged in a similar manner.
At 10:54 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed a catalytic converter from underneath the victim’s vehicle sometime during the night.

An officer conducted a traffic stop and after an investigation determined the driver was under the influence of alcohol.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and released to a sober family member.
At 1:01 pm, an officer reports an unknown person broke a front window to the business earlier in the day with and unknown object.
At 8:49 am, an officer reports a woman intentionally drove her vehicle into her boyfriend’s vehicle which was parked in the parking lot.

Officers went to an apartment for the report of a Spousal Battery.  When he arrived, he learned that the victim’s partner had strangled them during an argument.  The suspect was arrested, booked at ECPD, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 11:03 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect forced entry into the business and attempted to break into a locked office.  Unsuccessful in forcing entry, the suspect fled.
At 8:15 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the rear license plate to the victim’s vehicle. The crime occurred sometime over the previous night.

At 8:10 pm, an officer responded for a call of a fire in a field.  Officers contacted two subjects on scene, one of whom admitted to starting the fire for camp.  The 36-year-old male was cited for starting the fire and having an open container of alcohol.

At 9:01 am, officers responded to the 500 block of the El Cerrito Plaza to a disturbance between and woman and her boyfriend. Officers learned the boyfriend arrested several hours prior had been released on bail.  Despite the fact the victim was protected by an emergency protective order; the male returned to the residence and demanded entry. The man was arrested several blocks away for the violation and booked into the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:56 pm, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop and subsequently arrested a 44 year old male for an outstanding warrant. The male was taken into custody and transported to county jail.
An officer reports an unknown suspect attempted to steal white van. The incident took place over night.

At 5:12 am, officers responded to a disturbance between and woman and her ex-boyfriend. When officers arrived on scene, they learned the female cut the male several times with a pair of scissors. The male received medical care at the scene and released, and the woman was arrested. After being booked at the ECPD, the woman was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. Recovered Stolen Vehicle- 1751 Eastshore Boulevard: At 11:44 am, an officer recovered a stolen Audi station which was parked along the south side of the property.
At 11:58 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle some time during the evening.
At 12:25 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property.
At 5:26 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect made mention of a firearm while arguing with patrons on a bus. No weapon was seen and no injuries were reported.
At 11:19 am, an officer reports an employee of the business was slapped by an unknown suspect at the end of a verbal argument.
At 11:43 am, an officer reports a known suspect entered the business and selected an item of merchandise before walking out and failing to pay.
At 12:27 hours, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle sometime during the evening.
At 7:04 pm, an officer reports a male suspect, who is known to the business, entered the store and stole alcohol.
An officer reports an unknown suspect shattered a glass door the business sometime during the night of the 16th.
An unknown suspected spray painted on the business on the 15th.

At 7:16 pm, an officer was investigating a suspicious vehicle behind the abandoned building when he contacted a male seated inside. The male was found to have an outstanding $350,000 warrant for his arrest. The male was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 3:26 pm, officers arrested a 42-year-old Richmond man for peeping into residences and touching himself in an inappropriate manner.  During a search of the man, officers located drug paraphernalia and a GPS ankle monitor which had been apparently cut off.  The man, who was on probation and a registered sex offender, was arrested, booked at the ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:23 pm, an unknown suspect stole a backpack from a delivery truck during a delivery. The driver confronted the suspect who punched him in the face and brandished a knife.

At 11:17 pm, an officer reports a male suspect, who was known to the victim, pointed a handgun at him. The suspect was gone from the area upon police arrival. The victim did not report any injuries as a result.

At 6:36 am, a 36-year-old female from Oakland was arrested after she repeatedly rammed the car in front of her in the drive thru for no apparent reason. The woman was arrested a short distance away, booked at the ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
During the morning hours, a parking enforcement officer located a 2016 Toyota Corolla which was reported stolen to the Richmond Police Department on the 14th.

An officer went to the report of a stolen trailer that had occurred sometime in the previous twenty hours.  The officer learned that someone had hitched up a trailer that had been parked in the lot and drove away.

An officer who was patrolling in the El Cerrito stopped to examine a vehicle that looked suspicious.  He ran the vehicle identification number and learned the vehicle had been reported stolen in the State of Washington about a month prior.  The vehicle was impounded and its owner was contacted.

At 4:30 am the homeowner was awakened by a rock being thrown through her bedroom window.  Police officers arrived on scene and saw a rock that had been in the front yard of the home was thrown through a window of the home.

Officers were sent to a call where two roommates were involved in a physical fight and one of them used a stun gun on the other.  The roommate who had been attacked placed the other under citizen’s arrest.  The suspect was booked at ECPD and issued a citation and released.
At 11:59 am, an officer report an unknown suspect tricked the victim into sending the redemption codes to several hundred dollars’ worth of gift cards.

An officer went to the report of a stolen motorcycle that had occurred sometime in the last two and a half hours.  The officer learned that someone had stolen the motorcycle that had been parked on the street.
At 8:13 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect cut a lock to an outside storage area and stole merchandise from the business.
At 7:17 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s motorcycle from the driveway of a residence.
At 12:24 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the rear window to the victim’s vehicle some time during the evening, no loss was reported.

Location: Cutting Blvd. at San Pablo Avenue
Description: An officer pulled a vehicle over for a vehicle code violation and discovered the driver had two outstanding arrest warrants issued to her by the San Francisco Police Department and San Mateo County.  While the woman was being placed under arrest she was found to be in possession of ammunition, which she is statutorily prohibited from possessing.  The woman also told officers she had controlled substances in her vehicle.  A police canine was called to the scene and the dog found suspected heroin in the center console of the vehicle.  She was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 4:01 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s work truck and removed a bag containing tools.

At 8:15 am an unknown suspect stole the victim vehicle from in front of their residence. Later that same day, an El Cerrito police officer located the vehicle abandoned on Key Blvd. at Conlon Ave. Burglary Report: 11645 San Pablo Ave. (Freeway Motel): At 9:14am, El Cerrito police officers responded to a report of a suspect trying to enter an unoccupied room at the hotel by breaking a rear window.

Officers were sent to a call of a subject in someone’s side yard that did not belong.  An officer located the subject walking a short distance away.  The officer found the subject had an outstanding arrest warrant issued by the ECPD.  The subject was arrested, booked at ECPD, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Location: Bed, Bath and Beyond 6000 El Cerrito Plaza
Description: On August 23rd at approximately 1121 hours, the suspect entered the employee stock room area and staged three vacuums near the emergency exit.  After the suspect was asked to the leave the store, he returned, stole the staged merchandise and exited through the emergency exit doors.  The suspect was described as a black male adult, in his 30’s, 5’8”, thin build, wearing a blue surgical mask, and a black beanie.
An unknown suspect shattered the passenger side rear window to the victims vehicle and stole miscellaneous tools. The theft occurred on August 22nd, at 6pm to August 24th at 3:20 pm.
At 10:33 am, officers arrested a 51-year-old man for a parole violation warrant.

At 1241 hours, a traffic officer conducted a traffic stop and contacted a 22-year-old Richmond man who was wanted on $560k warrant for an assault with a firearm and shooting at an inhabited dwelling.  He was booked and transported to the County Jail.

At 0841 hours, a traffic officer conducted a traffic stop on vehicle he noticed casing the neighborhood.  A 32-year-old San Pablo man and a 48-year-old San Pablo woman were contacted.  The male subject was wanted on an El Cerrito Police Department warrant for residential burglary and the female subject was wanted on an Identity theft warrant.  Both subjects were arrested and transported to the County jail.  El Cerrito Police investigators later served a search warrant in San Pablo related to the burglary investigation.

Around 1:30 pm, officers responded to numerous calls of a suspicious male walking in the area. The callers reported seeing a suspect enter an unlocked vehicle, walking into a backyard, and entering a shed to steal a bicycle. Responding officers located the suspect, a 55-year-old man, walking towards the BART path. The man was identified by the victims, placed under citizen’s arrest, and transported to the ECPD for booking. Upon the completion of booking, the man was released with a citation.

At 5:05 pm, an officer reports the victim was assaulted by her husband during a verbal argument the prior evening.  Detectives later obtained a warrant for his arrest and arranged for his arrest at the El Cerrito Police Department.  He was arrested and posted bail.

At 8:20 am, an officer arrested a 35-year-old man was arrested after he was found in possession of a vehicle which was reported stolen to the Oakland Police Department several days earlier. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

An officer arrested two adults when they were found sitting inside of a vehicle that had been reported stolen to the Richmond Police Department about a week earlier.  Both of the adults were arrested, booked at ECPD, and released pending further investigation.
At 8:43 am, an officer reports two unknown suspects forced entry into a residence through a sliding glass door and stole property.
At 4:09 pm, an unknown suspect used a bb gun and shattered a window to the front of the business.

At 9:36 am, an unknown suspect entered the business, selected two hamburgers, two drinks and walked towards the exit. The employee chased after the suspect and confronted him outside of the business. While outside, the suspect punched the employee several times and was able to get away with the property.
At 6:15am, an officer reports an unknown suspect assaulted the victim with a belt after a verbal altercation. The victim sustained minor injuries. The suspect fled the area before police arrival.

At 1:09 am, an officer contacted a male after a report of a suspicious person. During the contact it was discovered the male was on probation and in possession of a controlled substance and a smoking device. The male was identified and then released with a citation.

At 10:16 pm, an officer conducted a traffic stop and subsequently arrested the driver, a 26 year old female from Elk Grove, for an outstanding warrant. The female was identified and then released on a citation. Stolen Vehicle – Liberty Street and Key Boulevard: At 10:45 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime during the day.
An unknown suspect cut the lock to a garage and stole several thousand dollars’ worth of tools. The theft took place on the 1st between 2:00 and 6:50 am.

At 11:26 am, officers responded to business on the report of a person trying to steal a trailer. Officers arrived on scene and accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 23-year-old homeless man. The man was booked at the ECPD and then released with a citation. Hit and Run- Knott and San Pablo Avenues: Officers responded to a hit and run collision where the suspect rear-ended the victim’s vehicle and fled.

At 1:30 am, officers were dispatched to a strong armed robbery. Officers searched the area and detained a 42 year old subject who matched the description. The male would then be identified by the victim as the responsible. the suspect approached the victim and snatched his apartment keys from his hand. During booking the male threw his shoes at an officer, striking the officer on the legs. The male was also on parole and hold was granted. The male was booked then taken to county jail.

Location: 6400 block of Conlon Avenue- An unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from a Prius sometime during the night of the 1st.
Description: Warrant Arrest- 600 block of Kearney Street- At 3:23 pm, officers responded to a disturbance at a residence and arrested a 27-year-old man who has a warrant from Texas. The man was booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. Grand Theft from Person, Battery on a Peace Officer, Warrant Arrest,
At 4:16 pm, an officer reports a known suspect entered the business and selected a beverage before leaving without paying.
Officers responded to a report of a domestic battery causing injury.  After an investigation, the suspect was arrested, booked and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 11:05 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the front and rear license plate from the victim’s vehicle sometime over the past several weeks.

Location: San Pablo Avenue near Cutting Blvd
Description: Officers responded to the report of a domestic battery where a subject spat upon and pulled their domestic partner’s hair.  The suspect was arrested, booked and transported to the MDF.
At 12:53 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 32-year-old man who was found to be in possession of a drug pipe. The man was issued a citation and released at the scene.

An officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and found out the driver had a warrant for their arrest issued by Marin County.  The subject was issued a citation for the warrant and released from the scene.
At 2:16 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect forced entry into an occupied dwelling sometime during the night; however, no loss was reported.

An officer responded to the report of a robbery.  The victim met the suspect after agreeing to meet via an online bartering website.  The suspect produced a firearm, stole the victim’s property and fled with a second armed suspect. (Please feel free to use the Safety Zone on Kearney Street directly behind the Police Department for these sorts of transactions.)

An officer located a stolen vehicle parked in the parking lot of the motel.  The officer was able to determine the person who was in possession of the stolen vehicle.  The subject was arrested, booked and interviewed and released pending additional investigation
An officer responded to report of a vehicle theft after the owner left it running in the driveway.
At 8:36 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s pickup truck sometime during the night.
At 4:23 pm, and officer made a traffic stop and arrested the driver, a 62-year-old probationer, for being in possession of meth. The man was booked at the ECPD and released.

Officers were dispatched to take a package theft report where the suspect was captured on video. Later in the shift, officers recognized and contacted the suspect who was still in possession of the pilfered porch property. The woman was issued a citation for the theft and the stolen items were returned to the victim.
Several unlocked vehicles had items stolen from them between 4:30 and 5:30 am.

At 3:28 pm, a patron was inside of the business when the security officer confronted her about not wearing a mask. The two got into an argument, which led into the parking lot.  The security guard sprayed the unmasked patron with pepper spray and then several punches were exchanged between the parties.  This case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s office for review.
At 8:01 pm, an officer contacted a male in the parking lot who had an outstanding warrant for his arrest. The male was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility and booked.

At 2:34 am, an officer located an occupied stolen vehicle parked in the lot. The driver was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. The vehicle was stolen from Southern California, so it was towed.
At 10:43 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle sometime over the past few days.
At 11:31 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the business and selected a piece of merchandise before exiting without providing payment.
At 4:20 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the backyard of a residence and stole hand tools and power tools.
At 1:41 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole a package from the victim’s porch earlier in the week.

CrimeType: Throwing Dangerous Object at a Vehicle
Description: At 8:09 pm, officers responded to assist a male who reported he was being followed by another male who was trying to cause a confrontation. As both drivers arrived in the area of San Pablo Ave and Central Ave, one male threw a small firework into the bed of the other male’s pick-up truck. The firework detonated and caused damage to the plastic bed liner. No injuries reported.
At 9:54 pm, an officer located a red 1991 Honda Civic which had been reported stolen to the Richmond Police Department on 9/11/2020. The vehicle was released at the scene to the registered owner.

At 3:09 pm, officers responded to the area on the report of a carjacking that just occurred. Upon arrival at the scene, an officer spotted the suspect, a 47-year-old parolee, who has just jumped in front of a vehicle travelling south on San Pablo Ave, forced his way inside and then  ordered the driver to drive away.  The driver, fearing for her safety, drove way on San Pablo Ave.  Once the vehicle was stopped in traffic and officers quickly arrived, the subject fled from the vehicle.  The subject was detained, and officers recognized that he was in medical distress.  Officers immediately called for emergency medical services to respond and the subject was transported to the Hospital for treatment.  He was also found to be in possession of a large quantity of MDMA.  The subject was released from the Hospital several days later and taken into custody on a warrant related to this investigation as well as an outstanding parole warrant.  He was booked and transported to the MDF.

At 10:35 am, a neighbor saw an unknown person climbing through the window of her neighbor’s house. Once officers arrived, they discovered the residence was burglarized and the victim was missing a laptop.
At 8:30 am, the victim was walking when she was all sudden pushed down from behind. Once on the ground, two unknown suspects rifled through the victim’s pockets and fled.

CrimeType: At 12:46 pm, an officer reports the victim’s Honda C
Location: V was stolen sometime during the night of the 14th.
Description: Failure to obey police officer- San Pablo Avenue @ Cutting Boulevard: At 4:53 pm, an officer tried to stop a vehicle for a traffic violation. The driver of the vehicle drove off at a high rate of speed into I-80 from Eastshore Boulevard.  The pursuit was cancelled by the officer due to the reckless way in which the suspect drove.

At 4:50 pm, an officer reports the victim was confronted by two suspects who attempted to steal the victim’s phone. The victim was able to run away and call for the police. The suspects fled the area and were not located.
While investigating the above listed burglary, officers discovered a second residence had been burglarized around the same time. The victim reported a small amount of money as being stolen.
At 6:36 pm, an officer reports a known suspect entered the store twice today and took items without paying.

At 7:16 pm, an officer reports an unknown male was seen by Starbucks staff defacing the outside dumpster. The staff member asked the male to stop or they would call the police. The suspect ignored the staff and continued to write graffiti on the dumpster. The male left the area on foot was last seen walking on San Pablo Ave towards McDonald’s.
At 7:53 pm, an officer contacted a male during a suspicious vehicle investigation. The male was arrested on a no bail warrant out of SF County for burglary.

At 6:27 am, an officer reports a shoplifting suspect was injured during a physical confrontation with store security. The suspect threw a bottle of wine at the security guards and then slipped and fell on the wet floor.  The subject was released at the scent so he could obtain medical treatment.
At 10:37 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect forced open two locked sheds sometime during the evening; no loss was reported.
At 9:48 am, an officer reports two unknown suspects entered the business several days earlier and stole several hundred dollars worth of merchandise.
At 3:57 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the victim’s bicycle from a bike rack in front of the business leaving behind the front wheel and lock.
At 4:10 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed a child’s bicycle from the front porch of a residence in the neighborhood.
At 7:56 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s pickup truck sometime during the evening.
At 6:36 am an El Cerrito police officer reports that an unknown suspect stole the victims silver Kia Sorrento.  The theft occurred on
At 10:09 pm, an officer located a parked and unoccupied stolen 2003 Kia Sorrento which was stolen from the 400 block of Everett St on 9/21/20. The vehicle was released to the owner at the scene.

At 4:04 pm, an officer stopped a car for a traffic violation and discovered the passenger had a warrant for his arrest stemming from a domestic violence case in Berkeley. The man was booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Berkeley City Jail.

At noon, the same female from Burger King, stole a bottle of wine from Del Norte Liquors. The female was placed under citizen’s arrest, booked at the ECPD, and then transported the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 8:36 am, employees from Burger King called to report an unknown suspect jumped over the counter and stole tater tots. Officers spotted the female several blocks away, obtained her statement, and released her pending a review of the case by the District Attorney’s Office.

At 12:16 am, an officer reports a male was seen smashing the windows out of the victim’s parked SUV. The victim confronted the male and asked why he did it. The male told the victim he thought it was his “friend’s” car. The male was arrested without incident and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer reports an unknown suspect entered the secured parking garage, broke into a vehicle, and stole tools. The theft took place sometime during the night of the 23rd.
At 1:27 pm, officers accepted the arrest of a 43 year-old man for stealing from CVS. The man was booked at the ECPD and then released with a citation.

An officer responded to the report of a theft.  An employee reports that his backpack containing a laptop computer was stolen from inside the business sometime during his shift between 1:00 PM and 9:00 PM.

At 5:00 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the business and selected several items before leaving. Store security attempted to stop the subject and he brandished a knife at them before fleeing the business.
At 4:46 am an officer responded to a call of an arson.  An unknown suspect set a piece of construction material on fire in the driveway of the home.

At 2:00 pm, officers responded to the report of a theft.  Six suspects entered CVS filled several bags with cosmetics, while an additional suspect shattered the glass to the high-end liquor cabinet and removed property. The suspects fled the business with over $2000 worth of merchandise.

Location: San Pablo Ave and Central Ave
Description: An online viral video was discovered by officers. In the video a suspect, a male subject, called another driver a racial slur.  The victim reported that he was called a racial slur and the subject identified himself as a police officer and threatened him after a road rage incident on I-80.  An officer identified the suspect through follow up investigation and determined the suspect who is not a police officer.  A Detective was able to contact the suspect and interview him regarding the incident.  The case was forwarded to the District Attorney’s office.

At 7:08 pm, an officer responded to the report of a mob theft at Walgreens.  A group of five suspects entered a business and broke a glass case. The group then removed merchandise and then fled the store. As they fled out the business, one of the suspects pepper-sprayed a security guard who attempted to stop them.

Location: Great American Dry Cleaners, 215 El Cerrito Plaza
Description: At 10:24 am, an officer responded to the report of a commercial burglary.  It was determined that at approximately 11:00 pm on the 28th, an unknown suspect entered the business, pried open two cash registers, and stole $350.
At 6:36 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 39-year-old female. The female was wanted on a warrant out of El Dorado County. The female was identified and then taken to county jail.
An unknown suspect stole a 1980 Suzuki motorcycle sometime during the night.
At 2:20 hours, an officer responded to the report of a burglary.  An unknown suspect attempted to break into a laundry room but was unsuccessful.
At 7:46 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect threw a metal garbage can lid through a window of the business.  Employees heard the crashing of the window but did not see a suspect.

Location: Mechanic’s Bank 9996 San Pablo Ave./Chase Bank 9950 San Pablo Ave
Description: At 10:41 am, officers responded to the Mechanics Bank on the report of an attempted bank robbery via a demand note. The teller did not give the suspect any money.  While responding to the scene, officers located the suspect attempting to flee the area.  The man was detained and identified as the suspect.  During the investigation, officers also checked in at Chase Bank and determined the suspect has attempted to rob that bank via a demand note as well.  Detectives located the demand notes discarded nearby.  The suspect was booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
Sometime during the night of the 30th, an unknown suspect broke into a residence which was under construction. The suspects stole tools and a large appliance.
At 5:47 pm, an officer reports a group of juveniles entered the business and broke open three glass display cases. They then loaded bags with property and then fled the area.

CrimeType: Failure to Yield, Hit and Run
Description: At 02:52 am, an officer attempted to make a traffic stop. The vehicle did not yield, and a short pursuit ensued. The driver of the suspect vehicle failed to negotiate a turn and collided with a street sign. The driver fled on foot from the vehicle.

At 12:35 am, officers were dispatched to a report of residential burglary in progress. The victim awoke to finding a subject inside her residence.  The subject kicked in a side door, waking the homeowner who then confronted the subject.  Officers arrived on scene and with the use of a police dog, cleared the residence.  The subject fled from the scene.

At 9 PM, an officer monitoring a car meet observed a 22-year old male from Brentwood driving recklessly, doing "donut" burnouts around the roundabout in the El Cerrito Plaza.  The male was issued a citation for reckless driving and his vehicle was impounded.
An unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter to the victim’s Toyota Prius.  The theft occurred sometime from September 20th at 12 PM to October 1st at 8:45 AM.
An unknown suspect shattered the rear passenger side window to the victim’s vehicle and stole a subwoofer.  The theft occurred sometime from September 27th at 6 PM to October 1st at 6 PM.

CrimeType: Arrest for Stolen Vehicle, Possession of Burglary tools and Stolen Property
Description: At approximately 6:44 AM, an El Cerrito police officer observed a stolen vehicle driving in the 1300 block of Kearney Street.   The vehicle fled the area and was located a short time later in the 6500 block of Donal Ave.  The vehicle has been reported stolen earlier in the evening to the Richmond Police Department.   The driver, a 37-year-old female from Richmond, was arrested after she was found hiding nearby.  The female was found with a shaved key that started the vehicle, and credit cards that did not belong to her.  The female was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 8:40 PM, an officer reports that two unknown suspects robbed the victims of their cell phones, a purse, and car keys. The suspects attempted to steal the victims’ car but were unable to operate the push start feature.  On October 6th, ECPD assisted an outside agency with the investigation of another carjacking with similar suspects that occurred just outside the El Cerrito border and located the victim’s vehicle unoccupied minus the car keys.  The vehicle was returned to the owner.  On Oct. 7th, an El Cerrito Police Department Traffic officer observed that same vehicle being driven recklessly near the intersection of Eastshore Blvd./Potrero Ave and conducted a traffic stop.  The driver, a 17-year-old male was arrested for possession of stolen vehicle.  A 15-year-old male was arrested after a loaded firearm was located inside the vehicle.  A female passenger was arrested for outstanding out of county warrants.

CrimeType: Arrest for Burglary, Grand Theft, and Conspiracy
Description: At 7:26 PM, an officer reports the arrest of two juveniles from San Francisco and a 19-year old male from Sacramento after a theft at the business. The suspects shattered a locked display case and stole over $2,500 in cosmetics. The suspects were booked at the ECPD. The two juveniles were released to relatives and the adult was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 8:10 AM, El Cerrito police officers responded to an attempted burglary that just occurred.  The suspect forced open the front door and confronted the homeowner.  The suspect fled the area in a black Saturn Ion.  The suspect is described as a black male adult, with a light complexion, 20-30 years old, approximately 6 feet tall, with a heavy build, wearing dark clothing.

At 9:30 AM, the victim left his Dodge 2500 van running and unattended, and an unknown suspect stole the truck and drove southbound on San Pablo Ave. At 12:46 PM, an El Cerrito police officer located the van traveling on Stockton Ave. at Pomona Ave.  The driver, a 38-year-old female, was arrested.  The female was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility and the van was released to the owner.

At 5:20 PM, the victim located their stolen vehicle in the 7-11 parking lot.  A 43-year-old male from San Francisco, attempted to drive out of the parking lot with the vehicle, but was immediately detained and arrested by responding officers.  The male was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 1:41 PM, the victim received a fake Amazon email regarding a bogus item she thought she purchased.  The victim called the phone number to stop the bogus order.  The suspect convinced the victim to purchase gift cards, and provide the card numbers to them, in order to refund the order.

At 12:16 PM, an officer reported that an unknown suspect(s) entered a vacant residence and set up camp inside.  The suspect(s) stayed in the residence from 6:00 pm on October 3rd to 6:00am on Oct. 5th.

Location: Waldo Lane at San Pablo Avenue
Description: Sometime during the night of the 5th, an unknown suspect entered the construction area and stole several items belonging to EBMUD.

Location: San Pablo Ave and Knott Ave
Description: At 11:46 PM, an officer attempted to conduct a traffic stop on a vehicle. The vehicle field to yield and fled from officers. Officers pursued the vehicle for a short distance but cancelled the pursuit over public safety concerns.
An unknown suspect entered the El Cerrito Corporation Yard and rummaged through several vehicles sometime during the night of the 5th.

At 7:38 PM, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop and subsequently arrested a 42-year-old male out of Richmond for an outstanding warrant. The male was issued a notice to appear in court and released.
An officer reports an unknown suspect stole a 1984 Toyota pickup truck sometime during the night of the 7th.
An officer responded to the report of a theft of a GMC van.  The van was stolen in between noon and 4:30 PM.

The ECPD learned that a car meet was being planned at the El Cerrito Plaza on Friday, October 9th at 8 PM.  Similar car meets at the EC Plaza resulted in sideshow activity, so the Department contacted the property management company who elected to close the EC Plaza with barricades.  Additionally, the Department arranged for assistance from the California Highway Patrol and support from the Contra Costa County Sheriff Office’s airship.  ECPD motor and patrol officers conducted high visibility patrol in the area as a deterrent.  Approximately 300 vehicles arrived and then promptly left.

Location: San Pablo Ave. at Conlon Ave.
Description: At 10:46 AM, an El Cerrito police officer contacted a male for having an open container of alcohol in public.  The 36-year-old male from Richmond had a warrant for his arrest out of Contra Costa County for battery.  The male was given a citation and was released.

At 2:56 PM, an El Cerrito police officer reports that an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter to the victims Toyota Prius.  The theft occurred sometime from September 3rd at 6:00am to October 8th at 11:15am.

Location: San Pablo Avenue near Carlson Blvd.
Description: At about midnight an officer pulled over a vehicle for traveling approximately 100 miles per hour on San Pablo Avenue and found the driver to be heavily intoxicated.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An unknown suspect stole the victim’s motorcycle from in front of their residence.  The theft occurred sometime on October 8th at 5 PM to October 9th at 12 PM.

At 7:52 AM, an El Cerrito police officer located an unoccupied Ford Crown Victoria parked in the parking lot, which was reported stolen out of Forestville, CA on October 10th.  The victim was notified, and the vehicle was stored.
An unknown suspect shattered the window to a vehicle and stole a car jack sometime during the night.

Location: & Hate Crime- Ohlone Greenway @ Eureka Avenue
Description: At 5:30 pm, the victim was walking along the BART path when he was confronted by several suspects. The suspects demanded the victim’s property and then punched him several times. After punching the victim, the suspect made several racially derogatory statements and fled.
An unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from a Prius between the 5th and the 12th.

At 9:49 pm, an unknown suspect attempted to take the victim’s vehicle while the victim’s girlfriend was still in the vehicle. The girlfriend ran into a business and told the victim. The victim confronted the suspect and removed him from the vehicle. The suspect then fled on foot.

An unknown suspect shattered the window to a vehicle and stole numerous tools. The theft took place between the 11th and 12th at 3:00 pm. Petty Theft- 7100 block of Cutting Boulevard At 12:30 pm, an unknown suspect stole a package from the front porch of the victim’s home. Vandalism- 6060 Central Avenue, Nations At 12:26 pm, an unknown female shattered the front window of Nations. Forgery- 11299 San Pablo Avenue, California Check Cashing An unknown suspect cashed a forged check in February.

At 5:24 am, an officer located an occupied stolen vehicle. The vehicle was reported stolen to Richmond Police minutes before. The occupants of the vehicle, a 44 year old male, were taken into custody. The male subsequently released with a signed promise to appear.
At 5:05 am, an officer reports the recovery of a stolen motorcycle. The motorcycle was reported stolen to Berkeley Police in July.

An unknown suspect stole a bicycle from the enclosed garage on the 7th. Possession of a Controlled Substance, Misappropriation of property – 10600 block of San Pablo Ave: At 1:28 am, an officer contacted a subject and discovered the subject was in possession of a controlled substance. The search also revealed the subject was in possession of another person’s wallet. The subject was released from the scene pending follow-up investigation.

At 9:31 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 49-year-old female and a 67-year-old male. The male and female, who are married, got into a physical altercation where they battered each other. Both were taken into custody, booked, and then taken to county jail.
During the morning hours, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 1998 Honda Prelude.

At 3:08 pm, an officer reports an unknown group of suspects entered the business and stole a large quantity of merchandise. During the theft, the doors to the business were damaged by one of the suspects.
At 4:52 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime during the evening.
At 4:50 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the rear license plate from the victim’s vehicle sometime during the night.
At 1:07 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole a license plate from the victim’s vehicle sometime earlier in the week.
At 2:10 pm, an officer reports a suspect entered a shed on the property and removed a weed whacker.
At 2:04 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle sometime during the evening.

At approximately 6:00 pm, the owner of a truck parked in a vacant lot saw someone slashing the tires through a video surveillance system.  When the officer arrived, he found two tires on a vehicle had been slashed and the suspect was gone.

At 1:13 am, an officer conducted a traffic stop and found the driver was on parole.  During a parole search both the driver and passenger were found to be in possession of controlled substance.  Both subjects were arrested, booked at ECPD, and then issued a citation and released.

At 1:04 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle during the evening and took a wallet. The victim was alerted to the crime after learning several unauthorized transactions had occurred on the credit cards from inside the stolen wallet.
At 11:16 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered a building via an unsecured window and stole property from inside.

At 5:09 pm, an El Cerrito police officer arrested a 35-year-old female pharmacy technician who stole approximately $4200.00 worth of narcotics in the last three months.  The female was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the County Jail in Martinez.
At 7:24 am, an officer located the victim’s stolen vehicle which had been reported stolen 3 days earlier. The vehicle was released to the victim at the scene.
At 10:06 pm, an officer recovered a truck which was reported stolen to the Berkeley Police Department in September.

At 7:00 pm, an officer reports the citizen’s arrest of a 35-year-old male from San Francisco. The male entered a business selected items and then left without paying. The male was booked and then released on a citation.
At 09:53 am, an officer responded to the report of an unknown suspect stealing a catalytic convertor from a Toyota Prius.  The theft occurred overnight.
At 6:25 am, an unknown suspect entered Denny’s and forcibly ripped the iPad from the hostess table.
At 4:16 pm, an officer responded to the report of an unknown suspect stealing a catalytic convertor from the suspect’s vehicle.   The theft took place sometime over the weekend.
At 4:11 pm, officers responded to the report of an unknown suspect stealing a catalytic converter from a Prius.  The suspect fled prior to police arrival.

At 5:03 pm, officers responded to a report of a male subject physically assaulting a woman.  As officers arrived, the suspect fled on foot into the adjacent residential area while holding a firearm.  The victim was located, and aid and resources were offered.  Officers set a perimeter around the area.  An extensive search of the area to ensure public safety was conducted with the use of airships, UAS and a Police K-9.  The suspect was not located.  A loaded, high capacity firearm magazine was found in the suspect’s vehicle.

At 0830 am, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary.   During the night of the 20th, an unknown suspect broke a window to a truck’s camper shell. The suspect then crawled into the bed of the truck and broke out the back window in an attempt to steal the vehicle.
At 9:00 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to a City of El Cerrito recreation building sometime during the evening.

At 7:45 PM officers were dispatched to a vehicle stopped in the middle of the intersection.  When the officers arrived, they found a woman passed out in the driver seat.  She was determined to be under the influence of controlled substances.  She was transported to the hospital via ambulance and later then booked at the Martinez County Jail.

At 8:24 PM officers were dispatched to the report of a bullet coming through the window of home.  Officers arrived and found that a bullet had shattered a sliding glass door and was located inside the residence.  No suspect was seen.
At 1:15 AM an officer pulled a vehicle over that had been reported stolen to the Vallejo Police Department.  The driver was arrested, booked at the ECPD, and then released on a citation.

At 2:49 pm, an officer contacted 37-year-old man to the rear of the closed business and found the man had a warrant for his arrest. The man was arrested and later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:56 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect approached the victim inside the business and rubbed up against the victim inappropriately several times while watching pornography on his phone. The crime occurred the day prior but was not reported until the 24th.

Location: Automatic Firearm – 11900 block of San Pablo Avenue
Description: At 3:14 AM officers were dispatched to a person in the roadway.  When officers arrived, they found a victim suffering from multiple gunshot wounds.  The victim ran to that location after being shot at the above address.  The victim was taken to a hospital with apparent non-life-threatening injuries.  The suspect fled onto the Ohlone Greenway.  Detectives arrived on scene and began to investigate the crime.

At 8:17 PM officers were dispatched to the report of a battery where the suspect inappropriately touched a juvenile victim while she was shopping.  The suspect was stopped outside of the store and was placed under citizen’s arrest after he was identified by the victim.
