The suspect was also identified as the suspect in the battery cases which was reported on Oct. 24th.  He was booked at ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 2:56 pm, an officer reports the victim inadvertently set his wallet down and when he returned the wallet was gone.  A review of camera surveillance footage showed the suspect pick up the wallet and walk away.
At 2:18 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business dressed in a delivery uniform and then exited the store with merchandise totaling $1K+.
At 2:39 AM an officer found an unoccupied stolen vehicle parked at this location.  The vehicle was reported stolen to the Fairfield Police Department.
At 4:51 pm, an officer reported a group of three juveniles entered the store and stole numerous items before fleeing to the rear of the business.

At 7:56 am, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 40-year-old woman after she flattened the victim’s vehicle’s tires with a pickaxe and then chased the victim around the vehicle with the axe.  The woman was detained by responding officers without incident and subsequently issued a citation and released at the scene due to a medical condition.
At 11:35 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the business and stole $1K+ of merchandise before fleeing the store.
An unknown suspect stole a Yamaha motorcycle which was parked along the curb. The theft took place sometime during the night of the 26th.

At 8:17 am, a male subject battered a security guard who had asked the subject to leave.  The security guard was punched in the face several times. The suspect was later identified by officers a local transient.

During the early evening hours, an unknown suspect cut the fencing to the City of El Cerrito Corporation Yard. Once inside the premises, the suspect gathered numerous items, placed them into a pile, but left without them.
An unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from a Prius sometime during the early morning hours of the 27th.

At 5:31 am, an officer reports two masked subjects entered a business carrying power tools and a large pry tool.  The clerk was too terrified to call for help.  They took turns cutting open a Bitcoin ATM machine and looking out the front.  After removing the cash box from the machine, the suspects fled to an awaiting vehicle and fled the area.

During the night of the 27th, a suspect entered an open garage and stole two bicycles and a leaf blower.  In the early morning hours, prior to the report being made, an officer stopped a subject pushing a road bike and carrying the leaf blower.  The subject was a 22-year-old Richmond man who was on three separate probation dockets from various offenses including burglaries in El Cerrito.  The leaf blower was collected at that time for observation and then returned to the property owner after the burglary was discovered.

At 8:29 am, officers were dispatched to a call of a person looking into apartments. Officers arrived on scene and contacted a 38-year-old man who was wanted on a parole violation warrant.  The man was booked and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 8:31 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 19-year-old woman who was observed by the officer running from the business with stolen property. The woman also had a warrant for her arrest and was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Location: San Pablo Avenue at Moeser Lane
Description: At 11:54 pm an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on a vehicle and found the driver was on probation for robbery.  During a probation search the officer found methamphetamine, cocaine, and heroin in the vehicle.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then issued a citation and released.

At 1:52 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 20-year-old woman while she was attempting to pass a fictitious check at the business. The woman was transported to the El Cerrito Police Department and later released on a citation.

At 2:33 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the parking lot of the business and broke a window to a vehicle parked there. The suspect rummaged through the vehicle but no loss was reported.
At 11:44 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 54-year-old man after determining the man had a warrant. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:53 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect shoved the victim’s to the ground while the victim was riding a bicycle.

At 1:53 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 62-year-old man who was found driving a vehicle reported stolen to the Emeryville Police Department several days earlier. The man also had several warrants for his arrest and was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer responded to the report of a residential burglary.  The homeowner reported someone entered their home and stole property from inside earlier in the day.

At 3:30 am an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on a vehicle.  One of the occupants of the vehicle was on probation and the driver was unlicensed.  During a search both occupants were found to be in possession of Methamphetamine.
At 2:32 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the front passenger side window to the victim’s vehicle and rummaged through the interior; however no loss was reported.
At 2:15 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the rear passenger window to the victim’s vehicle. No entry was made to the vehicle and no loss was reported.
At 1:47 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle sometime during the evening.
At 3:11 pm, an officer reports the rear plate was stolen from the victim’s vehicle sometime during the past several days.
While at the above call the officer noticed another vehicle that had the window broken.  The victim reported nothing had been stolen from inside the vehicle.

An officer responded to the report of vehicle tampering.  The victim reported someone broke the window to their vehicle sometime between 1:30 pm and 4:30 pm.  The suspect went through the vehicle but did not steal anything.

At 12:52 am an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on a vehicle and found the subject had two no bail Felony warrants for their arrest.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:46 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle sometime during the evening and rummaged through the interior; no loss was reported.

At 3:48 am officers were dispatched to the report that someone was stealing a catalytic converter off of a Toyota Prius parked in a car port.  Officers arrived on scene and saw the suspect, a 39-year-old male, running across the street holding a catalytic converter and a power saw in his hands. The suspect was found to be in possession of Methamphetamine.  He was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:57 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and removed $30 worth of clothing sometime during the evening.
At 1:01 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the rear window to the victim’s vehicle sometime over the past few weeks.
At 1:36 pm, an officer reports two unknown suspects forced open the victim’s work truck and took tools.
At 1:53 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect used the victim’s personal identifying information to obtain cell phone services.
At 8:06 am, an officer reports the citizen’s arrest of a 49-year-old woman for shoplifting from the business. The woman was issued a citation and released at the scene.

At 4:10 am, officers responded to an alarm at a school. Officers arrived on scene and found a bench propped against a room and a window which was forced open. Officers obtained keys for the room. Officers then located a 39-year-old male in the room. The male was taken into custody without incident. A search of the room revealed several cabinets and doors were rummaged through. The male was booked then transported to county jail.
At 4:40 am, an officer located a stolen refrigerator van which was reported stolen to San Pablo Police on 11/4/20.

Location: San Pablo Ave and Ohio St
Description: At 2:49 am, an officer stopped a vehicle for speeding. During the contact the driver, a 42-year-old female, could not explain who the vehicle belonged to. She was also on probation for possession of a stolen vehicle. Due to the driver not having a license the vehicle was towed. The next day the owner of the vehicle reported their vehicle as stolen to the Richmond Police Department. A criminal case will be authored against the vehicle.
At 7:14am, an officer responded to a call of a suspicious vehicle and located a Ford F-150 which was reported stolen to the Berkeley Police Department.
An unknown suspect broke into the residence sometime during the last two weeks and stole electronics.
An unknown suspect shattered a window on one of the roll-up doors to the corporation yard. It is unknown when the vandalism occurred.

CrimeType: Vehicle Tampering, Grand Theft, Possession of Burglary Tools, Driving Unlicensed
Description: At 11:11 pm, officers responded to someone attempting to steal a catalytic converter. The reporting party then saw suspects get into a vehicle and leave the area. An officer arrived and located the vehicle and contacted the driver, a 54-year-old male. Upon approaching the vehicle two saws and a floor jack could be seen in plain view. The reporting party identified the vehicle as the suspect vehicle and the driver was taken into custody. The other suspect was not located. The male was booked and then released on a citation.
An unknown suspect broke into the victim’s truck during the night of the 5th and stole a backpack.
At 8:58 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect opened a fraudulent bank account using the victim’s personal identifying information.

At 4:31 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the business and selected several items of merchandise before leaving the store without payment. Stolen Vehicle, Traffic Pursuit: At 10:39 PM, an officer attempted to stop a vehicle fleeing from a robbery alarm in a neighboring jurisdiction.  The vehicle fled on Central Ave. into Point Isabel park.  The driver of the vehicle collided with a police car and then into a fence in the park and fled on foot into the park.  The vehicle was previously reported as stolen.

Location: San Pablo Avenue / El Dorado Street
Description: At 10:11 PM, an officer observed a vehicle doing a burn out and then nearly colliding with parked cars.  The driver was determined to be under the influence of alcohol.  The driver refused to submit to a blood alcohol test, so the arresting officer obtained a search warrant.  The driver was booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 02:47 AM, an officer attempted to stop a vehicle over for speeding and reckless driving.  The vehicle finally was stopped approx. a half mile later and the officer determined the driver was under the influence of alcohol.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:11 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the business and walked out with merchandise.

At 10:42 PM, an officer responded to the report of a catalytic convertor theft.  An unknown suspect stole the catalytic convertor off of the vehicle that was parked on the street between 5:30 and 7:30 pm. Stolen Vehicle / Traffic Pursuit: At 11:41 PM, an officer ran the license plate of a stolen vehicle.  The officer attempted to conduct a traffic stop and the suspect fled in a reckless manner.  In the interest of public safety, the pursuit was cancelled.
At 7:36 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and stole property sometime during the evening.

Location: Ohlone Greenway / South of Fairmount Ave
Description: At 9:25 AM, officers responded to the report of a robbery.  A suspect approached the victim and wrenched a cellular telephone from a victim’s hand after a struggle.  The suspect ran to an awaiting vehicle was determined to be a vehicle stolen in a carjacking reported to another agency.

CrimeType: Grand Theft from a Person / Elder Abuse
Description: At 8:18 pm, officers responded to the report of a suspect grabbing the cell phone from the hands of a 67-year-old male victim and then fleeing on foot.
At 6:54 AM, an officer responded to the report of an auto theft.  The suspect stole the victim’s truck sometime during the previous night.
At 7:19 am, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from a Honda Accord.

At 11:38 pm, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop and subsequently arrested a 23 year old male from Richmond. The male was wanted on several outstanding warrants. The male was booked and then transported to county jail. Grand Theft – 7100 block of Cutting Boulevard: An unknown suspect entered a residence under construction and stole several thousand dollars in tools during the night of the 10th.

At 1:56 am, an officer located the responsible suspect from the grand theft on Cutting Blvd. the day prior.  The officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested a 41-year-old male from Richmond.  Following his arrest, he was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

During the above arrest, the officer found a female passenger in the vehicle had an outstanding warrant.  The 20-year-old transient woman was arrested and booked at the El Cerrito Police Department.  Following booking, she was cited and released.
At 9:59, officers arrested a 57 year-old man for an outstanding warrant.
An unknown suspect entered a garage and stole the victim’s bicycle between 4:15 am and 4:30 am.
An unknown suspect attempted to steal a Gator utility vehicle from the Corporation Yard sometime between the 18th and today.

At 12:56 am, officers responded to a commercial burglary reported by the business owner who saw the front door had been shattered via his video surveillance system. The video showed a male break the front door glass and enter the store. The male stole money from the cash drawer and fled the area on foot.

While responded to the above robbery, a suspicious vehicle was seen leaving the area.  The vehicle had a stolen license plate affixed to it.  When an officer attempted to stop it, it fled from the traffic stop.  The driver of the vehicle, a 19-year-old Oakland male was involved in a solo vehicle collision on I-80 in Emeryville.  The suspect fled on foot but was apprehended nearby without force.   A loaded firearm, power tools and catalytic converters were located in the vehicle.  The subject was booked into the MDF.
At 3:59 am, an officer reports the drive thru window was pried open and entry was made by an unknown suspect. It was unknown if there was a loss of property.

At 1:54 am, officers responded to an armed robbery at the 7Eleven involving three males.  Three subjects wearing dark clothing, masks and hooded jackets entered the store.  One of the subjects pointed a firearm at the cashier and the subjects committed an armed robbery.  The subjects fled the store.  Thankfully, the clerk was not injured.
At 2:23 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle sometime during the prior week.
At 4:18 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle sometime during the prior week.
At 5:56 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect was interrupted while attempting to steal items from the inside of the victim’s vehicle.
At 9:38 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke the front windshield of the victim’s vehicle which had been parked in front of the listed residence.
At 1:23 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle sometime during the evening.

At 2:05 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 19-year old male from Richmond after he was found asleep behind the wheel of his vehicle that was parked in the middle of the street. After conducting field sobriety tests with the male, the officer determined that he was under the influence of alcohol. The male was transported to ECPD for booking and later released to a sober relative with a citation.
At 5:39 pm, an officer reports 3 unknown suspects entered the business and smashed open the alcohol display case before removing merchandise and fleeing the store.
At 11:54 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle sometime during the evening.
At 12:04 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle sometime over the past several days.
At 8:08 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect dumped a large quantity of trash in the area which had to be removed by City employees.

An unknown suspect stole the victim’s Chevy Silverado between 10:00 and 10:30 am. Commercial Burglary – 3100 block of Carlson Boulevard: At 4:58 am, a suspect cut a lock to a door and entered the business. Once inside the suspect took lotto tickets and cigarettes. The suspect then fled in awaiting vehicle.

At midnight, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed a camper shell window and removed several boxes, one of which contained car parts. The suspect left the items in the roadway and fled the scene.
At 11:39 am, an officer reports two suspects entered the business and selected several hundred dollars’ worth of merchandise before fleeing the business.
At 8:19 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle from the street in front of their residence sometime during the evening.
At 11:53 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stolen the victim’s fishing boat and trailer from an apartment complex parking lot sometime over the past few days.

At 5:17 pm, an officer contacted the driver of a suspicious vehicle and observed several packages which he believed to have been recently stolen. The packages were confiscated and later returned to 8 different victims’ all of whom lived in neighboring cities.

At 7:28 am, an officer reports the Citizen’s Arrest of a 52-year-old man who had entered the business and threatened the victim with a baseball bat. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 3:57 am the victim called the police as his vehicle was being stolen and being driven away from the home.  An officer who was responding to the call located the vehicle parked with nobody inside of it about three blocks away from where it was stolen.  It was returned to the owner.

At 3:01 am an officer found a car parked in the middle of the roadway.  The officer contacted the occupants and found that one of them had an outstanding warrant for his arrest issued by Solano County.  The subject was arrested, booked at ECPD, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 4:10 am an officer pulled over a vehicle and found the driver was on Parole and had an outstanding warrant for his arrest issued by the California Department of Corrections.  The subject was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 2:36 pm, an officer reports the Citizen’s Arrest of a 38-year-old man who damaged the victim’s vehicle during an argument. The man was issued a citation and released at the scene with his written promise to appear.
At 4:35 pm, an officer arrested a parolee for being possession of methamphetamine. The man was booked at the ECPD and released with a citation.
An unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s Honda during the morning hours.
At 1:10 pm, an officer arrested a 38-year-old man for being in possession of methamphetamine. The man was released in the field with a citation.
An unknown suspect shattered a side window to the victim’s Mercedes sometime around mid-morning
An unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from a Prius sometime over the past few days.
At 3:17 pm, an officer arrested a 44-year-old man for an outstanding warrant. The man was transported directly to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 5:45 pm, CVS called to report that two suspects had come into the store and broke into a liquor display cabinet and stole approximately $500.00 worth of alcohol.  The suspects then ran to a car in the parking lot and drove away.
At 12:31 am, an officer pulled a vehicle over and after an investigation determined the driver was intoxicated.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then released with a citation.
At 4:30 am an officer responded to the report that the victim had just heard their vehicle drive away from the home.  The vehicle had been parked in front of the home before it was stolen.

At 12:24 am a delivery driver had exited his vehicle in the parking lot to make his delivery when he saw a subject walking towards him pulling a ski mask over his face.  The victim got back into his car and drove away when he saw the suspect was pointing a handgun at him.
At 12:50 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and opened the hood; no loss was reported.
At 3:32 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect approached the victim while she was inside her vehicle. The suspect exposed himself to the victim and then fled in a vehicle.

At 7:20 pm and officer went to 7-11 for the report of a theft that had occurred where food was stolen.  The officer viewed the surveillance footage of the theft and then about 5 hours later saw the suspect walking on San Pablo Avenue near the El Cerrito Plaza.  The officer stopped the suspect and spoke to him about the theft.  The suspect admitted he had stolen the items from 7-11 and the officer issued him a citation for the theft, and he was released.

At 8:00 am, an officer reports a known suspect entered the porch of a nearby home and removed a package from the porch. The woman was later contacted, and the package was recovered. The victim declined to prosecute the woman for theft.
At 11:10 pm and officer went to a report that a vehicle had been stolen from the Safeway parking lot sometime in the last ten hours.
At 6:32 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect approached the victim as he exited his delivery truck and punched the victim in the face before running off.
At 9:35 am, an officer reports two unknown juvenile suspects entered the business and loaded approximately $700 worth of beauty products and personal hygiene products into a bag and fled the scene.
At 1:16 pm, an officer reports the Citizen’s Arrest of a 54-year-old man who was contacted as he exited the store with stolen property. The man was issued a citation and released at the scene.
At 5:07 pm, an unknown female entered CVS and stole several bottles of alcohol.
At 8:42 am, a resident called to report an unknown male stealing a package from a neighbor’s doorstep.
At 5:29 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed the front passenger window of the victim’s vehicle.

Location: San Pablo Ave and Plumas Ave
Description: At 11:48 pm, an officer conducted a traffic stop and subsequently arrested the driver. The driver had an outstanding warrant out for her arrest. The driver was issued a citation and released at the scene.
At 8:29 pm, an officer reports a group of adults entered a business and concealed merchandise. The subjects then left the store without paying.

At 10:46 pm, an officer checked the welfare of a subject reported as passed out in a running vehicle.  The officer determined the subject was under the influence of a controlled substance and summoned medical assistance.  The subject was found to be in possession of prescription drugs not issued to them.  The subject was not arrested and released to EMS.
A package theft that occurred on Dec. 18th at 4:58 pm was reported.
At 3:57 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect drove off without the salesperson during a test drive of a used vehicle.

At 6:02 pm, officers responded to the gas station for the report of a robbery that had just occurred.  The suspect came into the business and stole the cash drawer from the cash register.  As the suspect was pulling the cash drawer the clerk grabbed onto the power cords in an attempt to retain it however the suspect pulled the cords out of the clerk’s hand.  The suspect entered a vehicle in the parking lot and drove away.

At 7:22 pm officers were sent to the report of a domestic battery that just occurred.  When the officers arrived, they learned that the suspect had pulled a knife and assaulted the victim.  The suspect was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Location: Carlson Blvd. at El Dorado Street
Description: At 6:45 pm officers were sent to a report that a carjacking had occurred minutes earlier.  The victim was loading items into their vehicle when they were approached by two suspects, one armed with a handgun.  The suspects demanded the keys to the car and the victim handed them over.  The suspects got into the car and drove away in it.  The vehicle was later recovered in Alameda County and ECPD Detectives responded to process it for evidence.
At 2:17 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s orange motorcycle from the driveway of a residence sometime during the past week.
At 9:59 am, an officer reports an unknown male and female suspect entered the business and loaded approximately $700 worth of merchandise into bags before fleeing the store.
At 4:26 pm, an officer responded to the report of an unknown suspect smashing the window of a vehicle.  The vandalism occurred the previous day.
At 10:31 am, an officer responded to the report of a shoplifter.  The suspect loaded up a duffle bag with merchandise and fled.
At 9:06 am, an officer responded to the report of an unknown suspect smashing the windows of the victim’s vehicle.
At 5:19 pm, a vehicle was stolen from the street in front of a residence sometime between 4:30 PM on 12/21/2020 and 5:15 PM on 12/22/2020.
During the above stolen vehicle investigation, it was discovered a second vehicle was stolen from the same location some time since late November.
At 11:14 am, an officer responded to the report of a shoplifter.  An unidentified suspect entered the store, selected merchandise and left without paying.
At 12:18 pm, an officer responded to a report of a vehicle that was stolen from the street some time overnight.
At 7:38 pm, an officer recovered a vehicle which was reported stolen to San Francisco police on 12/21/2020. The vehicle was towed for storage and the owner was contacted.

At 12:32 pm, an officer contacted a 41-year-old Concord man during a traffic stop.  He was found to be in possession of shaved keys and driving while his license was suspended.  The man was cited and released.

At 9:54, an officer responded to the report of a vandalism.  An unknown suspect attempted to defeat the catalytic converter security device on the victim vehicle causing approximately $3200 in damage to the vehicle.  The theft of the catalytic converter was not successful.
A package theft was reported.  The theft occurred at 3:16 pm.
At 9:44 am, an officer responded to a report of a vehicle theft that occurred overnight.

At 1:22 pm, an officer responded to the report of a vandalism.  Sometime overnight an unknown suspect keyed the victim vehicle and flattened three of its tires.  The suspect also poured an unidentified oily substance on the porch of the residence.  Damage to the vehicle was estimated at $1300.
At 11:17 am, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from under a GMC cargo van some time overnight.
A 42-year-old Richmond man was arrested on a warrant issued by the Pleasanton Police Department.  The man was transferred to the Pleasanton PD.
At 2:57 pm, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary.  An unknown suspect forced entry into the vehicle and stole a laptop computer.
At 12:39 pm, an officer responded to the report of a shoplifter.  The suspect loaded up two large duffle bags with merchandise and fled.
At 944 am, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary.  At approx. midnight, the night before, an unknown subject shattered the victim’s vehicle and stole property.
At 6:55 AM, an officer responded to the report of a theft of a license plate.  The theft occurred overnight.

At 8:21 AM, an officer responded to the report of a theft of a 1999 Lexus.  The vehicle was stolen after it was left running in a parking lot.  The unknown suspect was seen discarding a bicycle prior to the theft.  The bicycle was determined to have been stolen from Marin County.
At 8:09 AM, an officer reported the theft of a package from a front porch.  The suspect stole the package at 0232 hours.
At 1:13 pm, officers responded to the report of a victim being punched by the suspect.  Officers located the suspect who was placed under citizen’s arrest, issued a citation and released.
At 2:42 pm, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary that occurred overnight.

At 3:43 pm, an officer responded to the report of vehicle vandalism.   An unknown suspect maliciously scratched the paint of the victim’s vehicle causing approx. $4000 in damage. Robbery, Warrant Arrest – BART Path / Cutting Boulevard: At 5:29 pm, officers responded to the report of a robbery.  The suspect punched the victim in the face and stole cash.  Officer located the suspect nearby and determined that he was a registered sex offender and also wanted on a parole violation warrant.  The subject was arrested, booked and transported to the MDF.

At 10:13 am, an officer responded to the report of an unknown suspect entering an unlocked vehicle and stealing property.  The suspect also stole the front license plate.  The thefts occurred sometime during the prior eight days.

At 6:20 pm, officers responded to the report of a domestic battery.  The suspect was determined to have battered the victim.  The suspect was arrested, booked, and transported to the MDF.  The investigating officer obtained an Emergency Protective Order for the victim.
At 12:06 am, an officer attempted to stop a stolen vehicle.  The vehicle failed to yield, and a pursuit ensued. The CHP arrived and took over the pursuit near the city of Berkeley.
At 1:55 pm, a Community Service Officer recovered a 2009 Lexus which was reported stolen to the Oakland Police Department.
An officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the window to a Toyota Highlander

At 3:20 am, an officer attempted to conduct an enforcement stop on a vehicle. The vehicle did not yield, and a pursuit ensued. The vehicle yielded on I80 near Berkeley. The driver was detained. The passenger of the vehicle then got into the driver seat and fled the scene. After a pursuit from CHP, the second driver was taken into custody. The two males, a 31-year-old and 30-year-old, were booked. The 31-year-old was released on a citation while the 30-year-old, was taken to county jail for a parole violation warrant.
At 7:50 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.

At 11:35 am, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 45-year-old man after he was detained by BART PD for shoplifting $55 worth of toilet paper.  The man was issued a citation in the field and released.

At 11:46 pm, an officer attempted to conduct an enforcement stop on a stolen vehicle. The vehicle field to yield and a pursuit ensued. The pursuit was terminated when the driver began to drive recklessly.
At 11:21 am, an officer responded to the report of an auto theft.  An unknown suspect stole the keys and then a vehicle.

At 06:20 am, officers responded to the report of a robbery that just occurred.  An unknown suspect entered Chevron with his hands in his pockets.   The suspect appeared to have a firearm in his pocket and took cash and cigarettes.

CrimeType: Warrant Arrest, Domestic Violence Restraining Order Violation
Description: At 8:10 pm, an officer conducted a traffic stop.  The driver of the vehicle was found to have a felony domestic violence warrant from Los Angeles County.  The driver was also the subject of a domestic violence restraining order and the protected party was in the vehicle.  The subject was arrested and booked into the MDF.
At 8:20 am, an officer responded to the report of an unknown suspect using an unknown object to break the front window of the business. There did not appear to have been any loss.

At 3:59 pm, an officer responded to the report of an unknown suspect causing malicious damage to the victim’s vehicle.  On January 3rd, in between 1715 and 1815 hours, an unknown suspect caused a large dent in the hood of the victim’s vehicle resulting in an estimated $3000 in damage.
At 5:01 pm, an officer responded to the report of a theft.  In between 1/3/2021 and 1/5/2021 at 1600 hours, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s 2007 Honda CRV.

At 1:11 pm, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary that occurred in between 1/4/2021 at 2000 hours and 1/5/2021 at 1300 hours.  During that time, an unknown suspect smashed the rear windows to the victim’s vehicle and stole a box containing cleaning supplies.
At 8:45 pm, an officer reports an unknown person slashed a tire on the victim’s vehicle while it was parked and unattended.
At 10:00 pm, an officer reports the victim’s bicycle was stolen from a locked community bicycle storage area.
At 1007 hours, an officer recovered a 1998 Honda Civic which was reported stolen to Central Marin on 12/27/2020.
Between 01/08/2021 2230 hours to 01/09/2021 at 1200 hours, an unknown suspect broke the front passenger window of the victim’s vehicle and stole a $350 pair of Oakley Sunglasses.
At 6:30 pm, an officer reported the victim’s Toyota truck was stolen in between 0400 hours and 1850 hours.
At 0922 hours, an officer recovered a blue 1996 Toyota Corolla which was reported stolen San Francisco Police on 12/03/2020.
At 1657 hours, officers responded to a suspicious vehicle and found the front door the vehicle was parked in front of had been forced over and the interior ransacked.
At 5:18 am, an officer reported that an unknown suspect entered and started the victim’s vehicle but was unable to drive off due to a theft deterrent device.
At 6:57 pm, an officer reported that two unidentified suspects entered the business and selected over $700 in merchandise before fleeing the store.
An unknown suspect entered a shed sometime during the night and stole three high-end bicycles for a total loss of $20K.
At 7:40 pm, an officer reports that an unknown suspect entered the business sometime in the early morning hours and stole a safe from inside the business office.
At 7:55 pm, an officer accepted the Citizen’s Arrest of a 22-year-old man who threatened store staff with a metal pipe. The man was issued a citation and released from the scene.
At 9:12 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle from the parking lot sometime during the day.
At 5:28 pm, officers responded to several small fires which were started outside of the business. Upon officer’s arrival, bank employees had already extinguished them.
An unknown suspect attempted to steal a catalytic converter, but their efforts were thwarted when they saw an antitheft device protecting it.
At 12:01 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect demanded merchandise from the clerk without payment. When the clerk declined, the suspect kicked the front door damaging the glass.

At 2:09 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect set a dumpster at the business on fire. The Fire Department responded to the area and extinguished the fire. The following evening, an officer located the suspect and arrested the man. The 30-year-old man was then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:51 am, an officer contacted a 46-year-old man during a call for service at the business. A records check revealed the man had a warrant from a prior shoplifting case. The man was issued a citation and released from the scene.

At 9:17 am, a 47-year-old-man entered the home through an unlocked front door. Once inside, the man told the mother and her children that someone was trying to shoot him. Officers arrived on scene, arrested the man for trespassing and arranged medical treatment for him. It was discovered there was no threat of someone trying to shoot the man.
Around 1:00 pm, an unknown suspect entered the residence through an unlocked door and stole several items.

At 6:57 am, an officer spotted two subjects fighting near the business. After separating the two men, the officer learned the victim chased the suspect who had broken into his car at the Del Norte BART station. BART PD officers responded and took over the investigation.
At 7:54 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect used the victim’s credit card without the permission of the card owner.
At 10:14 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle sometime over the past few days and damaged the ignition cylinder and steering column.

CrimeType: Arrest for Assault with a Deadly Weapon, Battery, Criminal Threats and a Parole Violation
Location: (Baxter Creek) BART Path at Conlon Ave
Description: At 1:54 pm, El Cerrito police officers responded to two males fighting in the area.  Officers located a victim bleeding in the face and detained the suspect, a 29 year old male from Richmond.   The first victim confronted the suspect, who was in the victim’s homeless encampment in the creek.   The suspect punched the victim in the face and hit the victim with a metal chair.  When the second victim intervened, the suspect pushed her to the ground and chased her to her vehicle, which was parked nearby. While in her vehicle, the suspect threatened to kill her.   Both victims were treated for their injuries by paramedics.  The suspect who was on parole and was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:30 am, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victims Honda Accord.
At 7:31 pm, an officer responded to the report of an auto theft.  In between 4:00 pm, and 7:30 pm, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 1999 green Honda Accord.

At 10:52 am, an officer responded to the report of a catalytic convertor theft.  In between 9 pm, on 01/23/2021 and 8 am, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s Toyota Prius.

At 7:25 am, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary.  It was determined that on 01/24/2021 at 2325 hours, an unknown suspect pried open the locked utility cabinets on the victim’s pickup truck. The suspect stole a handgun, ammunition, and numerous tools.

At 10:15 am, employees and security asked a suspicious person in the store to leave. The suspect responded by pulling and brandishing a 6” knife. The suspect left the store and fled to the rear of the business on a bicycle.

At 3:00 pm, a man from Vallejo came to the ECPD with questions about his probation record. It was determined the man was wanted on an ECPD warrant from a 2020 case. He was issued a citation and released.

At 0946 am, an officer responded to the report of a suspicious circumstance after the a vehicle owner located her vehicle on a wooden block.  Sometime overnight, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s catalytic converter from their Toyota Prius.
At 6:30 pm, an officer located a gold 2005 Toyota Prius that had been reported stolen to Oakland PD on 1/22/21.

At 12:40 pm, an officer responded to the report of an auto burglary.  In between 01/27/2021 at noon and 8:15 am, an unknown suspect entered the victim’s garage through an unlocked exterior door.  The suspect rifled through a vehicle park in the garage. No property appears to have been stolen.
At 11:07 am, an officer responded to the report of an auto theft.  The victim’s 2018 Toyota Corolla was stolen between 01/27/2021 at 6:45 pm and 01/28/2021 at 11:07 am.

CrimeType: Shoplifting, Under the Influence of a Controlled Substance, Possession of Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 06:44 am, officers responded to a shoplifting in at Chevron in which the male suspect stole food items and then made his way to Denny’s where he began to cause a disturbance. The suspect was in possession of a glass pipe used to smoke methamphetamines and he admitted to smoking methamphetamines a couple hours earlier. He was transported to the hospital.

At 9:15 pm, an unknown suspect entered the business, concealed merchandise, and left without paying.  The suspect was described as a black male adult, 20-22 years old, 5’9”, skinny build, wearing a black windbreaker, red skinny jeans, and white shoes.  He fled the business in a dark colored sedan.

At 00:24 am, officers were dispatched to a residence after two subjects forced their way into a locked, enclosed porch of the victim’s residence.  The suspects removed the victim’s window screens.  The keys from the stolen vehicle on Knott Ave were recovered as well as additional property.  Officers arrived and arrested a 40-year-old transient man and a 24-year-old Richmond man.  After booking, both men were transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:24 am, an officer responded to the report of a vandalism.  It was determined that in between 0500-0530 hours, an unknown suspect broke a front window in the victim’s house.

At 9:30 pm, an unknown suspect entered the business, concealed merchandise, and left without paying.  The suspect was described as a black male adult, 19-20 years old, 5’8”, skinny build, wearing gray jeans.
At 3:49 pm, an unknown male suspect broke the front right window of the victim’s vehicle and then fled to an awaiting vehicle. Nothing was taken.
Between 11:30 am and 6:30 pm, an unknown suspect entered the victim’s residence via an unsecured window and stole property.

At 12:24 pm, Officers responded to a battery in progress. The suspect chased the victim and once he caught the victim, he physically assaulted him.  Citizens came to the victim’s aid and detained the suspect until officers arrived. He was arrested and transported to county jail.

Location: California Check Cashing, 10394 San Pablo Ave
Description: At 2:15 pm, a customer wanted a replacement pre-paid credit card. The clerk was unable to help the man and he became angry and smashed two point-of-sale ATM machines valued at $700 each. The suspect fled prior to police arrival.
At 7:10 pm, an unknown suspect entered the business, filled a large trash bag with cosmetics, and left without paying.

At 12:11 pm, an officer responded to the report of an auto theft.  In between midnight and 10:00 am, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s white 2005 GMC Savannah box truck. Domestic Battery – 7400 block of Park Vista: At 1246 pm, a 21-year-old male suspect and his girlfriend got into an argument. The female victim tried to leave. As she exited the front door, the male suspect physically assaulted her. The suspect left prior to officers’ arrival.
At 5:33 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect smashed the driver window of the victim’s vehicle sometime during the previous day.
At 12:05 pm, an officer repots a group of unknown suspects entered a business, concealed merchandise, then left the business without paying.
At 11:34 am, a male came to the station to turn himself for a warrant. The male was identified and then cited for the warrant.
At 9:49 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered a business and asked for water. An employee gave a cup of water to the suspect. The suspect then punched the employee and left the business.

At 10 pm, an officer reported that an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and rummaged through the glove box. There was no reported loss. The theft occurred sometime during the last 2 weeks.
At 1:34 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the victim’s residence by removing a window screen and entering through a window.

At 9:40 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered a vacant building by forcing open a door. The suspect then used the building to sleep where he was woken up by the owner. The suspect then left on foot.
At 6pm, an officer reported that an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and told $500 worth of tools. The theft occurred between 9am on the 7th and 7am on the 8th.

At 3:05 pm, an officer reported a 25-year-old male assaulted another subject with a knife causing a large laceration on the victim’s hand. The male was located by Richmond Police a few blocks away. The suspect was located by responding officers and then taken into custody. The victim was transported to the hospital for treatment.  The suspect was booked into MDF. Grand Theft – 3400 block of Santa Clara: At 3:06 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
At 3:57 pm, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime during the day.

At 3:26 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered a bank, and presented a demand note. The teller went to an employee and told them what was happening. The other employee then called police. The suspect fled the area before police arrived. The suspect did not receive any money before leaving.

At 9:45 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered a business, concealed merchandise, and then left without paying. An employee attempted to grab the bag out of the suspect’s possession and a “tug of war” ensued. The employee was able to recover the property and the suspect fled on foot.
At 12:11 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered a building and removed sound equipment worth over $10,000.
At 9:41 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the victim’s vehicle and tampered with the steering column but was unsuccessful in stealing the vehicle.

At 6:17 pm, an Officer reported that the victim and the suspect met at the hotel. The two began to argue and a physical confrontation ensued. The suspect stabbed the victim with an unknown object in the leg. The victim sought treatment at a local hospital and reported the incident to ECPD several days later.

At 11:40pm, an Officer reported the arrest of a 60-year-old El Cerrito male for an outstanding warrant for failure to appear from a previous ECPD drug offense arrest. The male was issued a citation and released while on scene.
At 4:07 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed narcotics from a delivery to a medical facility. The narcotics were likely stolen between the shipment or delivery process.
At 1:45am, an Officer located a vehicle that was reported stolen from Daly City.
At 1:22 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle. The theft occurred sometime overnight.
At 8:53 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered a business, concealed merchandise, and then left without paying.
At 9:16 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered a business, concealed merchandise, and then left without paying.

At 8:40 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime overnight. Later in the shift, a neighboring police department located the vehicle and arrested the driver. The vehicle was released to the owner at the scene.

At 10:41 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered a building by breaking a window. Then the suspect used a saw to cut through the dry wall and entered one of the suites. Once inside the suspect removed two pieces of leveling equipment worth over $3000.

At 01:07am, an officer responded to the area for the report of a male asleep behind the wheel of his vehicle in the middle of the roadway. Upon arrival, the officer determined that the driver, a 23-year old male from Richmond, was driving under the influence of alcohol. The male was placed under arrest, transported to ECPD for booking, and then released with a citation.

At 6:56 pm officers went to a call where someone had been robbed of their possessions.  Officers located the suspect in the parking lot.  It was determined the suspect had punched the victim in the face several times causing injuries and then stole property from the victim.  He was also found in possession of suspected methamphetamine.  The suspect was arrested and directly transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:02 pm an officer went to call where a suspect had stolen merchandise and left the store.  The suspect was not located.
At 1710 hours, an officer responded to a report of a battery.  The victim was battered by a known suspect but refused to prosecute.

At 12:51 am officers were sent to Nation’s for the report that a suspect had come inside and brandished a knife, scaring the employees.  The first officer arrived and located the subject in the parking lot.  The subject threw his knife and attempted to flee.  The suspect was arrested, booked, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 1740 hours, an officer responded to a suspicious vehicle and contacted a 45 year old male who was passed out with a meth pipe on his lap. The man was wanted on a Marin County misdemeanor warrant and was also found in possession of methamphetamines. He was issued a citation and released.

At 1425 hours, an officer responded to the report of a man under a car. Upon arrival, the officer determined the victim saw the suspect under his vehicle on 2/13/2021 at 0900 hours. An inspection of the vehicle revealed the catalytic converter was stolen.

At 0738 hours, an officer responded to an auto burglary and determined and unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle during the previous night. The suspect rummaged through the vehicle and attempted to steal the vehicle. Unsuccessful, the suspect stole a set of work keys.

At 1706 hours, an officer met with the victims at the ECPD regarding an El Cerrito man who was caught on video earlier in the day on the victims’ property, in their front yard, in violation of a restraining order. The man also threw yard debris over the back fence into the victims’ yard.  This case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s office for review.

At 2120 hours, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 29-year-old Richmond man after he entered the business, concealed merchandise, and left without paying.  The man was transported to county jail.

At 0335 hours, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and contacted the occupants.  Both were found to be on parole and a search of the vehicle was conducted.  The officer located several pieces of mail, credit cards, checks, and other persons’ identifying information.  These items were collected and the case will be submitted to the District Attorney’s Office for prosecution.
At 1257 hours, an officer responded to a restraining order violation. The suspect went to his child’s mother’s place of employment in violation of a Domestic Violence restraining order.
At 0713 hours, an officer contacted the victim and learned she received a threatening text message from an unknown suspect.
At 0847 hours, an officer responded to an auto burglary. An unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle sometime over the previous two days and stole personal property.

At 1544 hours, an officer responded and determined the victim was struck in the back of the head with a stick causing a laceration to the back of his head by an unknown suspect. The victim was transported to Kaiser.
At 1002 hours, while on the above reference call, the officer was flagged down regarding the theft of a bicycle on 2/11/2021. The suspect entered the bicycle storage room and stole a mountain bike.

At 1630 hours, an unknown suspect pulled the victim’s purse off her shoulder.  The victim followed the suspect from the scene and convinced him to return her purse.  The suspect returned the victim’s purse but not her wallet.

At 1010 hours, the victim arrived home and was approached by an unknown suspect who was swinging a baseball bat. The suspect threatened to beat the victim because she refused to allow him access to the courtyard of the apartment complex.
At 0438 hours, an officer made contact with a victim whose mail was located in the above traffic stop.  It was determined that the mail was stolen from his unlocked vehicle.

At 0905 hours, an officer responded to a vehicle burglary and determined the suspect entered the locked parking garage on 2/15/2021 at 2340 hours. The suspect entered an unlocked vehicle and stole personal property.

At 1308 hours, an officer responded to Big 5 and determined at 1215 hours, the suspect entered the store and was told to leave due to an attempted theft. The suspect lowered his mask and spit in the victim's face. The suspect then left the store.  The suspect was later identified and placed under citizen’s arrest by the victim and issued a citation.
Between 1300 and 1400 hours, an unknown suspect cut the screen door of the victim’s sliding glass door but did not enter the residence.
At 0623 hours, an officer contacted the victim and determined and unknown suspect also stole the victim’s Hyundai Tucson during the previous night.
At 0127 hours, an officer located a 1994 Ford Ranger that had been reported stolen to SFPD on 2/9/21.

At 2012 hours, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 29-year-old Richmond man after he entered the store, concealed merchandise, and left without paying.  Following the arrest, the man was cited and released.
At 1118 hours, an officer responded to a residence and determined an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from a Toyota Prius sometime over the previous two days.
At 0628 hours, an officer responded to the Honda dealership and determined an unknown suspect broke the windows of two vehicles during the night.

At 1206 hours, an officer responded to Walgreens regarding a theft and determined two suspects with backpacks went to skin care, filled their backpacks with a variety of skin care products, and fled without paying.
At 1118 hours, an officer responded to the apartment complex for a theft. An unknown suspect entered the bicycle storage room and stole a mountain bike sometime over the previous week.
At 0858 hours, an officer was dispatched to a phone report and determined the victim’s vehicle’s window was broken and miscellaneous tools and clothing items were stolen sometime the previous night.

At 1118 hours, an officer responded to the Recycling Center and met with an employee who told him an unknown male stole unknown electronic waste from the dumpster on 2/14/2021 at approximately 1325-1330 hours.

At 0924 hours, an officer responded to a theft and determined three suspects entered the store, went to the dental health section, and filled their backpacks with dental health products and then filled their backpacks with batteries in the front of the store. They left without paying.
At 1445 hours, officers responded to an in progress robbery. The suspect entered the store, presented a demand note to the manager, stole cash, and then fled the store.

CrimeType: Grand Theft, Possession of Stolen Property, Conspiracy
Description: At 0404 hours, officers were dispatched to the location after a witness observed suspects cutting a catalytic converter from under a vehicle.  The suspect vehicle then fled the scene.  An officer located a vehicle matching the description in the 800 block of Ashbury Ave. and conducted a traffic stop.  Upon contacting the three occupants, the officer observed a floor jack, sawzall, and drill driver.  The subjects were removed from the vehicle and a search of the vehicle revealed a freshly cut catalytic converter in the trunk.  Officers arrested a 23-year-old Oakland man, a 33-year-old Hayward man, and a 20-year-old Oakland man.  The men were booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to county jail.

At 0850 hours, an officer saw a man riding a Honda Moped eastbound on Central Ave and not wearing a wearing a helmet. A traffic stop was initiated and it was determined the moped was reported stolen to Berkeley PD on 2/12/2021. The suspect was arrested, booked, and transported to MDF.

At 1500 hours, officers responded to a home invasion robbery. The victim was approached in her front yard by a female suspect. The female was soon joined by four other suspects, armed with handguns.  One of the suspects assaulted the victim while the others rifled through the house and stole property.

Location: Bank of the West, 11100 San Pablo Ave
Description: At 1056 hours, an officer responded to forgery in progress and detained a man who attempted to pass checks which were stolen during a burglary in Richmond.
At approximately 3:11 pm, three suspects entered the store and filled a duffle bag with $130.00 worth of cosmetics and snacks.  The suspects fled the store without paying for the items.
At 6:18 pm, a customer borrowed a tool from the business to work on her vehicle in the parking lot and failed to return it.
A 7:30 pm, several suspects entered Walgreens and stole over $1100 worth of ice cream and left the business.
An unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle, stole cash and the victims work badges.  The theft occurred from February 20th at 8:30pm to February 21st at 6:30am.
An unknown suspect entered an unlocked vehicle and stole miscellaneous items sometime during the night of the 21st.
An unknown suspect stole the victim’s catalytic converter from their Honda CRV between the 19th and 21st of February.
At 2:11 pm, two suspects entered the business, concealed merchandise underneath their clothing and left without paying.
At 1:56 pm, the victim was seated along San Pablo Avenue working on her laptop when an unknown suspect grabbed the laptop and fled to an awaiting vehicle.
An unknown suspect entered an unlocked vehicle and stole miscellaneous items sometime during the night of the 21st.
At 11:00 am, an unknown suspect entered Big 5 Sporting Goods, stole over $700 in Nike products and fled the business in a Lincoln Navigator.

At 3:00 pm, an unknown suspect went into Big 5 and stole $400 in shoes and fled the business in a Chevy Suburban. Grand Theft- 7400 block of Sea View Place At 2:41 am, three unknown suspects removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle and fled the scene in a VW Passat.

At 6:00 am, a 46-year-old transient entered the business, stole a bottle of alcohol and was confronted by store employees as he left. Officers arrived on scene and detained the man who was later released at the scene.
At 7:17 am, an officer located a Ford E-350 van which was reported stolen to the Emeryville PD on the 27th of January.
At 1:45 am, an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle.

An unknown suspect shattered a back window to a new vehicle which was for sale at the Honda dealership. After reviewing video surveillance footage, it was determined the incident occurred at 9:30 pm on the 23rd.
At 10:31 pm, an unknown suspect broke the window to the victim’s vehicle.
At 2:06, an unknown suspect stole a catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
At 10:40 pm, two unknown suspects assaulted a McDonald’s employee and fled the business before police arrived.
An unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from a Prius between the 21st and the 26th of February.

At 12:55 pm, an unknown suspect stole several hundred dollars’ worth of clothing from the business. Vandalism- 2000 block of Key Boulevard- An unknown suspect cut the lock to an empty garage sometime during the night of the 25th.
An unknown suspect vandalized a USPS mailbox.
An unknown suspect stole a catalytic converter from a Land Rover at 4:00 on the morning of the 26th.
An unknown suspect stole four-thousand dollars in tools from a van during the night of the 23rd.
At 6:38 pm, a citizen called to report a woman had been knocked to the ground by an unknown male. The victim was located and declined to seek prosecution against the man.
At 8:58 am, an unknown suspect entered the business and stole a variety of snacks and left the business without paying.

At 12:26 am, officers responded to an alarm at a construction site. Upon arrival, officers located a 23-year-old transient exiting the property. A records check revealed the man had a warrant for his arrest. The man was released in the field with a citation.

At 10:45 pm, an officer located an unoccupied stolen vehicle parked on Lexington Avenue near Potrero Avenue.  The officer called the owner of the vehicle and they came to this location and took possession of their vehicle.
At 8:13 am, an officer responded to the report of unknown suspect filling up garbage bags full of property and then fleeing the store without paying.

At 8:37 pm, while an officer was driving through the parking lot an employee from Walgreen’s flagged down the officer and reported that a suspect who was walking through the parking lot had just stolen items from Walgreen’s.  The officer stopped the suspect and recovered stolen property that he had in his possession.  The subject was placed under citizen’s arrest by the Walgreen’s employee.  The suspect was also found to have a warrant for his arrest issued from San Luis Obispo County.  He was issued a citation for the warrant as well and then released from the scene.
At 3:14 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the business sometime overnight on February 23. The suspect then removed household products and a case of beer.
At 8:34 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect damaged the passenger side windows of a vehicle using a hammer. The incident occurred sometime overnight.

At 1549 hours, an officer was flagged down by employees regarding a shoplifting that just occurred.  The suspect was detained nearby.  He was placed under citizen arrest, issued a citation and released at the scene.

An officer went to the call of a reported stolen vehicle.  When they arrived and spoke to the victim, they were told that a motorcycle had been stolen from in front of a home between February 26th and March 1st.

At 9:24 am, an officer responded to the report of a shoplifting that just occurred.  An unknown subject entered the business and concealed merchandise in two large bags. The suspect then left without paying.

At 10:40 pm, an officer pulled over a vehicle for a vehicle code violation.  After an investigation it was determined that the driver was under the influence and was placed under arrest and booked at ECPD.  After booking, the driver was released to a sober family member.

At 3:54 pm, officers were dispatched to three subjects who appeared to be casing the neighborhood. Officers arrived in the area and located the group. During the investigation, one of the subjects had an outstanding warrant. During booking, investigators recognized the suspect as the person responsible for a residential burglary which occurred in 2020 on Glen Mawr Ave.

At 8:50 pm, an officer responded to the report of a shoplifting that just occurred.  When an officer arrived, they found the suspect walking away from the store through the parking lot and stopped him.  The stolen merchandise was recovered from him and given back to Walgreen’s.  The subject was placed under citizen’s arrest by a Walgreen’s employee.  The officer issued the suspect a citation for the theft and she was released from the scene.

At 4:02 am an officer pulled over a vehicle for a vehicle code violation and discovered the driver had a warrant for their arrest issued by San Pablo PD.  The driver was issued a citation for the warrant and released from the scene. Burglary – 3300 block of Carlson Boulevard: At 7:32 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect forced entry into a detached garage and removed a bicycle. The incident occurred sometime overnight.
At 12:03 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect forced entry into a detached garage and stole several power tools. The incident occurred sometime overnight.

At 12:39 am, an officer contacted a subject who had started a small campfire of the BART path.  The officer discovered the subject had three warrants for their arrest.  The subject was placed under arrest and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 5:27 am, an officer located a vehicle had been reported stolen from Sonoma County.

At 8:07 pm an officer went to a call where someone was reported as stealing items from the store.  When the officer arrived, they found the suspect walking away from the store through the parking lot and stopped her.  The stolen merchandise was recovered from her bag and given back to Walgreen’s.  The subject was placed under citizen’s arrest by a Walgreen’s employee.  The officer issued the suspect a citation for the theft and she was released from the scene.

At 7:31 pm, officers were dispatched for the report of a Robbery that had occurred when the victim left his bicycle parked out in front of a business and went inside.  An unknown subject stole the bicycle.  When the victim attempted to retrieve the bike, the suspect physically attacked them.

At approximately 7:40 pm, while an officer was looking for the suspect in the above robbery, they observed a subject who matched the description provided by the victim.  The officer stopped the subject and the subject provided false information about their identity.  The officer was able to determine the identity of the subject, who was on parole.  The subject was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then issued a citation and released from the station.

Location: Potrero Avenue near San Pablo Avenue
Description: At 1:45 am, an officer pulled over a vehicle for a vehicle code violation and after an investigation determined the driver was intoxicated.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then released to a sober friend.

At 3:05 pm, an officer reports two subjects got into a struggle over a pair of garden sheers during a verbal argument. During the struggle, one party suffered a small cut on the forearm.  Neither party sought prosecution.
At 1:14 am an officer was sent to a call where the victim’s catalytic converter had just been stolen from their vehicle.

Location: San Pablo Avenue at Potrero Avenue
Description: At 11:49 pm, an officer pulled over a vehicle for driving the wrong way on San Pablo Avenue.  After an investigation they determined the driver was intoxicated.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then released to a sober friend.
At 7:14 pm, an officer located and recovered a stolen Acura.
At 4:13 pm, an officer responded to the theft of a Ford F250. The theft occurred between 1800 hours on 3/13/21 to 1558 hours on 3/14/21.

Location: Central Avenue and San Joaquin Street
Description: At 1:57 pm, an officer arrested a 54-year-old man for a felony arrest warrant. The man was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence & Vehicle Pursui
Description: At 6:35 am, officers were dispatched to Safeway to check on a female driver who was disoriented. Upon arrival, officers contacted a 34-year-old Oakland woman who was intoxicated. The woman refused to speak with officers and drove off northbound on San Pablo Avenue. A brief pursuit took place, and the woman was taken into custody near the intersections of San Pablo and Clinton Avenues. The woman was booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:45 am, an unknown suspect shattered the window to a pickup truck and tried to steal several tools which were locked together with a steel cable.

Location: San Pablo Ave/ San Diego St.
Description: An officer reported the victim parked her Honda Accord at 0900 hours at the above listed location. When she returned to her vehicle around 1800 hours, she discovered her catalytic converter had been stolen.

At 2:32 am, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop for a vehicle code violation and contacted the driver, a thirty-year-old male from Richmond. During a warrants check, the Officer discovered the male had two outstanding warrants. The male was taken into custody without incident and transported to the MDF.
At 8:37 am, an unknown suspect entered the business and stole several premade Easter baskets.
At 8:59 am, two suspects entered the business and stole over $600 in deodorants.
An unknown suspect stole a 1994 Toyota pickup truck between the 12th and the 14th.

At 2318 hours, an Officer responded to the report of an unknown subject tampering with the victim’s vehicle. The suspect smashed out the window of the victim’s vehicle and rifled through the contents but did not take any property.

At 11:20 am, a 34-year-old man was arrested by store security for stealing over two-hundred dollars in groceries. While being detained, the suspect punched the security guard in the face in an attempt get away. The man was booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An unknown suspect broke into a van during the night of the 17th and stole several thousand dollars in tools.

At 12:12 am, an officer noticed a stolen truck near Potrero Ave. and Eastshore Blvd.  The vehicle parked quickly, and the occupants fled on foot.  One of the subjects was detained nearby while the other two eluded capture.  During an inventory of the stolen truck, several power tools, saws, and a car jack were located.  These tools are commonly used by catalytic converter thieves.
At 2:58 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the victim’s residence by breaking a window. Once inside the suspect removed the victim’s wallet.
At 7:14 pm an officer located a vehicle that was parked and unoccupied that had been stolen to the Oakland Police Department.  The vehicle was impounded.

At 8:12 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 59-year-old male. The male entered a business, filled a bag with merchandise, and then left without paying. The male was located, identified, and then released on a citation. Kidnapping / Assault with a Deadly Weapon / Spousal Battery: At 8:43 pm officers responded to the report of a stabbing occurring within a vehicle.  Officers located the vehicle traveling on Potrero Avenue at Eastshore Blvd.  It was determined that an altercation including a felony assault occurred in the vehicle and the suspect would not allow the victim to get out of the vehicle.  The suspect was arrested and booked at ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Officers responded to the report of a disturbance at the motel and discovered the subject had two separate non citable warrants for his arrest issued by the ECPD.  The subject was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 8:30 pm an officer went to Walgreen’s for the report that a theft had just occurred.  It was reported that a subject had come inside and loaded up merchandise into a bag and walked out of the store without paying for the items.

At 4:51 am an officer saw a subject walking that they knew had several warrants for his arrest.  The subject was contacted and placed under arrest.  The subject was cited on two separate warrants issued by ECPD and released at the scene.

At 12:48 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 36-year-old male for being in possession of a smoking device commonly used for a controlled substance. The male was also on probation. The male was identified and released from the scene with a citation.
At 8:35 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect threw a rock at a residence breaking one of the windows.

CrimeType: Prowling, Indecent Exposure, Possession of a Concealed Dagger and Probation Violatio
Description: At 4:30 am, a woman noticed an male sitting on her couch, exposing himself for the purposes of sexual gratification. The victim woke her partner, who confronted the man who ran off.  Officers located the suspect, a 34-year-old man from Richmond, nearby.  The subject, who was on felony probation, was found to have a dagger concealed in his sock.  The man was arrested, booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. During the investigation, detectives learned the man had prowled onto several other properties during a several hour time span. Shoplifting- 11565 San Pablo Avenue, Walgreens At 1:42 pm, two unidentified suspects entered the business and stole several cosmetic items. Shoplifting-2000 El Cerrito Plaza, Ross At 3:58 pm, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 46-year-old female for stealing several clothing items from the business. The woman was issued a citation and released from the scene. Shoplifting- 2000 El Cerrito Plaza, Ross At 4:22 pm, two unknown suspects entered the business and stole several items and left the store without paying.
At 7:25 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business and stole several items and left the store without paying.
An officer reported an unknown suspect used a marker to graffiti a bench.
An unknown suspect stole a Honda Civic sometime during the night of the 24th. Vandalism- San Pablo Avenue @ Eastshore Boulevard: An unknown suspect used a Sharpie to vandalize a US mailbox.

At 2:48 pm, a father and his five-year-old son were walking into DD’s Discount when a 60-year-old transient male pulled out a knife and threatened them. Officers arrived on scene and took the man into custody and transported him to the Martinez Detention Facility. The man was booked for brandishing a weapon and for being a parolee-at-large. Shoplifting – 10012 San Pablo Avenue, Pic-N-Pac: At 7:05 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business and selected two bottles of alcohol before exiting the store without paying. Shoplifting – 2000 El Cerrito Plaza, Ross At 8:23 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business and stole several items and left the store without paying.

Officers responded to the report of a man brandishing a knife at a woman during a verbal argument. Officers arrived on scene and located the suspect, a 60-year-old transient, who was in possession of a folding knife. The victim did not desire prosecution to the man was released at the scene and the case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office for prosecution.
At 11:27 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 33-year-old woman after a small quantity of controlled substance was found inside the woman’s vehicle. The woman was issued a citation and released.
An unknown suspect shattered the windows to seven vehicles in the neighborhood. Some vehicles were just vandalized, while others were rummaged through.

At 8:12 pm an officer was dispatched to CVS for the report that someone had just stolen items and fled the store.  Another officer located the suspect at the Plaza BART station and detained her.  The CVS employee looked at the suspect and confirmed she was the person who stole the items.  The stolen items were recovered from the suspect.  The suspect was found to have an outstanding warrant for her arrest for Grand Theft issued by El Cerrito PD.  She was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:03 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the driver side window of a victim’s vehicle. This incident happened sometime overnight.

Location: San Pablo Avenue at Madison Avenue
Description: At 10:57 pm an officer was patrolling when he noticed several subjects under a car removing the catalytic converter with a saw.  The officer detained three suspects and other officers arrived on scene and took them into custody.  All three suspects were taken back to El Cerrito PD and booked.  Two of the suspects were released from ECPD and one of them, who was on probation was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:34 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered a construction site and removed an air compressor. The theft happened sometime overnight.

At 10:19 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s trailer from their parking lot sometime overnight. Stolen Vehicle – 2900 block of Santa Clara: At 10:01 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s pick-up truck sometime overnight. Possession of a Controlled Substance, Warrant Arrest – Kearney Street and Wall Ave: At 12:14 pm, an officer reports the arrests of a 45-year-old male and a 50-year-old female. The male was in possession of a controlled substance and smoking device, and the female was wanted person. Both were positively identified and then released on citations.
At 9:30 pm, an officer was contacted by CVS employees who reported that someone had just stolen merchandise and fled the business.  The suspect was not located.

At 1:21 pm, the victim reports that a friend approached her and accused her of not paying money owed to him. The suspect then punched the victim on the head and back before fleeing the area on a bike. The victim did not sustain any injuries and declined medical attention.

At 2:14 am, officers were dispatched to Safeway for the report that someone had just brandished a knife at an employee.  Officers arrived and located the suspect outside of the store.  It was determined that the subject was sleeping near the front doors of Safeway and an employee went outside and asked him to move.  The suspect pulled out a pocketknife opened it up and pointed the blade at the victim employee.  The suspect was found to be on parole. He was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 7:30 pm, an officer was sent to Safeway for the report that someone had just loaded up a shopping cart with over $1000.00 worth of merchandise and wheeled the cart outside without paying for the items.  When confronted by an employee, the cart became stuck in the mud and the suspect fled.

At 2:57 pm, an officer reports a victim was approached by several subjects while in his vehicle. One of the suspects struck the victim’s vehicle with a baseball as he fled. While trying to evade the suspects, the victim was involved in a collision. The suspects continued to follow the second victim into Richmond. The second victim was able to elude the suspects.
An officer reported two unknown suspects entered a vacant residence and stole several electronic items from the interior. The crime occurred sometime overnight on April 1.
At 6:31 PM an officer located an unoccupied stolen vehicle that was reported stolen to the San Pablo Police Department.
At 2:40 am, an officer was sent to the business for the report that a suspect had come inside and pushed the monitors for the cash registers off of the counter causing them to break.

At 9:33 pm an officer responded for the report of a vehicle collision.  The officer investigated the collision and then learned one of the drivers had a warrant for his arrest.  The person was placed under arrest and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer pulled over a vehicle and found that the driver had a warrant for their arrest issued from Marin County.  The driver was issued a citation for the warrant and released at the scene.

At 5:10 pm, an officer responded to a report of two individuals on dirt bikes riding on the golf course and damaging the greens. The vandals were not located, and a report was taken for the resulting damage.

At 5:06 pm, after being released on a citation for shoplifting at Ross in the El Cerrito Plaza, the same 58- year-old male from San Francisco, went to CVS where he loaded up a backpack with $1074.00 worth of merchandise and left without paying.  The male was arrested by El Cerrito police officer, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released on a citation.
