At 7:30 pm while investigating the above vehicle burglary, the officer noticed another vehicle had a broken window.  It was determined that someone had reached into the vehicle to look behind the seat but had not stolen anything.
At 8:40 hours, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from a victim’s vehicle.
At 1812 hours an officer was sent to Ross for the report that a suspect had just ran out with property that they had not paid for.  The suspect was not located.
At 11:04 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 33-year-old female after the female broke a window to the business. The female was identified and then released on a citation.
At 3:22 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect forced entry into a locked shed and took two bicycles.

At 10:21 am, an officer responded to the report of a theft that had just occurred.  The suspect threatened staff and stole property.  The suspect, a 31-year-old male was located nearby and the property was recovered.  The subject was also wanted on a felony warrant from Solano County.  The suspect was booked and then taken to county jail.
At 9:48 pm an officer was sent to CVS for the report that a suspect had ran out of the store with $1000.00 worth of merchandise.  The suspect was not located.

At 11:10 pm, officers were dispatched to a home where a victim had been stabbed after the suspect forced his way into a structure where she was staying.  Officers rendered medical aid until EMS arrived.  The victim was transported to the hospital by an ambulance.  The victim named the suspect and gave officers a description of him.  About an hour later, an officer spotted the suspect in another part of the city.  The suspect, a 43-year-old male was taken into custody after fleeing on foot and hiding.  The suspect was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Location: San Pablo Avenue at Hill Street
Description: At 2:28 am an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on a vehicle and after an investigation determined the driver was intoxicated.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, issued a citation, and then released to a sober family member.

At 3:30 am, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on a vehicle.  The driver had a suspended driver’s license and during a search of the vehicle Methamphetamine was found in the driver’s purse.  The driver was arrested and issued a citation and released at the scene.
At 10:40 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed a window to a victim’s vehicle and removed property.

CrimeType: Possession of a Stolen Vehicle and Warrant Arrest
Description: At 7:51 am, an officer located an occupied stolen vehicle. The driver, a 33-year-old female, and the passenger, a 43-year-old male were taken into custody. The female was identified and then released on a citation. The male was wanted on two outstanding warrants and taken to county jail.

At 8:29 pm, officers were dispatched to a robbery that just occurred.  Two suspects forced the victim to the ground.  One of the suspects stood on her back and kicked her phone away from her and then picked it up and fled.  One of the two suspects was located at the Plaza Bart station and placed under arrest.  The victim’s stolen property was recovered from the suspect.  The suspect was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to Contra Costa County Juvenile Hall.
At 8:29 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect set a vehicle on fire. The owner was not located, and police responded to the address of the owner but did not locate them.
An unknown suspect stole a catalytic convertor from a Honda Accord between the hours of 5:45 am and 1:50 pm.
An unknown suspect entered a business over the weekend and stole several laptops. The theft took place between Friday the 21st at 7:00 pm and the 24th at 8:15 am.
An unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from a Ford F-250 during the course of the past several weeks.
An unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle some time during the past 3 weeks.
At 1:34 pm, an unknown suspect stole a lighter and cigarettes from the business.

At 9:05pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 57-year old male from Richmond for driving under the influence of drugs after he drove his vehicle into a business and 3 parked vehicles. The suspect was transported to ECPD for booking and released on a citation to a sober relative. Possession of Burglary Tools, Narcotics, Drug Paraphernalia and Warrant Arrest: At 3:10am, an officer conducted a traffic stop on vehicle that he recognized as the suspect vehicle involved in a catalytic converter theft a few days ago. The driver was in possession of shaved keys and a pipe used to smoke narcotic drugs. The passenger was in possession of narcotics and had a failure to appear warrant for burglary from Santa Clara County. The driver was released on scene on a citation and the passenger was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 4:50 pm, a man entered Safeway and assaulted the security guard when being confronted about not wearing a mask. The man punched the security guard and in turn was pepper sprayed. The man was placed under citizen’s arrest and released from the scene with a citation.
At 9:04pm, an officer reports that a known suspect stole $250 worth of clothing and bags from the business. The suspect was gone prior to the officer’s arrival.
At 9:00, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a female who assaulted a person during an argument. The female was identified and then released at the scene with a citation.
At 6:42 pm, an officer reports that an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s Honda Accord. The theft occurred between 9am-6:30pm.

License plates were stolen from a vehicle parked in the El Cerrito Plaza parking lot some time during the day.  This theft occurred later in the day and is not believed to be associated with the suspicious vehicle detail above.

At 11:31 pm, officers were dispatched to the store for the report that two people were walking around the store eating food items and placing items into their pockets.  Officers arrived and contacted the subjects who were still walking around the store.  One of the subjects was found to be on Parole and during a parole search the officer found suspected methamphetamine in their possession.  They were placed under arrest and issued a citation and released from the scene.

Officers were dispatched to a suspicious vehicle detail at this location.  The reporting party believed the vehicle was associated with the recent spate of catalytic converter thefts in the area.  When an officer located the vehicle and attempted contact with the occupants, the suspect drove the vehicle onto the sidewalk to get around the patrol vehicle then fled eastbound on El Dorado Avenue.  A brief pursuit took place but was discontinued due to excessively dangerous driving by the suspect.

An unidentified suspect gained access to the victim vehicle by cutting the convertible top.  Take was a tool bag and cologne.  The burglary occurred between 9PM Friday night and 7:30AM Saturday morning.
An unidentified suspect shattered the right rear window of the victim vehicle and ransacked the interior some time overnight.  No known loss.

At 5:31 am an officer saw a vehicle that was stopped facing the wrong direction in the lane of traffic.  The officer contacted the subjects in the car who were both found to be on active probation.  A probation search was conducted, and suspected Methamphetamine and Fentanyl were found in the car.  The driver was arrested, issued a citation, and released at the scene.

At 6:35 pm an officer was sent to the store regarding a theft that had occurred earlier in the day.  The suspect entered the store and left with two pairs of shoes that they did not pay for.  Two workers ran after the suspect and he assaulted one of the store employees and then fled the scene.

At 8:47 pm, officers were sent to a call where two suspects had attempted to take the tip jar and run out of the business.  An employee grabbed the suspect who had the tip jar and held onto him until he dropped it.  This crime had occurred 2.5 hours earlier and the same two suspects were also responsible for the theft at Kyoto Ramen. Warrant Arrest – Cutting Blvd. at I-80: At 9:18 pm, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and discovered the driver had an outstanding warrant for their arrest for arson issued by the Richmond Police Department.  The subject was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:25 pm, officers were sent to the El Cerrito Plaza for the report that a Robbery had occurred.  The victim reported that they were speaking on their cell phone in front of CVS when a suspect forcibly stole the phone from her hands and ran off.
At 6:57 pm officers were sent to the report that someone had just grabbed the tip jar and ran to a vehicle and drove away.  The suspects were not located.

CrimeType: DUI / Hit and Run / Driving on a Suspended License
Location: San Pablo Avenue at Manila Avenue
Description: At 1:08 am officers were dispatched for the report of a vehicle collision. When officers arrived, they found the person who had caused the collision had fled the scene, leaving behind their car.  An officer found the suspect a few blocks away and noticed they were exhibiting signs of being under the influence of a controlled substance and in possession of controlled substance paraphernalia.  The subject was transported to the county jail.
An unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from a Toyota Prius sometime during the night of the 31st.
At 7:11 pm, an officer reports two unknown suspects broke a window to the victim’s residence sometime during the weekend in an attempted burglary; no entry was made into the home.
At 3:00 pm, an unknown suspect entered the business, stole merchandise, and when security tried to detain him.

At 6:09 pm, an officer accepted the Citizen’s Arrest of a 50-year-old man after the victim was reportedly assaulted by the man; the male was released with a citation. The suspect eventually abandoned the stolen vehicle and fled in an accomplice’s vehicle. While fleeing, the suspect turned and pointed a firearm at the victim.
An unknown suspect shattered the window to the victim’s vehicle sometime during the night.

At 4:24 pm, officers were dispatched to motorist slumped over the wheel.  Officers arrived on scene and contacted the motorist. Officers spotted methamphetamine on the dashboard and the driver had heroin in his pockets. The man was issued a ticket and released at the scene.
An unknown suspect stole a white Honda Accord between the hours of 3:30 pm and 5:30 pm.
An unidentified suspect stole approximately $500 worth of cosmetics.  The suspect swung the handbasket at the manager causing her to fear for her safety before he walked out with the items.

At 8:48 pm, officers were dispatched to a call of a Robbery that had just occurred.  The store employees saw a suspect taking shoes from a box and placing them down his pants.  An employee went and locked the doors so the suspect could not get out.  When the suspect got to the front doors, he pulled a hypodermic needle out of his sock and pointed it at the employee and told him to unlock the door.  The employee fearing, they would be stuck by the needle, unlocked the door and the suspect fled with the shoes.
A 20-year-old man turned himself in on a DUI warrant in response to a letter mailed by ECPD.  The man was issued a citation with a new court date.
The victim’s 1997 Subaru was stolen by an unidentified suspect between sometime overnight on 6/6/2021.
An unidentified suspect entered the vacant residence by breaking out a window at some time between 11:00 am on 5/29/2021 and 11:30 am on 6/6/2021.  The suspect stole a television.
An unidentified suspect stole the victim’s Amigo Polo scooter between 3:00 pm on 6/5/2021 and 8:00 am on 6/6/2021.  The scooter was located a short time later in the 900 block of Lexington Ave.
A 2005 GMC Yukon was stolen between 10 PM on 6/5/2021 and 9 AM on 6/6/2021 by an unidentified suspect.
An unidentified suspect kicked the front door of the dry cleaner and entered the business some time overnight.    No known loss.

An unidentified suspect covered a motion light, stood on a trash can and attempted to open a window of the residence, causing minor damage to the window frame, at some time between 3:00 PM on 6/5/2021 and 9:30 am on 6/6/2021, but it appeared no entry was made.
At 6:48 pm an officer was sent to the report that someone had stolen the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle sometime in the last day.
At 11:09 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect damaged the victim’s flowering bush on three separate occasions: the most recent being earlier that same day.
At 7:12 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle from the street sometime over the past 2 days.

At 2:43 pm, the victim was walking when an unknown suspect touched the victim inappropriately and then fled in a red Prius.  Detectives later identified and contacted the suspect, a 26-year-old man who was out of the area.  He was interviewed and a referral was sent to the District Attorney’s office.

At 3:46 pm, an officer stopped a vehicle and spoke with the driver who was unlicensed and on probation. While speaking with the officer, the driver, a 22-year-old man admitted to having fentanyl with him. The officer searched the vehicle and located methamphetamine. The man was issued a citation and released in the field.
An unknown suspect attempted to steal the catalytic converter from a Yukon between the 8th at 6:00 pm and the 9th at 3:30 pm.

An officer reports a suspect cashed a fraudulent check at a business sometime during the month of May.

At 8:43 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 43-year-old male. The male was a wanted person out from Alameda County. The male was transported to the county jail.
At 4:32 am, an officer reports that an unknown suspect partially cut the catalytic converter from the victim’s Toyota Prius but was not able to successfully remove it.
At 10:53 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered a business, put on a pair of shoes, and then left without paying. The suspect was last seen running west on Cutting Boulevard.

At 6:46 pm, an officer located an unoccupied Chevy pickup truck that was reported stolen to San Rafael PD earlier in the day.

An unknown suspect stole over $1100 worth of batteries from an electric lift which was stored in the Denny’s parking lot.
At 6:50 pm, an officer discovered a vehicle with a shattered passenger window. An unknown suspect stole luggage from the backseat.

At 8:22 pm, officers were dispatched to the business for a bomb threat. An unknown suspect called CVS and told them he was going to blow up the business. The business was evacuated, and the threat was determined to be unsubstantiated.

At 2:53 pm, an unknown suspect entered the business and selected two empty shipping bins.  He then filled them with electronic items from the store.  When employees confronted the suspect, he removed a baton from his waistband and brandished it threateningly.  Employees also saw he had a firearm. The suspect fled into an awaiting vehicle with the stolen merchandise.

At 7:05 am, a 30-year-old male entered the gas station, selected food items and then left without paying.  The man was contacted by officers a short distance away.  After consulting with the business, the subject was admonished and released.
An unknown suspect stole a 2006 Dodge Charger sometime during the night of the 16th.
At 2:07 pm, an unknown female entered the business and left without paying for a bag of popcorn.
At 8:02 am, an unknown suspect stole two bottles of alcohol.

At 6:06 pm, officers responded to the report of a sexual battery.  An unknown suspect inappropriately touched a woman who was getting her children into a vehicle.  The suspect fled after she screamed.

At 1:02 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered a business, concealed merchandise, and then left without paying.

At 11:00 pm an officer found two subject under a truck sawing off the catalytic converters.  When the suspects saw the officer, they ran off and began to jump over fences.  A perimeter was set up around the block and both suspects were located taken into custody.     Both suspects were booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 10:58 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 33-year-old male. The male entered a business concealed merchandise, and then left without paying.  During a previous arrest, the subject had been admonished not to return to the business.  The male was located and detained, and during the detention he attempted to walk away from the officer. The male was detained and taken into custody. The male was subsequently booked and then released on a citation.

At 6:47 pm an officer was sent to this store for the report that a suspect had come inside and helped themselves to a beer and then left.  The subject was located nearby drinking the beer.  He was identified as the suspect, placed under citizen’s arrest.  He was found to be in possession of suspected methamphetamine.  He was booked and transported to the County jail.

At 9:04 pm officers responded to the report of a battery.  The suspect pushed the victim and then slashed his tires with a knife.  Officer located the suspect who was also found to be in possession of brass knuckles.  The suspect was placed under arrest, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:51 am an officer was sent to the report that someone had just stolen a catalytic converter off of a vehicle.

CrimeType: Possession of a Stolen Vehicle, Battery upon an Officer, Warrant Arrest
Description: At 9:02 am, officers responded to a report of a suspicious vehicle. Investigation revealed the vehicle was reported stolen. Officers located the driver of the vehicle, a 33-year-old woman nearby.  When officers took her into custody, she punched and tried to bite the officer.  As she was put into the patrol vehicle, she headbutted an officer causing injury.  She was booked into county jail on possession of stolen property, resisting arrest with violence and three warrants.

At 9:56 pm, an officer responded to the store on the report that two suspects had grabbed approximately $1000.00 worth of merchandise and ran out without paying.  The suspects got into a vehicle and drove away.

Location: San Pablo Avenue near Knott Avenue
Description: At 12:23 hours an officer pulled over a vehicle and found the driver had two outstanding warrants for their arrest.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

An unknown suspect stole a catalytic converter from a Toyota Prius between the evening of the 18th to 7:00 am on the 22nd. Once the victim got his drink back, the suspect punched him in the face and threatened to stab him. The suspect exited the business, opened his backpack and removed a package which contained a large machete.  Luckily, a Starbucks employee rushed to the door and locked it so the suspect could not get back in and he fled the area. Strong Arm Robbery - 9950 San Pablo Avenue, Chase At 10:14 pm, an officer reports a suspect grabbed a wallet from the victim and ran off; the wallet was later located but was missing an ID and access cards.

At 3:25 pm, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s purse which was left unattended in an unlocked vehicle. Vehicle Theft- 1700 block of Eastshore Boulevard An unknown suspect stole a Toyota Yaris between the 10th and then 22nd of June.

An unknown suspect stole a 2005 Infiniti G35 between the 23rd at 5:00 pm and the 24th at 10:45 am.

California Check Cashing Center reported that a known suspect passed a counterfeit check on April 9th of this year.

At 1558 hours, an officer responded to the report that an unknown suspect shattered the front passenger window and removed documents and cash from the glove box. The time of occurrence was between 1400 to 1600 hours on 6/25/21.

At 7:20 pm an officer went to a call that a catalytic converter had been stolen off of the victim’s vehicle sometime during the previous night.

At 3:04 am an officer pulled a vehicle over and after an investigation it was determined the driver was intoxicated.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 1200 hours, an officer responded to the report of an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and removed a purse containing credit cards. One of the credit cards was used in the City of San Pablo at approximately 0600 hours.

At 6:22 pm an officer was dispatched for the report of a strong-armed robbery that just occurred.  The Walgreens security officer attempted to intervene in a theft and the suspect shoved him violently to the ground and fled the scene.
At 1340 hours, an officer responded to the report of an unknown suspect removed the victim’s wallet from her purse while she was shopping.

Description: Shoplifting- 3160 Carlson Boulevard, Union 76: At 1:58 pm, an unknown suspect stole beer from the business.  The suspect fled in a stolen vehicle.

At 9:00 am, an officer responded to the report that an unknown suspect jumped the gate to the parking lot and stole a wheel from a bicycle. An unknown suspect entered the residence through a window and stole jewelry and laptops between 2:30 pm and 4:50 pm.
An unknown suspect found the victim’s lost wallet in the Plaza and then used a debit card to withdraw cash in Richmond.
An unknown suspect was able to obtain the victim’s social security number through a scam on Facebook.

At 5:15 am, several suspects stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s Prius. The man was also found to have a warrant for his arrest from a neighboring county. The man was transported to the El Cerrito Police Department and later released with a citation.
At 7:48 pm, officers were dispatched to the report that two suspects had just broken into three different vehicles and stole property from within.

At 3:15 pm, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 25-year-old female. The female got into an argument with a security guard and then spat on him. The security followed the female who then spat on him a second time. The female was positively identified and then cited.

At 11:39 pm, officers were dispatched to a subject in the drive thru damaging other people’s vehicles and getting in a physical fight with at least one person.  When officers arrived, they detained the subject who was showing the signs of being intoxicated.  It was determined he had wanted to exit the drive thru line however, there were cars in front and behind him.  He then got out of his car and kicked the person’s vehicle in front of him.  The kick caused damage to the person’s vehicle.  He then got into a physical fight with a person who was in the vehicle stopped behind him.  He was placed under citizen’s arrest for the Battery and Vandalism and arrested for DUI.  He was transported to the ECPD for booking and then to the Martinez Detention Facility after booking.

At 7:56 pm an officer saw people running from the store to a parked vehicle, carrying arm loads of merchandise.  As the vehicle left the parking lot the officer attempted to pull the vehicle over however it began to drive recklessly, and a pursuit was not initiated.

At 10:09 am, the victim was sitting in her vehicle in front of the business when the suspect stopped to video record her with his cell phone.  An argument ensued and the suspect slammed the vehicle’s door on the victim’s leg and fled the scene.

An unknown suspect damaged the padlock to the victim’s garage door and entered the garage.   The suspect ransacked the victims two unlocked vehicles parked inside but did not steal anything.  The burglary occurred between 4:30 pm on July 3rd to 7:30 am on July 4, 2021.

At 2:04 am, officers were dispatched to the Crown Bar on the report of a disturbance and a victim getting punched during an altercation.   After punching the victim, the suspect fled the scene.

At 9:51 am, the victim spotted several suspects removing the catalytic converter from their Prius. Petty Theft- 225 El Cerrito Plaza, Trader Joe’s On the 6th around 5:00 pm, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s wallet while they were shopping.
At 8:08 am, security personnel were patrolling in a golf cart when an unidentified woman threw a glass bottle and struck the cart. Fortunately, the security guard was not hit by the bottle.

An unknown suspect stole a motorized scooter sometime during the night of the 7th. At 6:24 am, an unknown male and two females went to the back parking lot and stole the catalytic converter from a Honda CR-V. As the three fled the scene, the male suspect was walking away with a handgun at his side.

The suspect then transferred over $5000.00 of the victim’s money, from a different bank account, into the fraudulent account.

At 6:52 pm an officer was sent to the report that a vehicle had been broken into and property stolen from inside sometime in the previous 24 hours.

At 12:20 am officers were sent to a business for the report that the alarm had been activated.  The officers arrived and found the doors were closed and locked.  The business owner arrived, and it was discovered that someone had entered the business through a hole in the roof and stole items from inside the business.

At 11:11 am, an officer reports the victim left a business and was approached by a male. The male asked to use the victim’s phone. After returning the victim’s cell phone, the suspect removed a handgun from his waistband and demanded the victim’s property. The suspect took the victim’s watch and necklace and fled in a vehicle.

At 3:57 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 35-year-old male. The male stole a pair of shoes from a residence and was located nearby. A warrant check revealed he was a wanted person. The male was transported to county jail.

CrimeType: Felon in Possession of a Firearm
Description: At 5:13 pm, officers responded to a distubance. During the investigation it was revealed a 33-year-old male, who is felon, was in possession of a rifle and was carrying it during an argument.  The male was taken into custody and transported to county jail.

At 2:25 hours, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole a package of the victim’s porch. Shoplifting – 5800 block of Cutting Boulevard: At 4:48 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect selected several pairs of shoes and backpack from a business and left without paying.

At 1:06 pm, an officer reports the victim was approached by two subjects who demanded property. The victim gave the suspects his belt and then one of the suspects rifled through the victim’s pockets and took his cell phone. The suspects then fled the area.
An unknown suspect entered the victim’s backyard sometime during the night of the 12th and stole a propane tank.

At 4:05 pm, a Marin County resident received a call from an unknown person saying that his daughter had been kidnapped. The called demanded the victim drive to the bank, withdraw cash and wire the money to a location in Mexico. After completing the transfer, the victim was told that his daughter would be killed unless he drove to an additional bank.  As the victim entered El Cerrito his phone died, so he drove to the police station.  A detective was able to get a hold of the victim’s daughter and confirm the man was the victim of a phone scam.

At 1:58 am an officer was sent to the report that someone had just been under their neighbor’s vehicle and then fled in a white SUV.  When the officer arrived, they found the catalytic converter had been stolen from the vehicle.
At 2:14 am, an officer was sent to the report that someone had just stolen the catalytic converter off of their vehicle ten minutes prior.  The suspect left in a white SUV.
A known suspect violated a restraining order by contacting the victim via text messages.
An unknown suspect stole the license plates off a 2011 Kia between the 14th at 9:00 pm and the 15th at 11:00 am.

At approximately 7:40am, a 48-year-old male after officers responded to the report of a domestic battery.  The male was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and then transported to County Jail in Martinez.

At 10:08 pm, El Cerrito police officers responded to the Ross store for a report of a found child.  A five-year-old girl was abandoned in the alley behind Ross.  Officer attempted to locate the victim’s mother however she could not be located.  Child Protective Services was notified and responded to take custody of the child.

At 7:18 pm, an officer was sent to a call where someone had pried open a sliding glass door at an apartment complex to get into a room with the mailboxes in it.  Once inside, the suspect apparently pried open all the mailboxes.  This was reported to have happened sometime during the day.
At 6:50 pm, an officer responded to the report of stolen license plates.   The suspect had replaced the victim’s stolen license plates with a license plate stolen in San Francisco.

At 9:10 pm, an officer was sent to the report that the victim had just walked to his car I the parking lot and there was someone under the car attempting to remove the catalytic converter.  When confronted by the victim, the suspect fled the area.

A 41 year-old transient was arrested after he put a knife to the victim’s neck during an argument. The victim was also strangled by the suspect. The suspect was booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. Recovered Stolen Plates – Schmidt Lane and Norvell Court At 2:47 am, an officer made a traffic stop and located a license plate attached to the rear of a vehicle which had previously been reported as stolen; the plate was removed from the vehicle.
(Delayed Reporting) An unknown suspect stole the license plates from the victim’s vehicle on the 27th of May.
An unknown suspect broke into a garage between 10:44 am to 11:26 am on the 19th and stole miscellaneous items.
An unknown suspect stole a 2005 Ford Mustang sometime during the night of the 14th.
An unknown suspect broke into the victim’s vehicle and stole property sometime during the night of the 18th.

At 2:20 am, officers were dispatched for the report of a subject breaking windows of a residence and trying to get inside.  Officers located the suspect inside the residence.  The suspect was barred from the property by a valid restraining order.  He had apparently kicked open a door to gain access inside the home.  After he was booked at ECPD, the subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:08 am, an unknown suspect stole a laptop from the front passenger seat of a vehicle as the victim was preparing to leave.

An officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s license plates sometime during the past several weeks. Robbery – 225 El Cerrito Plaza, Trader Joe’s At 4:15 pm, an unknown suspect wrenched the victim’s phone from her hand and fled on a bicycle towards Albany.

CrimeType: Trespassing, Assault, Resisting Arrest, AB109 Violation
Description: At 7:46 am, officers responded to call of a man hitting the glass windows of the business with a stick and cussing at employees. Upon arrival, officers learned that the man, a 41-year-old Richmond resident, had also kicked one of the security guards.  While being placed under arrest, the man resisted and refused to be handcuffed.  While struggling with the suspect, officers were able to remove several concealed knives from the suspect’s waistband.  Ultimately, the man was handcuffed and transported to Kaiser for a pre-existing medical condition.
At 10:45 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect pried open an apartment complex mailbox before being scared off by residents; it is unknown if anything was taken from inside the mailbox.
An unknown suspect stole a 1996 Honda Civic sometime during the night of the 19th.
At 8:30 am, an officer arrested a 48-year-old El Cerrito man for violating a restraining order. The man was booked at the ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
A Community Services Officer located a Ford F-150 that had been reported stolen to Concord PD on 7/18.

Location: 6009 Potrero Avenue, Hotel Mira Vista Berkley North
Description: At 11:05 pm, an officer reports a female companion intentionally damaged the interior of the victim’s vehicle during an argument.

Location: 6009 Potrero Avenue, Hotel Mira Vista Berkley North
Description: At 8:40 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect threw rocks at the hotel on several prior occasions; during one instance a window was broken.

A known suspect entered the tent of an unsheltered individual and stole over $800 of property on the 21st. Indecent Exposure, Probation Violation- 2000 block of Key Boulevard At 6:34 pm, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 52-year-old male after he exposed himself to the victim. The male, who is also on probation, was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:54 am, officers responded to the report of several suspects stealing the catalytic converter from a Honda Civic.
At 2:54 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect collided into the victim’s vehicle and left the scene without providing their information.
At 11:10 am, an officer recovered a vehicle which was reported stolen to the Berkeley Police Department early in the day.
At 2:47 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered a building by forcing a door open. The suspect ransacked the interior but appeared not to have taken any property.

At 10:14 am, an officer reports while the victim was sitting in her vehicle, a suspect reached into her vehicle and grabbed her laptop.  Meanwhile another suspect stood watch.  A struggle ensued and the suspect was able to overpower the victim and yank the laptop away. The suspects then fled the area on foot.
At 11:48 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime overnight.
At 7:43 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime overnight. The vehicle would be recovered later in the day.

At 4:04 pm, an officer reports a known suspect returned to a homeless encampment and threatened bodily harm to the victim. The victim was able to find phone and call police. The suspect who is known fled the scene prior to police arrival.
At 2:52 pm, an officer reports a group of juveniles entered a business, loaded up large bags with merchandise, and then left without paying.

At 5:29 pm, an officer reports the arrest of an 81-year-old male. The male struck a parked vehicle and then fled the scene without providing the necessary information. The driver was located in his vehicle nearby and was heavily intoxicated. The male was taken into custody, booked, and then released to a family member.
At 2:08 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered a business through the roof and once inside stole cash.
At 1:17 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime between 7/25/21 to 7/26/21.
At 10:38 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime overnight.
At 7:02 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered a business through the roof. Once inside the suspect rummaged through the business but did take any property.

At 8:16 pm an officer was sent to the report that a suspect had just stolen items from the store and had ran off towards the Bart station.  When the officer arrived they reported this crime as well as another person who had stolen items and ran out of the business.  Two separate theft reports were authored.
At 9:15 pm, an unknown suspect pried open the bank of mailboxes. Residents were unable to determine if anything was stolen.
An unknown suspect stole a 1988 Honda Civic between the 25th at 6:00 pm and the 27th at 1:20 pm.
At 11:45 am, several unknown suspects stole various items and fled the business in a blue Murano.
In between 0647 hours, an unknown suspect stole a scooter off a front porch.
At 4:38 am, a resident heard someone sawing the catalytic converter from his Honda Accord. As the victim ran outside, the suspects fled in a vehicle with the stolen catalytic converter.

An unknown suspect stole a 1996 Honda Civic between the 27th at 8:00 pm and the 28th at 9:30 am. Shoplifting-670 El Cerrito Plaza, CVS At 8:08 am, an unknown suspect stole miscellaneous items from the business.

An unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from a Honda Accord during the night of the 27th. Shoplifting-11565 San Pablo Avenue, Walgreens At 1:20 pm, two unknown suspects entered the business and loaded up a garbage bag with merchandise and then fled on foot.

In between 1415 – 1445 hours, an unknown suspect stole electronics valued at $4000 from an unlocked vehicle. Petty Theft- 6500 Stockton Avenue, EC Library Sometime during the day of the 27th, an unknown suspect entered a city work truck and stole two cell phones.
An unknown suspect stole the victim’s 2013 Chevy Camaro sometime between 2 pm and 6 pm.
An unknown suspect attempted to steal a Chevrolet pickup truck.

At 12:17 pm, a 42-year-old Richmond man punched a victim who was walking along San Pablo Avenue. After hitting the man, the suspect tried to rip the purse from a woman’s shoulder but was unsuccessful. Based on the description, an officer recognized the suspect and responded to a location where the subject was known to frequent. When the officer arrived, the suspect fled on foot but was quickly apprehended.  It was later learned that the suspect had attacked another victim in the area.  The suspect was taken to ECPD, booked and transported to the MDF.
During the early morning hours of the 25th, an unknown suspect stole a motorcycle.
At 11:19 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the business, selected merchandise, and then left without paying.
At 10:46 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed a passenger side window and rummaged through the interior. There were no items taken from inside the vehicle.

At 10:34 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 39-year-old male. The male entered a business, and used a key to open several coin dispensers, taking all of the money. The male then took the coins to a nearby Coinstar and cashed in the coins. The male was identified through surveillance footage and later taken into custody.

At 7:24 am, an officer reports a male and female got into a verbal argument. The argument escalated the next morning when the female ripped off a pair of glasses of the male’s face, causing minor scratches.
At 3:57 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect punched the driver side door of the victim’s vehicle leaving minor damage. The suspect fled the area on foot prior to police arrival.
At 5:17 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle. The theft occurred overnight.

At 6:20 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect attempted to remove a catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle. The suspects were not able to remove the converter as they were scared off by a neighbor.
At 9:04am an officer reports that an unknown suspect pried open a key-lock-box for the mail carrier and stole a key to the apartment complex.

Location: El Cerrito Honda, 11755 San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 8:25 am, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 2017 Honda Civic from the service bay.
Between 6 am and 12:30 pm, an unknown suspect entered the victim’s residence by prying open the front door.  Once inside, the suspect stole the victim’s property and fled.
At 12:03 am an officer was sent to the business for the report that someone had thrown a rock through a window on the second floor shattering it sometime earlier in the evening.
Sometime during the month of July, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.

An employee of the business stole over $600 worth of CA Lottery scratcher tickets.  Arrangements were made for the employee to reimburse the business.  However, the employee never showed up to work again.
At 7:12 pm, an officer reports two unknown suspects entered the business and filled trash bags with numerous beauty products before driving off.
Between 8/1/21 at 11:45 pm and 8/2/21 at 6 am, an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and stole property.
At 6:15 am, two unknown suspects stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.  The suspects fled in an older model pickup truck.
At 10:38 pm, a neighbor reported hearing a loud grinding noise outside her home and found three unknown suspects underneath the victim’s vehicle removing the catalytic converter.
At 8 am, the victim called police after finding the catalytic converter had been cut, but not removed from the vehicle.
Between 8/3/21 at 9 pm and 8/4/21 at 6 am, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 1996 Honda Civic.
At 11:45 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect punched the driver-thru window at the restaurant causing the glass to break.
At 8:39 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a juvenile who had an active warrant for their arrest; the juvenile was transported to Juvenile Hall in Martinez.
Between 8/2/21 at 1 pm and 8/3/21 at 7 pm, an unknown suspect stole the license plates from the victim’s vehicle.
Between 8/4/21 at 8:30 pm and 8/5/21 at 4:30 pm, an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and rummaged the interior.

CrimeType: Battery on Police Officer, Assault, Probation Violation
Description: At 3:15 am, officers responded to a report of an assault and located the 35-year-old male suspect still on scene. Officers attempted to arrest the man, who then became combative and fought with officers.  The subject was taken into custody and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:50 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s backyard and stole a bicycle.
Between 9 am and 9:30 am, an unknown suspect broke into two vehicles in the parking lot and stole backpacks containing computer equipment and tools.
At 7:19 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect rode off on the victim’s bicycle after cutting the bike lock.
At 5 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered a business, selected merchandise, and then left without paying.
At 5:00 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.

At 8:23 pm as an officer was driving through the parking lot, he was flagged down about a theft from the store that had just occurred.  Officers located the suspect nearby.  The stolen property was recovered, and the suspect was placed under citizen’s arrest, issued a citation, and released from the scene.

At 10:55 pm an officer conducted a pedestrian stop for suspicious behavior and discovered one of the subjects had an outstanding warrant for their arrest held by the San Francisco Police Department.  The subject was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then issued a citation and released.

At 8:49 pm while an officer was driving through the Walgreen’s parking lot the store security guard pointed out a subject that had just stolen from the store.  The subject, who was initially uncooperative was detained.  She was placed under citizen’s arrest, issued a citation, and released from the scene.

Location: San Pablo Avenue at Potrero Avenue
Description: At 11:32 pm an officer was on a traffic stop and a car kept speeding past his location and running red lights to make U-turns.  After it sped past the officer three times the vehicle pulled over behind the officer’s vehicle and approached the officer.  The officer detained the subject for the reckless driving, driving without a license and being a juvenile out after curfew.  The juvenile was brought back to the El Cerrito Police Department and was released to his father with a juvenile citation.

At 5:34 pm, an officer reports an ex-boyfriend sent the victim several threatening text messages throughout the day. The victim arrived at a business and saw the suspect and contacted security to call the police. The suspect left the scene before police arrival.

At 9:22 pm an officer contacted the occupant in a parked vehicle and discovered the subject was on probation out of Sacramento County.  During a probation search the officer found a glass pipe used for smoking Methamphetamine in the subject’s pocket.  The subject was issued a citation at the scene.
At 2:41 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the front license plate off their vehicle.

Location: San Pablo Avenue at Schmidt Lane
Description: At 1:03 am someone called the police to report that there was a person passed out in the driver’s seat of a truck and they were worried they may need medical attention.  Officers arrived and after an investigation determined the subject was under the influence of alcohol.  A person who saw the subject driving the truck was interviewed and then the driver was placed under arrest.  They were booked at ECPD and then released to a sober family member.
At 11:00 pm an officer was dispatched to the report that someone had just vandalized their vehicle and had driven away from the scene.
At 8:45 pm an officer was dispatched to the report that the victim’s vehicle was stolen sometime in the previous ten hours.

An officer reports the arrest of a 34-year-old male after he was determined to be intoxicated. The male got into a fight with another patron and slapped another female patron.  Both victims left the area before contacting police. The subject was taken to county jail.
An officer reports a suspect removed property from a business. The subject was contacted, and the property was recovered, however the victim refused to press charges.
At 2:37 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the rear license plate of the victim’s vehicle.
At 7:25 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed two laptop computers.
At 10:12 am, an officer reports a suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.

At 3:42 am the California Highway Patrol called and asked ECPD to go to a residence and see if the homeowner knew where their vehicle was.  CHP had the vehicle pulled over and believed the person who was driving it had stolen it.  The officer contacted the registered owner of the vehicle and they confirmed that the vehicle had been stolen from in front of the house sometime during the previous two hours.

An unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from a Honda Accord between the 9th at 6:00 pm and the 10th at 8:00 am. Grand Theft- 2200 block of Key Boulevard: An unknown suspect the catalytic converter from a Prius on the 10th between 6:00 am and 4:30 pm. Annoying or Harassing Phone Calls, 2000 block of Harper Street: A resident report she received numerous annoying calls from an unknown person.
An unknown suspect stole a bicycle sometime during the night of the 11th.
At 2:53 am, an officer accepted the Citizen’s Arrest of a 38-year-old man who has been stalking the victim over the past few days. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:44 pm, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 62 year old male after the male battered a neighbor over a dispute. The male was field cited and released at the scene.
At 4:08 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect threw a brick at the victim’s vehicle leaving a dent on a driver side door.

At 8:17 pm, an officer was sent to the store for the report that someone had just shoplifted items.  The officer detained the suspect outside of the store when they arrived.  The store employee identified the suspect as the responsible person for the theft and placed the suspect under citizen’s arrest.  The suspect was issued a citation and released.

At 7:06 pm, officers responded to the report of a man beating another with a golf club.  As the officers arrived, they located the suspect a short distance away and detained him.  Officers were told that the suspect had poured coffee on the victim and then tried to hit him with the golf club.  The suspect was arrested, booked at ECPD, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:45 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
At 11:23 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered a business, selected merchandise, and then left without paying.

At 8:05 pm an officer was sent to the report that someone had just shot the victims vehicle with a paintball gun about six times.  One of the paintballs dented the side of the vehicle.  After shooting the car the suspect drove off in their car.
At 10:59 pm, an officer was sent to a report that the victim’s scooter had been stolen approximately forty minutes earlier.

At 2:44 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered a business, selected merchandise, and then left without paying. Petty Theft – 7400 block of Seaview Place: At 10:35 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered several unlocked vehicles on the listed block removing property from one of the vehicles.

Location: San Pablo Avenue near Barrett Avenue
Description: At 12:30 am, an officer received a LoJack signal in their patrol vehicle.  They used the receiver in the vehicle to follow the signal to the parked vehicle where it was found unoccupied.  The owner of the vehicle was contacted, and they came to the scene and took possession of their vehicle.
At 5:35 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s SUV sometime during the earlier morning hours of 8/15/21.
At 3:05 pm, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a male after he slapped away the hand of another citizen during an argument.
At 12:46 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property.
At 9:41 am, a customer threw an item at a store employee after a disagreement.
At 6:00 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 29-year-old man who was in possession of suspected heroin. The man was issued a citation and released from the scene.

An unknown suspect entered an unlocked vehicle and stole the victim’s wallet which contained their credit cards. The theft took place between the 15th at 6:30 pm and the 16th at 7:45 am. The victim also reported that their credit cards were used in Oakland sometime after the theft.
At 12:21 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle.
At 9:04 pm, an officer reports two unknown suspects left the store with merchandise which had not been paid for.
At 7:30 pm, an officer reports three unknown female suspects entered the business and removed an undetermined amount of merchandise before running towards BART

Location: Santa Clara Ave / San Diego St
Description: At 10:09 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 38-year-old man for stalking. The man had previously been arrested for the same crime; the man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:50 am, an officer reports the victim’s vehicle was stolen from the apartment complex near their home sometime between 2:00 pm and 8:00 pm earlier that day.
At 12:51 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property.
At 8:49 pm, an officer reports two unknown suspects entered the store and took an unknown quantity of makeup before fleeing towards BART.

At 4:17 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 20-year-old man who refused to leave the front yard of a residence after being told he was unwanted on the property. The man returned and when officers attempted to take him into custody, the man resisted arrest. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:36 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect cut a hole in the fence to a storage yard and removed property.

At approximately 2:01pm an unknown suspect stole Walgreen’s from inside the store and left without paying. The suspect was described as a black male adult, 20-30 years old, wearing a black colored clothing, a backpack and carrying a music keyboard.

At 11:45 pm, officers were dispatched to the report that a house burglar alarm was going off.  When they arrived, they saw a subject walking from the house.  They tried to stop the subject so they could speak with him, and he refused to stop.  One of the officers attempted to detain the subject and he kicked the officer.  After he was handcuffed, a search revealed he was in possession of drug paraphernalia.  The subject had warrants from another county for sex crime related offenses.  He was arrested, booked at ECPD, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 6:44 pm an officer was sent to the store for the report that someone had just left the store and drove away in their vehicle with approximately $200 worth of CVS property that they did not pay for.

At 10:25 pm an officer was sent to a call where someone was reporting that a subject had gone into their neighbor’s parked vehicle and then walked away.  The officer arrived and spoke to the owner of the vehicle who confirmed someone had been inside the vehicle however nothing had been stolen.
At 8:33 pm an officer was sent to the call that two customers were involved in an argument and one of the subjects dented the others vehicle and then drove away.

At 8:56 pm while an officer was inside of CVS they saw the manager confronting a shoplifter that was about to leave the store with merchandise in their pockets.  The suspect did not stop and was detained by the officer and placed under arrest.  The stolen property was returned to CVS.  The suspect was issued a citation and released from the scene.

At approximately 11:21am, an unknown suspect entered the business, selected merchandise, and then left without paying. The suspect was described as a black male adult, 18-25, wearing a black sweater with a multi-colored logo, red basketball shorts, with green long pants, and red shoes. He was accompanied by a second suspect described as a black male adult, 18-25 years old, wearing a black shirt, camo pants, and brown boots.
At 6:24 pm an officer was sent to the report that someone stole the two license plates from the victim’s vehicle sometime in the previous twelve hours.
At 6:46 pm an officer was sent to a call where a juvenile and come into the store and stolen a bottle of alcohol and fled the scene on a skateboard.
At 4:36 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle sometime overnight.

At 10:34 pm, officers were dispatched to the motel for the report of someone trespassing in one of the unoccupied rooms.  When then officers arrived, they found the subject sleeping in one of the beds in the room and found him to be heavily intoxicated.  It was determined that he had rented a different room and had somehow wandered into this room thinking it was his.  The subject was found to have a warrant for his arrest held by Madera County.  He was issued a citation for the warrant and was released to go back into his own room.
At 8:40 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle sometime overnight.

At 9:13 pm, while an officer was inside the store the manager confronted someone who was exiting the store with merchandise that they had not paid for.  The officer detained and arrested the subject.  The property was recovered and returned to the store.  The suspect was arrested, issued a citation, and released from the scene.

At 5:49 pm, the security guard tried to stop a subject from leaving the store with property they had not purchased.  As the suspect left, they assaulted the security guard and then fled on foot with the property towards the Del Norte Bart station.

At 6:23 pm, an officer was dispatched for the report that a subject was concealing merchandise.  As the subject left the store they were detained by the officer.  The officer accepted a citizen’s arrest for the suspect and the stolen property was returned to the store.  The suspect was arrested, issued a citation, and released from the scene.

At 9:30 pm, officers were sent for the report that someone had just left the store without paying for merchandise.  As an officer was arriving, the suspect was leaving in their vehicle.  The vehicle was stopped, and the driver was identified by store employees as responsible for the theft.  The officer accepted the citizen’s arrest for the suspect and the stolen property was recovered and returned to the store.  The suspect was issued a citation and released from the scene.
At 7:15 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed an electric bicycle from the victim’s property during the previous day.
At 8:11 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole a battery from a scissor lift sometime over the last two days.

At 10:09 am, officers responded to a report of a man with a gun. Officers arrived on scene and contacted the possible suspect and discovered the man’s brother-in-law purposely called to report the male had a firearm to get him into trouble with police.
At 8:08 pm an officer was sent to the report that the victim’s vehicle had been stolen sometime during the previous twelve hours.
At 10:01 pm, an officer went to the business for the report that someone had left the store with merchandise they did not pay for.

At 9:16 pm, officers were called to the business by employees for someone who was inside and heavily intoxicated.  Officers arrived and determined the person was intoxicated; however, they were unable to locate a suitable guardian. The subject was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 4:19 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect took the victim’s bike off their porch sometime overnight.
An officer reported the victim had the license plates stolen from their vehicle sometime during the 24th.
An officer reported that a known suspect violated a civil harassment order by having mail sent to the victim’s residence.

At 5:59 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle sometime over the past two days. At 10:56 am, an unknown suspect went into a utility worker’s vehicle and stole a cell phone.

An unknown suspect entered a vehicle and stole miscellaneous items between the 24th at 6:00 pm, and the 25th at 9:45 am. Shoplifting - 11687 San Pablo Avenue, Valero: At 7:28 pm, an officer reported two unknown suspects entered the business and stole energy drinks and a hat. Stolen Vehicle - I-80 W/B @ Cutting Boulevard: At 2:36 am, an officer attempted to make a traffic stop on a previously reported stolen vehicle. The vehicle failed to yield, and the pursuit was terminated without incident.

Location: 11100 San Pablo Avenue, Bank of the West
Description: At 11:02 am, an unknown suspect entered the victim’s vehicle and stole her purse. Petty Theft - 5805 Cutting Boulevard, Sketchers: At 1:03 pm, the same suspect from the Walgreens theft earlier in the day stole a pair of shoes from Sketchers. CrimeType: At 5:49 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect approached the victim and pointed a firearm at the victim before demanding the victim’s jewelry. The suspect fled in a red Honda C
Description: Shoplifting- 11319 San Pablo Avenue, Chevron: At 6:37 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business and took food and drink items without payment.

At 7:18 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle sometime during the prior day. Shoplifting- 2000 El Cerrito Plaza, Ross: At 8:35 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspects entered the business and stole clothing, men’s shoes, and home goods.

At 2:44 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered a business and walked into the employee break area. When confronted by an employee, the suspect shoved the employee. The suspect was confronted by another employee and fled the business.

At 8:59 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect smashed a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed a duffle bag containing miscellaneous property. The suspect also smashed a window to a second vehicle but did remove any property.

At 11:46 am, an officer reported the victim’s ex-boyfriend sent several dozen text messages to the victim in attempt to reconcile their relationship. The ex-boyfriend also showed up at the victim’s residence unexpectedly on two occasions.
At 3:05 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered a business, selected merchandise, and then left the business without paying.

At 2:39 am, an officer conducted a check of the license plate number of a vehicle that he saw driving via Dispatch. The Officers was advised the vehicle was reported stolen to the Oakland Police Department.  A traffic stop was conducted, and the driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 3:28 pm, an officer reported two friends who were intoxicated got into a physical altercation over a bike. During the fight they assaulted each other with beer bottles causing lacerations to each another. Both subjects were transported to hospitals for medical treatment.
At 10:52 pm, an officer conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle and arrested the driver, a 30-year-old San Francisco woman, for driving under the influence of alcohol.

At 8:56 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 20-year-old Richmond woman who entered the business, selected merchandise, and fled the store without paying. Possession of Narcotics and Drug Paraphernalia – 1700 block of Eastshore Boulevard: At 10:27 pm, an officer conducted a suspicious vehicle check and arrested the driver, a 20-year old Oakland man for being in possession of methamphetamine and a device used to smoke methamphetamine.
At 4:14 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business, selected merchandise, and then fled the store without paying.
At 10:39 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the victim’s vehicle and removed property sometime during the evening.

At 12:16 am, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on a vehicle and found the driver to be on parole for firearm violations.  During a search of the vehicle a handgun was located that was reported stolen to San Diego PD.  The suspect was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 1250 hours, officers were dispatched to the school after a student called and stated that another student had assaulted him, threatened to shoot him, and appeared to have a firearm in his pocket.  Officers arrived and collaborated with school administration and determined there was no firearm.
At 6:25 pm, an officer reports two unknown suspects filled their bags with merchandise before fleeing the business towards BART.

At 6:28 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed the rear window of the victim’s vehicle and removed property from the cargo area. The suspect arrived in a newer model black Honda Pilot with tint on the rear windows.
At 6:54 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s shoes from the front porch of their residence and left behind an old pair of shoes

At 2:38 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s residence and gained access to the interior. Once inside, the suspect ransacked the interior and removed several items of value.

At 0913 hours, officers were dispatched to a residence after an acquaintance was found loitering on the property after being told to leave.  Officers contacted a 19-year-old transient man and escorted him down the street, away from the residence.  He was informed by officers that the family did not want him on their property again.  An officer remained nearby and conducted extra patrol at the residence.  At 1040 hours, the officer witnessed the man walking through the front yard of the victim’s home.  The officer stopped the man and subsequently arrested him for trespassing.  The man was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:21 pm, an officer reports the victim was kicked by his ex-girlfriend during an argument; the suspect fled prior to police arriving on scene.
At 9:51 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 57-year-old man following a domestic violence investigation. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

An officer reports an unknown suspect stole the metal container from behind the baseball dugout which contained approximately $1000 worth of baseball equipment.  The theft occurred in the early morning hours.

Officers were dispatched to the area after witnesses saw a 28-year-old man hitting and pushing a woman.  Officers arrived and contacted the couple.  The woman had torn clothing, visible injuries, and was sobbing heavily.  After speaking with witnesses and the victim, the man was arrested for domestic battery.  The man was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 5:29 am, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property.
At 4:14 am, an officer located a motorcycle which had previously been reported stolen to the Richmond Police Department. The motorcycle was towed and the owner was notified.
At 11:57 pm, an officer reports an unknown subject broke a window to the business with a brick; no loss was reported.

Location: Parking lot of 225 El Cerrito Plaza.
Description: At 1445 hours, an unknown suspect shattered the victim’s vehicle window and stole a backpack.

At 1123 hours, officers accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 48-year-old man after he entered the store, selected merchandise, and left without paying.  The man also had a parole violation warrant issued for his arrest.  The man was issued a citation for the theft and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility for the warrant.

Location: Injury Collision) – 10300 block of San Pablo Ave.
Description: At approximately 4:17pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect collided into the victim’s vehicle and fled the scene without exchanging information with the victim.
An officer reported an unknown suspect damaged the gas cap to the victim’s truck.  The incident occurred from August 31st, at 4pm to September 3rd, at 2:30pm.
At 10:33 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect had just stolen the catalytic converter off a vehicle.

Location: Ross parking lot 2000 El Cerrito Plaza
Description: An officer reported an unknown suspect shattered the rear passenger side window to the victim’s Mini Cooper and property.  The incident occurred from 8:50am to 8:55am.
At approximately 11:57am, an officer reported that an unknown suspect stole the rear license plate to the victim’s vehicle.
An officer reported an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s Honda Accord.  The theft occurred from Thursday September 2nd, at 6pm to Friday September 3rd, at 11:30am.
At 8:53 am, an officer reported a known suspect forced open a window to the victim’s residence and stole kitchen appliances.  The suspect fled the area prior to police officers arrival.

An officer reported an unknown suspect entered the residence via an unlocked door. The suspect then rummaged through each room and stole property.  The burglary occurred from Tuesday August 31st, at 5pm to Wednesday September 1st, at 7:30am.
At 11:23 pm, an officer was sent to the report that someone had just stolen the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.

At 8:15 pm, officers responded to the store for a report of a shoplifting.  As one of the officers was arriving, the suspect exited the store with the stolen property.  The officer detained the suspect and employees placed the suspect under citizen’s arrest. All the property was returned to store employees.  The subject was issued a citation and released from the scene.
At 9:45 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect shattered the window to the victim’s vehicle and stole property.
At 9:22 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect attempted to steal a catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.

At 12:28 am, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop, and after an investigation determined the driver was under the influence.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and released to a sober family member.

At 11:09 pm, officers were dispatched to the report that there was a stolen vehicle at the Chevron gas station. Officers attempted to conduct a traffic enforcement stop, but the driver failed to yield. Officers pursued the vehicle but terminated the pursuit after the vehicle was driving recklessly. Officers were able to identify the driver and will forward the case to the Contra Costa DA’s Office to file charges.

At 2:00 pm, a 64-year-old Richmond man entered the business, selected a box of shoes, and exited the store without paying.  An employee contacted the man and asked for the property back.  The man assaulted the employee and fled the parking lot.  Officers located the man less than a block away.  The man was arrested, booked, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer reported between September 4th and September 5th, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
An officer reported an unknown suspect shattered the front door of the business, but did not enter.  The incident occurred sometime while the business was closed.
At approximately 7:53 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect shattered the right rear window of the victim’s vehicle and stole a backpack.
At 3:10 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect smashed the rear right window of the victim’s vehicle and stole two children’s backpacks.
At 9:08 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed the rear license place from the victim’s vehicle sometime earlier that same day.
At 10:53 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect threw an open soda can at the victim, striking him in the arm.
At 4:45 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole property from the business.
At 11:25 am, an unknown suspect became angry and threw empty cups and garbage at an employee.
At 5:34 pm, an officer accepted the Citizen’s Arrest of a 61-year-old woman who refused to leave the business after requested by staff. The woman was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer reported sometime during the night, an unknown suspect stole the rear license plate from the victim’s vehicle.
An officer reported between 9/4 and 9/9, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from a vehicle in the lot.
At 7:11 pm, an officer recovered a vehicle previously reported as stolen to the Vallejo Police Department.
At approximately 4:00 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and stole $20 in loose change.
At 7:24 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered a commercial building and removed several power tools. The theft occurred sometime overnight.

At 11:57 am, officers were dispatched to the area for several subjects engaged in a brawl in the parking lot of a business. During the altercation, one of the subjects displayed a handgun. All of the subjects fled prior to officers arriving on scene.
At 8:44 am, an officer reported two unknown suspects removed a catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
At 12:07 pm, an officer reported two unknown suspects entered a business, selected merchandise, and left the business without paying.

At 9:55 pm, an officer was in his patrol vehicle handling an unrelated call when he noticed a vehicle speeding through the parking lot of the police station.  A second vehicle appeared and blocked the first vehicle from exiting the parking lot.  The officer went to investigate and discovered the two vehicles were involved in a collision a few blocks away.  After the collision, the first vehicle had driven off with the second one following.  During the investigation, the officer determined the driver of the first vehicle was heavily intoxicated.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then released to a sober friend.
At 7:28 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect damaged several vehicles in the parking lot of the business, causing thousands of dollars in damages.
At 2:26 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business, selected merchandise, and then left the business without paying.

At 12:54 am, officers were dispatched to a fight in front of the store.  Upon officers’ arrival, they found two subjects physically fighting and  separated them.  During the investigation, officers learned both subjects were friends. They had got into an argument which turned into a physical fight.  Neither party wished to pursue the matter criminally and were both released at the scene.
At 1506 hours, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the store and tried on a couple pairs of shoes.  When the clerk walked to the back, the suspect left the store with two pairs of shoes.
An officer reported sometime during the night of 9/11/21, an unknown suspect stole the license plate from the victim’s vehicle.
At 1250 hours, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 34-year-old Rodeo woman after she stole clothing from the department store.  The woman was cited and released.

CrimeType: Driving under the influence, Hit and Run
Description: At 1615 hours, officers were dispatched to the El Cerrito Plaza after witnesses saw a vehicle collided into two parked cars and flee the area.  Witnesses provided the police with the vehicle description, license plate number, and also stated the driver appeared intoxicated.  Officers responded to the listed address on the vehicle registration and saw the vehicle driving into the driveway.  Upon contacting the driver, the officer found that she was intoxicated.  The 41-year-old El Cerrito woman was arrested and booked at the El Cerrito Police Department.  After booking, she was released to a sober relative.
At 6:37 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect tricked an elderly victim into purchasing several thousands of dollars’ worth of gift cards in exchange for computer repairs.
An officer reported sometime during the night of 9/13/21, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s leaf blower from the backyard.

At 9:08 pm, an officer was conducting a walkthrough of Ross when he witnessed a male exit the store with merchandise, setting off the anti-theft system.  The male was detained without incident. An officer was dispatched to the business at 4:15 pm, for a theft detail. The officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business, selected approximately $700 worth of jackets, and left without paying.

At 8:44 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime between 9/22/21 and 9/23/21.
At 11:03 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.

At 9:00 pm, officers were dispatched to the report of a robbery with a firearm in the Safeway parking lot.  The victim stated he was standing in the parking lot when a suspect armed with a firearm walked up to him and demanded his personal property. The suspect threatened to shoot the victim’s juvenile son who was with the victim.  The suspect took the victim’s property and assaulted the victim prior to fleeing the area.
At 2:25 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.

At 7:22 pm, officers were dispatched to the report of a residential burglary. Upon arrival, the officers discovered the suspect forced entry via a back door and removed property. The burglary occurred within the past two weeks.

Location: San Pablo Ave. near Carlson Blvd.
Description: At 3:50 am, an officer was patrolling in the area when he contacted a male subject and discovered he had four separate arrest warrants.  The subject was taken into custody for the warrants without incident. He was booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:37 pm, an officer reported two unknown suspects entered the business, selected merchandise, and then left without paying.
At 9:53 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business, selected merchandise, and then left without paying.

At 2:05 am, an officer was patrolling the area when he was flagged down by a community member that reported the front window of the bank was shattered.  Officers responded to the bank and found a large window was shattered.  The interior of the bank was searched by an ECPD canine and officers. Officers did not locate anyone inside the bank.
At 8:34 pm, an officer was dispatched to the report of a theft. The officer reported an unknown suspect had just stolen merchandise from the store and had fled the scene.

An officer contacted the male driver, and noticed the driver displayed objective symptoms of being under the influence of alcohol.  The officer conducted a DUI investigation and determined the subject was under the influence. He was taken into custody without incident and placed under arrest.  The subject was booked at ECPD and then released to a sober friend.

CrimeType: Vehicle collision / Driving Under the Influenc
Location: San Pablo Ave. and Carlson Blvd.
Description: At 3:56 am, officers were dispatched to a vehicle collision involving a car and a public bus.  An officer contacted the driver of the car and noticed he was displaying objective symptoms of being under the influence of an alcoholic beverage.  The officer conducted a DUI investigation and determined the driver was under the influence of alcohol. The driver was taken into custody without incident and placed under arrest.  He was booked at the ECPD and released to a sober friend.
At 8:31 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and stole a tool box.

At 8:49 pm, while investigation the above incident, an officer noticed a subject walk into the store with drug paraphernalia in plain view.  The officer detained the subject without incident. During the officer’s investigation he located methamphetamine in the subject’s possession.  The subject was placed under arrest and issued a citation and released from the scene.
An officer reported sometime between 9/24/21 and 9/27/21, an unknown suspect(s) pried open a bank of mailboxes at the complex and stole mail.

An officer reported sometime between 9/21/21 and 9/27/21, an unknown suspect entered the victim’s house by damaging the locking mechanism.  Once inside, the suspect stole the victim’s property.

At 4:20 pm, an officer reported an argument ensued following a minor collision between the driver of an SUV and a parked sedan while parallel parking.  The owner of the parked vehicle and her daughter confronted the SUV driver.  The SUV driver armed herself with a hydraulic shock absorber.  The woman’s daughter sprayed the SUV driver with pepper spray and ran back to the parked vehicle with her mother.  The SUV driver retaliated by striking several areas of the woman’s vehicle with the shock absorber, causing minor damage.
