At 3:57 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect pried opened a roll up garage door and stole property from the business.

CrimeType: Stolen Vehicle, Evading Police, Theft from A Vehicle
Description: At 0747 hours, officers were dispatched to the area for a male rummaging through a parked vehicle.  The caller stated the subject arrived in a grey sedan and provided the license plate number.  Dispatch notified officers the sedan had been reported stolen to Richmond PD on 9/20/21.  Officers responded to the area and located the subject driving the vehicle northbound on Avis Dr. approaching Moeser Ln.  Officers attempted to conduct an enforcement stop but the subject fled in the vehicle at a high rate of speed.  A brief pursuit was initiated and quickly cancelled after officers determined the risk to vehicle, bicycle, and pedestrian traffic greatly outweighed any benefit of apprehension.

At 10:00 am, officers were dispatched to the Chevron gas station after a community member observed a heavily intoxicated subject attempting to inflate his vehicle tires.  Officers arrived on scene and contacted a 20-year-old Richmond man and conducted a DUI investigation.  The man was subsequently arrested for driving under the influence of alcohol.  The man was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released to a sober relative.

An officer reported sometime between 9:30 pm on 9/27/21 and 8:30 am on 9/28/21, an unknown suspect entered the victim’s residence by forcing open the garage door.  Once inside, the suspect stole property.

The manager detained the subject and told the officer that the business just desired the stolen property to be returned.  The subject returned the stolen property to the store and was released.
At 11:01 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect damaged the victim’s vehicle while it was parked in the driveway of their home.
At 8:21 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle.

Security officers confronted the suspect and grabbed the cart.  A struggle ensued, with both parties pulling the cart back and forth.  Eventually, the cart fell over and security recovered half the merchandise.  The suspect fled westbound through the parking lot.

At 5:00 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the store, selected approximately $700 worth of clothes, and exited the store without paying.

At approximately 8:30 am hours, an officer was dispatched to a robbery detail and reported the suspect approached the victim and asked for money. When the victim refused, the suspect grabbed property from the victim.  The victim suffered minor injuries from the incident but declined medical assistance.
At approximately 1:21 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the store, selected merchandise from the display shelves and fled the store without paying.

CrimeType: Arrest for Vandalism, Battery, and Resisting Arrest
Description: At approximately 10:18 am, officers were dispatched to Nation’s for a male subject throwing items and scaring people inside the business.  The suspect, a 25-year-old male from Richmond, went behind the counter and began throwing pies all over the restaurant.  The suspect threw a trash bin at one of the workers, hitting them. The suspect fled the restaurant prior to officers’ arrival.  Officers located the suspect three blocks away walking. Officers verbally identified themselves and gave verbal commands to the suspect to stop. The suspect did not comply with the officers’ commands and ran from them. Officers pursued the suspect while continually giving verbal commands to him to stop. During the foot pursuit an officer deployed his taser and the suspect was safely taken into custody. Officers immediately summoned an ambulance to provide medical assistance to the suspect for the taser deployment. He was transported to Richmond Kaiser Hospital by ambulance for further medical evaluation.  Once medically cleared from the hospital, the suspect was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.  Nation’s estimated the suspect caused over $7000.00 worth of damage.

At 10:11 pm, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on a vehicle. During the investigation, the officer located suspected methamphetamine, heroin, and drug paraphernalia inside the driver’s vehicle.  The driver was placed under arrest for possession of the narcotics.  The passenger in the vehicle was found to be a sex registrant that had not registered with the law enforcement agency where he lived in for almost two years.  The driver was issued a citation and released from the scene.  The passenger was arrested and transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking.  At the end of booking, the passenger was issued a citation and released.

The officer discovered they both had outstanding warrants.  They were  taken into custody without incident. During a search incident to arrest, one of the subjects was found to have suspected methamphetamine in his pocket.  They were both transported to the Martinez Detention Facility after booking.

CrimeType: Recovered Stolen Vehicle / Evading / Possession of Controlled Substance / Felon in Possession of Firearm and Ammunition
Description: Around 1215 am, Dispatch advised officers a Richmond PD officer was in pursuit of a stolen vehicle, but lost it in the area of I-580 and Central Ave. An El Cerrito officer located the vehicle within the city of El Cerrito and attempted to conduct an enforcement stop. The suspect attempted to flee from officers. During a brief pursuit, the suspect collided into a fixed object and fled on foot. Officers quickly set up a perimeter and conducted a search with the assistance of a canine. The canine located the suspect, a 28-year-old male from Richmond hiding in the crawl space of a nearby home. During a search incident to arrest, the male was found to be in possession of a large quantity of narcotics on his person. During a search of the vehicle, a large package of suspected controlled substance and a loaded stolen gun was located. After the male was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, he was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 11:45 am, an officer reported a known theft suspect entered the business and was told to leave by the store manager. The suspect threatened the manager and stated that she was going to take what she needed.  The suspect then fled the store with a couple handfuls of merchandise.
An officer reported that sometime overnight, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
At 7:20 am, an officer located a U-Haul Ford Transit Van that had been reported stolen to Berkeley PD on 10/5/21.
An officer reported that sometime overnight, an unknown suspect kicked the glass front door of the business, shattering the glass.  No entry was made into the restaurant.
An officer reported an unknown suspect stole several silver coins from a package delivered to the victim’s residence.

Description: At 12:12 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the keys to a patron’s vehicle at the business for service.

The manager told the suspect to leave the business and the suspect threatened to physically assault the manager.  When officers detained the suspect, she became verbally assaultive and failed to identify herself.  She was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:15 am, an officer located a 17-year-old Richmond male juvenile that had been reported as a runaway on 9/18/2021.  The juvenile was reunited with his legal guardian and taken out of the system as missing.
At 8:10 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered Walgreen’s, filled up a duffel bag with miscellaneous makeup, and left without paying.
At 11:31 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business, concealed store merchandise, and left without paying.

At 12:38 pm, officers responded to a report of a batter. During their investigation, officers discovered a 51-year-old El Cerrito woman slapped the victim on the shoulder and fled prior to police arrival. The victim declined prosecution.
At 6:57 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the victim’s vehicle and punched the ignition.
At 7:59 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business and destroyed a display before taking 3 cell phones from the business.

Location: Barnes and Noble, 6050 El Cerrito Plaza
Description: An officer reported sometime overnight, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s rented U-Haul box truck.
An officer reported sometime between midnight and 7:30 am, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 2000 Mazda Miata.
At 7:41 pm, an officer reported 3 unidentified juveniles entered the business and stole a large quantity of personal hygiene products before running off without paying.

At 4:15 pm, an officer reported the restrained party showed up at her ex-girlfriend’s residence in violation of a restraining order in place.  The restrained party was directed to leave the premises and she complied.  The case will be forwarded to the Contra Costa County District Attorney’s Office for prosecution.
At 7:40 am, an officer reported two unknown suspects stole a generator and tools from the victim’s truck.
At 10:01 pm, an officer reported an unidentified suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property from inside before fleeing in a vehicle.
At 12:52 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime between 10/10/21 to 10/15/21.
At 11:22 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed property from an employee area sometime between 10/6/21 to 10/7/21. The estimated worth of the property was approximately $5000.
At 1:24 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed the license plates off the victim’s vehicle sometime between 9/24/21 to 10/14/21.
At 5:09 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle overnight.

At 8:45 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect approached the victim and asked to use his cell phone. When the victim refused, the suspect pushed the victim and snatched the victim’s cell phone from his hand. The suspect then fled on foot on the BART Path.

CrimeType: Public Intoxication, Battery on an Officer
Description: At 9:40 pm, officers responded to the business for a male in the drive-thru yelling and cursing at customers and employees. When officers arrived, they noticed the 55-year-old male from Richmond walking away and entered the roadway causing a major traffic hazard. The male was contacted and escorted off the roadway. During the officers’ investigation, the male was determined to be under the influence of alcohol. While speaking with officers, the male spat on one of the officers, which resulted in his arrest. The male was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:39 pm, an officer reported that 5 unknown suspects entered the business and stole various items from the business.

CrimeType: Grand Theft, Possess Stolen Property, Resisting Arrest, Warrant
Description: At 4:10 am, an officer reported that the suspects from the previous grand theft returned. The victim heard the sawing noises and saw two suspects attempting to steal his catalytic converter from his vehicle. The victim called 911 and officers quickly arrived to find the suspects still on scene. Two suspects fled the area on foot into Central Park. An officer chased the suspects and after a short foot pursuit, the officer detained one of the suspects. During a records check, officers discovered the 36-year old male detained resides in the City of San Pablo and had two outstanding warrants for prior catalytic converter thefts. After booking, the male was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:09 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole 30 cartons of cigarettes valued at $3000.00.
At 6:18 pm, an officer reported  an unknown suspect shattered the rear right window of the victim’s vehicle and stole 2 backpacks containing textbooks.
At 7:18 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and stole miscellaneous items.
At 1:17 am, an officer reported that an unknown suspect attempted to cut the catalytic converter from the victim’s Honda Element but was scared off by the victim.
At 2:00 am, an officer conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver, a 31-year-old El Sobrante male, after he was found to be driving under the influence of alcohol.
At 6:09 pm, an officer reported that a previously banned customer returned to the business but was refused service. When asked to leave, the suspect threatened to come back and assault the employees.

At 6:31 pm, an officer reported that an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle. When the victim confronted the suspect, the suspect brandished a handgun at the victim and fled the scene.
At 6:18 pm, an officer reported that the victim’s 1998 Honda Accord was stolen from the parking lot sometime between 4:00-4:45pm.

At 12:25 am, an officer contacted a 33-year-old Richmond male. During a records check the officer discovered the male had two outstanding arrest warrants. The male was booked and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 5:01 pm, an officer reported the arrest of a 29-year-old female. The female in a drunken rage assaulted her boyfriend and destroyed several items in the residence. The female was taken into custody without incident. After booking, the female was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 4:17 pm, an officer reported a subject of a restraining order attempted to contact the protected party several times by calling and sending letters.

Location: San Pablo Ave. and Lincoln Ave.
Description: At 7:04 am, an officer reported the arrest of a 64 year old male. The male set three large piles of garbage and foliage on fire in an empty parking lot near residential homes and businesses. The male was detained by officers and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility after booking.
At 9:45 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect lit a can on fire causing damage to an electric box to a business.
At 8:48 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect sprayed graffiti on a sign of a business sometime overnight.

CrimeType: Drug Paraphernalia, Probation Violation, Burglary Tools
Description: At 10:22 pm, an officer reported the arrest of a 37-year-old Richmond male for being in possession of burglary tools and drug paraphernalia.
At 4:01 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.
At 5:33 am, an officer reported that an unknown suspect forced their way into a locked common area at an apartment complex and then pried open several mailboxes before removing mail from the boxes.

At 3:30 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the store, concealed merchandise, and fled without paying.  Store security attempted to stop the suspect, but he struggled with security, trying to get away.  The suspect dropped the merchandise and fled on foot.
An officer reported sometime overnight, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
At 1:31 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed property from the interior of the victim’s unlocked vehicle earlier that same evening.

At 2:15 am, an officer reported the victim’s vehicle was stolen from in front of the residence.  At 11:00 am, the victim located his stolen vehicle several blocks from his residence.  The victim discovered tools and personal belongings had been stolen from his vehicle.
At 12:45 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business and stole $2,000 worth of clothing and fled in a silver SUV.
At 7:30 pm, an officer reported, a 40-year-old San Francisco woman stole mercandise from CVS.  The property was recovered and returned to CVS.  The female was released after being issued a citation.

At 1:00 am, officers investigated an argument between two homeless women in the tent encampment.  During the arguement, the suspect drew an unloaded revolver and threatened the victim, then stole her jacket.  The victim fled toward the BART station and was not located for her statement.  An officer detained the suspect nearby without incident and the firearm was located nearby. The suspect was identified and was released on a citation for being in possession of a firearm.

An officer reported that an unknown suspect arranged a wire transfer to himself from the victim’s bank account.  Luckily, the victim caught the transition as it was “pending” and the bank was able to stop the transaction.
A 28-year-old Richmond man turned himself in at the police department to be booked on an outstanding warrant.  After booking, the man was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:22 am, an officer was patrolling in the area and located a 1994 Honda Prelude that had been reported stolen to Berkeley PD on August 28th.
At 2:42 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect shattered the right front window of the victim’s vehicle. The crime occurred between 10:00 am and 2:00 pm.

At 12:12 pm, an officer reported a female was sitting on the conveyor belt of a checkout stand and refused to get off when asked by security guards. A security guard attempted to escort her outside, the female attempted to assault the guard. Upon officers’ arrival, they contacted the female.  Officers obtained statements from the involved parties and the case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office for review.

Location: San Pablo Ave. at Conlon Ave.
Description: At 10:33 pm, an officer contacted a subject riding his bicycle in the area.  The subject was found to be on probation and during a probation search a glass pipe used to smoke narcotics was located.  The subject was issued a citation for the possession of the pipe and a probation violation and released.

At approximately 8:17 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 2021 Kia Ria. The victim’s wallet was left in the vehicle and their credit/debit card was used multiple times at various locations.
At approximately 2:33pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect attempted to open the mail box to the apartment complex, damaging the box.

At approximately 10:04 am, an officer was patrolling in the area when she was flagged down regarding a vandalism. El Cerrito police officers detained a 29 year old male, after a witness saw him a punch a vehicle window and shattering it.  The male displayed behaviors of being in a mental health crisis so he was placed on a 5150 W&I hold and was transported to the County Hospital in Martinez for evaluation by AMR.

At approximately 10:26 am, an officer reported an elderly victim received a phone call from an unknown person who told her that her son was involved in a DUI collision and needed to post bail. The victim wired the suspect $10,000 for his release.

During the above arrest a stolen motorcycle that was reported stolen to the Fairfield Police Department was found parked near the subject who had the warrants.  The motorcycle was recovered and the registered owner was told his motorcycle was located.
At 7:54 pm, an officer was dispatched to a call that someone had just stolen from the store and fled the area.
At 2:59 pm hours, an officer reported an unknown suspect placed merchanidse in his bag and fled the store without paying.

At 4:00 am, an officer spotted a subject who he knew had several felony warrants for his arrest.  The subject was detained for the warrants, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:29 pm, an officer was dispatched to a call that an unknown suspect had stolen $400.00 worth of cosmetics and fled the area.
At approximately 8:21 am hours, an officer reported three people entered the store, placed over $1000.00 worth of makeup from the display shelf in bags and fled the store without paying.
At 8:40 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim's catalytic converter. The theft occurred sometime during the previous two days.

At approximately 10:16 am, an officer reported the victim was standing by his tent, when he was approached by an acquaintance (suspect).  The acquaintance punched the victim on the side of the head and they began to wrestle on the ground.  The suspect got up, ripped the victim’s tent and ran northbound on the BART path.  The victim did not suffer injuries and declined medical assistance.

Location: St. John the Baptist School, 11156 San Pablo Ave.
Description: An officer reported an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s 2012 Honda Ridgeline.  The incident occurred on October 23rd from 9:00 pm to 10:30 pm.
An officer reported an unknown suspect pried open the community mailbox at an apartment complex and stole the victim’s mail.  The incident occurred between 1:00 pm to 2:00 pm.
At 3:13 pm hours, an officer reported the suspect entered the store and stole over $100.00 worth of hair products.  The suspect fled on foot thru an unknown direction in the parking lot.

At 1:30 am, officers were dispatched to an alarm activation at this location.  When the officers arrived, they found an unknown suspect had forced opened the front door and taken property from within the business and fled the area. Commercial Burglary - El Cerrito Plaza Mezzanine At approximately 6:59 am, El Cerrito police officers responded to the offices above Trader Joe’s, where an employee located a shattered glass of the access door to the mezzanine businesses. An unknown suspect forced entry into several of the offices and ransacked the interior.  A K9 search was conducted, but no suspects were located inside the offices. Owners of the businesses responded and confirmed property was taken.
At 2:18 pm hours, an officer reported two suspects entered the business and stole merchandise.  The suspects fled on foot towards the Del Norte BART.
At 8:36 pm, an officer was dispatched to a call of a stolen vehicle that occurred during the previous six hours.
An officer reported an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle, rummaged the interior, but did not take anything.
At 5:31 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect accessed the victim’s bank account and wired a large amount of money to another account out of state.
At 8:26 pm, an officer reported two suspect entered the business and stole multiple pairs of shoes. As the suspects left the business they dropped the stolen merchandise before running off.
An officer reported sometime between 10/23/21 and 10/25/21, an unknown suspect siphoned ¾ tank of gas from the victim’s vehicle.
An officer reported sometime between 10/22/21 and 10/25/21, an unknown suspect stole the rear license plate from the victim’s vehicle.

At 7:37 pm, an officer reported the Citizen’s Arrest of a 34-year-old woman after she was found in possession of stolen merchandise from the business. The woman was issued a citation and released at the scene.
An officer reported sometime between 10/22/21 and 10/25/21, an unknown suspect shattered the rear left passenger window of the victim’s vehicle and stole tools.
An officer reported sometime between 10/22/21 and 12/24/21, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
An officer reported between 10/24 at 3:00 pm and 10/25 at 9:00 am, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
At 5:24 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle.
An officer reported at 12:30 pm, 3 juvenile suspects entered Walgreen’s, concealed $400 worth of toiletries, and fled the store without paying.
An officer reported on 9/7/21, an unknown suspect changed the victim’s credit card address to an address in Pennsylvania.
At 9:29 pm, an officer reported an unknown female suspect stole merchandise from the business.
An officer reported at 3:10 pm, a young male victim was approached by an unknown suspect who asked to borrow a cell phone.  The victim gave the suspect his phone and the suspect fled on foot.
At 11:21 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect set fire to one of the walk/don’t walk pedestrian signals in the intersection.

At 5:45 am, an officer observed a 34-year-old Merced man sleeping in the driver’s seat of a vehicle near the tennis courts.  A records check revealed that the vehicle had been reported stolen to Merced PD on 10/21/21.  The man was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:25 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect threw an object at the victim’s vehicle causing damage.
An officer repored at 2:00 pm, a 66-year-old Richmond woman walked away from her family after an argument.  The woman was entered into the Missing and Unidentified Persons System.
At 6:04 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s pickup truck from the business earlier that same day. The vehicle was later located in San Pablo and returned to the victim.

CrimeType: Violation of Domestic Violence Restraining Order, False Imprisonment
Description: At 2:10 pm, an officer reported the victim was waiting for her children to be released from school. The victim’s ex-boyfriend, a 21-year-old Richmond man drove into the parking lot, blocked the victim’s vehicle into a parking spot, and climbed into the victim’s vehicle.  Once inside, the man began to argue with the victim about their relationship. There is an active restraining order protecting the victim from the suspect.  The man was arrested and booked at the El Cerrito Police Department before being transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:20 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect shattered the window of the victim’s vehicle and stole a backpack containing a laptop.
At 9:33 pm, an officer reported three suspects entered the business and worked together to pry open a display case and remove a large quantity of perfume.

An officer reported at 3:35 pm, an unknown suspect walked around the exterior of a residence while peering into windows and checking to see if any would open.  Following his prowl, the suspect sat on a bench in the front yard of the residence and admired the view.
At 7:39 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle.
An officer reported an unknown suspect entered the victim’s residence and stole a black bag containing a work identification card.
At 6:48 pm, an officer responded to a call of a suspect who entered the store, selected merchandise off the shelf, and left the store without paying for the merchandise.

At 6:45 pm, officers were sent to a call where a suspect was in the store and had opened alcohol that she did not pay for and consumed it inside the store. The store employees believed the person was intoxicated.  When officers arrived they found the suspect outside the store sleeping on the ground.  The suspect was placed under citizen’s arrest for the theft and was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:15 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime overnight.
At 3:11 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect shattered two windows of a business.
At 1:30 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered a business, selected merchandise, and left without paying.

At 2:43 pm, an officer reported the arrest of an adult male who was caught stealing packages off porches. The male was positively identified and then issued a citation. The male was also in possession of another’s property which was returned to the rightful owner.
12:05 pm, an officer reported an unknown juvenile female entered a business and permanently damaged property sold by the business.
At 3:45 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered a business, selected merchandise, and then left without paying.

At 4:40 am, officers were sent to the report that someone was outside the victim’s house and trying to get into the front door.  Upon arrival officers found the suspect on the front porch of the home.  The suspect was detained without incident. After the initial investigation, the male was placed under arrest.  During a search incident to arrest, the male was found to be in possession of burglary tools and had a warrant for his arrest issued by the State of California Department of Corrections.  The suspect was booked at ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 11:25 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime overnight.
At 11:32 am, an officer reported the arrest of an adult female. The female assaulted her boyfriend after an argument. The female was taken into custody and transported to county jail.

At 1840 hours, an officer was dispatched to a report of two suspects had stolen merchandise from the store. When a customer in the parking lot tried to get a photograph of the suspect vehicle it almost hit the witness as it drove off.  Officers discovered the vehicle was reported stolen out of Oakland.
At 3:05 pm, an officer reported two unknown suspects entered the business, selected merchandise, and left without paying.

At 9:26 pm, officers were dispatched to the store for the report that a suspect was inside concealing merchandise in his jacket.  As officers arrived the suspect came running out of the front doors and was immediately detained.  The person was placed under citizen’s arrest, the stolen property was recovered, and the suspect was issued a citation.
At 11:42 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime overnight of 10/30/21.
At 7:28 pm, an officer was sent to a call where a suspect had come into the store, selected merchandise off the shelf, and left without paying.
At 12:04 pm, officers responded to a report of a shot being fired. Officers arrived on scene and discovered an unknown suspect had fired a single round into a vehicle. No one was injured.
At 6:32 pm, an officer reported the victim’s motorcycle had been stolen from the street sometime the previous day.
At 3:34 pm, an officer reported two unknown suspects entered the business, selected merchandise, and left the business without paying.
At 7:24 pm, an officer was sent to the call of a theft. Four suspects entered the store, selected over $1000.00 worth of merchandise from the shelves, and left without paying for the items.

Location: San Pablo Ave. near Cutting Blvd.
Description: At 2:06 am, an officer was patrolling in the area when she saw a subject walking down the street that she recognized as a wanted individual on an outstanding warrant issued by the El Cerrito Police Department. The subject was detained and placed under arrest.  The individual was issued a citation for the warrant and released.
At 6:43 pm, an officer was sent to a call where someone had entered the store, selected merchandise off the shelf, and left without paying.

At 2:19 PM, an officer reported a male and a female suspect known to store staff for previous shoplifts entered the store, selected over $1000 worth of shoes, concealing some of the shoes in bags, and headed for the exit. The manager attempted to stop the theft by contacting the suspects. During the contact, the male suspect punched the manager on his head twice before both suspects fled with the shoes that were concealed in their bags.  The suspects fled the scene by vehicle.
An officer reported at 3:00 am, an unknown suspect entered the business via a rear door and stole cash and jewelry.
At 9:58 am, an officer located a Chevy pickup that had been reported stolen to the San Pablo Police Department on 10/31/21.
At 7:00 am, an officer located a Honda Fit that had been reported stolen to the El Cerrito Police Department on 10/31/21.

At 12:58 pm, El Cerrito PD officers responded to a report of shots fired near the intersection of S. 55th St. and School St. Upon their arrival, officers found shell casings in the roadway and broken glass. Neither the victim nor suspect remained at the scene. Detectives and a Crime Scene Investigator responded to assist patrol officers with this investigation. While officers were processing the scene, a Richmond Police Officer located the victim’s vehicle in the City of Richmond. Officers interviewed the victim and learned that the suspect in this case became angry after a traffic maneuver and escalated a minor conflict into violent action. The victim was not injured from this incident and only suffered damage to his vehicle. Brandishing a Knife, Warrant Arrest – Moeser Lane / Ohlone Trail At 10:55 pm, an officer accepted the Citizens Arrest of a 53-year-old man after he brandished a knife at the victim during an argument. The man also had a warrant for his arrest and was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 6:27 am, an officer detained a 51-year-old transient male in the drive-thru of McDonald’s in connection with the early morning robbery attempt on the 2nd.  The male was subsequently arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 6:35 pm, officers detained a man after store staff claimed the man had brandished a knife at them during a failed shoplifting attempt. The man was identified but the victim did not wish to see him arrested so he was released at the scene.
At 7:16 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business one day earlier and used a key to open a cash register and remove money.
At 1:26 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property; the crime is believed to have occurred sometime around 8:30 pm.

Location: Larb Thai Food, 10166 San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 9:18 pm, an officer was dispatched to a suspicious person detail. Upon arrival, the officer contacted a 32-year-old man outside the business and determined the man had two warrants for his arrest from Southern California. The man was issued a new court date for both warrants and released with a signed promise to appear.
A 7:23 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business and selected clothing before leaving without payment.
At 8:41 pm, an officer arrested a 31-year-old male after the male was determined to have assaulted his girlfriend during an argument. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
While investigating the above crime the victim advised their vehicle had also been burglarized sometime on October 22nd.
At 7:53 pm, an officer went to the report that someone had their mail stolen from their mailbox.

CrimeType: Vandalism, Drunk in Public, and Assault on Peace Officer
Description: At 11:22 am, an officer reported the arrest of a 35 year old male. The male went to a family member’s residence and attempted to kick in the front door causing damage to the door and frame. The male was also very highly intoxicated. During the arresting process, the male attempted to bite an officer. The male was taken into custody and transported to county jail.

At 2:22 pm, an officer accepted the Citizen’s Arrest of a 17-year-old after the juvenile was identified as the responsible party in a shoplifting. A parent of the juvenile was contacted and gave permission for the child to be released with a signed promise to appear.
At 9:35 pm, an officer was sent to the report that a catalytic converter had been stolen from the victim’s vehicle sometime in the last several hours.

Location: One Stop Laundromat, 10628 San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 7:42 am, an officer reported a known suspect entered the business and caused a yet to be determined amount of damage during the evening. The suspect was identified and a police report will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office for review.

CrimeType: Assault with a Deadly Weapon and Warrant Arrest
Description: At 2:38 am, an officer was patrolling in the area and noticed an occupied vehicle parked in the plaza that matched the description of a vehicle that was used in a shooting in El Cerrito three days earlier. When officers contacted the occupants of the vehicle, an officer noticed a firearm inside the vehicle. The subjects detained matched the description of the suspects in the shooting and were arrested. During a warrants check, one of the suspects was found to have an active warrant for his arrest for homicide issued by the Oakland Police Department.  Both suspects were booked at ECPD for their involvement in the shooting. Detectives were called out to interview the suspects. A search warrant was obtained for the vehicle and three additional firearms were located inside the vehicle. The subjects were both transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. This case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office to file charges.

At 4:12 am, an officer was sent to the call where the victim had scared off someone that was under their vehicle sawing off the catalytic converter.  The suspect did not steal the catalytic converter before he was scared off.

At 3:35 am, officers were dispatched to a burglary that had just occurred at Panda Express. During the investigation, an officer located a suspect that matched the description of the suspect that was seen on video committing the burglary. The suspect was detained and subsequently arrested for the burglary. Officers conducted a warrants check and discovered the subject had two active warrants for his arrest.  One of the warrants was issued by the El Cerrito Police Department for a theft and the other was issued by the Albany Police Department for burglary. The suspect was also found to be in possession of stolen property that had been stolen during another commercial burglary from the El Cerrito Plaza earlier in the week. Detectives were callout to interview the suspect due to him being suspected of a series of burglaries that occurred at the EC Plaza. After the interview, the suspect was booked at ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Brandishing a Firearm, Hit and Run
Location: Arlington Blvd. / Potrero Ave. & 6800 block of Glen Mawr Ave.
Description: At 10:54 am, officers were dispatched to a hit and run that just occurred involving a silver SUV.  Witnesses in the area stated the suspect fled on foot, northbound on Glen Mawr Ave.  Soon after the hit and run was broadcasted, officers were dispatched to the intersection of Arlington Blvd. and Potrero Ave. after a suspect pointed a firearm at two victims in their vehicle.  The suspect description, vehicle description, and license plate confirmed that the brandishing suspect and hit and run suspect were one in the same.  Officers were able to identify a 19-year-old Richmond man as the responsible suspect.  The man came to the police department 2 days later to speak with officers.  He was booked on the charges and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 5:20 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect threw a rock at a home breaking a second story window.
An officer reported between 11/3/21 at 8:00 pm to 11/8/21 at 8:45 am, an unknown suspect entered a common bicycle storage area and stole two electric bicycles.
At 2:58 am, an officer reported two unknown suspects cut a lock to the gate and entered a yard where they attempted to steal copper wire.
An officer reported in between 11:00 am and 11:30 am, an unknown suspect shattered the rear sliding glass door of a residence, entered, and stole the victims’ belongings.

At 1:30 pm, an unknown suspect parked his vehicle next to a female standing on the sidewalk.  The male rolled down his window which got the attention of the victim.  When the victim looked inside the vehicle, she could see that the suspect was masturbating.  The victim immediately called 9-1-1 but the suspect had fled prior to police arrival.
At 8:26 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business and selected several cans of energy drink before fleeing the store.
At 8:33 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 2002 BMW sedan.
At 9:00 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business and stole miscellaneous merchandise worth approximately $300.
An officer reported in between 11/5/21 at 12:00 pm to 11/7/21 at 7:00 pm, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s RV.
An officer reported in between 11/9/21 at 6:00 pm and 11/10/21 at 12:00 pm, an unknown suspect stole the license plate,from the victim’s vehicle.

CrimeType: Violation of Domestic Violence Restraining Order
Description: At 10:17 pm, officers were dispatched to the area for a loud verbal argument.  Officers arrived and contacted a 43-year-old Richmond man and his girlfriend.  During the investigation, officers learned the man had three active domestic violence restraining orders barring him from contacting the female.  The man was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 1:04 am, an officer reported the victim was confronted by an ex-boyfriend who pepper sprayed the victim in the face and stole her purse. The suspect fled prior to police arrival; the victim was treated by EMS.
At 9:49 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime over the past two days.
An officer reported at 11:08 am, an unknown suspect entered the business, stole multiple hygiene products and fled without paying.
At 11:22 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect sprayed painted on the victim’s rented vehicle. The time of occurrence was sometime overnight.
At 2:55 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect smashed a window of the victim’s vehicle and removed luggage. The suspect then fled in an awaiting vehicle.
At 5:02 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect keyed the victim’s vehicle.

At 3:37 pm, an officer reported the arrest of an adult female. The female approached the victim and punched her. After punching the victim the female demanded the victim’s coins. Afraid she would be hit again; the victim gave the female her coin purse. The female was located nearby and taken into custody. The female was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 6:23 pm an officer was sent to the report that a victim had a wallet that was stolen from an unlocked locker in the locker room sometime in the last four hours.

At 2:43 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect shattered a window to the victim’s vehicle and then removed property from the interior. A suspect also removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s other vehicle.
At 9:01 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed property. The theft occurred sometime over the weekend.
At 4:32 pm, an officer reported three unknown suspects entered a business, filled two large bags with property, and then fled.
At 6:59 pm, an officer was sent to the report that a suspect had entered the store, selected merchandise, and fled the store without paying for the items.
At 8:12 pm, an officer was sent to the report that someone had thrown an object through the front window of the business causing the window to shatter.

At 10:17 pm, an officer was sent to the report that a suspect had entered the store, selected merchandise, and fled the store without paying for the items. The officer located the suspect a few blocks away.  The subject was placed under citizen’s arrest by store employees and the stolen merchandise was returned to the store.  The subject was found to have five warrants for his arrest issued by the San Francisco Police Department.  The subject was booked at the ECPD and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Vehicle Tampering, Possession of Burglary Tools, Probation Violatio
Description: At 2:26 pm, an officer reported the arrest of an adult male who was seen attempting to steal a vehicle. The male was identified and found to be on probation. The male was taken into custody, booked, and then released on a citation.
At 11:50 pm, officers were dispatched to the alarm sounding at the business.  When officers arrived, they found the front door forced opened and found electronics had been stolen from inside.

At 2:35 am, an officer was patrolling in the area when he noticed a suspicious vehicle. The officer conducted a check of the license plate of the vehicle and discovered it was reported stolen to the San Mateo County Sheriff’s Office. When the officer attempted to stop the vehicle, the vehicle fled and drove the wrong way up the freeway off-ramp; the pursuit was terminated due to public safety.

At 9:43 pm, an officer was patrolling in the area when he noticed a vehicle travelling in the wrong direction of San Pablo Ave. The officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and contacted the driver, a 21-year-old woman. The officer conducted a DUI investigation and determined the woman to be DUI and was arrested. After booking she was released from the El Cerrito Police Department to a friend with a citation.

At 9:22 pm, an officer accepted the Citizen’s Arrest of a 33-year-old woman after the woman was detained with a shopping cart full of merchandise which she had pushed out of the emergency exit. The woman was issued a citation and released on her written promise to appear.
At 1:46 am, an officer contacted a 33-year-old man during a traffic stop and learned he had a warrant for his arrest. The man was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:15 am, an officer reported 7 unknown suspects cut a chain link fence and damaged a roll up garage door in an attempt to gain access to a commercial property. No property loss was reported.
At 7:22 pm, an officer reported two unknown suspects entered the business and collectively stole over  $1000 in clothing and merchandise.
At 8:20 pm, an officer reported three unknown suspects entered the business and stole several bottles of alcohol from a display case.
At 1:29 pm, officers responded to the business for a theft that just occurred.  The suspect stole approximately $1000.00 worth of merchandise and fled in a green minivan with no license plates.
At 7:16 pm, an officer reported that three unknown suspects stole merchandise from the business.
At approximately 3:00 am, unknown suspects entered the victims’ shared garage and stole over $3000.00 worth of gardening equipment.

Location: Lincoln Ave. at the BART Path
Description: At 5:30 pm, the victim was walking home when the suspect ripped her purse from her arm and ran southbound on the BART path.

Location: San Pablo Ave. at MacDonald Ave.
Description: At approximately 5:25 pm, an officer was flagged down by a citizen at the Del Norte BART station regarding a hit-and-run that just occurred.  The officer witnessed the suspect vehicle driving erratically out of the BART parking lot and onto San Pablo Ave.  The officer conducted a traffic stop on the vehicle and learned that the driver, a 74 year old female from Richmond, showed objective signs of being under the influence of alcohol.  The female driver was uncooperative and very abrasive towards officers.  After further investigation, she was arrested for driving under the influence of alcohol.  The female was transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking and released with a citation to her adult relative.  BART PD could not locate any victims or witnesses for a hit-and-run.
At 9:15 pm, an officer reported that an unknown suspect threw a brick through the windshield of the victim’s vehicle between 2:00 pm -9:00 pm.

Location: San Pablo Ave. at Central Ave.
Description: At 6:49 pm, an officer reported that a BART PD Officer collided with another vehicle while responding to an emergency call. The other driver, a 71 year old male from Oakland, was determined to be driving under the influence of alcohol.

Location: Jack in the Box, 5920 Cutting Blvd.
Description: At 2:39 am, an officer reported 5 suspects were in the drive-thru being difficult with employees and began calling them names through the intercom system. Employees refused to serve the group. Once at the drive-thru window, 3 female suspects exited the vehicle and begin punching and kicking the window. The drive-thru window was broken out. 1 suspect spit at the employees and tried to pepper spray them.
At 6:03 pm, an officer reported that an unknown suspect shot a bb gun pellet into the rear window of the victim’s vehicle as he was driving eastbound on Fairmount Ave.

At 6:07 pm, an officer reported that an unknown suspect shattered one of the window panels of the main doors at the Royale. The suspect probably used a slingshot to shoot a ball bearing at the window.

CrimeType: Attempted Theft and Brandishing of a Weapon
Description: At 5:02 pm, a citizen prevented the suspect from exiting the store with a cart of unpaid merchandise.  The suspect brandished a kitchen knife at the citizen then fled towards Plaza BART.

At 12:17 pm hours, an officer reported the suspect vehicle was leaving the Chase bank driveway onto San Pablo Ave., when the victim walked northbound on the sidewalk in front of the vehicle. The driver and victim got into a verbal argument.  After the argument, the suspect followed the victim, parked, exited her vehicle and physically assaulted the victim.  The victim suffered minor injuries but declined medical treatment.

At 7:00 pm, an officer reported that an unknown suspect stole items from Ross. As the suspect exited the business, Loss Prevention confronted the suspect. The suspect brandished a pocket knife at the employee before fleeing with $1200 worth of items.

CrimeType: Vehicle Tampering, Possession of a stolen vehicle, Possession of Narcotics, Warrant Arrest
Description: At 1:49 am, officers responded to the apartment complex for the report of a male trying the door handles on multiple vehicles. Officers arrived on scene and located a 41-year-old male from San Francisco. The caller identified the male as the person he saw trying car door handles. Officers located three vehicles in the parking lot that were ransacked. The vehicle the male arrived to the location in was reported stolen to the Dublin PD. During a search incident to arrest, the male was found to be in possession of fentanyl and methamphetamine. During a records check, officers discovered the male had three outstanding warrants out of Marin County. The male was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:49 am, an El Cerrito police officer arrested a 56-year-old male for a warrant from East Bay Regional Parks Police Department (EBRP).  EBRP Detectives interviewed the suspect at ECPD and transported him to County Jail in Martinez.

Location: Hotel Mira Vista Berkeley North (parking lot) 6009 Potrero Ave.
Description: At approximately 10:26 am, an officer reported the victim left his vehicle's engine running with the doors unlocked overnight,.  The victim’s vehicle was equipped with GPS and was tracked to the area of Carlson Blvd at Hartnett Ave. in the City of Richmond.  El Cerrito police officers located the Lexus, unoccupied, in the 4700 block of Hartnett Ave. The vehicle was returned to the owner.
An officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle overnight.
An officer reported sometime in between 11/21, at 7:30 am and 11/22, at 6:00 am, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s black construction trailer.

CrimeType: Prowling / Possession of Burglary Tools / Probation Violation
Description: At 11:33 pm, officers were dispatched to the area for a suspicious person detail. An officer located a 32-year-old Richmond man lurking in the driveway of a residence staged for sale.  A pair of bolt cutters was located on the driveway below the realtor key boxes and a rear window screen had been removed. The man was found to be on community supervision, and subject to search. A search of his vehicle resulted in the seizure of numerous shaved keys, powered cutting tools and other burglary tools. The male was arrested and issued a citation.
An officer reported the victim received dozens of text messages and telephone calls from her ex-husband in violation of a restraining order on file.

At 12:10 am, an officer reported the arrest of a 39-year-old male after the male and his romantic partner engaged in a fight over sleeping arrangements. After booking, the male transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

An officer arrested a 38-year-old El Cerrito man for an arrest warrant issued after violating a restraining order. The man was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Since Monday 11/15, Korematsu Middle School has experienced incidents of vandalism and theft. Beginning last week, school staff noticed damage to some of the school windows, likely caused by a BB gun or sling shot. This morning, school staff discovered additional damaged windows along with other vandalism. All told, school staff found at least 14 damaged windows on the campus. In addition, a vandal used a pen to write derogatory messages about the principal as well as race-based messages directed toward Black people on the concrete near the upper basketball court.  The suspects also broke a light fixture in the quad and stole the letter “L” from the sign in front of the school. The principal has estimated the total damages to the school to be at least $16,000.
At 5:30 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property.

At 10:00 am, officers were dispatched to a woman screaming in a stairway. Officers arrived to find a 19-year-old female who had been assaulted by her 23-year-old boyfriend. The victim sustained a broken finger and was transported to the hospital.
An officer reported sometime between 11/23, at 7:30 pm and 11/24, at 7:50 am, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 2000 Ford Econoline E150.

At 2:30 pm, an unknown suspect entered the store, grabbed over $3000 worth of sweats, and fled without paying. Battery – 100 Block of Wildwood Place At 5:30 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect called the victim and met him in front of his residence. Once outside, the suspect punched the victim in the face, injuring his lip. The suspect fled in a light-colored vehicle.
At 10:35 pm, an officer reported two men forced their way into a residence and robbed two occupants of their property while armed with a firearm.
At 6:44 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle.

At 7:00 pm, an officer reported that an unknown suspect stole a subwoofer and tools from the victim’s detached garage sometime overnight. Grand Theft – 7400 Block of Park Vista At 8:48 pm, an officer reported that the victim’s iPhone was stolen by his girlfriend after an argument.

An officer reported on 11/25, at 9:30 pm, an unknown suspect parked next to the victim’s vehicle. After 15 minutes, the suspect exited the vehicle and shattered the right rear passenger’s window of victim’s vehicle. There was no property loss reported.
An officer reported unknown suspect(s) removed the lock from the front door of the victim’s house and stole several power tools. The theft occurred in between 11/24, at 8:00 pm and 11/25, at 8:00 am.
An officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s bicycle from the storage area of the condominium.  The theft occurred in between 11/24, at 5:00 pm and 11/26, at 10:00 am.
At 11:50 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole merchandise from the business.
An officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle. The theft occurred in between 11/25, 8:00 pm and 11/26, 8:57 am.
At 8:53 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole clothing and purses from Ross totaling $750. When confronted by Loss Prevention, the suspect brandished a pair of pliers.

At 9:24 pm, an officer observed a vehicle speeding through the area. The officer conducted a traffic stop and arrested the registered owner for an outstanding warrant. The 36-year old Richmond woman was issued a citation at the scene.

At 11:44 am, officers were at the El Cerrito Plaza on an unrelated matter when they were flagged down by Ross employees regarding a theft that had just occurred. Officers detained the male outside the store with unpaid merchandise. The employees advised they wanted to place the male under citizen’s arrest for the theft. During a records check, the male was found to have two active ECPD warrants for theft. The male was field cited.
At 12:05 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed the rear license plate off the victim’s vehicle.
At 11:23 am, an officer reported the arrest of an adult female after it was discovered she had an outstanding warrant out of Contra Costa County.

At 3:21 am, an officer conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver for driving under the influence of alcohol. The 29-year old Richmond man was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, issued a citation, and released to a sober relative.

At 7:07 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect attempted to remove the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle. The suspect was scared away by the owner before being able to remove the part.
At 4:41 am, an officer reported the arrest of a 40-year old Richmond man that was a wanted “parolee at large”. The man was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 3:35 am, an officer conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver for driving under the influence of alcohol. The 48-year old Richmond man was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, issued a citation, and released to a sober relative.
At 7:43 am, an officer reported during a verbal altercation, the victim was assaulted by the male suspect.
At 9:25 pm, an officer reported that an unknown male suspect spray painted graffiti on the victim’s box truck.
At 9:00 pm, an officer reported that three unknown male suspects stole $800 worth of cosmetics.
At 3:57 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s Honda CR-V.
At 8:08 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle overnight.

CrimeType: Possession of a Concealed Firearm and Possession of a Controlled Substance
Description: At 5:50 am, officers were dispatched to a report of a suspicious vehicle. Officers arrived and discovered two occupants inside the vehicle who were asleep. As officers contacted the subjects, officers could see drug paraphernalia on the driver’s lap. During the investigation, an officer searched the vehicle and located a loaded firearm and several types of narcotics. The driver, an adult male, was taken into custody, booked, and then released on a citation.
At 4:49 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.
Sometime on Sunday, 11/28, an unknown suspect removed a 60 amp main fuse block from the victim’s electrical box.
At 8:33 pm, an officer reported the arrest of a 47-year-old man for shoplifting; the man was later released with a citation.

While dealing with the shoplifting incident above, an officer contacted a 34-year-old male as he exited the store with stolen merchandise. The merchandise was recovered and the man was later released with a citation.
At 12:10 pm, an unknown suspect kicked the victim off his bicycle while he was riding on the Ohlone Greenway. No injuires were reported.
At 9:25 am, an unknown suspect entered the store carrying a black trash bag and proceeded to fill it with medications, snacks, and soda.  He then fled the business without paying.

At 4:49 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect attempted to remove the catalytic converter from the underside of a delivery truck in the area. The suspect was scared off by an officer patrolling in the area and fled to a nearby vehicle.
At 2:30 pm, an unknown suspect entered the store, selected multiple toothbrushes, and fled without paying.

At 11:45 am, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of an 18-year-old Richmond woman after she confronted the victim and struck her several times.  The suspect fled but was apprehended by officers a few blocks away.  She was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released.
At 10:54 pm, an officer reported that 4 juveniles entered the business and stole numerous items before fleeing.

At 6:56 pm, an officer accepted the Citizen’s Arrest of a 22-year-old female after she exited the business in front of the officer with stolen merchandise. The woman was later released from the scene with a citation.
At 10:54 pm, an officer reported the arrest of a 46-year-old female following a solo vehicle collision. The woman was arrested for DUI and was later released from the ECPD with a citation.
Sometime between 12/1 at 5:00 pm and 12/2 at 8:00 am, an unknown suspect pried off the back truck window and stole several power tools.
At 3:50 pm, 4 unknown suspects entered the business, grabbed armfuls of clothing, and fled without paying.  The reported loss was over several thousand dollars.
At 5:59 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s Ford pickup sometime earlier that same day.
At 8:33 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect broke a store front window to the business earlier in the evening.

At 7:00 pm, an officer reported a male and female entered the business, selected clothing, then left the business without paying. Officers located the suspects nearby and arrested a 60-year old Oakland woman and a 45-year old Stockton man. The pair were issued citations and released at the scene.
At 1610 hours, an officer reported a male and female entered the business, selected alcohol, and then left the business without paying.

Location: Bank of the West, 11100 San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 11:20 am, officers were dispatched to a report of a male subject trying to cash a forged check. Upon officers’ arrival, they contacted the male, who advised he was at the bank to verify if the check he received in the mail was authentic. The male was positively identified and released from the scene. The officer will conduct further follow-up.
At 1:36 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business, concealed merchandise, and then left the business without paying.
At 8:06 pm, an officer reported an unknown male stole $250 worth of jackets.

At 8:39 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business and concealed merchandise in a garbage bag. The suspect then exited the business without making any attempts to pay for the concealed merchandise.
At 12:10 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect smashed the window, slashed the tire and dented the victim’s vehicle while it was parked on the street.
At 5:20 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business and stole a large quantity of cold medication before fleeing the store.
At 6:30pm, an officer reported that 4 juveniles entered the business and stole alcohol before fleeing the store.
At 3:05 pm, an officer reported two unknown suspects entered the business and pried open the high end alcohol display case and stole a large quantity of merchandise.

At 10:20 am, an officer met with a homeowner who found an expended bullet on his property and a hole in the gutter of his residence. It appears the round fell from the sky and was not shot at the home intentionally.
At 7:03pm, an officer reported the arrest of a 23-year old El Cerrito male for being in possession of fentanyl.
At 7:14 pm, an officer reported that an unknown suspect stole $150 worth of jackets.
At 7:46 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect broke the rear passenger window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property earlier in the day.
At 9:23 pm, an officer reported a former dating partner had sent several emails and text messages to the victim in violation of a current court order prohibiting electronic contact.

Location: Jack in the Box, 5920 Cutting Blvd.
Description: At 4:48 am, an officer investigated the attempted battery of staff and arrested a 62-year-old woman for the crime. While taking the woman into custody, the woman kicked an officer and stomped on the officer’s foot. The woman was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 12:09 am, an officer stopped a vehicle and contacted the 39-year-old female driver who was determined to be DUI. The woman also had 2 warrants for her arrest. The woman was arrested and later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:08 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle the previous day.
At 6:08 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked apartment and removed property earlier that same day.
At 8:58 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect removed approximately $100 worth of merchandise and fled the business.
At 9:11 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole an employee’s personal car keys from an unattended register yesterday.

At 10:10pm, an officer reported that a 49-year old El Cerrito woman collided into a parked car and tree. The woman was determined to be driving under the influence of alcohol. The woman was issued a citation for DUI and transported to the hospital via ambulance.
At 11:20 am, an officer reported two unknown suspects forced their way into a locked bicycle storage area and stole bicycles and tools.
At 6:27 am, an officer reported a known suspect brandished a knife while confronting employees at the business.
At 7:39pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the rear license plate from the victim’s vehicle.

Location: Jack in the Box, 5920 Cutting Blvd.
Description: At 2:15am, an officer reported the arrest of a 42-year old Richmond man for driving under the influence of alcohol. The man fell asleep behind the wheel of his vehicle while in the drive-thru. Officers contacted him and determined that he was intoxicated. The man was placed under arrest, booked at ECPD, then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 1324 hours, an officer reported that an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and rummaged through the interior; however, no loss was reported. The theft occurred in between 12/10 @ 9:00 pm and SAT 12/11 @12:00 pm.
At 9:12pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect shattered the back window of the victim’s vehicle.

At 11:05 am, an officer was patrolling in the area when she was flagged down by employees of a suspicious male inside the storage room area. The 44-year-old male from San Francisco was located in the parking lot inside his vehicle. The employees advised they did not want him to return to their business. As the officer was speaking to the male, a manager advised the male had stolen from the business. The male then turned on the engine of his vehicle and drove away. Officers caught up with the male a short distance within the parking lot after the male’s vehicle had mechanical issues. Initially the male disobeyed officers’ orders to stop the vehicle and exit it. After several verbal commands, the male complied and was safely taken into custody. The male was placed under Citizen’s Arrest for the theft and disobeying an officer. After booking the male was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1042 hours, an officer reported an unknown suspect used the victim’s Personal Identifying Information to obtain utilities.
At 6:28pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect shattered the front passenger window of the victim’s vehicle and stole miscellaneous items from the glove box.
At 8:18 hours, an officer reported an unknown suspect smashed the window to the victim’s rental vehicle and removed a cell phone. The time of occurrence was between 12/11@ 10:00 pm to 12/12@ 8:00 am.
At 6:04 am, an officer reported an unknown suspect took the victim’s Nintendo Switch while he was asleep in the backroom of the business. The theft occurred between 3:30 am and 5:24 am.
At 12:38 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business, loaded a shopping cart with merchandise, and left without paying.
At 9:43 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole $600 worth of purses the day prior.

An officer reported an unknown suspect smashed the driver’s front window of the victim’s vehicle and rummaged through the vehicle. No property loss was reported. The incident occurred in between 12/12@ 8:00 am and 12/13@11:00 am.
An officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 2001 silver Honda CRV sometime during the night.

Location: Parking lot of Bank of America, 10422 San Pablo Ave.
Description: Between 1:00 pm and 1:40 pm, an unknown suspect shattered the rear left passenger window of the victim’s vehicle and stole property.
At 4:15 pm, an unknown suspect entered the business, filled a canvas shopping bag with merchandise, and fled without paying.

CrimeType: Possession of Stolen Vehicle, Possession of Stolen Property
Description: At 6:33 pm, an officer contacted the 39-year-old driver who had a stolen license plate on the rear of their vehicle. During the officer's investigation he learned the vehicle was also reported stolen. The inside of the vehicle contained tools and equipment consistent with those used by a catalytic converter thief. The man was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

On 12/13, a parcel delivery driver exited his vehicle to deliver packages. Unfortunately, he left the doors unlocked and the vehicle’s engine running.  When he returned to the vehicle, he saw two suspects inside.  The suspects fled the area in the victim’s vehicle.
Between 7:53 am and 9:00 am, an unknown suspect shattered the rear left passenger window of the victim’s vehicle and stole property.
At 2:30 pm, an officer located the above stolen vehicle, parked in the fire lane behind 2000 El Cerrito Plaza.

At 7:45 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect gained access to a vacant home and left behind a gym bag contiang clothing. There was no damage done to the home and no loss was reported. The incident occurred sometime during the month of December.

Location: Parking lot of Chipotle, 9901 San Pablo Ave.
Description: Between 2:30 pm and 3:30 pm, an unknown suspect used a sharp object to scratch the exterior doors and fenders of the victim’s newly purchased vehicle.
Between 12/10 at 6:00 pm and 12/11 at 3:45 pm, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s Yamaha X-Max scooter.
At 8:14 pm, an officer reported a group of 7-8 suspects entered the business and stole several bottles of alcohol; the suspects ranged in ages from 16-50 years old.
At 8:25 am, an unknown suspect stole a package from the victim’s porch.
At 10:16 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle from the street in front of the residence sometime during the past two days.
Sometime overnight, an unknown suspect used a blunt object to strike the plate glass window of the victim’s business, leaving an indentation.

At 12:35 pm, an unknown suspect entered the business, selected merchandise, and left without paying.  The clerk followed the suspect outside and demanded the merchandise back.  The suspect pulled a firearm from his waistband and pointed it at the clerk.  The suspect then fled on foot.
At 6:42 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect entered the business and removed several items of men’s clothing before exiting without payment.
At 6:55 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect opened an unsecured garage door at the victim’s residence and removed two bicycles.
At 7:22 pm, an officer reported the victim located their stolen vehicle which had been stolen several days earlier. The vehicle was released to the owner who drove their vehicle home.
At 8:20am, an officer reported that an unknown suspect shattered the front door to the business, no property loss was reported.
At 1:00pm, an officer reported that an unknown suspect stole property from the victim’s unlocked vehicle.

At 6:46 pm, an officer was sent to a call where the victim had just interrupted people breaking into her vehicle.  The suspects shattered a window, but did not get inside the vehicle. The owner had walked up and scared the suspects off.
At 3:36 pm, an officer reported that an unknown suspect stole property.
At 11:47 am, an officer reported that an unknown suspect stole the victim’s white 2015 Ford Transit van sometime in the past 24 hours.
At 2:14 pm, an officer reported that an unknown suspect stole $600 worth of cosmetics.

At 9:10 pm, an officer was sent to the report of a theft that occurred a few minutes earlier.  Two suspects entered Ross and selected over $1000.00 worth of merchandise and walked out of the store without paying for the items.
At 7:43 am, an officer reported that an unknown suspect stole property from the victim’s unlocked vehicle.
At 2:26 pm, an officer reported an unknown suspect pried open the mailboxes at an apartment complex and stole an unknown quantity of mail and packages sometime overnight.

At 12:48 pm, an officer reported that the El Cerrito Fire Department discovered a Conex box was on fire at the abandoned building. The contents of the box were destroyed. A search of the area suggested that unhoused persons were squatting on the premises.
At 6:00 pm, an officer reports two unknown suspects entered the business and selected approximately $700 worth of women’s clothing before fleeing the store.

CrimeType: Possession of Controlled Substance, Probation Violation
Description: At 1:16 am, an officer reports the arrest of a 33-year-old man who was found to be in possession of methamphetamine. The man was also on County probation. The man was issued a citation and released in the field.
At 4:39 am, an officer was sent to the report of an auto Burglary that had occurred a few minutes earlier.  The suspect shattered the window of the vehicle and rummaged through the interior.
At 12:15 am, an officer reports the victim witnessed an unknown suspect drive off in the victim’s white truck. The victim gave chase but lost the vehicle several blocks away.

At 9:46 pm, an officer reports the Citizen’s Arrest of a 49-year-old and a 26-year-old woman who were detained after leaving the business via the emergency exit door. Both women were in possession of stolen merchandise and were issued a citation and released at the scene.
At 6:09 pm, an officer reports two unknown suspects entered the business and removed $600 worth of clothing.

CrimeType: Petty Theft, Conspiracy, Possession of a Firearm
Description: At 8:50 pm, an officer reports the arrest of a 22-year-old man after the man and another male arranged to meet with the victim to sell a video game console. The sale was not legitimate and the suspects ran off with the victim’s money. One of the suspects was arrested and while his vehicle was being towed, a firearm was located under the front seat. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 3:40 am an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on a vehicle and discovered the driver had a non-citable warrant for their arrest held by Napa County for theft charges.  They were arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:09 pm an officer was sent to the report that five people had just come into the store and two of them stole $300 worth of merchandise.
At 4:10 am officers were dispatched to an alarm on the site with someone seen on video inside the yard.  It was determined the suspect left prior to police arrival however some items had been stolen.
At 2:41 am an officer was sent to the report that someone had just stolen the catalytic converter off of the victim’s vehicle and fled the area.
At 6:57 pm an officer was sent to the report that an unknown suspect had broken into three lockers in the locker room and stole property from inside.

At 7:51 pm an officer was sent to the report that the victim was robbed by a person with a knife approximately ten minutes earlier in the parking lot of the business.  The victim was walking to their car when the suspect produced a knife and demanded property from the victim.  The suspect obtained some property and was last seen by the victim running west on Cutting Blvd.
At 2:14 am an officer was sent to a call that someone had just opened several boxes on a utility pick up bed however did not steal anything since they were empty.
At 6:59 pm employees at Walgreen’s reported that a suspect came into the store and loaded merchandise into his bag and walked out without paying for it.

At 7:25 pm an officer pulled over a vehicle for not having a rear license plate attached and when the officer ran the Vehicle Identification Number through Dispatch he was informed the vehicle was reported stolen out of San Francisco.  Both people in the vehicle were placed under arrest and the vehicle was impounded to be returned to its owner.  The occupants were booked at ECPD and then issued citations and released after booking.
At 12:09pm, an officer reports that an unknown suspect cut the lock to the victim’s garage, entered his unlocked vehicle, and stole $20 and a jacket.

At 12:54 am officers were dispatched to the report that several people aggressively approached a subject inside the bar because they did not like them.  Feeling threatened, the subject pulled out some pepper spray and deployed it on 2-3 people.
At 9:37am, an officer reports that two unknown suspects stole $500 worth of nicotine patches and gum from the business.

At 11:35am, an officer reports a 41-year old Fairfield male used a broken piece of glass to put two scratches into Fire Engine #65 while it was parked in front of the business. The male had two outstanding warrants stemming from a previous El Cerrito arrest and a San Diego County arrest. The male was booked at ECPD and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 6:22 pm an officer was dispatched to the report of a vehicle theft that had occurred over the last several days.
At 2:55pm, an officer reports that an unknown suspect stole the victim’s Chevy Silverado between 2:30-2:45pm.
At 7:04 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect spit in the face of an employee after the suspect was asked to leave the business.
At 7:54 pm, an officer spoke with the victim of a robbery which occurred on New Year’s Eve. The victim reports being robbed by two men, one of which was armed with a pistol.

At 9:02 pm, an officer reports two suspects assaulted staff at the restaurant after a disagreement. During the assault, one of the suspects struck the employees with a crowbar and used the object to damage property at the restaurant.

Location: and-Run – San Pablo Ave / Carlson Blvd
Description: At 6:44 pm, an officer reports a pedestrian was struck by a blue Honda CR-V which fled after the accident.
At 8:08 pm, an officer reports that an unknown suspect accessed the victim’s account and withdrew money from an ATM inside the business.

At 6:39 pm, officers arrested a 20-year-old man after he was identified as a suspect in two prior cases where man sold bogus goods to unsuspecting victims. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 6:27 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the rear license plate from the victim’s vehicle sometime between Christmas Eve and January 5th. Commercial Burglary, Robbery – 3160 Carlson Boulevard (76Gas): At 7:23 pm, an officer reports a known suspect used an object to break the glass front door and entered the business. While inside, the suspect took property from the business before fleeing. The suspect has been identified but has not yet been taken into custody.
At 11:25 pm, an officer arrested a 19-year-old man after the man damaged a vehicle and was found inside the vehicle. The man was arrested and later released from the El Cerrito Police Department.

CrimeType: Possess Stolen Vehicle, Possess Controlled Substance
Description: At 10:09 pm, an officer contacted a 38-year-old male during a traffic stop and located a quantity of methamphetamine in the man’s possession.
