During further investigation, a motorcycle in the possession of the man was determined to be stolen. The man was arrested and later released from the El Cerrito Police Department.

At 11:00AM an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole 25-30 tubes of toothpaste worth $400.  When the manager approached the suspect as he continued to “conceal” the merchandise in a clear plastic bag, he threatened to “beat her up” if she said anything.  The manager claimed to be fearful for her safety because the suspect comes into the store several times every day to steal. The suspect was described as a black male adult in his 40’s, 5’5” to 5”6” tall, weighing approximately 200 lbs.  The man was wearing a red beanie, grey jacket and black pants.  He fled on foot toward the rear of the store.

At 11:48AM an officer was flagged down regarding a gold 2002 Chevy Suburban (4VJE976) parked on the Ohlone Greenway behind the old burned out Taco Bell.  The vehicle was reported stolen to San Francisco PD on 1/8/2022.  The owner was not available to respond to collect the vehicle.
At 9:45AM an officer reports the victim’s credit information was used by an unknown suspect without authorization over the past several months.
At 1:20PM an officer reports an unidentified suspect used a corporate credit account to purchase items from O’Reilly Auto Parts in November and December 2021, totaling $370.

At 5:17 pm, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole women’s underwear, accessories handbags valued at $200.  The suspect was described as a black female, 55 years, 5’9”in height, average build, wearing a purple jacket, grey pants and black boots.  She was last seen toward the rear of the building on foot.

At 2:58PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect entered the victim’s Ford Transit work van via unknown means and stole tools, laptop and keys worth an unknown amount.  The theft occurred between 1/14/2022 – 7:00 pm and 1/16/2022 at 5:00 am.

At 9:13AM, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 31 year old Richmond man for shoplifting $60 worth of ice cream and other food items.  The man was still sitting out front eating the stolen food when officers arrived.  Citation issued.

At 2:22 pm, an officer reports an unidentified suspect entered the store, selected twelve bottles of alcoholic beverages worth approximately $350 in a basket and went to the cash register.  The man attempted to purchase the alcohol, but the sale was refused because he did not have his ID.  After the sale was refused, the man then attempted to leave the store with the basket of alcoholic beverages.  When the assistant manager attempted to stop him, the suspect produced a pepper spray canister from his pants pocket and sprayed it towards the store employees.  Most of the spray missed the employees.  The suspect then entered the front passenger seat of a waiting vehicle and fled westbound toward San Pablo Avenue with the alcohol.  When a store employee attempted to photograph the suspect, he again sprayed the pepper toward the employee, again mostly missing. The suspect was described as a black male, 35-40 years, 5’9”, fit build, wearing dark clothing and a multi-colored face mask.  The suspect vehicle, driven by an unidentified female, was described as a bright metallic royal blue Honda Civic (CA 8URX728) registered out of San Francisco.

At 11:50 am an officer reports the arrest of a 49 year old Richmond man after he was stopped by Lucky employees while stealing a bag full of candy.  The suspect punched one of the female clerks in the nose, giving her a bloody nose and knocking her to the floor.  The man then attempted to flee the store with other candy he picked up, and was tackled by several store employees.  The thief eventually fled to the platform at the Plaza BART station, chased by eight Lucky employees, where he was arrested.  The man was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

An officer reports on 1/18/2022, at 6:30 am, an unidentified suspect shattered the right front window of the victim’s silver Honda CR-V, removed a $20 pair of sunglasses and the remote to the parking garage.  The apartment complex video captured the suspect vehicle driving around in the parking garage, then parking next to the victim vehicle before the suspect broke the window.  The video was not of sufficient quality to provide a suspect description, but the suspect vehicle was seen as a gold 2000 Ford Taurus (CA 4ESP644) registered out of San Jose.

At 2:28 pm, an officer reports an unidentified suspect entered the T-Mobile store, walked straight to the Apple Watch display and used scissors to cut the security cables on two Apple Watches worth over $1000.  The suspect then fled the store on foot with the Apple Watches in an unknown direction.  The suspect was described as a black male, 6’0”, black hoodie and black pants. Forgery – 11299 San Pablo Ave, Ste. L (Check In To Cash) At 2:30 pm, an officer reports a 45 year old Richmond woman attempted to cash a check for $542.50 made out to a local trash company after hand writing her own name above the company name.  The suspect fled, without the check, prior to police arrival.
At 2:48 p,, an officer reports the rear license plate was stolen from the victim’s 2015 Toyota Corolla sometime between 3:00 pm on 1/17/2022 and 8:00 am on 1/18/2022.

At 1:56PM an officer reports a 45 year old Fremont man pointed and clicked what appeared to be a handgun at the passenger of a vehicle in the Safeway parking lot after the vehicle narrowly missed him as it entered a parking stall.  The occupants called police, even after the man told them it was a lighter.  The man was detained by BART PD at the Del Norte BART station and was positively identified by a witness.  The victim declined prosecution.  No firearm was located; however the man did have a lighter on his person.
At 4:07AM, an officer reports unknown suspects entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and removed property.
At 1:49PM, an officer reports unknown suspects damaged the locked gate to the businesses dumpster area and pried open the dumpster.
At 9:47AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect opened a credit card account using the victim’s identifying information.  The victim has reported having identity theft issues for over 5 years.

At 9:46AM, an officer reports a black Nissan NV200 work van was stolen when the driver left the keys in the van while making a delivery.  The van was wrapped with a “Great American Cleaners” logo.   Occurred between 9:30-9:40AM.

At 7:59AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect shattered the rear passenger side window of the victim’s 2003 Dodge Dakota and stole the vehicles front license plate which the victim kept in the glove box.  Also stolen was a checkbook and several gospel music tapes.  Occurred between January 24th at 10:00PM and January 25th at 7:50AM.
At 4:22AM, an officer reports unknown suspects entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and ransacked the interior; no loss was reported.

At 11:51AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect filled a trash bag full of merchandise and left without paying.  The suspect was described as male, 28-32 years old, 5’10” tall, wearing dark colored jeans, lime green Adidas shoes and a lime green windbreaker.
At 8:34AM, an officer reports the victim’s white 2000 Chevy van was stolen sometime overnight.

At 8:50AM, an officer reports a white 2006 Chevy Silverado was stolen between 11:45PM on 1/26/22, and 8:10AM on 1/27/22. Vehicle Theft – 1400 block of Navellier St: At 8:47AM, an officer was flagged down regarding the theft of a 1999 Chevy Express van which was taken sometime between 10:00PM on 1/26/22, and 8:45AM on 1/27/22.
At 9:25PM, an officer reports unknown suspects removed both the front and rear license plate from the victim’s vehicle earlier that same day.
Between 9:10 am and 9:25 am, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
At 8:49 pm an officer went to the report that the victim’s vehicle was stolen sometime during the day.
At 3:39 am an officer went to the report that the victim’s vehicle was stolen sometime earlier in the evening.

CrimeType: Delaying or obstructing a Police Officer
Location: San Pablo Avenue at Eastshore Blvd
Description: At 1:15 am an officer attempted to stop a subject for committing a vehicle code violation.  The subject would not stop and was found to be carrying an axe.  Other officers arrived on scene and the person eventually stopped and put down the axe.  He was placed under arrest for Delaying or Obstructing and violating his probation.  The axe was confiscated and the subject was issued a citation and released.

At 5:10 pm, officers were dispatched to the area after callers stated they heard 4-5 gunshots.  Officers circulated the area and located spent shell casings near the intersection.  After a thorough check of the area, officers were unable to locate any additional evidence or involved parties.

Location: San Pablo Avenue at Wall Avenue
Description: During the above arrest the passenger in the vehicle, who was also intoxicated, refused to call anybody to come and pick him up so he was placed under arrest.  He was booked at ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:10 am, the owner of a residence called for a security check of her property because she found evidence that an unknown suspect forced their way into the residence.  Officers conducted a security check of the interior and located a 43-year-old transient man and a 38-year-old transient woman.  Officers accepted the citizen’s arrest of the pair. They were removed from the property, cited, and released from the scene.

At 9:10 pm, officers were dispatched to the area after callers stated they heard 3 gunshots.  Officers circulated the area and located spent shell casings near the intersection.  After a thorough check of the area, officers were unable to locate any additional evidence or involved parties.

Location: San Pablo Avenue at Wall Avenue
Description: At 1:09 am an officer pulled a vehicle over and after an investigation determined the driver was intoxicated.  The driver was arrested and booked at ECPD.  He was also found to have a warrant for his arrest issued by Santa Clara County.  He was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 2:22PM, an officer reports a 47 year old was discovered by a passerby deceased on his cot from an apparent traumatic head injury.  No suspects have been identified and the case is still under investigation.

At 10:34AM, an officer reports a 33 year old man ordered food at Church’s Chicken, then when his EBT card was declined during his payment attempt, the man produced a realistic replica BB gun and pointed it at the cashier.  The cashier ran away in fear, and the man took the food and fled toward Walgreen’s.  He was located sitting in front of Walgreen’s dining on the food.  The suspect was booked into county jail.
At 11:00AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole $10 worth of food items during several trips in and out of the store.

At 10:24AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole a black 2022 Texoma 20 ft. flatbed car trailer after detaching it from the victim’s parked truck sometime between 9:00PM hours on 1/31 and 10:20AM on 2/1.  The trailer was purchased on 1/31 and had no plate.

At 3:22PM, an officer reports a green 1992 Honda Accord, reported stolen to ECPD on 1/29/2022, was recovered.  The vehicle was called in as a parking complaint because it was conspicuously wedged between the BART electrical substation and the fence to the rear of CVS.
At 9:26PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole a vehicle which had been parked on the street sometime earlier that same day.
At 1:33AM, an officer located a stolen vehicle and attempted to stop the vehicle which fled. The officer gave chase but lost the vehicle.
At 3:00AM, an officer located a stolen vehicle and attempted to stop the vehicle which fled. The officer gave chase but abandoned the pursuit after the suspect began to driver erratically.
At 3:07PM, an officer reports the license plates were stolen from the victim’s 2019 Toyota Corolla sometime between 9:00PM on 1/27/2022 and 2:30PM on 2/3/2022.

At 12:51PM, an officer reports two neighbors began to argue because the victim was standing in his own driveway and recording the suspect with his cell phone while calling him names.  The suspect, a 62 year old El Cerrito man, advanced on the victim repeatedly as the victim alternately stood his ground and backed away.  The suspect then punched the victim twice in the abdomen and head butted him in the chest causing him to lose his balance and fall to the ground.  The entire event was captured on the victim’s cell phone recording.  The victim claimed pain and dizziness and was transported to the hospital by ambulance.  The victim declined prosecution.
At 7:59PM, an officer reports two unidentified suspects stole candy before fleeing the store.
At 5:11AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect set fire to a vehicle parked in the driveway of a complex.

At 2:37PM, an officer reports a 38 year old Richmond man turned himself in at the lobby in response to a letter from El Cerrito Police notifying him of warrants for his arrest.  The man had two El Cerrito and three Richmond arrest warrants, none of which were citable.  A detective also interviewed the regarding a separate El Cerrito case.  The subject was booked into county jail.
At 10:30 am, officers contacted a tenant who stole another tenant’s package from the mail room.  The subject was contacted at his apartment and agreed to return the package to the victim.
At 9:22 pm an officer was sent to a call where the victim’s vehicle was stolen earlier in the evening.

CrimeType: Driving under the influence, Vehicle collision
Description: At 10:15 am, officers were dispatched to a collision with injuries.  Upon arrival, they found one of the drivers; a 26-year-old El Cerrito man was under the influence of alcohol.  The man was arrested, transported to the hospital by ambulance and cited.
An officer reports that an unknown suspect attempted to pry open a storage closet at the apartment complex. The victim caught the suspect sleeping in the laundry room and the suspect fled on foot.
The suspect from above entered the store and left with merchandise they did not pay for.  They were stopped by the officer outside and placed under arrest.
At 2:20 pm, three suspects entered the store, concealed $600 worth of merchandise, and fled without paying.
Sometime overnight, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s license plate.
At 6:41 pm an officer went to the call that the victim went inside the store to get food and left their vehicle running parked outside.  An unknown suspect entered the vehicle and drove off with it.

While investigating the above two incidents Ross store employees pointed out another theft suspect that was exiting the store.  He was detained by officers outside, placed under arrest, booked at ECPD, and issued a citation.
At 6:10 pm an officer went to the report that two people got into an argument inside the store and the suspect threw a drink and spit on the victim.  The suspect then fled the plaza.
At 2 pm, three suspects entered the store, concealed $400 worth of merchandise, and fled without paying.

At 1907 hours an officer was taking a report about a suspect who had stolen merchandise from the store.  Just then the suspect exited Lucky’s followed by security because they had stolen from that store.  The Ross employees identified that suspect as the same suspect responsible for the theft the officer was taking the report for.  She was placed under arrest for both thefts and issued a citation.
An officer recovered a 2000 Volkswagen that had been reported stolen to Oakland PD on 1/29.

At 9:34AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect entered the vacant house under remodel by breaking the lockbox and retrieving the house key.  The suspect stole $8000 worth of tools belonging to the contractor, sometime between 1600 hours on 2/7/2022 and 0800 hours on 2/8/2022.
At 11:30AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole a package off the porch containing prescription medication, sometime between 5:51PM – 6:15PM on 2/7/2022.
At 6:34PM, an officer reports two unknown suspects entered the business and stole handbags; the crime occurred two days earlier.
At 8:15AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect entered the victim’s vehicle by breaking out the right rear quarter window, and stole a wallet.

At 1:30PM, an officer reports a 33 year old transient woman stole hair products.  The suspect was located at Potrero Ave and S.55th St., trying to pedal her bicycle while pushing a shopping cart, and she was detained.  The call was originally reported as a robbery by the store owner, but the store video did not support her claim.  The suspect was also found to be in possession of less than one gram of methamphetamine.  The arrested woman was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department then released on a citation.

At 9:23AM, an officer reports the victim went to the Bank of America ATMs on 2/4/2022, approximately noon, and attempted to make a withdrawal.  The first machine would only allow a $20 withdrawal, the second machine refused to accept the victim’s card, and the third machine finally allowed the $1000 withdrawal.  The victim later discovered an unauthorized charge on his account for over $1200 and he believed there was a skimmer installed in the ATMs at the time he attempted to use them.  B of A was notified but said there was no evidence of a skimmer.

At 4:30PM, an officer reports the victim came to the police department lobby to report her ex, a 54 year old Richmond man, showed up at her apartment in violation of a restraining order.  The man let himself in using a key he had from when he also lived there.  The suspect made vaguely threatening statements toward the victim, and the victim fled the apartment and ran to the police department.  A copy of the latest served criminal protective order was received from the issuing agency.  Officers attempted contact with the suspect at the apartment, but he was no longer there.

At 8:57AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the vacant residence under remodel by unknown means, and stole tools worth over $8000, sometime between 5PM on 2/7/2022 and 8:30AM on 2/8/2022.

At 2:37PM, an officer reports three suspects entered Ross, selected armfuls of clothing totaling $450 and fled the store without attempting to pay for the merchandise.  Suspect #1 was described as a black male in his 40’s, wearing a yellow SFMTA vest and a hat.  Suspect #2 was described as a black female in her mid-30’s, heavy-set, wearing a dark grey hoodie and sweatpants.  Suspect #3 was described as a black female in her late 40’s, wearing sunglasses, light grey hoodie and sweatpants.  Unknown direction of flight after the suspects went out the front doors.
At 2:45 pm, an unknown suspect ran up behind the victim and ripped her cell phone out of her hands while the victim was talking on it.  The suspect fled on foot.
At 1:40 pm, an unknown suspect entered the store, concealed merchandise, and fled without paying.

At 9:59 PM, an officer accepted the Citizen’s Arrest of a 25-year-old woman after the woman was identified as having just stolen property from the business. The merchandise was recovered and the woman was released with a citation.

At 2:20 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect cut a hole in the fence of a commercial property in an attempt to steal property. The suspect left before removing the property and no loss was reported.

At 2:46 PM, an officer reports the arrest of a 54 year old local man when he once again arrived at the victim’s apartment in violation of a domestic violence restraining order.  The subject was arrested and transported to the MDF.
At 11:25 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the right rear window of the victim vehicle and stole a backpack, sometime between 7 PM on 2/13/2022 and 11:25 AM on 2/14/2022.
At 0342 hours an officer was sent to the report that someone had shattered a window of a vehicle and stole property from inside approximately 10 minutes earlier.
At 9:40 PM, an officer reports the arrest of a 30-year-old woman who was found to have a warrant for her arrest. The woman was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 10:20 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect threw a rock through the front window of the acupuncture office and entered the business.  The suspect stole a credit card machine worth $200. Occurred between 5 PM on 2/12/2022 and 6:15 AM on 2/13/2022.

At 10:15 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect shattered the rear window and cracked the windshield of the victim vehicle with an unknown object, sometime between 9 PM on 2/13/ 2022 and 10:15 AM on 2/14/2022.
At 12:28 PM, an officer reports the victim’s black 2001 Lexus GS300 was stolen from the street between 8:30 AM on 2/13/2022 and 10:00 AM on 2/14/2022.

At 12:35 PM, an officer reports an unidentified male suspect stole $300 worth of razor blades.  When the store manager was trying to call 911, the suspect pressed the button to hang up the phone, then pushed the Plexiglas COVID barrier over causing it to crack.
At 2:41 AM, an officer reports an unknown male ordered food as a dine-in patron but fled the restaurant when it came time to pay.
At 10:32 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect withdrew $2000 from the victim’s bank account on 1/23/2022 without authorization and by unknown means.

At 2:15 PM, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 32 year old Oakland woman for shoplifting $271 worth of clothing, luggage and reusable bags from Ross.  The woman was issued a citation and released and the stolen property was returned to Ross.

CrimeType: Battery on Peace Officer / Attempt to Disarm
Description: At 12:13 PM, an officer reports the arrest of a 38-year-old man after officers responded to a report that he was lying in a disabled person’s parking stall.  The subject attacked the officer, punching her in the head and forcing her onto the ground.  While on the ground, he attempted to steal her baton.  The man was eventually subdued and was taken into custody.  The District Attorney later charged felony battery on a police officer charges.

At 3:06 PM, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of the same 32-year-old Oakland woman from the previously mentioned shoplifting case for shoplifting after she stole merchandise.    The woman was spotted by an officer committing the Walgreen’s theft while he was driving in the area looking for her in relation to the Ross shoplifting case. The woman was issued a second citation and released, and the stolen property was returned to Walgreen’s.

At 8:14 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed a security plate in an attempt to steal the victim’s catalytic converter sometime overnight.  The converter was not taken but the vehicle was left up on jacks.

At 12:02 PM, an officer responded to the report of a group of four suspects who attempted to steal a woman’s purse as she walked through the parking lot.  The victim was dragged and suffered minor injuries. Battery – 8300 Terrace: At 2:08 PM, an officer responded to the report of a subject spitting on their neighbor during a dispute that happened earlier in the day.
At 3:09 PM, an officer reports an unidentified female concealed merchandise belonging to the store in her bag and then left without paying.
At 7:02 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle sometime within the last week.
At 6:20 PM, an officer reports an unknown subject has gained access to the convenience store structure at the former gas station on the corner of this location.

CrimeType: Warrant Arrest, Drunk in Public, Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 7:11 PM, an officer reports the arrest of a 54-year-old male who was found to have a warrant for his arrest. The man was heavily intoxicated and also was in possession of drug paraphernalia. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:59 am, an officer was sent to the report that four suspects had come into the store, grabbed thirty-five pairs of shoes, and ran out without paying for them.
At 7:11 pm an officer was sent to the report that someone had ran out of the store with merchandise that they did not pay for.

At 8:47 pm an officer found a vehicle using the LoJack system that had been stolen in a Car Jacking earlier in the day in Oakland.  There was nobody inside the vehicle.  The Oakland Police Department was notified, and they responded and took over recovering the vehicle.
At 9:04 pm an officer was sent to the report that someone had stolen the U-Haul van that someone had rented and had parked here.

At 2:17 am an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and after an investigation determined the driver was under the influence of alcohol.  The driver was arrested, booked at ECPD, issued a citation, and released to a sober friend.
At 1:25 am an officer located a vehicle that had been reported stolen to El Cerrito PD they day before.  The vehicle was released to the owner.

At 12:26 am an officer located a stolen car using the LoJack system that had been reported stolen to Richmond PD.  The owner of the vehicle in El Cerrito was contacted and they were reunited with their vehicle.
At 4:25 am an officer was sent to the report that someone had just stolen the catalytic convertor from their neighbor’s vehicle.

An officer recovered a stolen Honda CRV. Restraining Order Violation: An officer responded to the report of a subject violating a domestic violence restraining order.  The subject fled the scene prior to the report.
An officer responded to the report of an unidentified suspect stealing a 1999 Honda CRV sometime overnight.

At 2:32 am an officer was dispatched to the report of an auto Burglary.  When he arrived in the area, he found the suspect vehicle a few blocks away.  Two people in that vehicle were detained.  At the original scene a second suspicious vehicle was located and determined to be reported as stolen.  Additional stolen property was located within the stolen vehicle.  The two suspects were arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer reported the theft of a catalytic converter from a vehicle.  The theft occurred overnight.
At 1044 hours, officers responded to the report of a suspect attempting to steal a parked vehicle.
At 1447 hours, an officer responded to the report of a theft of a license plate.
At 8:40 pm, an officer responded to the report of a domestic battery. The suspect had previously fled the scene.

At 8:44 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect removed yard equipment from the bed of the victim’s pickup sometime during the evening. Shoplifting – 2000 El Cerrito Plaza, Ross At 9:36 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the business and selected $400+ worth of merchandise; fled on foot towards the BART path. Trespassing, Drunk in Public – 11821 San Pablo Ave, McDonalds At 10:49 pm, an officer reports the Citizen’s Arrest of a 62-year-old man who was heavily intoxicated and refusing to leave the business. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:28 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and removed vehicle insurance and registration paperwork.
At 8:01 pm, an officer responded to the report of a domestic battery that had previously occurred.  The suspect was not on scene.

At 6:42 pm, an officer reports the victim located their stolen vehicle which had been reported stolen earlier that same day. When the victim went to drive home, they learned the catalytic converter had been stolen.
At 7:10 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect set fire to a mini-fridge which damaged the fridge and the grass around the appliance.

An officer reports that an unknown suspect arranged to buy the victim’s bicycle for sale.  The suspect mailed a fraudulent check to the victim and tricked the victim into paying shipping costs to a fake company.
Between 2/19 and 2/27, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s bicycle from their storage shed, sometime between 2/19 and 2/27.

At 1:56 am officers were dispatched to the report of a fight in the area.  Officers located a subject who was bleeding and reported that he was involved in a fight inside the bar and someone hit him in the head with a beer bottle.  The suspect had fled prior to police arriving.  The victim was transported to the hospital by ambulance.

At 10:05 pm officers were dispatched to the report of an unwanted subject refusing to leave the business.  The officers contacted the subject and while they were speaking to him, he turned and charged at one of the officers.  The officers grabbed onto the suspect and pulled him to the ground.  While on the ground the subject tried to kick the second officer.  The suspect was handcuffed, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Location: Ohlone Greenway and Blake St., Knott Ave., and Wall Ave.
Description: An officer reports that an unknown suspect lit fire to various items while traveling northbound on the Ohlone Greenway.

At 11:53 pm someone flagged down an officer on San Pablo Avenue about a brandishing that occurred at their house about four hours earlier.  This person had someone over their house that they knew casually.  That person said they were going to get something out of their car and when they opened the front door there were two people on the front porch pointing firearms at the resident.  The casual friend was trying to hold the door open while the victim was trying to close the door.  The victim was able to close the door with the friend and the suspects outside and they all fled the area.

Location: San Pablo Avenue between Ohio Avenue and Cutting Blvd.
Description: At 7:36 pm officers were dispatched to the first of three fires to garbage and a dumpster at three different locations in this area.  No suspects were located.
At 11:54 pm an officer was sent to the report that someone had just seen someone steal a catalytic converter from a vehicle parked in the parking lot.  The suspect fled the scene on a bicycle.
At 9:58 pm, an officer was sent to the report of the victim receiving threatening text messages.

At 12:01 am an officer was sent to the report of a fraud case.  The victim reported that they received a telephone call from someone that identified themselves as a Police Officer and told the victim that they had missed a court date so they would have to pay $8000.00 or get arrested.  The victim wired $4500.00 to the suspect before she realized it was a scam.

At 8:45 pm an officer was sent to the report of a victim being assaulted being by someone they had an ongoing dispute with.   The suspect punched the victim several times.   The suspect then fled the scene in a car.

CrimeType: Vandalism, Possession of drug paraphernalia, Warrant arrest
Description: Officers were dispatched to the gas station after a subject was seen prying open the clothes donation bin on the property.  Officers detained a 38-year-old transient man and found that he had a warrant issued for his arrest and was also in possession of drug paraphernalia.  The man was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 10:46 AM, an officer reports video was obtained of an unidentified suspect who entered the garage area of the apartment building by prying a wood vent and then reportedly broke into a car, although the vehicle could not be located and no victim called police.  The suspect continued their way into the apartment building by prying a metal door with an unknown tool and pried four mailboxes.
At 7:15 PM, an officer reports a group of five unknown suspects arrived at the victim’s residence and began banging on the sliding glass door; the door was damaged as a result.

CrimeType: Possession of a Firearm on School Campus / Assault with a Deadly Weapon
Description: At 11:19 AM, officers were dispatched to the El Cerrito High School regarding a fight.  One of the students used what appeared to be a firearm with an extended magazine as a striking implement during the fight.  The suspect fled campus.  Detectives obtained a warrant for the arrest of the suspect who is currently at large.
At 4:06 PM, an officer reports an unidentified female suspect stole items and left without paying.
At 5:09 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect spray-painted graffiti on the victim’s stucco retaining wall sometime in the past week.
At 7:53 AM, an officer located an unoccupied 2016 Honda Civic which was reported as stolen.

At 2:02 PM, an officer reports a 2003 Ford E-350 van marked for a water heater repair service was stolen from the street between 1:30 and 1:50 PM on 3/9/2022 while the driver was servicing a water heater in the apartment building.
At 4:38 PM, an officer reports the victim’s black 2000 Mazda B2500 pickup was stolen from the street between 6:00 AM and 3:45 PM on 3/9/2022.
At 11:45 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect filled a plastic bag with merchandise belonging to Walgreen’s and left without paying.
At 8:25 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect filled a black garbage bag with merchandise belonging to Walgreen’s and left without paying.
At 10:10 AM, an officer reports the victim’s vehicle was keyed sometime between 1600-1800 hours on 3/7/2022.
At 2:10 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect selected merchandise and left without paying.  Last seen on a blue bicycle toward JoAnn.
At 7:41 pm an officer was sent to the report that someone had a package stolen off of a front porch two days earlier.
Sometime between March 8th and March 11th, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 1998 Ford van.
At 4:30 pm, a known suspect entered the check cashing business with a forged check and attempted to cash it.
At 7:26 pm an officer was sent to the report that someone had broken the windshield on the victim’s vehicle sometime during the previous hour.

At 8:15 pm an officer was flagged down by the victim of a stolen vehicle.  The victim was selling their car and made plans to meet the suspect in a parking lot of a business.  The suspect handed the victim an envelope full of money and got into the car and drove away before the victim counted the money.  The victim attempted to follow the stolen vehicle in another vehicle but the vehicle that the suspect had arrived in and driven by another suspect blocked his path.  Someone in the backseat of the suspect vehicle pointed a handgun at the victim.  When the victim looked at the money in the envelope they found it was counterfeit currency.
At 9:45 pm an officer was sent to the report that someone had entered the store, selected merchandise, and walked out without paying for the items.
At 9:36 pm an officer was sent to the report that someone had entered the store, selected merchandise, and walked out without paying for the items.
Sometime between 3/11 at 5 pm and 3/12 at 10 am, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s 2002 Honda Odyssey.

At 6:30 am, a passerby notified police of a shattered window at the business.  When contact was made with the owners later in the day, officers learned the vandalism occurred at 4 am on the morning of March 11th.
At 12:16 am an officer was sent to the report that someone had shattered a window on the victim’s vehicle sometime in the last hour while it was parked in the parking lot.

At 1:35 pm, an officer was flagged down by employees at CVS regarding a theft of alcohol.  Officers detained the suspect, a 40-year-old transient man.  The bottle of alcohol was recovered and the man was found to be on parole for burglary.  His parole agent issued a hold after the man violated the terms of his parole.  The man was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1846 hours an officer was sent to the report that someone had pried open the door lock on the victim’s vehicle however was scared off by a neighbor before they could steal anything.
Sometime between 3/12 at 6:30 pm and 3/13 at 2 pm, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s 2003 Honda Accord.

At 8:34 PM, an officer reports an ex-girlfriend confronted the janitor of the business about outstanding keys to her residence. When the janitor returned the keys, the woman kicked the door to the business breaking the glass.
At 12:32 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect set fire to a green mesh covering the chain link fencing at a construction site in the area.
At 3:31 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect placed a burning propane torch up against the business front door which caused damage.
At 6:11 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect threw a plastic container at an employee inside the business during an altercation. The suspect fled prior to police arriving on scene.
At 10:53 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim’s vehicle and removed property from inside the vehicle.

At 8:33 PM, an officer accepted the Citizen’s Arrest of a 33-year-old man who was found inside a construction site in the area. The man was also in possession of drug paraphernalia; he was released after being issued a citation.
At 8:24 PM, an officer reports two unknown suspects stole the catalytic converter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle earlier that same day.

At 6:52PM, an officer accepted the Citizen’s Arrest of a 50-year-old man who had damaged the windshield of a nearby vehicle with a golf club. The man was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 11:33 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect approached the victim and a friend and pointed a gun at the victim before demanding money. The suspect fled with the victim’s wallet.
At 6:50 PM, an officer reports the Citizen’s Arrest of a 24-year-old man for shoplifting from the business. The man was issued a citation and released at the scene.
At 5:08 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect made entry into a residence some time over an 8-day period.

An officer was dispatched to AT&T for a subject that just broke a large plate glass window in the front of the business.  He then fled on foot, eastbound to the BART Path.  Officers located and detained a 36 year old Richmond man.  The suspect was most likely high on a central nervous system stimulant and complained of chest pains.  The man was checked by paramedics and declined any further medical treatment.  The man was transported to the county jail.
At 6:35 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect broke a window to the victim vehicle sometime during the night and removed property.
An officer reports that an unknown suspect shattered the rear sliding glass door of the victim’s residence and lightly ransacked the interior; no loss has been reported at this time.
At 12:59 PM, an officer reports the front license plate from a 2020 Ford F-150 pickup was lost or stolen sometime between August 2021 and today.
At 9:54 AM, an officer reports a red 2000 Honda CR-V was stolen from the street between 6:00 PM on 3/28 and 9:45 AM on 3/29.
At 8:48 AM, an officer reports a 1999 black Honda Civic was stolen from the street between 9:00 PM on 3/28 and 8:30 AM on 3/29.
At 11:11 AM, a Community Service Officer reports an unidentified suspect stole $1500 worth of tools from the back of a pickup truck between 10:30-10:40 AM while the victim was in his house for lunch.
At 1:15 PM, an officer reports the recovery of a 2017 Nissan Sentra reported stolen to the Vallejo Police Department.
At 2:03 AM, an officer reports an unknown removed the catalytic conve3rter from the underside of the victim’s vehicle.

CrimeType: Possess Firearm on Campus / Possess High Capacity Magazine / Juvenile Possess Firearm / Unserialized Firearm / Resist Arrest
Description: At 2:43 hours, officers were dispatched to a report of a student on campus with a pistol.  A 17 year old male student and an 18 year old male student were both determined via video surveillance to have been in possession of the backpack containing the loaded Polymer 80 9mm “ghost gun” with high capacity (50 round) drum magazine.  The juvenile was detained in handcuffs while a search was conducted to attempt to locate the backpack.  The juvenile attempted to run while handcuffed, but was taken into custody after a short distance. The adult student was located on the BART path near Schmidt and ran when confronted by officers.  He was taken into custody near Tap Plastics.  The backpack containing the “ghost gun” was located abandoned on school grounds by school administrators, and was collected as evidence.  The 18 year old student was transported to the county jail, and the juvenile student was released to his parent after Juvenile Hall declined to accept him.
At 2:49 PM, an officer reports the victim’s ex-boyfriend, a 52 year old Richmond man, kicked the victim’s vehicle causing dents.
At 3:33 PM, an officer reports the theft of three large potted plants from the front yard of the residence.
At 7:34 PM, an unidentified suspect stole $30 worth of food items.

At 8:38 PM, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a 27-year-old unsheltered woman when she was discovered inside the previously burned vacant residence.  The arrested woman also had two no-cite warrants for her arrest.  She was issued a citation for trespassing and transported to the County jail on the warrant.
An unknown suspect stole the victim’s black Mercedes SUV between 5/17/22 - 6/18/22 while the victim was away on vacation.

At 9:00 PM, an officer reports a 27-year-old El Cerrito man purportedly hit the victim on her head and legs several times during an argument.  He also pinned her down several times when she tried to leave the residence, resulting in bruises on her arms.  The victim fled to the Pastime ACE Hardware parking lot with most of her possessions to call the police.  The suspect fled the area prior to the arrival of the police.
At 4:43 pm, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
An unknown suspect stole the victim’s Honda Civic.  The theft occurred sometime overnight.

At 1228 hours, two suspects shattered the victim’s vehicle window and stole a purse while the owner was seated inside the vehicle.  The suspects then fled the scene in a vehicle northbound on San Pablo Ave.
On 7/23/22, between 4:00 and 4:50 pm, an unknown suspect stole a package from the lobby of the apartment complex.
At 0807 am, Officers recovered a stolen work van originally reported stolen to ECPD in June.

At 7:52 PM, an unidentified suspect stole the catalytic converter off the victim’s Honda Accord that was parked in her driveway.  The suspect fled in a green Nissan, possibly a Leaf or similar.  Last seen eastbound on Potrero.
Between 0900-1000 am, an unknown suspect shattered the window of the victim’s vehicle and stole a backpack containing a wallet.
At 1:07 pm, Officers arrested a male subject for an outstanding no bail misdemeanor warrant.
Three unknown suspects cut the front door lock of the restaurant, entered, and stole approximately $3000.00 worth of miscellaneous tools.  Denny’s was currently closed for renovations.

At 8:52 pm officers went to a report of vandalism inside Lucky.  An officer located the suspect in another part of the El Cerrito Plaza.  The subject was placed under citizen’s arrest and was issued a citation for the crime and released.

CrimeType: Arrest for Felony Vandalism, Probation Violation and Warrants
Description: At approximately 10:03 am officers responded to the Ross store for a male breaking store windows with a brick.  The male also damaged several cars in the parking lot.  An El Cerrito police K9 officer detained a 22-year-old male on San Pablo Ave at Blake St.  The victim positively identified the male as the suspect who vandalized the store and the vehicles.  The male was on probation out of Contra Costa County for a weapons charge and had three Contra Costa County warrants for his arrest.  The male was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the County Jail in Martinez.

At 2:05 am El Cerrito police Officers and El Cerrito firefighters were sent to the report that someone had started a fire in front of the store.  When the officers and firefighters arrived, they found a transient had started a fire on the sidewalk in front of the store.  The fire was extinguished, and the subject was placed under arrest.  They were issued a citation and released.
At approximately 4:51 pm a subject walked out of the store with a pair of jeans and a backpack without attempting to pay for the merchandise.
At approximately 7:17 am an unknown suspect drove their Ford SUV into the south side fence of the business and fled the scene.
An officer located a 1992 Toyota pickup that had been reported stolen to San Rafael on 7/22/22.

CrimeType: Brandishing a weapon, Criminal Threats, Failure to register as a sex offender
Location: Pic N’ Pac, 10012 San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 4 pm, a 57-year-old man was arrested after he pulled a knife on the victim and threatened his life.  The subject was also out of compliance with sex registration requirements.  The man was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:15 pm, an unknown suspect shattered the victim’s passenger window and stole a designer purse from under the seat.  The suspect then fled on foot.
An unknown suspect entered an unlocked city vehicle and used it as a shelter for the night.
Sometime over the weekend, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.

Two lockers were broken into between 8:00 – 8:45 PM and a cell phone was taken.  No suspect info. Assault with a Deadly Weapon – 10203 San Pablo Ave (Idaho Apartments: At 9:13 PM, officers responded to a third-hand report of a male with a cut on his face.  Officers arrived and contacted the victim who was bleeding from cuts to his hand.  It was determined that a 33 year-old male associate suddenly pulled out a knife and “came at him”.  The suspect was arrested and booked at the El Cerrito Police Department.  He was later released per 849PC due to a pre-existing medical condition.  The case will be forwarded to the District Attorney for review.

An officer reports a stolen 2004 Chevy pickup was located near Cerrito Vista Park by its owner around 6:30 PM.  The vehicle was reported stolen to Richmond Police Department earlier in the day.  The vehicle was recovered and released to its owner at the scene.

At 3:30 pm, officers arrested a 29-year-old El Cerrito man after he injured his girlfriend by grabbing her arm during an argument.  The man was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 6:45 am, witnesses interrupted the theft of their neighbor’s catalytic converter.

A neighbor in the area heard a loud bang and looked outside to see two vehicles in the roadway.  The two vehicles were the recently stolen Chevy Express van and F350 box truck.  She saw the suspects flee in a dark-colored SUV and called police.

At 7:04 PM, officers were dispatched to multiple calls of shots fired in the area.  A witness described seeing a male subject, wearing a grey hoodie and red shorts firing multiple shots toward the BART path from a handgun.  The suspect then fled westbound on Willow Ave and southbound on Liberty St. while tucking a black semi-automatic handgun in his waistband. Expended casings were located near the sidewalk at the southwest corner of Willow Ave and Oak St.  Two parked and unoccupied vehicles on Elm St at Willow Ave were struck by bullets, and several apparent bullet impacts were located on the BART pillar adjacent to the location.   No victims were located and an extensive search for the firearm was conducted.
An officer reports that an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 2012 Ford F350 box truck.
At 5:30 am, two unknown suspects shattered the driver window of the victim’s vehicle and stole property.
An officer reports that an unknown suspect stole the victim’s, Chevy Express Van.

At 11:27 PM, officers responded to a report of a possible commercial burglary at the business.  Employees responded to the business to check on an alarm about an hour earlier and discovered a light ransack in the front office.  The point of entry was through a skylight.  Unknown loss.

An unknown suspect accessed the victim’s EDD account and transferred several thousand dollars to an unknown account. Battery – 11450 San Pablo Ave (Safeway): At 10:17 PM, officers were dispatched to Safeway regarding a person assaulted with pepper spray.  Upon further investigation, it became apparent the “victim” a 33-year-old unsheltered woman had been pepper sprayed by Safeway security guards when she swung and spat at them as she was being escorted out of the store as a trespasser due to previous thefts.  The prosecution was declined by security for the battery.  The woman was treated at the scene for pepper spray exposure and released.

CrimeType: Hate crime, Battery, Battery on a police officer, Warrant arrest
Description: Officers were dispatched to a fight involving two men on the BART Path.  The investigation revealed that the suspect used derogatory homophobic slurs and punched the victim in the face after an argument.  When officers detained the suspect, they found he had a warrant for his arrest.  When officers attempted to handcuff the suspect, he resisted arrest and kicked an officer.  The officer suffered minor injuries.  The 41-year-old man, who was on probation for robbery and theft in Contra Costa County was subsequently arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 6:30 am, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
At 7:45 am, an unknown suspect attempted to steal the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.

Sometime between 12/18/22 and 1/1/23, an unknown suspect gained entry into the victim’s vehicle by shattering a window.  Once inside, the suspect destroyed the steering column in an attempt to steal the vehicle.
Sometime overnight, an unknown suspect stole 4 mailboxes from the complex.
On 12/29/22, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 2007 Chevy 3500 pickup.
At 4:00 PM, an unknown suspect ran towards the victim while making a slashing motion.  The victim was able to flee on his bicycle and call police.

Sometime overnight, an unknown suspect shattered the window of the victim’s vehicle and stole property. Burglary – 10636 San Pablo Ave (24 Hour Fitness): At 7:00 PM hours, an officer reports two lockers in the women’s locker room were broken in to during the previous two hours and purses, wallets, house keys and car keys were stolen.  One victim reported her cards had already been used at a  store in in another city. Robbery (Firearm) – Potrero Ave / S.55th St.: On 12/31/2022, approximately 3:30 PM, the victim was stopped by two unidentified suspects as he rode his bicycle toward Safeway.  (S-1) produced a black handgun and demanded money.  The victim gave up his $800 yellow bicycle (NFD) and the suspects fled in an unknown direction.
Sometime overnight, an unknown suspect climbed into the victim’s residence through a window and stole belongings.

CrimeType: Shoplift / Possess Paraphernalia / False Identity to Peace Officer / Arrest Warrants
Description: At 6:32 PM, while conducting a security check in the shopping center, an officer was flagged down by Ross employees regarding a theft in progress.  A suspect detained in the parking lot, and was positively identified and placed under citizen’s arrest by Ross staff.  A crack Cocaine pipe was located on him during the arrest. The name and date of birth provided verbally by the arrested man at the scene returned with no match, he had no hard ID, and he claimed to be out of Connecticut, so he was transported to ECPD for booking.  The man provided his true identity once he was in booking.  He had a $100K traffic warrant out of Nevada County and a $50K theft warrant out of Pleasanton.  The man was cited for the new charges, then taken to the Martinez Detention Facility for the warrants.

At 7:39 PM, an officer was flagged down in the parking lot regarding a stolen license plate.  The vehicle had been parked in the parking lot for about five hours and the victim believed the theft occurred there.
Officers were dispatched to a shoplift and the suspect was contacted an admitted to the theft.  He had an outstanding warrant out of San Francisco and was booked into MDF.

At 1:30 AM, an officer was dispatch to a possible shooting that occurred two hours before.  The victim said he was confronted by an acquaintance in regards to a debt. When he did not pay, she began throwing rocks at his apartment window causing it to shatter. The victim did not have any injuries and there was no evidence that a shooting had occurred.
At 8:43 PM, an officer reports two suspects entered the business and stole merchandise ($560).
An unknown suspect shattered the rear passenger window to the victims white Lexus sedan.  The suspect stole the victim’s purse and lunchbox.  The incident occurred between 1:30pm to 3:20pm.

On January 5TH, the victim called an “800” number she found on the internet to help fix issues with her IPhone.  The suspect convinced the victim into opening a Zelle account and gained remote access to her cellphone.  On January 6th, $989.90 was fraudulently transferred from the victims Zelle account to two unknown accounts.

At approximately 2:45pm, the suspect entered the store, stole a pair of shoes and fled on foot towards BART.  The suspect was described as a black male adult, 50 years old, bald, wearing all black clothing, brown boots and carrying a black backpack.

At 2327 hours, officers were dispatched to a glass break alarm at the business.  Upon arrival, a window on the east side of the building had been shattered by a large rock.  The front counter had been ransacked and there was blood on the lobby floor.  Officers checked the rear of the business and observed a male subject walking in the area.  The male had blood on his clothing and facial lacerations.  The male subject was detained in handcuffs and medical was requested.  Officers conducted an interior search of the business which met with negative results.  Video surveillance was reviewed and showed the male subject breaking into the business. The male subject also had a no bail warrant out of San Francisco for burglary.  The subject was later transported to county jail.

At approximately 1:07pm, the victim came to the police department to report that they never received their new license plates from DMV, for their 2022 Mercedes. According to their records, DMV advised that the license plates were delivered.
At approximately 10:29am a suspect entered the store and stole $111.00 worth of laundry detergent.

Location: Jack in the Box 5920 Cutting Blvd.
Description: At approximately 11:06am, the victim tracked her cell phone, which was in her stolen Honda CRV, in the area of San Pablo Ave/Cutting Blvd.  The victim reported her vehicle stolen today to Suisun City PD.  The vehicle was located parked unoccupied in a parking stall.  The vehicle was impounded and will be released back to the victim.

At approximately 11:36am, a person was contacted for loitering near a residence.  Officers learned that the person had a Parole Violation warrant. The person initially provided his twin brother’s first and middle name, however, his true identity was revealed.  The person was arrested,  booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At approximately 10:08am, a 31 year old male came to the police department to retrieve his belongs from a prior El Cerrito PD arrest.  Officer’s learned he had a warrant for his arrest.  The male was booked at ECPD and released on a citation.

At 1847 hours, Officers was dispatched to a robbery that just occurred.  The suspect stole items and threatened to assault the employee.  The suspect was last seen walking towards the creek.  Officers circulated the area and located a subject matching the description as Masonic Ave / Portland Ave in Albany walking on the BART path.  The suspect was positively identified during an in-field show-up and placed under citizen’s arrest.  All stolen property was recovered and returned to the business.  The suspect was later released on a citation.

At approximately 4:37pm officers were dispatched to the store for an unwanted female subject.  Officers contacted the female, who stated a loss prevention employee pushed her.  The female did not have any visible injuries and declined medical treatment.  The loss prevention employees denied any physical altercation, and stated the female refused to leave after attempting to bring numerous items of clothing into a dressing room without checking them in per store policy.
At 9:38pm the victim received a Kohl’s credit card in the mail, with her name embossed on it.  The victim never applied for the card, learned no transactions were made and cancelled it.
At 2:28 PM, an unidentified suspect smashed the front window of the victim’s vehicle and stole her purse from the front seat.

An unidentified suspect stole packages worth approximately $74 from a residential porch at 3:00 PM. The victim saw the theft on camera, then searched the neighborhood and located his property down the block.  All items were recovered.

At 6:18 PM, officer reports employees advised the uniform security guard of a chronic shoplifter. The guard contacted the suspect and asked him to leave the store. While walking towards the front of the store, the suspect challenged the guard to a fight and started punching him. The guard defended himself by deploying mace and his Taser. The suspect fled the business after leaving clothing and his wallet containing identifying information.

At 12:14 PM, two unidentified suspects entered Ifshin while masked and hooded to conceal their identities.  The men walked straight to the vault, and each selected two violins, then walked out of the store with the violins.  Total value of the stolen violins was estimated at $16,000.
The catalytic converter was stolen from a 2015 Prius between 1/12/2023 – 4:00 PM and 1/13/2023 at 10:00 AM.
A 2014 burgundy Kia Optima was stolen from the resident parking lot overnight between 6:00 PM and 6:30 AM.

The victim was unable to locate his black Taurus 9mm handgun after he moved to El Cerrito from San Antonio.  The victim believed one of the movers stole the gun but had no evidence to support his thesis.  Entered in Automated Firearms System.

At 8:20 AM, the victim heard his car alarm sound.  Upon checking his video, he discovered footage of a person apparently attempting to break in to his vehicle.  No apparent entry or theft.  A screwdriver was left behind and collected as evidence.
A white 2008 Honda CBR600 motorcycle was stolen from the driveway between 11:00 PM on 1/13/2023 and 8:00 AM on 1/14/2023.

CrimeType: Stolen Vehicle and Hit and Run
Description: At approximately 6:44 am, two suspects stole the victim’s vehicle, by pushing it down the hill towards Knott Ave. The victim’s husband received a motion alert on their video surveillance camera, went outside and scared the suspects off.  The suspects got into a dark colored sedan and fled eastbound on Knott Ave.  As the suspects fled, they pushed the victim’s vehicle, into a parked GMC SUV, causing minor damage to both vehicles.

An unknown suspect opened the unlocked door to the victim’s vehicle and stole a medical work bag containing patient medical records. The victim checked their video surveillance footage and noticed that the incident occurred on January 15th at approximately 5:30 am.

Location: El Dorado Ave at San Pablo Ave.
Description: An unknown suspect stole the victim’s catalytic converter from their grey Toyota Prius. The incident occurred on 1/15/23, between 5:00 pm to 1/16/23 at 11:00 am.

On January 16th, at 0318 hours, officers attempted to conduct a traffic stop on a vehicle for no front license plate.  The DMV hit came back with one lost or stolen plate.  The vehicle was a white 2005 Infinity 4-door sedan.  The vehicle failed to yield and ran the red light at Eastshore/Potrero as it entered onto I-80 WB at a high rate of speed.  The Infinity had lost control, spun out and the rear of the vehicle collided into a tree as it entered onto the I-80 westbound onramp.  Two suspects fled on foot from the vehicle along the sound wall.  Both suspects jumped over the sound wall onto S. 55th St near Creely Ave and eluded capture.
At approximately 5:00 am, an unknown suspect shattered the rear sliding glass door to the victim’s residence, with an unknown object.
At approximately 6:23 am, the victim heard sawing noises and saw a light under her parked Toyota Prius.  The victim checked her vehicle and saw that the catalytic converter was stolen.
At 9:07 am an unknown suspect stole the victim’s mail from their mailbox while they were out of town on vacation.
The victim never received the license plates in the mail for their vehicle which they bought in late 2022.  They believed they may have been stolen in the mail.

At 2042 hours, officers were investigating an unrelated call at the business when they were flagged down by loss prevention regarding a theft that an hour prior.  The suspect entered the store and selected several personal body accessories and left without paying.  The suspect fled in a red sedan NFD.  Total loss was $686.
An unknown suspect stole the rear license plate to the victim’s vehicle.  The theft occurred from January 16th at 8:00 am to January 17th at 12:05 pm.

At approximately 9:42 am, the suspect returned a game console controller.  After his money was reimbursed on his credit card, the suspect became upset, argued with the employee and left the store with the game console controller.
At 12:07 pm, an officer reports two unidentified suspects attempted to carjack the victim at the gas pumps.  The victim fought back, with help from a witness, and the suspects fled the scene.

At 0318 hours, Officers were dispatched to the 3300 block of Santa Clara Ave.  The victim was woken by the sound of sawing noises.  When the victim went outside to check, he was met by a suspect holding a gold handgun.  The second suspect was underneath his Toyota Prius cutting the catalytic converter.  The victim went back inside his residence and called 911.  The suspects fled in an older model gold Honda Accord 4-door or similar northbound on Santa Clara Ave.  Total loss, $1,000.00.
At 2044 hours, officers were dispatched to a subject who entered the business and selected several clothing items and fled the store without paying towards BART.  Total loss, $40.00
An officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s grey Mercedes C300 this morning between the hours of 2:15 am and 8:10 am.
At 3:30 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 2002 Cadillac Escalade. The theft occurred at 0300 hours, and was captured on video.
At 3:14 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s catalytic converter from the victim’s Toyota Prius. The theft occurred sometime between 1/18@1600 hours and 1/19@1200 hours.
At 1:19 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the rear license plate from the victim’s vehicle.  The theft occurred in between 0800-1600 hours on 1/18/2023.

At 10:25 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect entered the store on two occasions, 1/3/2023 and 1/6/2023, and presented receipts for online orders of guitars.  On both occasions, the suspect took possession of the guitars.  On 1/17/2023, the store received a notification the credit card transactions for both purchases had been reversed by the credit card company as fraudulent.  The store was left without the money or the guitars.  Total loss was approximately $1800 between the two transactions.
At 4:50 AM, an officer reports three suspects stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s Toyota Prius.

At 1:07 PM, an unidentified suspect attempted to run the victim down with her car after a road rage incident that was repeatedly escalated by both involved parties.  Suspect vehicle was described as a maroon Nissan Altima driven by a black female, last seen toward San Pablo Ave.
At 1:47 PM, an officer took a telephone report of identity theft after the victim was unable to make an online report.

At 4:31 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect entered the residence by removing the window screen and opening an unlocked window sometime between 8:00 PM on 1/17/2023 and time of call today.  The only known take was $200 cash.
At 3:03 PM, an officer recovered a 2020 Vespa scooter reported stolen to Albany PD on 1/19/2023.  Released to owner at scene.  Removed from SVS.
At 12:39 PM, an officer took a lobby report for theft of packages totaling $145 from the residence, at 0220 hours on 1/19/2023.  The theft was captured on video.

At 9:50 PM, an officer reports the victim heard sawing noises coming from in front of his house. The victim went outside and noticed a subject standing next to his vehicle. The subject fled the area after seeing the victim. The victim checked his vehicle and discovered there was damage to the exhaust pipe near the catalytic converter.

Location: San Pablo Ave. and Manila Ave.
Description: At 2:24 AM, an officer noticed two subjects in a verbal dispute. Officers contacted both subjects and discovered there was a dispute over marijuana. During a records check, one of the subjects was found to have an active no bail warrant out of Alameda County Warrant. The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:22 PM, an officer reports a black 2020 Nissan Maxima rental car was stolen between 11:45 PM on 1/19/2023 and 7:00 AM on 1/20/2023.

At 3:55 AM, El Cerrito Fire Department requested police assistance after they were dispatch to a fire in a locked bathroom. An unknown suspect lit several pieces of paper on fire and left them in the sink and trashcan. There was no damage.
At 8:24 AM, an officer reports a white Ford box truck was once again stolen from the street sometime overnight.
At 12:50 PM, an officer arrested on a CDCR parole warrant during a pedestrian stop.  Booked at ECPD then to county jail.

At 11:12 PM, officers were dispatch to a residence for a disturbance detail. During the investigation, officers discovered one of the involved subjects had an active warrant out of Alameda County. The subject was transported to the El Cerrito PD for booking and issued a citation and released.

At 6:12 PM, an officer reports a Lyft driver picked up two passengers in Richmond who wanted to go to Del Norte BART. While driving the passengers requested to be driven to Oakland. When the victim advised them he was not allowed to drive them to Oakland, one of the passengers started punching the victim in the face with her fists. Upon arrival at the Del Norte BART, both passengers exited the vehicle and walked southbound towards Hill St.
An unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle last night, rummaged through the interior, but did not steal anything.
At 8:21pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect left the store with a basket full of unpaid merchandise ($250).

On January 22nd, at approximately 9:50am, a suspect kicked the front glass door to the business and shattered it.   The store had video surveillance camera footage and the investigating officer recognized the suspect from previous police contacts.
An unknown suspect spray painted graffiti on the north wall of business.

In the early morning hours, the suspect robbed the victim, while in the Sketchers parking lot.  The suspect cut the victim in the head, several times with a box cutter.  The suspect stole the victims bicycle and three bags containing the victim’s personal belongings. The victim was transported to the hospital for his injuries.

On Sunday January 22nd, at approximately 3:15pm, the victim went to the suspect’s residence to watch football.  After drinking some alcohol with the suspect, the victim remembers waking up at 5 hours later.  The victim left the residence that evening, but the next day, complained of vaginal pain. The victim went to the hospital to be examined.

At 9:21pm, officers were dispatch to an unwanted subject. Upon arrival, officers contacted the subject and conducted a warrants check. The subject was found to have an active Alameda County Warrant.  After booking, the subject was transported to the MDF.
At 8:59pm, an officer reports two suspects entered Ross and loaded a Ross shopping bag with $400 worth of merchandise. The subjects left the store without paying.

At approximately 1:22pm, officers arrest the suspect of after she entered her ex-partners residence attempting to retrieve her mail.  The victim has an active restraining order against the suspect.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 1:10am, an officer reports the victim heard sawing noises coming from the front of her house. When she went to investigate, she heard two subjects speaking to each other and standing next to her Prius. The victim yelled out to them to scare them off. The victim checked her vehicle and discovered the catalytic converter ($2500) had been stolen. The incident occurred at 0050 hours
The victims white 2014 Kia Forte was stolen some time from midnight to 3:30am.
At approximately 8:41am, a homeowner confronted a suspect who was loitering in his backyard. The suspect towards the Ohlone Greenway prior to police arrival.
The victims silver 2021 Honda Pilot was stolen sometime from 1/24 at 3pm to 1/25 at 9:45am.

At 8:09pm, officers were dispatch to Ross for two unwanted subjects that have stolen in the past. Upon arrival, store employees pointed out a subject who had just exited the store with unpaid merchandise ($60). Officers detained the subject and employees placed him under Citizen’s Arrest. The subject was issued a citation and released from the scene.

At 7:41am, an officer took a report of a theft at Ross. An hour later, the officer was patrolling in the area of Ross when he noticed a subject matching the description of the suspect from the theft. The officer contacted the subject and confirmed he was the same person from the theft earlier. During a records check, the officer discovered the subject is currently on probation out of Alameda County for possession of a firearm. The subject was issued a citation and released from the scene. Strong Armed Robbery – Wells Fargo Bank (parking lot) At approximately 1:46pm, while the victim was seated in her parked vehicle the suspect shattered her passenger side window and stole a backpack from the front seat.  The second victim was in the parking lot walking towards the front doors, when the same suspect approached her and ripped a tote bag off her shoulder.
At approximately 9:14am, a suspect shattered a window to a parked US Postal truck and stole mail.  The suspect fled in a white BMW four door, northbound on Everett St.

Location: Yosemite Ave and San Diego St.
Description: At 0807hrs, an officer recovered an unoccupied Ford box truck that was reported stolen to ECPD on 1/23/23.  The truck was released to the owner at the scene.
At approximately 12:10pm an unknown suspect set a portion of the victim’s backyard play structure on fire.
At 9:51 PM, an officer reports two unknown suspects stole the victim’s front license plate from their vehicle.
At 8:49 PM, an officer reports two unknown suspects worked together to steal merchandise ($600).
On January 9th to January 10th, while at school, the victim’s laptop was stolen.
The victim’s catalytic converter was stolen on Saturday evening.
While the victim was seated in her vehicle at a gas pump, an unknown suspect walked up to her car window and exposed his genitals.

At approximately 8:31am the victim was sitting in his parked vehicle when he was approached by the six suspects.  Three of the suspect punched then pulled the victim out of his car.  All of the suspects got into his vehicle and drove northbound on San Pablo Ave.

At approximately 3:43pm, an unknown suspect shattered an exterior window to the residence with a rock.  The residence backs up to Summit K2, and the victim believes a student from the school threw the rock.
At approximately 12:03pm, a US post office truck was broken into and mail was stolen.
At 9:09 pm, an officer reports an unknown suspect exited the store with unpaid lotion/ cosmetics ($150).

At approximately 1:53pm, two suspects stole miscellaneous clothing and walked towards the front doors of the store.  One of the suspects brandished a black handgun that was in his waistband, at Loss Prevention employees who attempted to stop them.
On January 17th, the victim’s Toyota Prius’ catalytic converter was stolen.
At approximately 12:38pm the suspect broken into the victims vehicle and stole miscellaneous items.
At 1:40 PM, an unidentified suspect stole seven pairs of shoes worth $200.
At 11:06 AM, an unidentified suspect kicked and broke the door to the ATM lobby, causing damage estimated at $1000
At 2:55 PM, Officer Randhawa reports an unidentified suspect reached in through the open window of the victim’s vehicle and stole a $40 backpack containing immigration documents.

At 6:28 PM, an officer reports unknown suspect(s) shattered the rear sliding glass door of the victim’s residence to gain entry and conducted a ransack of the house. The victim discovered $400 was missing from the master bedroom.

At 2:50 AM, an officer reports the victim was in his upstairs bedroom when he heard sawing noises coming from outside of his residence. The victim went downstairs and looked out the window and saw the suspect near his vehicle. The suspect noticed the victim and ran to his vehicle with the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle

At 12:20 PM, a suspect entered the Marshall’s store to shoplift.  When a Marshall’s LP Agent took the property back from the suspect inside the store and told him to leave, the suspect punched her on her shoulder with a closed fist.  He was escorted out of the store by other LP Agents with no known theft.
At 4:55 pm, an unidentified suspect robbed the cashier at Valero at gunpoint.  The suspect was last seen on foot northbound on the San Pablo Avenue.
A coin collection worth $1000 was stolen at an unknown date and time.

CrimeType: Recovered Stolen Vehicle / Weapons Violations / Drug Violations
Description: At 10:41 AM, officers were dispatched to investigate a suspicious vehicle backed up to the sheds at the dead end of the El Cerrito Royale parking lot.  A 35 year old Sacramento man and a 42 year old San Leandro woman were contacted in the rear seat of a running Chevrolet Suburban with no plates.  The pair were removed from the vehicle based on plain view drug paraphernalia, and the man admitted to smoking fentanyl.  The vehicle was reported stolen out of San Francisco two days ago.  A search of the vehicle revealed more suspected fentanyl and a Glock “ghost gun” firearm with a high capacity drum magazine and no serial number.  Both subjects were on probation out of Alameda County.
At 1:20 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s wallet containing credits cards from an unlocked locker. The suspect attempted to make a purchase at a gas station in Richmond.
At 9:32 PM, an officer reports a suspect broke into the victim’s locker and removed her property.
At approximately 7:47 PM, an officer reports a suspect entered the business and brandished a firearm at the cashier and demanded cash.
At 10:42 AM, a suspect stole power tools and fled the business.
At 3:33 PM, an unidentified white male repeat offender suspect stole merchandise and left the business.
The victims power tools that were in his garage, and cash, were stolen from January 23rd to February 7th.
At 3:37am, a suspect used a large rock and shattered the northern glass door to the business.

At 4:00am, officers patrolling in the area notice an occupied suspicious vehicle. During the investigation, the officers discovered the vehicle had been stolen out of Richmond. The suspect was taken into custody without incident. After booking, the suspect was transported to the MDF.
At approximately 2:23pm.the suspect kicked the front screen door to the victim’s residence, damaging it.
At 7:54pm, an officer reports the theft of $180 worth of merchandise.
At approximately 11:18am an officer recovered an unoccupied parked Mercedes sedan that was reported stolen to Oakland PD in September 2022.
At approximately 2:04pm. the suspect entered the store and stole miscellaneous clothing items.

The victims white 2010 Audi A5 was stolen last night from 11pm to 12pm this afternoon.  During the investigation, Berkeley PD contacted the victim and advised they recovered the vehicle in their City.
At approximately 2:22pm, the suspect stole a $200.00 vacuum and fled.
At approximately 12:53pm the suspect used an unknown tool and punctured a tire on the victim’s vehicle.
The victim’s catalytic converter was stolen from their vehicle from February 9th at 9:15am to February 9th.
At 7:12pm, an officer took an auto burglary report.

At 2:57 PM, an officer reports a backpack containing clothing the victim just bought from Ross was stolen when he left it at the table while using the restroom. Residential Burglary – 1000 block of Villa Nueva: At 4:07 PM, an officer reports the victim returned home to find the suspect running down his driveway carrying a bag. The suspect entered by forcing the front door and stole items from inside the home.
At 2:53 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole a pair of shoes.

At 7:26 PM, officers were investigating a theft at one of the businesses and discovered the suspects arrived in an unreported stolen vehicle. Both suspects were arrested and transported to the ECPD for booking. After booking, the suspects were released on citations.
At 2:38 PM, an officer reports two unidentified suspects stole menswear, luggage and Michael Kors handbags.  Loss prevention agents recovered some property before the suspects fled with the rest.
At 5:05 AM, an officer reports two unknown suspects splatter paint on the wall of the business.
At 8:40 AM, an officer reports the arrest of a 55 year old Kensington man for DUI after he crashed his car into a parked vehicle.
At 8:58 AM, an officer reports the theft of a catalytic converter at 2:30 AM this morning.

At 7:29 PM, an officer was dispatch to the business for an unrelated matter and was flagged down in regards to a theft. The officer detained the suspect who was in possession of unpaid merchandise. The suspect was issued a citation and released at the scene.
At 7:51pm, an officer reports the theft of liquor.

At 3:59 PM, an officer reports unidentified suspect(s) attempted to steal the victim vehicle by cutting the club off the steering wheel and pushing the vehicle, however they were unsuccessful due to a second club.  Time of occurrence was between 2/11/2023 at 7:00 PM and 2/12/2023 at 8:15 PM.

At approximately 1:14pm, an officer conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle, and arrested the passenger on a warrant out of San Jose PD for Battery. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released on a citation.
