An unknown suspect used a brick to shatter the rear glass door to enter the business. The suspect rummaged through the office, but it is unknown what, if anything was stolen at the time of the report.  The incident occurred from February 11th at 11am to February 13th at 6:20am.
At 2025 hours, one subject entered the business and stole $250 worth of shoes.

At 2201 hours, officers conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle.  The passenger had an outstanding felony warrant from his arrest and was found in possession of 26.6 grams of methamphetamine.  The subject was transported to county jail in Martinez.
At 0348 hours, officers responded to a catalytic converter theft that just occurred.  Suspects fled prior to officers arrival.
At 1910 hours, two subjects entered the business and stole $450 worth of shoes.

At 1730 hours, the suspect entered the store and after getting in an argument with employees, produced pepper spray and threatened to spray the employee while make racial slurs.  The suspect left the store and there were no injuries to employees.
The victim’s license plates were stolen from their vehicle from February 13th midnight to February 14th at 4pm.
The victim’s vehicle was stolen from Feb. 13th at 2:00am to Feb 14th at 8:30am.
The victim’s vehicle was stolen from the rear parking lot of the apartment complex from February 12th at 3pm to February 14th at 7:20am.

An unknown suspect stole the victim’s wallet, clothing and keys to a rented vehicle, from the victims unsecured gym locker.  The victim went outside and found that his vehicle was stolen.  The incident occurred from 6:05am – 7:05am.

At approximately 5:19pm, three suspects attempted to leave the store with unpaid merchandise.  An employee grabbed the items from the suspects as they passed the registers and walked towards the exit doors.  The suspects pushed the employee to the ground and fled.

CrimeType: Possession of a Controlled Substance, Drug Paraphernalia and Probation Violation
Description: At approximately 8:24am an officer contacted as subject to the rear of the business and learned the subject was on searchable probation out of San Francisco. During a search of the subject they were found to be in possession of a suspected fentanyl and drug paraphernalia.  The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released on a citation.
At approximately 2:59pm, while the victim was sitting in his vehicle the suspect pointed a black semiautomatic handgun at the victim.  The suspect then fled.

At 2049 hours, officers were dispatched to an unwanted subject inside the business.  The subject grabbed an employee’s breast and left the store.  The subject later returned to the business and was arrested by officers.  The subject was later transported to county jail.
At approximately 2:42pm the victim had a package stolen from their front porch.

CrimeType: Residential Burglary, Vandalism, Brandishing a Weapon
Description: At 2104 hours, officers responded to a call of a prowler and the suspect was trying to get inside the residence through the front door.  As officers arrived, the suspect started breaking windows to the home.  The suspect was arrested on scene and later transported to county jail.
At approximately 1:20pm, an officer located a white Hyundai Sonata that was reported to El Cerrito PD on February 14th.  The vehicle was towed and stored.
The victim’s motorcycle was stolen from February 14th at 6pm to February 15th at 12pm.
The victim’s vehicle was stolen from February 15th at 11pm. to February 16th at 10:00am.

At approximately 8:42am, a witness saw the suspect enter the residence and notified the police.  When officers arrived, the suspect exit the building, did not comply with officers commands and walked towards the backyard.  The suspect was last seen walking on Hagen Blvd towards Knott Ave.  Officer circulated the area but the suspect escaped.
At 2201 hours, three subjects entered the business and walked behind the counter stealing several boxes of cigars and tobacco products.  The suspects fled.
At approximately 1:13pm, the suspect parked near the gas pumps and began pleasuring himself.
At approximately 2:05pm, the catalytic converter to the victims Toyota Prius was stolen this morning between 5:00am to 5:10am.
An officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s pickup truck. The theft occurred in between 2/17 at 9:30 AM and 2/18 at 3:30 AM.
At 7:29 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect cut the exhaust pipe of the victim vehicle in an apparent attempt to steal the catalytic converter.  Occurred sometime overnight.
At 1:08 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect forced entry to the vacant residence sometime in the past week and stole mail.
An officer reports at 5:30 PM, an unknown suspect stole the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.

At 7:18 PM, an officer responded to a non-injury collision. During the investigation, the officer determined one of the drivers was under the influence of alcohol. The driver was placed under arrest and transported to the El Cerrito PD for booking. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.
At 9:17 PM, an officer reported a suspect brandished a firearm at the clerk and demanded money. The suspect fled the business with cash and merchandise.

At 11:30 AM, an officer reports the suspect entered the store with her face concealed and robbed the cashier at gunpoint.  The wig she was wearing when she arrived was left in the parking lot in her haste to leave, and was collected for DNA evidence. The suspect vehicle turned up at Casino San Pablo later in the day, and at 3:35 PM, San Pablo PD detained a woman who closely matched the suspect description, and appeared to be wearing the wig that was stolen in the robbery.  The woman was booked and transported to the county jail. Carjacked Vehicle (Recovered) – 11700 block of San Pablo Ave: At 1:05 PM, an officer recovered a silver 2000 Toyota Solara reported stolen to the Antioch Police Department on 2/10/2023 in an armed carjacking.  The vehicle was abandoned in the rear parking lot of the apartment complex.
At 4:22 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect used a rock to break a window to gain entry into business and stole property.

An unknown suspect threw a small concrete paver through the front glass door to gain entry into the business.  The suspect rummaged through the store, but nothing was stolen.  The incident occurred from February 20th at 9:15 PM to February 21st at 9:15 AM.
At 2118 hours, an unknown suspect shattered a glass door to the business causing $1000.00 worth of damage.
In the early mornings on February 17th an unknown suspect shattered a small window to the building.
At 12:16 PM a suspect stole a package from the victim’s porch.
At 2203 hours, the suspect entered the business and stole approximately $100 worth of cologne and fled the business on foot.

Location: San Pablo Ave at Central Ave.
Description: At approximately 6:58 AM officers arrested a subject for a warrant out of the Contra Costa Sheriff’s Office.  The male was booked at El Cerrito PD, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
The suspect shattered the victim’s front driver side window, placed the victim’s garden hose in the car, filling the interior with water.  The incident occurred from 4:00 AM to 7:40 AM on the 23rd .

Location: Parking lot of Lucky 1000 El Cerrito Plaza
Description: On Feb 22nd between 12:45 AM and 9:00 AM, the passenger side window to the victim’s vehicle was shattered and property was stolen.

At approximately 10:39 AM officers were dispatched to suspicious vehicle parked in the area.  When officers contacted the driver, they learned the vehicle was reported stolen to the Richmond Police Department.  The driver fled from officers and led them on a vehicle pursuit.  The suspect vehicle was last seen traveling into the City of Richmond, where officers terminated their pursuit.

At 10:38 PM, an officer reports two suspects were asked to leave by employees after they attempted to steal merchandise. As they were exiting the store, one of the suspects knocked over a display of picture frames causing damage. The suspects fled prior to officers’ arrival.

At 6:45 PM, an officer reports unknown suspect(s) attempted to steal the victim’s vehicle. Unable to steal the vehicle, the suspect(s) stole several items. The theft occurred on 2/23/2023, between 12:30 AM and 12:00 PM.

An officer reports, at 9:00 PM, the victim placed his property in one of the lockers in the men’s locker room and secured it. The victim returned to the locker at 11:00 PM, and discovered his lock and property were missing.

At 8:43 PM, employees called and advised the suspects had just stolen merchandise and were trying to enter their locked vehicle parked in the parking lot. Officers responded to the scene and located the vehicle, but the suspects had fled prior to their arrival.  At 9:24 PM, employees called back and advised the suspects had returned to their car. An officer arrived on scene and noticed the suspects driving away. The officer located the vehicle parked in a driveway several blocks away and detained the driver. The passenger fled on foot. The driver was positively identified by store employees as the person responsible for the theft and placed under Citizen’s Arrest. The suspect was transported to the ECPD for booking. After booking, he was released on a citation.

At 3:00 PM Officers were dispatched to the 1300 block of Liberty Street, for the report of trespassing.  A subject had worn out his welcome during a party at an apartment.  The person was under the influence of alcohol and was convinced to leave with a friend.  The victim went for a walk and returned at 4:40 PM. The suspect decided to return to the apartment and took a shower and refused to leave and was taken into custody.  Further investigation revealed the suspect vandalized some property causing over $2000.00 worth of damage. The subject was placed under arrest, booked at El Cerrito PD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

An officer reported, at 9:00 PM, the victim left his wallet and keys in an unsecured locker while he went to work out. At 11:45 PM, the victim returned to the locker and discovered an unknown suspect went into his locker and stole his property. The suspects then stole the victim’s vehicle from the parking lot.

At approximately 9:16am an officer responded to an unwanted subject inside the restaurant, who was yelling at staff, and refusing to leave.  The subject was asked to leave by the officer, but still refused.  The subject was placed under citizens arrest for trespassing.  The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At approximately 1:20pm, the victim’s catalytic converter was stolen from their vehicle.  The three unknown suspects fled in a vehicle.
At 9:54 PM the victim reported he left his cellphone in the bathroom.  When the victim realized he forgot his phone, he returned and the phone had been stolen.
At 9:22 PM ECPD officers took a report of a catalytic converter theft that occurred sometime overnight between 1800 hours on 2/26/23 and 0800 hours on 2/27/23.

The victim’s bicycle was stolen from their patio area from 9pm on the 26th to 7:30am on the 27th. The victim had placed an Apple Air Tag on the bike frame, and located their bike in the City Richmond.

At 11:21 PM officers were dispatched to a possible commercial burglary.  The suspect forced open the locked lobby doors and entered the business.  The suspect refused to leave and was looking for his phone.  The suspect was highly intoxicated and arrested.  There was no property damage to the business and no burglary.  The suspect was transported to MDF.

Location: Hill St. at San Pablo Ave
Description: At approximately 8:29am, officers attempted to stop a vehicle where the driver brandished a knife and attempted to run over citizens in the Home Depot parking lot.  The vehicle fled from officers and was last seen on San Pablo Ave at Cutting Blvd.
At approximately 11:21am, the suspect followed the victim into the store, exposed himself.

At 6:05 PM a suspect entered the store and selected 3 pairs of shoes.  The suspect paid for one pair and attempted to return the other 2.  After being denied the return, the suspect left the store with all 3 pairs of shoes.

CrimeType: Possession of a Firearm on School Campus/Battery on a School Official/Brandishing a Weapon
Location: El Cerrito High School 540 Ashbury Ave.
Description: At approximately 10:37am, officers responded to a report of a student on campus with a gun.  The student struck a staff member before fleeing the campus.  Another student attempted to intervene, but the suspect brandished a black handgun in his waistband, at the other student.  The suspect was arrested by ECPD officers the following day and transported to Juvenile Hall.

CrimeType: Possession of Controlled Substance and Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At approximately 12:47pm, the suspect came into the police department lobby and wanted to self-commit himself for a mental health evaluation.  The subject was found to be in possession of methamphetamines and a pipe used to smoke methamphetamines.  The subject was transported to the hospital by ambulance for a psychiatric evaluation.
At 4:14pm, the victim’s rear windshield to their parked vehicle was shattered with an unknown object.
During a traffic stop, the driver was arrested for two outstanding warrants and transported to MDF.

CrimeType: Possession of a Spring Loaded Knife/ Drunk in Publi
Description: At 8:23 PM, officers received several calls of a subject stumbling around in the area. An officer located the subject passed out on the sidewalk in front of the Post Office. Officers determined the subject was intoxicated and unable to care for himself. The subject was placed under arrest. During a search incident to arrest, a spring loaded knife was found in the subject’s possession. The subject was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:30 PM, an officer reports an employee was in line making a purchase when an unknown suspect began yelling at him and spat on him and then fled the area.

At 7:39 PM, an officer reports the suspect was asked to leave by employees after they suspected her of stealing. While exiting the business, she threatened to spray employees with pepper spray. The suspect was contacted in front of the business and admitted to making the threats because she felt as if the employees were harassing her. Employees did not want to pursue this matter criminally.  The suspect was released from the scene.
At 12:41 PM two suspects entered the store and stole a car battery and then fled the business.
At 9:11 AM the victim called the police department to report that an unknown suspect opened a bank account in the victim’s name and attempted to deposit a fraudulent check into the account.

At 6:16 PM a suspect entered the business and concealed one pair of shoes and fled the store without paying.  The suspect noticed the manager following and turned around and threatened to shoot the manager.
At 4:40 PM the victim placed a packaged cellphone in the FedEx drop box, but FedEx reported the box was empty when they received it.
At 1:32 AM officers conducted a pedestrian stop on a known subject to have a felony warrant.  The subject was arrested and transported to county jail.
At 6:35 PM the suspect attempted to pay with two fake $100 bills.  The purchase was declined and the suspect fled prior to police arrival.
The victims’ package, which contained a Mac Laptop, was stolen from behind the counter.
At 2:08 AM the suspects stole a catalytic converter from the victims Prius and fled the scene.
At 9:21 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered the driver’s door window of the victim’s vehicle. No other damage or loss reported. The incident occurred in between 9:30 AM and 8:30 PM.

At 10:00 AM, a known suspect tore the branches off a neighbor’s freshly planted tree during a mental health episode. The victim did not desire prosecution, due to the suspect’s mental health status, and only “wanted it to stop”.
At 10:46 hours, a suspect attempted to steal seven pairs of shoes. Loss prevention recovered some of the shoes but the suspect was successful in stealing two pairs of kid’s shoes and two bras.

At 9:41 AM, an officer reported the theft of a 2007 Honda CR-V sometime overnight. The vehicle was located unoccupied at the scene of a collision in Richmond, and was impounded around 3:30 AM this morning.
At 6:30 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s van. The theft occurred between 3/9 at 5:00 PM and 3/11 at 5:58 PM.

At 4:33 PM, two suspects attempted to steal the catalytic converter from an employee’s vehicle in the parking lot. The theft was interrupted and the suspects fled empty handed in a vehicle that was already stacked full with catalytic converters.
At 4:59 PM, two suspects stole merchandise from Ross and fled towards BART.
At 10:47 AM, an unidentified suspect stole merchandise from the store and fled.
At 10:57 PM, an officer reports a suspect broke into the victim’s secured locker and stole the victim’s property. The theft occurred in between 9:30 PM and-10:53 PM.
On March 10th from 7:30am to 1:30pm, victim’s catalytic converter was stolen from their vehicle.
At 5:11 PM the victims’ vehicle was stolen from the driveway.  The vehicle was recovered later that evening in San Pablo.
At 7:13 PM the suspect entered the store and stole food and fled the store.

At 6:54 PM an officer reports the victim left his car keys in the door lock of his pickup parked in front of the residence. Around 5:11 PM, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle. The incident was captured on video surveillance. The vehicle was later located by San Pablo PD in their city unoccupied.
At 7:13 PM an officer reports the suspect exited the business without paying for merchandise.

Location: Parking lot of 10650 San Pablo Ave
Description: At 9:21 PM an officer reports the victim was seated inside his vehicle with the windows rolled down when an unknown suspect threw a liquid substance hitting the vehicle and the victim.  No injuries reported.

At 9:16 PM an officer reports the suspect entered the store and selected several items.  As the suspect exited the store with the stolen merchandise, they were detained by officers.  The suspect also had an outstanding felony arrest warrant from Marin County for burglary.  The suspect was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 7:04 PM an officer reports the suspect entered the store and selected merchandise and walked towards the front of the store.  When employees attempted to stop the suspect, the suspect pushed past the employees and a struggle ensued over the stolen merchandise.  The employees were able to recover the property and the suspect fled the scene.
The victim left their keys in their parked vehicle from 3:30 PM to 3:40 PM and when they returned their vehicle had been stolen.

CrimeType: Arrest for Attempted Burglary and Warrants
Description: At 8:13 AM the suspect kept banging on the victims door and removed window screens to several other residences in the area.  Officers were unable to locate the suspect at this time.  At 9:39 AM the same suspect attempted to open the front door to a residence in the 5600 block of Central Ave.  Officers located the suspect and learned he had three warrants for his arrest.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An unknown suspect shattered a window to the victim’s vehicle and damaged the ignition.  The incident occurred on March 13th at 4:00 PM to March 14th at 5:00 PM.

At approximately 4:45 PM a subject was arrested after he was found sleeping in a resident’s vehicle that was parked in the carport.  The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At approximately 5:32 PM officers responded to a fight involving 4-5 individuals.  The victim attempted to intervene and was punched in the face by a suspect.  The subjects involved in the fight fled in their vehicles prior to police arrival.
At approximately 3:57 PM the suspect entered the store and stole merchandise and fled the business.

At 4:39 AM an officer located the victim’s 2000 Lincoln Continental that had been reported stolen to the Oakland PD on 3/14/2023. Officers conducted an enforcement stop and arrested the driver.  The driver was booked at ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 8:33 PM an officer reports two suspects entered the store and began loading duffle bags with merchandise. Loss Prevention agents contacted the suspects and asked them to pay for the merchandise. The suspects walked up to the cash register line as if they were going to pay. Moments later both suspects walked towards the front door and attempted to exit when contacted by security, one of the suspects brandished a Taser. Both suspects fled the store with stolen merchandise.

At 9:41 PM, officers were dispatched to a suspicious person detail. The caller reported several subjects were going through their garbage bins. Officers responded to the residence and located a broken window to a home that was under remodel. Officers located and detained a suspect that was inside the house. The homeowner was contacted and placed the suspect under Citizen’s Arrest. After booking, the suspect was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:18 AM, an officer reports unidentified suspects jacked up a Toyota Prius and partially cut the catalytic converter in an apparent attempt to steal it.  The suspects were scared off by an alarm and left empty-handed.  Surveillance video footage existed that showed the time of occurrence as 5:23 AM.

At 8:16 PM, an officer reports the victim was sitting at the bus stop when the suspect thought the victim had taken something from their bag. The suspect punched the victim several times in the face with their fists. Several witnesses began yelling at the suspect and they fled into the El Cerrito Plaza.
At 8:34 AM, an officer reports theft of license plates from a 2007 Mitsubishi sometime between 10:45 PM on 3/16/2023 and 8:30 AM on 3/17/2023.

At 10:51 AM, while investigating the above vehicle tampering, the officer was flagged down by a neighbor who reported an unidentified suspect shattered the right rear window of his parked Audi at 5:24 AM this morning.  There was surveillance video footage of the incident.

At 6:20 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unsecured vehicle and caused damage to the ignition, but was unable to start the vehicle. The suspect then stole the radio. The incident occurred in between 1:00 PM and 5:00 PM on March 17th.

At 9:52 PM, an officer conducted a security check at Ross when he was flagged down by an employee in regards to a theft. The officer detained the suspect without incident and the suspect was placed under Citizen’s Arrest by the employee. The suspect was issued a citation and released at the scene.
At 12:44 PM, a repeat offender known to store employees selected merchandise and fled the store without paying for it.
At 9:03 PM, an officer was dispatched to an unwanted subject known to shoplift. Upon arrival the officer’s arrival, the suspect had fled the store with unpaid merchandise.
At 12:32 PM, a repeat offender known to store employees stole merchandise and fled the store.
At 11:22 AM, an unidentified suspect stole two pairs of shoes.  When asked if he was going to pay he fled the business on foot toward the BART station.

At 8:11 PM, officers were dispatched to the business for an unwanted subject. An employee advised the male suspect walked into the women’s restroom and was smoking narcotics in the sauna. Officers located the suspect inside the restroom and detained him.  During a records check, officers discovered the suspect was currently on active probation and conducted a probation search. The suspect was found to be in possession of a glass pipe commonly used for smoking narcotics. The suspect was issued a citation and released from the scene.

At 11:12 PM, an officer was dispatched to the business for an unwanted subject. The subject was contacted and detained.  During a records check officers discovered the subject had an active warrant out of Solano County. During a search incident to arrest the subject was found to be in possession of suspected methamphetamine. After booking, the subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:35 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect broke in to a locker in the women’s locker room, removed a purse and stole car keys.  The purse was later located stuffed in another locker.  The car was rifled through but no known loss.  Occurred between 3:30-5:30 PM on 3/17.

Location: Parking lot of 11450 San Pablo Ave
Description: At 7:43 PM, an officer checked the license plate of a vehicle and discovered one of the plates had been reported stolen. The officer conducted an enforcement stop on the vehicle and discovered the driver had an outstanding warrant for their arrest.  The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito PD and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:24 AM, an officer reports the catalytic converter was stolen from a Toyota Prius between 3/17/2023 at 9:00 PM and 3/18/2023 at 9:00 AM.

At 10:27 AM, two unidentified suspects entered an enclosed sun room at the rear of the residence, then shattered the sliding glass door that led in to the residence.  The occupants of the residence went to check on the noise made by the breaking glass and scared the intruders off.
At 10:57 AM, an officer reports the victim’s claims that an unidentified suspect had been breaking in to their garage / storage unit for over a year now.
At 12:24 PM, two unidentified suspects left the store, each carrying a backpack full of unpaid cosmetics worth an estimated $1000.
At 12:45 PM, the suspect stole (3) pairs of shoes and a backpack. The suspect fled towards the Del Norte BART station.

At 11:39 AM an officer reports that last week an unknown suspect called the victims and identified themselves as an Apple employee.  The suspect told the victim that they were in trouble with the FBI for visiting illegal websites.  The victim was told to make wire transfers to four different accounts to exonerate them from being prosecuted.
From March 20th at 9:00 PM to March 21st at 1:00 PM, the victim’s license plates were stolen from their vehicle.
At 9:58 PM, the victim reported getting into a Lyft at 12:45 PM on 3/21/23 and got sprayed with Lysol.

At 11:46 PM, the owner arrived home to find a vehicle parked in their carport of the apartment complex.  When asked to move, the driver drove away in the vehicle and crashed into a parked vehicle.  The driver was highly intoxicated and arrested for drunk driving.  The driver gave a blood sample and was released on a citation to a sober friend.

CrimeType: Stolen Vehicle / Possession of Stolen Vehicle
Description: At 4:00 AM, officers located a parked stolen vehicle.  The driver was asleep in the driver’s seat.  The driver was arrested and transported to county jail.

CrimeType: Assault with Deadly Weapon / Peeping / Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 9:01 PM, officers observed two subjects fighting on the sidewalk.  One of the subjects walked away after from the scene and was detained down the street.  It was later determined the suspect was peeping through the window of a motel room and was chased off.  When the suspect was confronted on the sidewalk, the suspect hit the victim in the face with a glass candle.  Both, the suspect and victim were transported to the hospital.  After being medically cleared, the suspect was transported to county jail.
At 5:18 AM, officers responded to a catalytic converter theft in progress.  The victim yelled at the suspects who then fled the scene in a vehicle.  Total loss, $1,500.
The victim’s vehicle was stolen on March 23rd from 12:30 AM to 8:00 AM.

CrimeType: Burglary / Unauthorized Use of Access Cards
Description: Between the hours of 11:05 PM – 11:30 PM, an unknown suspect broken into a locker and stole a bag containing personal items.  After fleeing the scene, at 11:37 PM, the victim’s debit card was used at Nations in El Cerrito.

At 2:04 PM the suspect was placed under citizen’s arrest after they threw food at an employee, after being told to leave for bothering customers. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released on a citation.
At 8:34 AM, an officer responded to a lobby detail regarding the theft of a backpack from atop a shopping cart while the victim was shopping at Harbor Freight.  Occurred at 6:30 PM on 3/16/2023.
At 7:08 AM, an officer reports the front door glass at Donut Time was shattered sometime overnight.  Mountain Mike’s Pizza next door also sustained similar damage.  No apparent entry.
At 9:20 AM, an officer reports the theft of a Trek mountain bike worth $2000, between 8:40-9:00 AM.
At 11:13 AM, an officer reports the theft of a white 2007 Jaguar during the previous ten minutes.

At 4:50 AM, an officer was dispatched to an abandoned auto detail. During the investigation, the officer discovered the vehicle was reported stolen to the San Francisco PD on 9/22/2022. The vehicle was recovered and the victim was advised.

At 5:25 PM, promptly upon being released on the above citation, the suspect walked to the San Pablo Avenue side of the El Cerrito Police Department and began breaking the windows.  She broke twelve windows with an estimated value of $5000.  She was re-arrested and directly transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 2:30 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect entered CVS and attempted to shoplift merchandise.  She was confronted by loss prevention, who dumped the items out of her bag.  A few minutes later, the suspect’s cousin entered the store looking for the LP agent.  She confronted him and grabbed his hands in an attempt to restrain him.  The LP agent was able to break her grip and the suspects left the store empty handed.

At 4:10 PM, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a woman who refused to leave the AT&T store when ordered by store staff.  The suspect was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then issued a citation and released.

At 9:24PM, the victim parked the vehicle in front of Walgreens, leaving it running but locked.  The victim walked into the store possessing the key fob.  The suspect smashed the front driver side window and stole the victims’ vehicle.

At approximately 4:07PM the first suspect stole miscellaneous clothing and exited the store.  As employees were at the door, the second suspect exited through the doors and pushed an employee from behind.
The victim’s electric scooter was stolen from their front yard on 3/26 from 9PM – 10PM.
The the victims catalytic converter was stolen off their vehicle from 3/26 at 10PM to 3/27 at 1PM.

At 1:44PM., officer’s responded to the 5300 block of Cypress Ave, for a suspicious person walking in the backyard of a residence. Officer’s contacted the suspect and learned he had a warrant for his arrest issued by the California Department of Corrections.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 8:24PM, officers were flagged down regarding a shoplifting that just occurred.  The suspect was detained and arrested for shoplifting after the business placed the subject under citizen’s arrest.  The suspect was issued a citation and released at the scene.
At 11:10PM, the suspect smashed the driver side window of the victims’ vehicle and stole property.  Occurred between 10:30PM-11:10PM

At 3:21PM the suspect entered the store, damaged a refrigerator glass door before being asked to leave by security.  The suspect threw food items at the security guard and manager as he exited the store towards San Pablo Ave.
The victim’s license plates were stolen off their vehicle from March 27th at 4:07PM to March 28th at 8:00AM.
A suspect cut the lock off the victims purple bicycle and stole the bike.  The theft occurred from March 27th at 8:00PM to March 28th at 1:00PM.

At 1:16AM, officers conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver on a felony warrant from Napa County.  The driver was also in possession of burglary tools.  Cited on the burglary tools and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility on the warrant.
At 9:26AM an officer recovered a reported stolen vehicle.  The vehicle was released to the owner at the scene.
At 1:37PM the suspect broke into a parked vehicle and stole miscellaneous items.
At 11:53AM a former tenant had their package delivered to their old address.  The current tenant forged their signature and kept the package.
The victim’s vehicle was stolen from March 27th at 10PM to March 28th at 7:45AM.
At 6:02PM, the victim walked out to her parked vehicle and found a subject inside.  The victim yelled at the suspect who exited the vehicle with the victim’s backpack and fled the area.

CrimeType: Stolen Vehicle / Probation Violation / Drug Possession / Fraud / Possession of Stolen Property / Warrant Arrest
Description: At 1:31AM, ECPD officers conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle with stolen license plates.  The vehicle was also reported stolen.  The driver and passenger were arrested and both transported to county jail.

At 3:01AM, ECPD officers conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle with no license plates.  A check of the VIN revealed the vehicle was reported stolen.  The driver was arrested and transported to county jail.
At 11:17AM an unknown suspect started a small brush fire in a homeless encampment on the northwest side of the building.  The fire was extinguished prior to police officers arriving on scene.
At 9:43PM, an unknown suspect shot a “BB” gun at the window of the business causing it to shatter.
At 1:44 hours, an officer reports an unidentified black female suspect stole merchandise from the store and fled.
At 9:04 AM, an officer reports the theft of a black Kia Sorrento sometime between 5:00 PM on 3/30 and 9:04 AM on 3/31.
At 1:31 PM, an officer reports the theft of a red 2020 Hyundai Elantra sometime between 5:00 PM on 3/30 and 1:30 PM on 3/31.
At 11:16 PM, an officer reports the victim looked out the window and noticed two subjects standing in her backyard next to her window. The suspects fled prior to officers arrival.
At 11:48 AM an unidentified suspect was captured on video reaching in to the victim’s mailbox and stealing mail.
At 9:29 AM, an unidentified suspect stole a power inverter worth $120 while an accomplice distracted store employees.

At 10:11 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole $130 worth of jeans and fled without paying.  On the way out the door, he shoved a loss prevention agent out of the way to make his escape.

At 8:10 PM, officers were dispatch to O’Reilly’s for a stolen vehicle that just occurred. Officers located the vehicle unoccupied. During the investigation, officers determined the person attempting to report his vehicle stolen appeared to be intoxicated.  The vehicle was found in a parking lot nearby parked on the curb.  It quickly became obvious that the owner of the vehicle had been operating the vehicle under the influence of alcohol and forgot where he parked it.  Since none of the officers observed the subject driving and there were no witnesses that saw him driving, he was placed under arrest for public intoxication, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Strong Armed Robbery/ Conspiracy/ Warrant Arrest
Description: At 11:22AM, officers were dispatched to a strong armed robbery that just occurred inside the store. The first suspect stole items from the store and when confronted by store security, the suspect punched him in the face.  When the security guard attempted to chase after him, a second suspect physically blocked the security guards path. Both suspects were arrested at the Plaza BART station, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:28PM, the suspect entered the store and started filling a suitcase with items.  The suspect was confronted by loss prevention and escorted out the store.  As the suspect was being escorted out the store, the suspect pushed the loss prevention agent and fled the scene prior to police arrival.
At 12:53AM, an officer made a traffic stop on a vehicle and arrested the driver for two outstanding warrants out of Solano County.  The driver was issued a citation and released at the scene.

At 9:14PM, the victim reported the theft of a catalytic converter and the vandalism to a second vehicle in an attempt to steal the catalytic converter.  This took place between 4/3/23 at 8:30PM and 4/4/23 at 6:50AM.

Location: San Pablo Ave at Fairmount Ave.
Description: At 3:05PM the victim was in their vehicle stopped at the intersection when the suspect shattered the rear driver side window and stole their backpack.
At 8:09PM, an officer conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle and arrested the driver for a misdemeanor warrant out of Contra Costa County.  The driver was later transported to county jail.
At 1:26PM a police officer located a reported stolen vehicle traveling southbound on Liberty St towards Potrero Ave.  The vehicle fled from officers and eluded capture.
At 9:39AM while an employee from Safeway was picking up trash, the suspect kicked them and fled on a blue bicycle towards the Del Norte BART.
At 8:19AM the suspect took the victim’s wallet and used one of his credit cards at a gas station in El Cerrito.

At 8:14PM, officers were dispatched to an attempted catalytic converter theft.  The theft was captured on the victim’s security cameras and occurred on 4/6/23 at 2:09AM.  The two suspects attempted to steal the converter but were defeated by an anti-theft device.

At 3:19 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect entered the store at 2:14 PM and passed counterfeit bills to successfully reload a gift card.  The bills were not checked for counterfeit at the time of the sale, and were not discovered until later in the day.

At 1:38 PM, an officer took a report of a theft that occurred about an hour earlier.  The store staff saw a subject leaving with a garbage bag and checked the video, which is when they discovered the theft.  The suspect stole $800 worth of shoes and fled on foot.
At 7:49 AM, an officer reports the theft of a vehicle between 12:00 PM - 4:00 PM on 4/7.  The vehicle was recovered by Berkeley PD shortly after the report was taken.

At 10:24 PM, an officer reports the victim confronted the suspect for starting a small fire in front of the business.  When confronted, the suspect brandished a chisel and threatened to harm the victim.

At 2:03 PM, two suspects approached the victim while he walked on the BART path.  The first suspect put his arm around the victim’s neck from behind while the second suspect emptied the contents of the victim’s pockets onto the ground and stole property.  Both suspects then fled the area.
At 7:49 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect slashed two tires on the victim’s vehicle. The incident occurred on 4/8/2023 between 1:55 PM and 3:00 PM.
At 3:20 AM, an officer reports the suspect was upset with the service. He verbally assaulted an employee and threw a food container at her.
At 7:01 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect was texting and messaging the victim via Facebook nonstop.

At 12:50AM, an officer conducted a traffic stop and arrested the passenger on two outstanding misdemeanor warrants.  The subject was cited on one warrant and transported to county jail on the other warrant. Warrant Arrest – Potrero Ave / I-80 Eastbound Off Ramp. At 4:09AM, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop and arrested the subject on two outstanding warrants.  The subject was transported to county jail on both warrants.
At 12:28PM the suspect entered the store, stole miscellaneous cosmetics products, and fled the store.
At 11:45AM the suspect entered the store, filled a shopping cart full of miscellaneous items and exited the store without paying.
At 8:03PM, the victim arrived home to find a shattered bedroom window.  A roommate was home at 5:15PM and heard a loud bang but thought it was the roommates and did not investigate.  No loss.
At 8:47AM the victim reports that their van was stolen this morning from 4:20AM to 4:30AM.
At 7:37PM, the suspect stole several items and was confronted by store security outside the store.  The business was able to get back the stolen items.  The suspect fled in a vehicle.
At 7:32PM, the suspect stole several items totaling and fled out the emergency exit in an unknown direction.

At 7:50PM, the suspect broke into (3) locked lockers and stole personal items.  The suspect then used the car keys from one of the lockers to gain access into the vehicles vehicle stealing more items.  The suspect then used the stolen debit cards in two other nearby cities in an attempt to make purchases.

At 8:19PM, the victim and suspect got into a verbal argument inside the business.  As the victim walked out of the business he was followed by the suspect.  The suspect engaged the victim in another verbal argument then punched the victim in the face causing a mark.  The victim reported the incident once they arrived home.

At 12:14AM, officers responded to an alarm at the business.  The owner saw the suspect on video surveillance leaving the store with the safe.  Officers located a shattered front window and the suspect fled prior to officer’s arrival.

At 9:02AM the victim followed the suspect vehicle off the freeway into the City of El Cerrito, because of a road rage incident.   The suspect vehicle stopped and the driver pointed a black handgun at the victim.  The victim stopped following the suspect, and the suspect fled in an unknown direction on Central Ave.
At 2:21 PM, an officer took a report for the theft of a catalytic converter between 6:00 PM on 4/12 and 8:00 AM on 4/14.

Shortly after taking the above report, the officer was summoned back to the Mayfair apartments after the victim in the above case was served an eviction notice by the building manager.  The woman tore the notice to bits and threw it in the manager’s face, purportedly causing small abrasions.
At 5:17 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect shattered the window of the victim vehicle about 5 minutes earlier, and stole property from within.

An officer was patrolling in the area when an employee flagged the officer down and advised of a theft that occurred yesterday around 8:37 PM. The employee stated three suspects entered the store and stole $900 worth of shoes.
At 2:15 PM, an officer reports the theft of a silver 2012 Hyundai Sonata four-door between 6:30 PM on 4/11 and 8:30 AM on 4/12.

At 3:10 AM, an officer was dispatched to a suspicious occurrence at the apartment building.  The victim believed someone had entered her apartment without her permission, and she then discovered missing items.

An officer reports an unknown suspect pried the patio door of the victim’s residence to gain entry. The suspect then ransacked the apartment and stole property. The burglary occurred between 9:25 AM and 5:45 PM.

At 10:45 AM, an officer saw a known suspect leaning in to an abandoned vehicle in the CVS lot.  As the suspect meandered in the area, the officer confirmed he was on felony probation with a full search clause, then conducted a pedestrian stop.  A search was conducted, during which assorted burglary tools were located in his backpack, in violation of his probation. The man was arrested and transported directly to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:15 PM, an officer reports the theft of the rear license plate from a vehicle between 4/14 at 6:00 PM and 4/15 at 2:00 PM.

At 10:57 PM, an officer reports the security guard of the business recognized a known shoplifter and asked them to leave. While walking towards the front of the store, the suspect pulled out a small knife and pointed it at the security guard and made threats.

At 8:46 PM, an officer reports a loss prevention agent contacted the suspect who was attempting to leave the business with unpaid merchandise. The agent knocked the merchandise to the ground in an attempt to recover it. The suspect balled his fists and threatened the loss prevention agent. The agent fearing for his safety, backed away and the suspect fled the scene with some of the merchandise.

At 3:05 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect was caught tampering with an employee locker inside the restaurant but was scared off by employees of the restaurant.  The suspect then vandalized the back wall of the restaurant with an ink pen as he left.
At 7:00 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and removed property.

At 6:53 PM  an officer reports the victim received an e-mail advising she had purchased a protection plan from Norton Security. The victim obtained a number for Norton Support via Google and called the number. The male who answered the phone advised the victim in order to reverse the charges she would have to deposit $10,000 into a Bitcoin account. The victim withdrew the money from her bank and deposited it into an ATM at a gas station in Richmond. The victim soon realized she had been scammed.
At 7:00 AM, an officer reports the theft of a burgundy four-door Kia Optima from the west curb of San Pablo Avenue between 6:00 PM on 4/16 and 6:00 hours on 4/17.

At 9:03 PM, an officer reported loss prevention agents noticed a subject enter the business with empty reusable bags. The subject loaded up the bags with merchandise and attempted to leave the business without paying.  When confronted, the subject return some of the merchandise to the agents, but left with several bags of unpaid merchandise.

At 6:10 PM, officers responded to the business for a fight.  During the investigation, officers discovered two subjects got into a physical altercation over a shower in the men’s locker room. Officers obtained statements from the involved parties and the case will be forwarded to the DA’s Office for review.

El Cerrito Police Officers responded to a report of a shooting on Sunday, April 23, 2023, at 4:32 PM. Callers reported multiple shots fired in the 900 block of Galvin Drive in El Cerrito. Officers arrived at the scene and found the victim in the street. Members of the El Cerrito Police Department, the El Cerrito Fire Department, and American Medical Response began providing aid to the victim. Paramedics transported the victim by helicopter to a trauma center for treatment. The victim died from his injuries at the hospital.

At 3:24 AM, an officer reports two unknown suspects attempted to siphon gas from the victim’s vehicle when the suspect’s vehicle caught on fire. The suspect’s vehicle was fully engulfed and the fire caused damage to the victim’s vehicle.
At 8:49 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s scooter. The theft occurred on 4/23/23 between 9:30 AM-08:48 PM.
At 9:20 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect used a BB gun and shot four small windows of the victim’s house causing damage.

An officer reports that in the evening of April 22nd the victim’s vehicle was stolen when the keys were left inside of it.  The officer located the vehicle parked about a block away with the keys missing.  The vehicle was returned to the victim.
At 3:35PM, the bank manager located an ATM skimming device in one of their machines.
At 4:41AM, an officer conducted a traffic stop and arrested the passenger on two felony burglary warrants out of Solano County.  The subject was later transported to county jail.

At 3:57PM a subject walked into the lobby of the police department to pick up his belongings from his previous arrest. The subject inebriated, argumentative and urinated on the lobby floor.  The subject was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 10:05AM, officers responded to a fight between two subjects.  While jogging on Richmond St, the suspect parked his car in front of the victim, got out, punched the victim and kicked him.  The victim suffered minor injuries and was transported to the hospital.
The victim’s rear license plate was stolen off their vehicle from April 24th at 12:00PM to April 25th at 7:00AM.
The victims was stolen from April 24th at 7:00PM to April 25th at 5:00AM.

CrimeType: Possession of Stolen Property / Possession of Burglary Tools / Warrant Arrest
Description: At 3:58AM, an officer conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver for possession of a stolen catalytic converter and burglary tools.  The driver also had a misdemeanor warrant for DUI out of Berkeley.  The subject was later cited and released at ECPD.
The victim’s catalytic converter was stolen from their vehicle from April 24th at 1:00PM to April 25th at 7:20AM.
At 4:38PM, the victim reported that her rear license plate was stolen from her vehicle while she was shopping.
At 9:37AM, an El Cerrito Police Officer recovered the victims reported stolen vehicle.  The vehicle was returned to its owner.

At 1128 hours, an Officer reports an unidentified suspect punched the door locks on both the driver and passenger doors to gain entry to a vehicle.  The suspect destroyed the ignition cylinder and managed to turn the ignition on, but was unable to start the truck. Occurred between 1700 hours on 4/28 and 0900 hours on 4/29.

At 1105 hours, an Officer reports in the early morning hours of 4/29 the 16 year old victim willingly sent sexually explicit photos of himself to a user on the “Wizz” social media platform, who then demanded money to “make the issue go away”.  The victim made several attempts to send money by various means, but was only successful in sending an Apple gift card via his Apple account.

At 3:05 AM, officers were dispatched to the location for a subject who vandalized a vehicle with a metal pole. Responding officers located a subject who matched the description several blocks away and detained him. The subject was positively identified and placed under Citizen’s Arrest. During a records check, officers discovered the subject had an active warrant out of Solano County for his arrest. The subject was transported to the El Cerrito PD for booking and released on a citation.
At 8:30 PM, an officer reports the victim was making a delivery and left his vehicle running. Upon return to his vehicle, the driver discovered it had been stolen.

At 3:22pm after security told the suspect to leave, the suspect tossed items all over the store.  As the suspect exited, they struck a customer in the chest.  The suspect fled the store towards the Plaza BART station.

CrimeType: Trespassing and Fighting on School Grounds
Description: At 12:21pm, a juvenile, who is not a student at the high school, entered the campus and got into a fight with a student. The juvenile was placed under arrest, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released to his guardians.
At 12:02PM the suspect pulled the victim to the ground by her hair and punched her several times on the face and torso.  The suspect was suspended from school.
At 9:19PM unknown suspect cashed two fraudulent checks using the victim’s business accounts.
At 4:51PM the suspect stole several electronic items on display by cutting the security wires off, and fled southbound through the parking lot.

At 11:01PM, the victim was riding his bicycle home when one suspect pushed him off the bicycle and the other suspect pistol whipped the victim.  The victim’s cellphone and wallet were stolen.  Both suspects fled southbound on the BART path.
At 7:43PM, ECPD officers were dispatched to an armed robbery at the ATM.  Two armed suspects robbed the victim at gunpoint.  The suspects then fled in a vehicle northbound San Pablo Ave.
At 12:23PM the suspect stole items from inside the store and fled in a vehicle.
At 10:10 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect attempted to steal the victim’s vehicle by damaging the ignition. The incident occurred in between 10:45 AM and 9:45 PM.
At 11:56 AM, three unidentified suspects stole three large bags of dog food worth a combined total of $351.00
At 6:34 PM, an officer reports the manager confronted two suspects for stealing. One of the suspects gave the merchandise back, while the other left with eight pairs of shoes.

At 2:05 AM, an officer reports a towing company had removed a vehicle from the parking lot at the apartment manager’s request. The tow employee later discovered the vehicle had been reported stolen to the Richmond Police Department on 03/24/23.

At 4:24 AM, officers were dispatched to a possible burglary in progress. Upon arrival, officers located a vehicle backed into the business. As officers approached the vehicle, the suspect exited the business with property and jumped into the vehicle. The suspect fled the scene after colliding into a patrol vehicle.
At 7:06 PM an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s 2019 Kia Optima. The vehicle was located unoccupied by RPD in Richmond.
At 6:37 PM, an officer reports the suspect entered the store and used a pair of pliers to cut the security wires on several items. The suspect fled the store with the items without paying.
At 6:13PM, a subject was arrested for public intoxication after causing a disturbance inside the business.  The subject was arrested and transported to county jail.

CrimeType: Arson / Commercial Burglary / Possession of Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 10:17PM, officers responded to a commercial burglary in progress.  The subject was detained at the backdoor attempting to lite the wooden door on fire.  The subject also used a piece of metal to break apart a wooden door to an exterior electrical closet.  During the subject’s arrest, the subject possessed drug paraphernalia.  The subject was placed under arrest, booked at ECPD, and later transported to county jail.
At 2:00PM the suspect rammed the victim with his shoulder, as they walked past each other.  The victim fell to the ground and sustained bruises and abrasions.
At 1:32AM, an unknown suspect broke into the victim’s locker and stole the keys to the vehicle.  The suspect then stole the victim’s vehicle which was later recovered that morning in San Francisco.
At 10:36AM an officer recovered a vehicle that was reported stolen to San Pablo PD on 5/5/2023. The vehicle was released to the registered owner at the scene.
The victim’s vehicle was stolen between May 7th at 5PM to May 8th at 9AM.
Sometime last night the suspect entered the victim’s vehicle and stole an unknown amount of U.S. coins.
At 4:41PM the suspect stole shoes and fled on foot northbound on San Pablo Ave.
At 11:57AM an unknown suspect used the victim’s debit card and withdrew money from the ATM.
At 9:23AM the suspect pried open a glass case with a screwdriver, stole a speaker and fled.

At 2:39PM the suspect became angry at the manger over an order and threatened to shoot her.  The suspect reached into his jacket as if he had a gun, but no weapon was seen.  As the manager was calling the police, the suspect ran away.
At 2:29PM, an officer located an unoccupied vehicle which was reported stolen on May 3rd to the Richmond Police Department.  The vehicle was towed from the scene.
At 7:20PM, an unknown suspect walked into the gas station, selected merchandise and fled the store on foot without paying.
At 9:04PM, the victim placed his personal items into an unsecured locker and went to workout.  When the victim returned two hours later his items were gone.

At 2:09PM, an officer located an unoccupied suspicious vehicle parked in the parking lot with the engine on.  The vehicle was reported stolen out of Daly City on May 3rd, 2023.  The vehicle was towed from the scene.

At 1:26PM the suspect grabbed miscellaneous items from the display shelf and placed them into a bag.  The suspect pushed the security guard against the shelf, and pushed store security again, as they exited the store with the stolen merchandise.  The suspect walked back into the store momentarily and said he was going to come back to shoot everyone.  The suspect fled northbound through the parking lot.

At 3:03AM, officers were dispatched to a subject refusing to leave the business at closing.  After 30 minutes of trying to get the subject to leave, the subject was placed under citizen’s arrest by the business.  During the arrest, the subject kicked officers repeatedly and attempted to spit on them.  The subject was later transported to county jail.

At 11:58AM the victim saw the suspect take money on the ground that belonged to another customer. The suspect spat in the victims face and damaged the victim’s cell phone, as the victim physically detained the suspect inside the store.  Prior to police arrival, the suspect fled and the citizen whose money was recovered by the victim, did not want to cooperate with the investigation.
At 3:20PM the suspect selected merchandise and fled without paying for them.

At 1:23 PM, a man got angry while turning in a job application and struck two employees, causing a small scratch on the face of an employee.  The suspect was in and out of lucidity and vomited while he was detained.  He was transported to the hospital for medical treatment and a report will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office for review.
At 6:39 PM, two irate customers were upset over the service they received from the clerk and damaged several packages of merchandise.
At 8:20 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed out several windows of the victim’s vehicle to gain entry. The suspect rifled through the vehicle but did not take anything.

Officers were dispatched to a disturbance. During the investigation, officers conducted a records check of the involved subjects and discovered one of them had an outstanding warrant. The subject was placed under arrest and transported directly to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 10:06PM Officers responded to the report of shots fired in the area.  The suspect vehicle was stopped at the intersection and when the light turned green, the driver fired a gun three times into the air and fled northbound on San Pablo Ave.  No injuries or property damage reported.  Three shell casings were located in the intersection.

CrimeType: Forgery and Possession of a Controlled Substance
Description: At 10:09AM the suspect deposited several fraudulent checks into the ATM and immediately withdrew money.  The suspect was also in possession of possible fentanyl.  The suspect was taken to Kaiser Richmond after complaining of chest pains, and was released to the hospitals care.  The case will be sent to the Contra Costa County District Attorney’s Office for review.

CrimeType: Identity Theft and Unlawful Use of Debit Card
Description: At 4:41PM an unknown suspect withdrew money from the victims EDD card at different ATM’s in the Bay Area.
At 9:32AM the suspect fraudulently placed their business name on a pre-signed check and deposited the check into their account.

Location: San Pablo Ave at Fairmount Ave.
Description: At 4:39PM two suspects shattered the driver side window to the victim’s vehicle, rummaged through the interior but was nothing was stolen.
At 6:54PM an officer was sent to the report that a suspect was unsuccessful in attempting to steal the catalytic converter from the victim’s vehicle.
At 2:46PM an officer was sent to a report that the suspect sent the victim over fifty threatening text messages earlier in the day.

At 1:42PM the suspect demanded a female give them their child, and attempted to grab the child from their mother’s arms. The victim ran away.  The suspect then attempted to open a door to a vehicle, while the driver was still inside, demanding to be let in, but the driver drove away. The suspect was detained by Police Officers on Manila Ave. near Kearney Street.  The suspect was found to be a parole-at-large.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the police station and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:13PM the suspect stole the victim’s motorized scooter while it was parked outside the business.

At 2:52 PM an officer reports the theft of a FedEx package from the front porch of a neighbor’s house after FedEx delivered it to the wrong address at 12:30 PM on 5/10/2023, confirmed by a FedEx delivery photo.  By the time the victim got to the neighbor’s porch at 5:30 PM that evening, the package was gone.
At 8:12 AM an officer reports the theft of a catalytic converter from a 2005 Toyota Corolla between 8:00 PM on 5/18/2023 and 8:00 AM on 5/19/2023.
At 12:31 PM an officer reports two unidentified suspects stole $1000 worth of shoes and clothing, and fled in a silver Kia van reported stolen to the San Francisco Police Department on 5/11/2023.
At 3:13 PM an officer recovered a maroon Ford Mustang reported stolen to Berkeley PD on 5/12/2023.

At 5:00 PM an officer reports a male suspect, who happened to be a regular thief from Ross, lost his temper when store employees wouldn’t allow him to come into the store to steal.  The suspect punched two loss prevention agents and spat on a third before fleeing the store.  One of the punched employees suffered a split lip and was treated by medical staff at the scene.

At 9:16 PM an officer reports the suspect entered the business and concealed items into a bag. Employees confronted the suspect and was able to recover some of the merchandise however, the suspect fled with approximately $200 worth of merchandise.
At 7:48 PM an officer reports the suspect broke into the victim’s secured locker and stole property.
At 8:22 AM two unidentified suspects loaded up a shopping cart with $889 worth of frozen shrimp, pushed the cart out the rear doors of the store, and loaded it in to a waiting car and fled.
At 1:07 PM, an officer responded to a fraud detail in the ECPD lobby.  The victim was swindled out of $7500 via a computer technical support gift card scam.
At 8:46 PM, an officer reports the victim secured his Ecotric electric bicycle next to a tree and went inside Lucky to shop. The victim returned later and discovered the bicycle had been stolen.

At 8:25 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect shattered a side window of a vehicle parked on San Pablo Avenue sometime overnight, but there was no known loss.  Window replacement estimate $800.

At 1:52 PM, an officer reports during a road rage incident the unidentified suspect spat from his vehicle window and the spittle landed on the victim’s vehicle and hands.  The victim then followed the suspect vehicle in an attempt to get his vehicle information.  The suspect threw an unidentified metal object from his vehicle at the following victim, and the object struck her vehicle.  The suspect then entered I-80 and the victim stopped following him.
At 8:49 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect fled the store with 10 pairs of shoes.
At 10:06PM, the victim was riding his scooter and robbed by two suspects, one of which was armed with a handgun.  The suspects fled the area.

CrimeType: Auto Burglary and Fraudulent Use of Access Card
Location: Pomona Ave at Moeser Ln./11450 San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 12:00PM the suspect shattered the front passenger side window to the victim’s vehicle and stole her wallet. The suspect attempted to use the victim’s credit card at Safeway in El Cerrito, but the purchases were declined.

At 5:01AM, officers were dispatched to a panic and glass break alarm at the business.  The front window had been rammed by a vehicle and an unknown amount of product stolen.  Three vehicles arrived with at least (6) suspects.  One of the vehicles was used to ram the front window.  All three vehicles fled the scene prior to officers arrival.
At 6:57PM, the suspect shattered the window to the victim’s apartment.
At 3:18PM, the suspect entered the store and placed merchandise in his backpack.  The suspect fled on foot.
At 10:38PM, the victim’s vehicle was vandalized.
At 5:55PM, the victim participated in an online shoe raffle.  The victim paid the suspect $222.00 using Apple cash and never heard from the suspect again.
At 12:44PM the suspects used four counterfeit $100 bills to purchase items.
The victim’s vehicle was stolen last night from 11:30PM to 5:30AM this morning.
At 1:45PM the suspects entered the store and filled a large bag with shoes.  The suspect exited the store without paying and got into a vehicle and fled the area.
At 11:52 hours, the suspect got into an argument with the victim.  During the argument, the suspect knocked the victim down.

At 8:18 PM, an officer responded to the business for an unwanted subject. During the investigation, the subject attempted to leave the business with merchandise when asked to leave. The subject was placed under citizen’s arrest. During a search incident to arrest, the subject was found to be in possession of a glass pipe used to smoke methamphetamine. A records check of the subject revealed he had an active warrant out of Marin County. The subject was booked at the ECPD and released on a citation.
At 10:59 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect shattered the left rear window of the victim's vehicle and stole items between 8:00 AM on 5/25 and 10:45 AM on 5/26.
At 9:59 AM, a known suspect came to victim’s residence and caused damage to the front door.

At 9:09 PM, an officer was patrolling in the area and located a vehicle collision. As the officer approached the vehicles, a subject attempted to flee the scene. The officer chased the subject a short distance until the subject fell to the ground. The subject, who appeared to be intoxicated was determined to be the driver of the vehicle that collided into a parked vehicle. The subject suffered minor injuries to her face from either the fall or the collision and was transported to a nearby hospital for medical treatment. At the hospital a blood sample was obtained from the suspect and the case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office for review.

CrimeType: Possess Controlled Substance / Possess Paraphernalia / Warrant Arrests
Description: At 12:05 PM, an officer reports the arrest of a 37 year old Vallejo man on a Vacaville PD drug warrant during a suspicious vehicle contact.  During a search incident to arrest, the man was found to be in possession of .7 grams of methamphetamine and a pipe.  The passenger in the vehicle was also arrested on a $25K no cite grand theft warrant out of San Francisco.  The man was field cited for the warrant and the new charges.  The woman was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the county jail.
At 1:40 PM, an officer recovered a stolen black Land Rover reported stolen to the Richmond Police Department on 5/25/2023.
At 10:17 AM, an officer recovered a silver Kia Optima that was reported stolen to the Oakland Police Department.

At 12:21 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect damaged the convertible top on the victim’s Mazda MX-5 to the tune of $1000 in an effort to enter the vehicle, before realizing the vehicle was unlocked.  The suspect stole items from the vehicle.  Occurred between midnight on 5/26 and 10:30 AM on 5/27/2023.
At 6:10 PM, an officer reports two subjects exited the business with two carts full of merchandise.

At 7:49 AM, an officer reports the arrest of a man after he was found unresponsive in the driver seat of his vehicle.  The man was heavily intoxicated and was unable to definitively identify himself.  During a search of his person for identification, methamphetamine was recovered from his pocket.  AMR transported the man to Kaiser Richmond where he was fingerprinted and released.
At 6:30 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.
At 6:09 PM, the victim reported an unknown suspect attempted to steal the victim’s vehicle sometime overnight.

At 3:08 PM the suspect entered the store and placed hygiene products in a garbage bag.  The suspect brandished a pair of scissors and threatened to stab the security guard.  The suspect fled prior to police arrival.

CrimeType: Warrant Arrest / Possession of Narcotics / Possession of Drug Paraphernalia / Probation Violation
Description: At 2:29 AM, a suspicious vehicle check was conducted and the driver was arrested for the above charges and transported to County jail.

CrimeType: Vehicle Tampering / Petty Theft / Possession of Burglary tools / Possession of Stolen Property / Parole Violation
Description: At 2:38 AM, officers responded to the report of a suspicious vehicle parked in the middle of the road.  As officers arrived on scene, the vehicle fled the area.  Officers later located the vehicle and conducted a traffic stop.  It was later determined the suspect stole items from the victim’s parked vehicle.  The suspect was arrested on the above charges and transported to County jail.

At 7:14 AM officers responded to a subject attempting to steal a catalytic converter from a parked vehicle.  When officers arrived they saw the suspect attempting to pry open the front door of the victim’s vehicle.  The suspect saw an officer and got into an awaiting vehicle and fled. Officers pursued the suspect vehicle, but due to its reckless driving, officers discontinued the pursuit.
The victim’s vehicle was stolen from 11:30 PM last night to 7:30 AM this morning.
At 4:27 PM the suspect entered the store, stole miscellaneous items and fled on foot.
At 1:03 PM, an officer reports the victim was swindled out of $800 in a gift card telephone scam.  The suspect claimed to be an Xfinity employee helping the victim to lower her monthly bill.
At 4:14 PM, an officer reports the victim was swindled out of $300 in Target gift cards via another Xfinity discount telephone scam.

CrimeType: Hit and Run / Recovered Stolen Vehicle
Description: At 10:26 AM, an officer recovered a 2019 Kia Optima after the suspect driver lost control of the vehicle and slid into the front yard of the residence.  The suspect was seen on camera as he fled on foot. The vehicle was reported stolen on 05/31 to the Oakland Police Department.
At 7:54 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered a window on the victim’s vehicle to gain entry. The ignition of the vehicle was damaged and no property was missing.
At 6:30 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s catalytic converter. The theft occurred between 06/02/23 9:00 PM and 06/03/23 12:00PM.
At 4:40 PM, an officer reports on 4/29/2023, the victim was scammed out of approximately $25,000 on an investment website.
At 4:35 PM an officer recovered the victim's unoccupied stolen vehicle that which reported to ECPD last week.
At 4:19 AM, officers were dispatched to contact the owner of a vehicle that was involved in a collision in Richmond.  The owner’s vehicle was stolen sometime overnight.
On 05/31/23 the suspect withdrew $600.00 and deposited a $1700.00 check into the victim’s bank account. The suspect fraudulently added a vehicle to the victim's auto insurance.
At 9:02 AM an officer located the victim’s unoccupied stolen vehicle which was reported to Oakland PD on June 5th.

Location: San Pablo Ave at Wall Ave
Description: At 10:22 AM the victim reported that an unknown suspect shattered the driver’s side window to the victim’s vehicle and damaged the ignition in an attempt to steal the car.
At 5:05 AM, officers were dispatched to a commercial burglary in progress at the new dispensary. Three vehicles fled the scene prior to police arrival.
At 1:48 PM the victim reported the rear window of their vehicle was shattered and their purse was stolen.
On 06/01/23, an unknown suspect stole the victim’s unattended purse that she left on her front porch.
Between 2:01 AM-2:11 AM, an unknown suspect spray-painted three of the victim’s vehicles. The victim was able to remove the spray paint before it caused damage.
At 1:57 PM, the victim reports that their rear license plate was stolen from their vehicle.
At 7:23 PM, the victim reports an unknown suspect stole the rear license plate from their vehicle between 06/07/23 – 06/08/23.

At 1:04 PM the suspect attempted to steal two cases of beer.  As the suspect attempted to exit the store the store clerk grabbed the cases of beer from him.  The suspect grabbed a water bottle off the shelf and pushed a customer out of his way as he exited the store.
At 4:51 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s residence via an unsecured window. The suspect located the keys to the victim’s vehicle and stole the vehicle.

At 6:44 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed the driver’s door window of the victim’s vehicle and rifled through the vehicle. The incident occurred between 5:30 and 5:40 PM. No loss was reported.
At 10:41 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect shattered the window of a 2019 BMW and stole a backpack containing a wallet and cash.  The theft occurred between 9:39 – 9:45 AM.
At 11:36 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect damaged four windows at the business with a BB gun.
At 10:31 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect shattered the driver's window of a 2006 Honda vehicle.  The interior appeared undisturbed.  Occurred sometime overnight.
At 7:07 PM, an officer reports two customers got into a verbal altercation over accidentally making contact with a shopping cart. During the altercation, the suspect pushed/punched the victims.
At 1:12 PM, an officer reports two unidentified suspects stole $400 worth of merchandise and fled in an unknown direction.

At 6:24 AM, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a man who was camped out in a booth at Denny’s overnight.  The man was asked several times to leave by Denny’s staff and officers, and repeatedly refused.  The man was transported directly to county jail.

At 10:35 AM, an officer reports the theft of a green 2000 Honda CR-V on 6/5/2023, between 0700-1700 hours. The owner did not immediately report the theft because he believed the vehicle may have been towed by the business.

At 9:39 PM, an officer reports the victims were walking to the business when the suspect punched them from behind. Upon arrival of officers, the victims pointed out the suspect who was walking across the street. Officers detained the suspect, and he was placed under Citizen’s Arrest by the victims. After booking, the suspect was released on a citation.
At 6:30 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s bicycle that was locked IFO a business.
At 4:32 PM a suspect stole several pairs of shoes and fled on foot towards San Pablo Ave.
The victim’s vehicle was stolen today from 6 AM to 2 PM.
The victim came to the PD to report their vehicle was stolen from June 12th at 4 PM to June 13th at 7:47 AM.
At 9:23 AM the victim reported that their vehicle was stolen from their driveway last night.
At 3:56 PM, when the employee retrieved a pack of cigarettes from the display shelf, the suspect pulled the employee's arm, grabbed the pack of cigarettes from them, and fled the store.
An unknown suspect shattered a window to the business, with an unknown object.  The incident occurred from June 12th at 9 PM to June 13th at 9:20 AM.
At 7:46 AM an unknown suspect threw a large cinder block and broke off the driver’s side view mirror to the victim’s parked vehicle.

The suspect broke into the victim’s vehicle and drilled the ignition in an attempt to steal it.  The incident occurred from 11 PM last night to 8 AM this morning. Stolen Vehicles Recovered – School Ave at S 53rd. At 8:42 AM officers were dispatched to two unoccupied suspicious vehicles parked in the area.  Officers located a vehicle that was reported stolen to Berkeley PD on 6-10-23 and another vehicle reported stolen to Oakland PD on 6-14-23.  Both vehicles were towed for storage and the owners were contacted.
At 3:40 PM, an officer reports Bank of America collected a skimmer and camera combo off one of the ATMs.

At 7:50 AM, an officer took a report of another bicycle theft.  The same suspect from the above case was captured on video stealing a grey bike valued at $1000.  This was the same bike he arrived on when he committed the above theft on Everett St.  The bicycle was returned to the owner.

At 7:03 AM, an officer reports the arrest of a 58-year-old Berkeley man after he removed a bicycle from the backyard of the residence and the homeowner caught him removing a second bike from the backyard shed. The combined value of the bikes was over $1000.  Direct transport to county jail.

At 9:59 AM, an officer reports a 23 year old man and his 33 year old female partner were thwarted from shoplifting by Walgreen’s security.  The man grabbed at the security guard, but the guard deflected and got them to leave the store.  The man then picked up a rock and threw it at the security guard, narrowly missing him.  The woman became upset at the treatment she felt her partner was receiving, so she picked up the rock and threw it at the front window of Walgreen’s, causing it to shatter. The pair fled on foot and a passerby saw them running from the Jack in the Box parking lot and hiding in the bushes along the freeway onramp. They were later located and detained.  After a positive ID, both were transported to county jail.

CrimeType: Stolen vehicle / Stolen Property / Narcotics / Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 9:13 PM, officers located a stolen vehicle and conducted a traffic stop. The driver was taken into custody without incident. Officers recovered stolen property, drugs, and drug paraphernalia from inside the vehicle. The driver was later transported to county jail.
The victim’s catalytic converter was stolen from their vehicle sometime between 6 PM on 6/18/2023 and 9:45 AM on 6/19/2023.

At 2:37 PM an unidentified suspect shattered the right front window of the victim’s vehicle. The suspect stole the victim’s wallet containing their credit cards. The credit card was fraudulently used in Berkeley.
At 3:46 PM an unidentified suspect intercepted a company check that was mailed to a vendor and cashed it.
At 4:39 PM the victim reported her license plates were stolen and replaced them with different ones.
At 9:54 PM, officers located an unoccupied stolen vehicle, and it was returned to its owner.
At 10:16 AM the victim reported their truck was stolen sometime last night.

At 1:45 PM, officers responded to a report of two roommates arguing. One of the subjects was found to have a warrant for their arrest out of Sacramento County. The subject was arrested, booked and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Location: Peerless Ave. at San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 6:55 AM an officers located a stolen vehicle traveling northbound on San Pablo Ave from Peerless Ave. Officers stopped the vehicle, and the driver was arrested. A meth pipe was found in the vehicle. The suspect was booked and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 5:01 PM, the suspect punched the victim in the face and hit the victim with the handle of a squeegee several times. The suspect was arrested, booked and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:38 PM the victim reported their catalytic converter was stolen from their vehicle sometime last night.
At 11:33 AM the victim reported their vehicle was stolen sometime last night.

At 8:34 PM, officers responded to the business for a theft detail. Officers located the suspect several blocks from the business and detained her.
