The suspect was positively identified by employees and placed under citizen’s arrest. During a records check, officers discovered the suspect had an outstanding warrant out of Alameda County. The suspect was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility after booking.
At 2:26 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole $994 worth of merchandise and fled through an emergency exit door toward the vacant CVS building.

At 3:25 AM, officers were dispatched to the business for a subject passed out behind the wheel of a vehicle in the drive through area. Officers contacted the driver to check on his welfare. During a records check officers discovered the driver had a no bail warrant out of Contra Costa County. The driver was taken into custody and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:24 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect shattered the rear window of the victim’s vehicle during the previous hour.
At 8:37 AM, an officer reports the theft of a 2020 Kia Forte from the apartment complex parking lot between 9:00 PM on 6/24 and 8:00 AM on 6/25.
At 9:53 AM, an officer reports the theft of a necklace from the victim’s neck after suspect asked for directions and “thanked” the victim with a hug.  Occurred just prior to the time of call.

At 8:32 PM, officers responded to a suspicious person detail. The victim reported she was leaving her house and noticed a suspicious male IFO her residence. The victim drove around the corner and parked down the street from her house. She noticed the male put on a ski mask and walked up to her porch. The male opened the unlocked front gate and checked the locked front door. The male then stole a piece of mail and walked to a neighboring apartment building. An officer checking the area located a subject matching the description of the suspect approximately a block away. The suspect was positively identified during an infield show-up and placed under citizen’s arrest.  While waiting for the victim for the infield show-up the suspect attempted to flee from the officer, but was quickly taken into custody. After booking, the suspect was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An unknown suspect shattered the rear driver side window of the victims vehicle and damaged the ignition.
The victims’ front license plate was stolen from their vehicle from June 15th to June 27th.
On June 25th at 6:48AM a suspect entered the victim’s unlocked truck and stole miscellaneous tools.
At 4:14PM the victim reported that the rear windows to the victim’s vehicle were shattered and miscellaneous paperwork was stolen.

At 7:33PM, the victim reported his vehicle stolen from 24hr Fitness while he was inside working out.  At 8:37PM, officers located the same vehicle unoccupied near Eureka Ave / Kearney St.  The vehicle was returned to its owner.
At 4:20PM an El Cerrito Police Officer located an unoccupied parked vehicle that was reported stolen to the Richmond Police Department on June 27th.
At 4:55PM while the victim was walking along San Pablo Ave., the suspect, unprovoked, pushed the victim and spat towards the victim’s direction.
At 8:30AM the victim reports that the suspect stole the victim’s child-alert-safety-sign from in front of the residence.

At 1:36AM, the victim reported her vehicle stolen from her driveway.  At 2:28AM, officers located the stolen vehicle driving through El Cerrito.  The vehicle was followed to Castro Valley and a pursuit ensued.  The pursuit ended in Hayward after crashing into a fence at a golf course.  Three suspects fled from the vehicle, and one was arrested.  The other two eluded capture.  The arrested subject also had a felony warrant for their arrest.  The subject was later transported to county jail in Martinez.
At 6:27PM, the victim reported her ex-boyfriend came to her residence to pickup his belongings and physically assaulted the victim and threatened to kill her.

At 9:05AM the victim reports that while they were walking in the park, the suspect stopped her, brandished a knife, demanded the victim give her money and threatened to kill her.  The victim ran away and called the police.
At 4:36PM a suspect stole $50.00 worth of merchandise and fled the store.
At 6:44 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect attempted to steal the victim’s vehicle. The incident occurred in between 6/29 @1800 and 6/30 @ 1809 hours.
At 12:37 PM, an officer reports the theft of a 1987 Subaru hatchback between 10:30 PM on 6/29 and 7:00 AM on 6/30.

At 12:05 PM, an officer reports unidentified suspects left Denny’s without paying.  As the suspects left, they were confronted by a staff member, and one of the suspects pulled her hair in order to get by her.  Last seen northbound in a grey Toyota.
At 3:10 PM, unidentified suspects stole $323 worth of shoes.

At 8:11 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the store and walked to the toddler section. A loss prevention agent noticed the suspect quickly randomly picking up clothing. Moments later the suspect exited the business with the clothing without making any attempts to pay. The Loss Prevention Agent contacted the suspect and asked her to return to the store to pay for the items. The suspect refused and fled the area.
At 11:15 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect attempted to steal the victim’s vehicle.

While taking the above report, the officer was flagged down about several subjects breaking into a vehicle down the block. Officers responded to the area and discovered an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.
At 5:29 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed the rear passenger’s side window of the victim’s vehicle to gain entry and stole property.
At 6:59 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole two e-bikes from a bike rack affixed to the victim’s vehicle sometime between 8:00 PM on 7/1 and 6:00 AM on 7/2.
At 7:22 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect shattered windows of the camper shell on the victim’s Toyota Tacoma and stole property from within.
At 2:01 AM, an officer reports a skimming device was located in the ATM in the 7-Eleven by a repair tech who was servicing the machine.
At 4:29 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed the driver’s side window of victim’s vehicle and stole tools.
At 4:47 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle. The theft occurred in between 7/3 @12:00 PM and 7/4 @9:30 PM.

At 9:58 PM, a subject flagged down officers at the police station advising she was suffering from a medical issue. Officers summoned an ambulance for the subject. Upon the arrival of the ambulance, the subject declined to be transported to a local hospital and walked away. The subject later returned to the station and officers discovered they had an outstanding warrant. The subject was taken into custody and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 10:01 PM, an officer reports the victim heard a loud noise coming from the front of her residence. The victim went outside to investigate and noticed two subjects running from her vehicle. The victim went to examine her vehicle and noticed a rear window had been smashed out.

At 10:05 PM, an officer located a suspicious vehicle. During the investigation they discovered the vehicle was an unreported stolen vehicle from Richmond. The officer processed the vehicle for evidence and released the vehicle to the owner at the scene.
At 1:00 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle. The theft occurred in between 11:00 PM and midnight.

At 6:54 PM, officers responded to a residential burglary that had just occurred. During the investigation, officers discovered the suspect was a neighbor of the victim. Officers located the neighbor and took him into custody. After booking, the suspect was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:41 AM, an officer reports an unidentified female suspect hit the security guard and tried to take her phone.  The security guard was trying to take the suspect’s picture because she suspected a theft was in progress.

At 2:22 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect entered the victim’s garage and stole items sometime over the previous three days. Shoplift/False Fire Alarm 11565 San Pablo Ave (Walgreens): At 10:44 PM, officers were dispatched to Walgreens for a subject taking merchandise off of the shelves and refusing to leave. When employees locked the doors due to the store closing, the subject then pulled the fire alarm. Officers arrived on scene and detained the subject. The subject was placed under Citizen’s Arrest by employees. The subject was transported to the ECPD for booking and issued a citation.
At 7:13 AM, an officer reports the theft of a 2006 Dodge Ram pickup sometime overnight.  The victim tracked the vehicle to San Pablo via GPS and San Pablo PD recovered it a short time later.

At 11:02 PM, officers responded to a disturbance. During the investigation officers conducted a records check and discovered one of the involved parties had an outstanding warrant out of San Francisco County. The second subject was found to be intoxicated in public and placed under arrest. The subject with the warrant was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. The intoxicated subject was transported to a local hospital for an unrelated medical issue.

At 2:42 PM, an officer responded to the business for two unwanted subjects. After the first subject left, the second subject refused to leave and began breaking dishes. As officers were taking the subject into custody, she kicked an officer. The subject was transported directly to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:20 PM an officer reports the theft of a 2021 Dodge Charger which was captured on video at 10:28 AM.
At 6:16 PM an officer recovered the victim’s vehicle that had been reported stolen to the ECPD earlier in the day.
At 11:32 AM an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole four pints of ice cream.
At 3:22 AM an officer reports unknown suspect(s) stole the rims and tires from the victim’s vehicle. The theft occurred in between 7/10@2230 hours and 7/11@0320 hours.

At 11:55 AM an officer reports the arrest of person on a no cite $5,000.00 warrant, during a traffic stop for a stop sign violation.  They were arrested, issued a citation for the warrant, and released.

At 11:30 PM officers were dispatched to a disturbance at the business. During the investigation, officers discovered the involved subject had an outstanding warrant for his arrest. The subject was taken into custody and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer was sent to the report that between 5:45 am and 10:30 am on July 8th someone stole all four rims and tires from the victim’s vehicle.
At 6:32 PM an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole a pair of shoes.

At 12:45 pm an officer was sent to the report that while the victim’s vehicle was parked in the parking lot someone shattered the window, got inside the vehicle, and broke the ignition column in an attempt to steal the vehicle.

An officer was sent to the report that someone had come into the store and stole a pair of eyeglass frames and fled the business.  As the suspect fled the scene in a vehicle he collided with another vehicle and fled the scene of that collision.
The victim reported to an officer that a package had been stolen that had been left in front of the business.

Location: 10890 San Pablo Avenue – 10890 San Pablo Avenue
Description: City staff reports that a check that they had issued to a vender in Southern California was cashed by someone other than the vender.
An officer was sent to the report that two suspects stole 4-5 packages from a delivery truck.

At 6:53 AM an officer was sent to the report of a stolen vehicle that had occurred between 4:00 pm on the 12th and 6:00 am on the 13th.  At 10:29 am the vehicle was captured on the new Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) cameras and an officer located it near San Pablo Avenue at Moeser Lane.  After an attempt to stop the driver of the truck he sped away and did not comply.  A vehicle pursuit was not initiated however officers stayed in the area so they could observe the vehicle.  The vehicle collided with another vehicle at the intersection of Potrero Avenue at S. 56th Street and the driver got out of the vehicle and ran away.  Officer pursued the driver and were able to catch him on Potrero Avenue at I-80.  The driver was booked at ECPD and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 6:16 AM an officer was sent to the report that someone had cut the lock on the fence, entered the yard, and then forced entry into an office, stole keys to a truck parked outside, and then stole the truck.  PG&E later were able to use the GPS device on the truck to locate it in Unincorporated Richmond.  The vehicle was recovered by ECPD and returned to PG&E.

At 1:46 AM officers responded to the business for an audible alarm and located a smashed-out window near the front entrance. Officers searched the interior of the business and did not locate any suspects.
At 1:39 PM an officer reports the theft of a front license plate from the victim’s vehicle.  The theft occurred sometime in the last 24 hours.

At 12:46 PM an officer reports a neighbor witnessed the suspect climbing into the victim house through a side window.  A few minutes later the suspect left toward Carlson Blvd. Warrant Arrest x3- Eastshore Blvd/Potrero Ave: At 10:42 PM an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and discovered the driver had three outstanding warrants from Solano County. The driver was taken into custody and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 8:12 AM an officer reports an unidentified suspect broke into a mailbox, but there was no known loss.  The suspect then shattered the right front window of a burgundy Hyundai Sonata and damaged the steering column in an apparent attempt to steal the vehicle, but were unsuccessful.

At 2:26 AM officers located a driver passed out in the driver’s seat of his vehicle stopped in middle of the roadway. Officers contacted the driver and conducted a DUI investigation and determined the driver was under the influence. The driver was booked at the ECPD and released to a sober relative.
At 10:34 AM an officer arrested a person on two non-citable Lake County misdemeanor warrants.  The person was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:37 PM an officer reports the theft of a vehicle sometime between 8:00 PM on 7/14 and 1:00 PM on 7/15.
At 10:36 AM an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole shoes and fled.

At 5:08 AM an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the investigation, the officer discovered the driver had in his possession several driver licenses and credit cards belonging to four different people. During a records check, the officer discovered the passenger had an outstanding parole warrant. Both subjects were transported to the ECPD for booking. After booking the driver was issued a citation and the passenger was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility for his warrant.

At 1:42 AM an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During a search of the vehicle the officer located two glass pipes used for smoking methamphetamine. The driver was issued a citation and released at the scene.

Location: El Dorado Ave/ San Pablo Ave
Description: At 2:32 PM while circulating the area on the previous vehicle theft, the officer recovered a seperate vehicle reported stolen to Martinez PD on 10/22/2022.

At 6:19 AM an officer reports an unidentified suspect removed the bathroom window screen from the victim’s apartment.  The resident heard the noise and called out, causing the suspect to flee before they could enter.

At 3:56 PM, an officer reports the recovery of a white VW Jetta.  The registered owner was driving the vehicle after having located it in Oakland several days prior, but failed to notify OPD to remove the vehicle from the stolen vehicle system.

At 12:19 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect entered the Valero and wrestled the lottery scratcher rack off the counter.  He then took the rack and the tickets and entered the rear seat of a waiting vehicle and fled.
At 3:20PM, an officer reports the theft of a motor scooter from the carport sometime overnight.
At 7:29 PM, an officer reports a female suspect fled the store with a pair of shoes without paying.

At 9:37 PM an employee was walking to their car in the parking lot when they noticed the victim and noticed the suspect removing items from a donation bin. The suspect cursed at the victim and approached the victim’s vehicle with a toilet seat as if he was going to hit her or her car.   The victim backed away, and the suspect fled prior to police arrival.

At 5:49 PM two suspects attempted to leave the store with unpaid merchandise. When employees confronted the suspects, one of the suspects brandished a can of pepper spray and threatened to spray the employees.  One of the suspects was detained by officers at the Del Norte BART station parking lot.  The suspect was positively identified by one of the employees and placed under citizen’s arrest.  The suspect was found to have two warrants out of San Francisco.  The suspect was released on a citation and was taken to the hospital for an unrelated medical issue.
At 6:49 PM four suspects concealed merchandise into their bags and left the store without paying.

CrimeType: Assault on a Police Officer/Resisting Arrest/Drunk in Public/Warrant Arrest
Location: San Pablo Ave at Moeser Ln.
Description: At 1:05PM officers responded to 24hour Fitness for an unwanted person who was inebriated and yelling at staff. The suspect exited the business when the officers arrived. The suspect was found to have a felony warrant for his arrest. When officers attempted to arrest him, the suspect threw a glass bottle at a police officer and ran away. The suspect was immediately apprehended and placed under arrest. The suspect was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:09 PM the victim parked her vehicle in the parking lot with the engine running. When the victim returned from shopping, her vehicle was stolen. At 10:21 PM hours, a Richmond police officer located the vehicle in the City of Richmond and arrested the driver and passenger.
At 8:22 PM hours, the suspect threatened to shoot the victim during an argument about a rental property.  The victim feared for their safety, however, no weapons were seen or used.
The victim reported that on Sunday July 16th at 5:49AM an unknown suspect jumped over their side fence and pried off a window screen to the residence.
At 12:0PM the victim reported her front license plate was stolen from their vehicle.
All four of the victims’ tires and rims were stolen from the victim’s vehicle sometime from July 17th at 6PM to July 19th at 8AM.
The victim’s power tools were stolen from the backyard, last night from 5PM to 9PM.
At 8:09 AM the victim reported that their vehicle was stolen from their driveway last night.
At 5:36 AM the victim reported that all the tires and rims were stolen off their vehicle sometime last night.
An officer reports an unknown suspect cut the lock off of the victim’s detached garage and stole a bicycle and helmet. The incident occurred between 7/17 @ 7:00 AM to 7/19 @ 7:00 AM.

At 10:22AM the suspect was inside Starbucks brandishing a machete at customers.  Officers detained the suspect inside and located three machetes in his property.  The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released on a citation.
At 12:54PM, Officer Mitchell located an unoccupied Volkswagen reported stolen to San Francisco PD on July 16th.
At 4:09PM, the victim reported that unknown suspect(s) miscellaneous tools from their construction site.

At 11:15 PM, an officer reports the victim exited the BART station and was walking to her parked vehicle when she noticed the suspect standing next to it. When the suspect saw the victim, he quickly jumped into his awaiting vehicle and fled the area. The victim examined her vehicle and noticed a puncture on her gas tank with gas leaking.  ECFD responded for the leaking gasoline.

At 2:08 AM, the victim heard the sound of his vehicle’s alarm and went outside to investigate. He noticed an unknown suspect had stolen his vehicle and last heard it going northbound on San Pablo Ave.
At 11:23 PM, an officer located a 2000 GMC Savana van unoccupied. The van was reported stolen to Richmond PD earlier in the day. The victim responded to the location and took possession of her van.
The victim’s vehicle was stolen from their driveway from July 19th at 5:30PM to July 20th 2:29PM.
At 5:27 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole a 2003 Ford Van sometime during the night.

At 7:13 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole a Specialized Stump Jumper bicycle from inside an unlocked vehicle.  The victim had a tracker attached to the bike, and it last pinged in Richmond at 6:16 AM.
At 8:33 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole tools from a van between 6:29 AM and 7:48 AM.
At 4:02 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed out a front window of the business to gain entry. The suspect fled with merchandise.

At 6:43 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect confronted the victim about being inside the store. The suspect then punched the victim approximately five times in the face before fleeing the scene. The victim was transported to a local hospital for medical treatment.

At 12:34 AM, an officer reports the victim noticed the suspects exiting the women’s locker with her property. The victim attempted to retrieve her property, but the suspects ran to a vehicle in the parking lot and fled.
At 12:04 PM, an officer arrested a person on a non-citable warrant out of San Pablo PD.  The person was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:36 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole a package from an Amazon van while the driver was making a delivery.

At 1:22 AM, officers were dispatched to a theft that had just occurred. A sergeant located the suspect several blocks away in possession of the property. The property was returned to the victim. This case will be forwarded to the Contra Costa County District Attorney's office.
At 9:23 AM, an officer reports the theft of a vehicle between 9:45-11:00 AM on 7/23.
At 3:05 PM, an officer reports the theft of the front plate from a vehicle.
At 6:08AM the victim reports that the suspect entered their unlocked vehicle, rummaged through the interior but did not steal anything.

CrimeType: Armed Robbery/Possession of Drug Paraphernalia/Possession of a Controlled Substance/False Information to Officers/Arrest Warrant
Description: At 3:25PM officers responded to Ross for an armed robbery. The two suspects stole items from the store and when confronted by employees, the suspects threatened them with a taser.  Both suspects were detained at the El Cerrito Plaza BART station and were positively identified by a Ross employee.  The stolen Ross merchandise was returned to the store.  The suspects were in possession of a meth pipe and crystal methamphetamine.  One of the suspects had a warrant out of San Francisco for burglary and the other suspect provided false identifying information to police officers.  The suspects were arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Battery / Possession of a Concealed Weapon / Attempting to Prevent a 911 call / Warrant
Description: At 10:26PM, officers responded to a battery between two subjects while inside a vehicle.  The suspect was also attempting to prevent the victim from calling 911.  During the investigation the suspect was also found to have an outstanding warrant for their arrest.  During the arrest, the suspect was found in possession of a dirk or dagger.  The suspect was arrested and transported to county jail.
At 7:45PM, officers responded to a reported burglary.  Two suspects shattered the front door glass window and stole several items.  The suspect fled westbound on Potrero Ave prior to police arrival.

At 10:15AM officers assisted BART PD with an auto burglary that just occurred in their parking lot.  An El Cerrito Police Officer detained the two suspects in the parking lot.  BART PD arrived and took over the investigation.  One of the suspects was armed with a loaded handgun.

CrimeType: Violation of a Domestic Violence Restraining Order
Description: At 4:43PM officers arrested the suspect after the victim, his ex-girlfriend, saw him lingering at a store she was at.  The victim has a restraining order against the suspect.   The victim was terrified and ran to the El Cerrito Police Department for safety.  The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:44PM, the victim reported his locker had been broken into and his backpack was stolen.
At 12:44PM a suspect stole a portable generator from a City of El Cerrito work truck and fled in a silver SUV westbound on Potrero Ave.
At 10:40AM the victim reports that one hour prior to calling police, a suspect shattered the rear window to their vehicle and damaged the steering column in an attempt to steal it
At 11:10AM the suspect walked out of the store with a shopping cart full of unpaid items.  Store employees retrieved the cart full of merchandise and the suspect fled in their vehicle empty handed.
At 6:48PM, the suspect stole perfume from the store and fled the scene prior to police arrival.
At 8:18AM the victim reported that he got into an argument with the suspect.  The suspect attempted to hit the victim and spat on the victim.  The suspect fled prior to police arrival.

Location: San Mateo Ave at Central Ave.
Description: At 8:58AM the victim reported that their front license plate was stolen from their vehicle last night.
At 6:35PM, the victim had his bicycle stolen from in front of Safeway.
At 12:42PM three suspects entered the store, grabbed over $600 worth of merchandise and fled the scene.
An unknown suspect sprayed painted graffiti on the exterior cinema signs and rooftop area.  The incident occurred from 7/26  at 11PM to 7/27 at 5PM.
At 7:12PM, a stolen vehicle was located unoccupied.
The victim reported that their vehicle was stolen sometime last night from 7:30PM to 12PM today.

The victim’s vehicle was stolen last night while they left it parked near the Del Norte BART station.  The Richmond Police Department recovered the victim’s vehicle in their City when they found it abandoned in the middle of the street.

At 6:44Am an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle. Commercial Burglary – 1600 Liberty St. Giovanni’s Market At 9:26PM two suspects damaged the exterior front door lock, entered the business and stole cash from the registers.   The suspects fled in a vehicle prior to police arrival.
At 2:37PM the victim reports wallet was stolen from their unlocked vehicle. The suspect attempted to purchase items with the victim’s credit card in Berkeley.
The victim’s vehicle was stolen from 10PM last night to 3AM this morning.

At 10:17 AM officers were dispatched to a fight call at the Popeyes Louisiana Kitchen. One minute later, Dispatch advised of a robbery occurring at the Tobacco Outlet which turned out to be the fight call.  ECPD officers responded with RPD providing a cover officer. The suspect attempted to rob the store. The victim locked the suspect inside of the store in which the suspect began to smash out the window. A Richmond PD Officer arrived and detained the suspect.  The suspect was medically cleared at a hospital, booked at ECPD, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Evading Police / Tampering with a Vehicle / Resisting Arrest / Felon in Possession of a Firearm
Location: San Pablo Avenue at Columbia Avenue
Description: At 8:48 AM the Flock Safety camera alerted officers of a vehicle entering the City of El Cerrito. The vehicle was wanted by Newark PD (Alameda County) for a robbery with a firearm that had occurred in their city. Officers drove to the area, but were not able to locate it. At 9:49 AM the Flock camera hit on the same vehicle at San Pablo Avenue at Potrero Avenue. Officers learned that the Toyota matched the suspect vehicle from an attempted auto Burglary that had occurred minutes earlier in El Cerrito.  Officers located the vehicle travelling on San Pablo Ave at Columbia Ave. Officers attempted a traffic stop on the vehicle on Central Ave at Yosemite Ave which failed to yield. Officers pursued the vehicle onto I-80 westbound where it was involved in a solo vehicle collision with the cement center median prior to the Gilman St. exit. After the collision, the driver fled on foot and was apprehended by an El Cerrito Police Department canine. A passenger was detained by officers in the vehicle. The suspect had in his possession property which appeared to be from the Newark robbery.  A loaded firearm was located in the vehicle when it was searched by officers.  Both suspects were transported to a hospital for medical clearance. The passenger of the vehicle was released to a parent at the El Cerrito Police Department.  Newark PD came to the hospital and took custody of the suspect in their Robbery.

At 9:40 AM officers were dispatched to an auto burglary in progress. The victim provided officers with video footage of the suspect vehicle which appeared to be the vehicle that was wanted for the Newark Robbery.  The suspect tampered with the rear driver side door of the victim’s vehicle trying to enter the vehicle but was scared off by the witness.

At 2:01 PM a BART Police Officer was flagged down by the Walgreens security guard regarding a shoplifting that occurred. The BART PD Officer detained the suspect, who was on the platform at the Del Norte Bart Station, until ECPD officers arrived. An Officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of the suspect, and they were placed under arrest.  The suspect was booked at ECPD and then released with a citation.

At 10:43 AM the suspect stole items from the store and when security attempted to stop him, the suspect attempted to punch the security guard.  The suspect threw a canned good at the security guard striking him.  Officers detained the suspect in the Jack in the Box parking lot.  The suspect was positively identified during an infield show up, placed under arrest and booked at the El Cerrito Police Department.  The suspect had a warrant out of Oakland PD for robbery.  The suspect was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 12:38 PM the suspect stole food from the counter and fled the restaurant on foot.  The suspect was detained and positively identified during an infield show up.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released on a citation.
At 10:05 AM officers located a parked unoccupied stolen vehicle.
At 4:31 AM the victim’s vehicle was stolen from their carport.  The victim was carjacked of his other vehicle last night in Oakland and his keys were stolen.

At 3:38 AM officers responded to commercial burglary in progress.  Two vehicles arrived with many suspects and smashed through the front doors with one of the vehicles.  The suspects stole an unknown amount of product and fled the scene prior to police arrival.

At 4:09 AM, officers responded to a commercial burglary at the business.  One vehicle arrived after the business had closed and smashed the front door glass.  The suspects stole several items but dropped them on the way out.  The suspect fled in a vehicle prior to police arrival.
At 4:26 PM the suspect stole merchandise and fled on foot.

At 5:33 AM officers were dispatched to a fire inside the gym.  The suspect was upset over their shoes getting stolen and started a fire in the sauna.  The suspect was arrested for arson and had a warrant for their arrest.  The suspect was cited for the arson and transported to county jail on the warrant.
At 6:54 AM the victim reports that the front passenger side window to their vehicle was shattered and miscellaneous items were stolen from inside their vehicle.

At 9:07 AM an officer reports the theft of a vehicle between 9:00 PM on 8/3 and 8:30 AM on 8/4/2023. Vehicle Theft – 815 Park Wy: At 11:41 AM, an officer reports the theft of a vehicle between 8:00 PM on 8/3 and 11:41 AM on 8/4/2023.
At 7:04 AM an officer reports the theft of all rims and tires were stolen from the vehicle sometime between midnight and the time of call.
At 6:59 AM an officer reports an unidentified suspect punched the driver door lock and steering column in an apparent unsuccessful attempt to steal the vehicle sometime overnight.
At 7:26 PM officers recovered a stolen vehicle reported stolen to Alameda County SO back in April.

At 10:35 PM officers responded to a call of a man with a gun.  After speaking with a witness, the suspect had fired 4 rounds at a vehicle driving westbound on Moeser Ln approaching San Pablo Ave.  The victim vehicle fled the area and did not call to report the shooting.  The suspect fled the area on foot prior to police arrival.
At 1:21 PM, an unidentified suspect stole three pairs of shoes.

At 9:01 PM officers were dispatched to the report of a fight inside of Denny’s with one of the people armed with a machete.  Officers arrived on scene and detained the subject who had been armed with the machete.  During the investigation it was determined the suspect had stolen merchandise from the Chevron gas station across the street.  When the suspect fled the Chevron employee ran after the suspect.  When he caught up to him inside of Denny’s the suspect pulled out a machete in an effort to retain the stolen items.  The stolen merchandise was recovered on the suspect and returned to the store.  The suspect was placed under arrest, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:00 AM three suspects wearing ski masks attempted to steal the catalytic converter from a vehicle, but they were scared off by a neighbor.  All three fled in a vehicle prior to police arrival.

CrimeType: Assault with a Deadly Weapon / Prowling
Description: At 12:14 PM officers were dispatched to a second-hand report of a male entering through the front door of an unlocked apartment and fighting with a resident.  Upon arrival it was determined there was no fight, and the man had entered the apartment and spoken briefly with the occupants, then left when he was asked.  The residents pointed down the path at the man, who was still walking away from the location. A sergeant recognized the man as having been in the area during a suspicious person investigation the day before on Cypress Avenue.  Based on the behavior pattern, the sergeant stopped and identified the man who claimed he was trying to rent one of the apartments, and picked the wrong one to enter.  When no crime could be substantiated, the man was released with an admonition to stop going on to people’s property. At 1:42 PM, the residents called again and said the man was back, and was trying to enter their apartment again.  After finding the door and windows locked, the man refocused his attention on the apartment next door, trying all the doors and windows but again not able to make entry.  This apartment was also occupied at the time, but the resident did not notice the man.  When the original caller confronted the man, a brief argument ensued during which he picked up a 12 inch long brick garden border and raised it at the victim as if he was going to hit him over the head with it.  When the victim started recording with his cell phone, the suspect dropped the brick in the street and fled on foot.  The suspect was detained by an officer a short distance away and was positively identified during an in-field.  He was placed under arrest, booked at ECPD, then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 3:38 PM a suspect entered the CVS store pulling a rolling duffle bag.  The man walked straight to the liquor aisle, broke the glass door to the high-end liquor cabinet and stuffed the duffel with assorted bottles of Hennessy and Courvoisier.  He then left the store without paying.
At 2:37 PM, an unidentified suspect stole food items, and threw an open pocket knife at a Loss Prevention agent as she left the store, striking the agent in the face and causing cuts.
At 4:27 hours, an unidentified suspect stole merchandise and fled on a bike.
At 6:12 AM the suspects stole a tire rack and fled.  Officers located the tire rack and returned it to Honda.

CrimeType: Petty Theft and Tampering with a Vehicle
Description: At 10:20 AM the suspect attempted to steal the car battery by cutting the wires.  The suspect stole the vehicles registration and jumper cables.

CrimeType: Possession of Controlled Substance / Possession of Stolen Property
Description: At 12:42 AM officers were dispatched to a prowler in the backyard of a residence.  The subject was located a few blocks away and detained.  During a probation search, the subject was found in possession of narcotics and stolen property.  The subject was arrested and booked at ECPD and later cite released.

At 12:44 AM officers responded to a vehicle tampering.  The subject arrived in a truck and was checking car doors in the area.  The subject entered one vehicle but did not steal anything.  The subject fled in the truck prior to police arrival.

CrimeType: Possession of a Controlled Substance and Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 7:14 AM an El Cerrito Police Officer made a traffic stop.  During a search of the vehicle, the officer found 38.5 grams of suspected crystal methamphetamines and a meth pipe.  The suspect was arrested and released on a citation.

At 9:40 PM employees at the business flagged down officers regarding a theft.  The subject was detained and found in possession of drug paraphernalia.  The subject was arrested, issued a citation, and released at the scene.

At 8:28 PM the suspect called the business and identified himself as a representative from the corporate helpdesk.  The employee was instructed to start the “Updating Processes” and in doing so, wired $2,789.00 to an unknown business.

At 4:17 PM the suspect attempted to steal items from the store.  When employees confronted the suspect he threatened them with a hatchet.  The suspect was arrested and found to be on parole for Assault with a Deadly Weapon.  The suspect was booked at ECPD and transported to the Martinez County Jail.
At 1:35 PM the victim reported that their vehicle was stolen overnight.
At 2:47 PM a customer presented a counterfeit $20.00 bill to the cashier.
At 3:01 PM the victim reported that the suspect stole miscellaneous tools from the victim’s truck.
At 2:48 PM the victim reported that their vehicle was stolen sometime last night.
At 1:21 PM two subjects threw a rock through the victims’ garage door window.
At 12:50AM, the suspect attempted to pry open the front doors to the business but was unsuccessful.
At 1:00 PM the suspect pried open a shed and stole miscellaneous tools.
At 2:56 PM an officer located an unoccupied stolen vehicle.
At 2:08PM, the suspect stole shoes and fled the store.
At 11:12 AM the suspect smashed the victim’s window and stole her purse.
At 8:16 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect shattered several windows of a white commercial van sometime overnight.  No known property loss.

At 9:21 PM, an officer was patrolling in the area and was flagged down in regards to a theft that had just occurred involving two suspects. The officer detained one of the suspects with unpaid merchandise. The second suspect fled the area after a loss prevention agent retrieved the stolen property from her. The first suspect was placed under Citizen’s Arrest by an employee. After booking, she was issued a citation and released.

At 4:26 AM, officers were dispatched to the area for a suspicious vehicle that was involved in a collision. Upon arrival, officers located the vehicle with frontend damage and a male in the driver’s seat exhibiting signs of intoxication. An officer conducted a DUI investigation and determined the driver was under the influence. The driver was placed under arrest and transported to the ECPD for booking. After booking, the male was issued a citation and released to a sober family member.

At 6:20 AM, two unidentified suspects stole a Bicycle from the rear bike rack of the victim’s vehicle, which was parked in their driveway.  The suspects were also captured on a nearby ring doorbell camera rooting through the property on the front porch of a neighbor’s residence but it appeared they were scared off before stealing anything else.
At 1:12 PM unidentified suspect(s) entered the residence by shattering a rear window.  The suspect(s) tore the alarm panel off the wall, but the homeowner was unsure if there was a property loss.
At 5:20 PM a vehicle drove alongside the victim, a suspect exited the vehicle and took the victim’s purse from their shoulder, then the vehicle and occupants fled.

At 11:07 AM an officer reports the arrest of a suspect after he stole miscellaneous items from Walgreen’s and brandished a pocket knife when confronted by security.  The man also had a no cite warrant out of Sonoma County.  Booked at ECPD then to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:37 AM a suspect shattered the right front window of the victim’s vehicle and stole the vehicle registration card.

At 10:15 PM an officer reports an unknown suspect walked into the business after closing time and started to consume merchandise. As employees were escorting the suspect out of the business, he attempted to hit one of the employees with a closed fist. The suspect then started stabbing several windows of the business and scrapping off the business logo. An employee confronted the suspect and he started throwing items at the victim.
At 1:42 AM, an officer reports unknown suspect(s) stole items from the victim’s vehicle.
At 2:58PM the victim reports that her friend came to her hotel room.  When asked to leave the friend threatened to kill her.
At 8:40 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.
At approximately 3:50 PM a suspect entered T-Mobile and took a cellphone from the display.  The suspect then fled in a vehicle.
At 3:40 PM, the Barnes and Noble manager witnessed a suspect walk out of the store with a small piano.  The suspect placed the piano inside a vehicle and fled.
At 11:23 AM a suspect walked out of Luckys with merchandise they did not pay for.
At 8:36 am, the victim reported their vehicle was broken into overnight.  The suspect stole items from within the car.
At 12:08 pm the victim reports their catalytic converter stolen.

At 7:52 PM, an officer reports an employee noticed a male attempting to leave the business with unpaid merchandise. The employee confronted the suspect and retrieved the property. The suspect initially walked away, but returned and made several verbal threats.

At 11:55 PM officers conducted a traffic enforcement stop and conducted a DUI investigation. The driver was arrested for driving under the influence. During a records check, officers discovered the passenger had two outstanding warrants. The driver was released on a citation and the passenger was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
An officer reports an unknown suspect stole several packages from the victim’s porch. The theft occurred in between 2:41 PM and 4:00 PM.
At 3:09 PM a suspect was arrested for a warrant and possession of drug paraphernalia.  The suspect was cited and released from the police department after booking.
At 8:00 PM an officer reports unknown suspect(s) smashed out a window of the victim’s vehicle and stole property.
At 8:32 AM an officer reports the victim parked their vehicle in front of their home overnight.  In the morning, the vehicle was gone.
At 8:40 PM an officer reports an unknown suspect loaded a shopping cart full of merchandise and exited via the emergency exit without paying.
At 8:05 AM the victim reports that their vehicle was stolen.
At 1:00 PM the victim was walking to her vehicle when two suspects approached her, pushed her to the ground and stole her purse.  The suspects fled the area in a vehicle.
At 12:14PM, the suspect stole items and fled in a vehicle.

At 7:24 PM, officers were dispatched to a theft that had just occurred at the business. An officer located the suspect vehicle and detained both suspects. During an infield show-up the suspects were positively identified and placed under Citizens Arrest. The suspects were booked at the ECPD and released on a citation.

At 3:24 PM four victims were robbed at gunpoint in front of the ATM’s at the Wells Fargo bank.  The suspects took wallets, purses, cell phones and cash.  Flock camera data assisted in identifying the suspect vehicle.  The vehicle was reported stolen from Lafayette PD and had been taken in a Home Invasion Robbery.
At 6:32 PM an officer reports a suspect shattered the glass case containing bottles of liquor. The suspects loaded several duffle bags and exited the business.

At 8:42 AM an officer reports a suspect pried the driver door handle of a vehicle and moved the vehicle from its parking stall in an apparent attempt to steal it.  The suspect was able to roll both windows down, but was not successful in taking the vehicle.  The suspect stole the vehicle registration and a yoga mat from the vehicle.  The suspect caused heavy damage to the ignition of the vehicle. Assault with a Firearm- MacDonald/Mono: At 10:17 PM officers were dispatched to the area for several gunshots and a collision. Upon arrival officers located three victims that were suffering from gunshot wounds.  The victims stated they were driving in Richmond when a suspect shot at them. The victims attempted to evade the suspect and were followed into El Cerrito where the victims collided into a park vehicle. The suspect shot at the victims several more times before fleeing the area.
At 6:32 PM an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed out on of the victim’s window to her rental property.

At 9:48 AM a suspect was confronted by a resident as he attempted to climb in through a bathroom window.  When the victim yelled, the suspect dropped to the ground outside, jumped the fence and ran away.

At 4:05AM the victim was riding his skateboard and hit a parked vehicle.  The owner/suspect exited the vehicle and threatened the victim with a machete.  The suspect fled the area in the vehicle prior to police arrival.
At 6:09AM the suspect shattered the rear window of the victim’s vehicle and stole miscellaneous items.
At 5:17PM the suspect ripped off the display of lotto tickets and fled in a vehicle driven by a second suspect.

CrimeType: Stolen Vehicle/Possession of Stolen Property/Possession of Other Identification
Description: At 3:23PM the El Cerrito Police Department’s FLOCK system alerted to a reported stolen vehicle.  The vehicle was located parked in the DMV parking lot.  An occupant was arrested and found to be on probation out of Alameda County for weapons charges.   The subject was also in possession of a victim’s personal check and a United States Postal Service master mailbox key.  The suspect was booked at the police department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:28AM the victim reports that their vehicle was stolen sometime from July 25th to August 23rd.
At 1:56PM the victim reports their vehicle was stolen sometime August 19th to August 23rd.
At 6:35AM officers accepted the citizen’s arrest of a suspect who damaged property to the business and refused to leave.  The suspect was booked at the police department and released on a citation.
At 4:32PM the suspect entered the victim’s residence via an open window and stole miscellaneous items.
At 8:46AM the suspect stole miscellaneous items and fled on foot.
At 1:24PM. the suspect shattered the front passenger window of the victim’s vehicle and stole a wallet containing two debit cards. The suspect fled in a vehicle southbound towards Stockton Ave.

Location: San Pablo Ave. at Potrero Ave.
Description: At 3:18PM the police departments FLOCK system alerted to a stolen vehicle traveling on San Pablo Ave.  Officers located the vehicle and arrested two suspects inside of it.  Both suspects were booked at the El Cerrito Police Department.  The driver was transported to County Jail and the passenger was released.
The victim’s rear rims and tires were stolen off their vehicle from August 13th to August 17th.

At 3:21PM the victim’s rear license plate was stolen. Auto Burglary – 670 El Cerrito Plaza (CVS) – At 4:00 PM a suspect shattered the driver’s side window of the victims vehicle and stole items from inside.
At 6:47 AM an officer contacted a subject and learned they had an outstanding warrant for their arrest.  They were arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:32PM the victim reported being pushed against a railing inside of the store.  The suspect was given a citation.
At 11:47 AM an officer was sent to the report that the victim had paid someone money for services that were never completed.
A person reported to an officer that they had left personal property inside of the business the day before and when they returned to get their property they found someone had stolen it.
At 4:11 PM Officers recovered a stolen vehicle in the back parking lot of the El Cerrito Police Department after receiving a Flock camera alert.

At 11:28AM store security confronted the suspect at the door for stealing items.  The suspect hit the security guard, and the security guard pepper sprayed the suspect.   The suspect was arrested, treated at the hospital, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:36PM, while assisting the victim online with a transaction, the suspect as able to withdraw money from the victim’s bank account.

At 7:31AM, when the victim confronted two suspects breaking into a parked vehicle, one suspect pointed a handgun at the victim and the other suspect brandished a handgun in his waistband at the victim.  The suspects fled in a vehicle.
At 12:33PM the victim reports their vehicle was stolen while her son was driving it Southern California.
At 10:33AM an officer located a parked unoccupied stolen vehicle.

Location: San Pablo Ave at Potrero Ave.
Description: At 6:14AM officers responded to the area for a naked female crying and asking for help.  Officers contacted the victim who stated she was picked up by the suspect in Oakland.  While in his vehicle, the suspect pointed a gun at the victim and sexually assaulted her.  The victim was transported to the hospital.
At 4:24PM the suspect exited the store with unpaid merchandise and fled in a vehicle.
At 7:36AM the victim reported their vehicle was stolen. Richmond Police Officers found the vehicle parked unoccupied in their city.  The vehicle was released to the owner.
At 9:53AM the victim reported their vehicle was stolen sometime last night.
At 7:29AM the victims reported that the suspects broke into their vehicle and stole miscellaneous items.  The suspects fled in a vehicle.
At 10:41AM an officer arrested a suspect on a theft warrant.  The suspect was booked at the police department and released on a citation.
At 11:05 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole property from the victim’s unlocked vehicle. The theft occurred in between 5:00 PM and 5:15 PM.

At 11:29 AM, El Cerrito Police Officers received a Flock hit on a stolen Hyundai Elantra traveling northbound on San Pablo Avenue at Potrero Avenue.  Officers circulated in an attempt to locate the vehicle.  A records check confirmed the vehicle was reported stolen to Oakland PD on 8/31/2023. A sergeant located the vehicle occupied in the Safeway parking lot.  A traffic stop was attempted and a brief vehicle pursuit ensued but was terminated when the vehicle entered the eastbound I-80 off ramp against traffic.

While investigating the other vehicle thefts at this location, an officer discovered a vehicle unoccupied and running in the parking lot.  The right front window was broken out and the ignition was destroyed.  The vehicle was an unreported stolen vehicle out of the City of Alameda.  Flock showed the vehicle entering the city at 2:49 AM.  A courtesy vehicle theft report was taken to be forwarded to Alameda PD, and the vehicle was recovered.

An officer reports the theft of a vehicle which was captured on Ring video at 3:10 AM.  The victim left the vehicle unlocked, and a suspect appeared to be ransacking the vehicle when he located the key that was hidden inside.  The suspects arrived in another vehicle, which fled along with the victim vehicle.
An officer reports the theft of a vehicle from the rear parking lot between 6:00 PM on 9/2 and 11:15 AM on 9/3.

Unidentified suspect(s) entered a house under renovation via an unlocked window and stole power tools sometime between 3:30 PM on 9/1 and 8:00 AM on 9/2. Evading Police- Liberty/ Gladys: At 2:12 AM, an officer was on patrol and noticed a vehicle driving the wrong direction on a one way street. The officer activated his emergency lights in an attempt to conduct a traffic enforcement stop. The vehicle accelerated and began to drive recklessly. Due to safety concerns to the public the officer elected not to pursue the vehicle. The officer observed the vehicle from a distance and noticed it was involved in a solo vehicle collision. Upon arrival witnesses advised the suspect had fled the scene. While processing the vehicle for evidence, officers located several burglary tools commonly used for catalytic converter thefts.

At 5:15 AM, the victim called the Dispatch Center and stated she heard noises in her kitchen located on the first floor of the residence and believed someone was inside her home. Officers arrived on scene and had the victim exit the front door of the residence. As the victim was exiting her residence, the suspect followed closely behind her. When the suspect saw officers outside the residence he quickly shut the front door and locked it. Officers gave the suspect several commands to exit the residence but he refused. Officers located an unsecured window where the suspect had entered the residence from and made entry. Officers safely escorted the other occupants of the residence who were asleep out of the residence. A Canine Officer arrived on scene and gave several commands to the suspect to surrender. The suspect continued to refuse and the Canine was deployed. The canine made contact with the suspect and officers were able to safely take the suspect into custody.  The suspect was transported to the hospital for medical clearance before being transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Officers responded to a suspicious circumstance call and learned the victim had chased a suspect out of his vehicle around 3:10 AM.  He did not call police at the time, and he was not concerned until his automatic garage door opened again around 9:30 AM.  The garage door remote from the victim vehicle was located in the street where the suspect had entered the passenger seat of the suspect vehicle.
An officer reports the theft of a vehicle from the street in front of the address between 9:56 PM on 9/2 and 10:42 AM on 9/3.  The vehicle hit on the Flock camera at Potrero and S56th at 3:14 AM.
An adult tricycle was stolen from in front of the victim’s residence approximately 1:40 PM, by an unidentified suspect.
At 5:11 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed the window of the front door to gain entry into the business and stole property.
At 8:05 AM the victim reports that sometime last night an unknown suspect shattered the driver’s side window to their vehicle.
At 7:47 PM the victim reported that their vehicle was stolen during the night.  Alameda PD found the vehicle abandoned in the middle of the street in their city.
At 2:37 PM the victim’s purse was stolen from her cart, while she was shopping inside Safeway.
At 7:56 PM officers conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle and arrested the driver for an outstanding warrant.  The driver was later transported to county jail on the warrant.
At 8:19 PM officers responded to a shoplifting at the business.  Two suspects stole merchandise and fled the business prior to police arrival.
At 3:54 PM the victim tracked their stolen vehicle to this location.  Officers located the vehicle parked unoccupied and released it to the victim.
At 6:18 PM the victim reported their electric bicycle was stolen from their side yard sometime overnight.

At 4:59 PM officers located a subject slumped behind the wheel of his vehicle.  The subject showed signs of being under the influence of narcotics and was placed under arrest for DUI.  The subject was transported to Kaiser Richmond by ambulance and released on a citation at the hospital.
At 3:42 PM an officer arrested the subject for a warrant out of Solano County.  The subject was booked at the police department and released on a citation.
The victim reported their vehicle was stolen this morning from 8:00 AM to 9:50 AM.
At 3:01 PM the victim reports that an unknown suspect kicked the front door to their residence causing damage.

Location: El Dorado Ave / San Pablo Ave
Description: At 7:09 AM an officer arrested a woman during a check of a suspicious vehicle with no plates.  The woman had a checkbook in her possession that belonged to someone else.  Contact with the victim revealed several months ago, the woman had tried to cash one of the checks.  The woman was field cited and the vehicle was impounded.
At 8:35 PM an officer reports store employees contacted a female who was opening packages of unpaid merchandise and asked her to stop. The female exited the store with several of the items.
At 5:20 PM a suspect stole merchandise and fled the store.

At 6:45 PM an officer reports a witness heard a banging noise coming from outside his residence. When he went to check on the noise, he noticed a male suspect sitting in the driver’s seat of the victim’s vehicle. Moments later the suspect drove the victim’s vehicle away. Officers located video of the incident and noticed the previous reported stolen vehicle was at the scene. Evading the Police- Carlson Blvd./I-80 While circulating for the above suspects an officer located the first stolen vehicle and suspect vehicle. The vehicles entered onto I-80. The officer activated his emergency equipment in an attempt to conduct an enforcement stop when the vehicles sped away. The suspect vehicle led the officers on a short pursuit and exited the Hilltop offramp and began to drive erratically on city streets. Officers terminated the pursuit due to public safety concerns.

At 6:18 PM an officer reports the victim heard her vehicle alarm sounding in front of her residence. The victim went outside to her vehicle and saw a male suspect sitting inside the driver’s seat of her vehicle. The victim went inside to call the police to report the incident when the suspect drove away.

At 11:14 PM officers responded to a possible collision in the area. Officers located a vehicle with  damage in the middle of the roadway. Officers contacted the driver and noticed she had objective signs of intoxication. An officer conducted a DUI investigation and determined the driver was under the influence of alcohol. The driver was transported to the ECPD for booking. After booking, she was issued a citation and released to a sober relative.
At 7:53 PM an officer reports three suspects entered the business and randomly selected merchandise and fled the store without paying.
At 3:11 PM a suspect stole merchandise and fled the store.
At 4:02 AM, a suspect shattered the right front window of the victim’s vehicle and ransacked the interior, stealing items.
At 8:10 AM, a neighbor saw an unidentified suspect drive off in the victim’s 2019 vehicle.
A suspect shattered the right front window of the victim’s vehicle and ransacked the interior but took nothing at 0341 hours.

At 8:52 AM, a suspect used the victim’s stolen credit card at Walgreen’s to buy gift cards.  The card was in a wallet that was reported stolen in Pinole, and the victim was referred to ECPD for the fraudulent card use.

CrimeType: Recovered Stolen Vehicles / Possess Stolen Property
Description: At 9:05 AM, El Cerrito units received a Flock hit for two stolen vehicles traveling southbound on Carlson Blvd at San Jose Ave.  An officer located both vehicles parked in the Nation’s lot, and the female driver of one of the vehicles was arrested.  The male driver of the other vehicle was also arrested as he came out of Nation’s.
At 7:14PM victim was walking northbound on the BART path when the suspect approached her from behind and removed a cellphone from the victim’s pants pockets.
At 11:06PM officers arrested a suspect who shattered the windows to the business.  The suspect was booked at El Cerrito PD and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 1:46PM an officer conducted a suspicious vehicle check and arrested the passenger for being in violation of a court protection order.  The subject was booked at the police department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:12PM a suspect stole the victims’ mail from their mailbox.

CrimeType: Stolen Vehicle/Hit and Run/Fleeing from Police
Description: At 7:50PM the Flock system alerted to a stolen vehicle in the south end of the City.  An officer located it parked in front of the CVS store.  When officers attempted to stop the vehicle, the driver drove on the sidewalk, striking two pillars, and fled out of the El Cerrito Plaza
At 8:50PM, the victim’s vehicle was stolen from in front of their residence.
At 11:52AM a technician located a skimming device in an ATM machine.
At 9:52AM an unknown suspect threw a rock through the front window of a residence.

At 9:18PM the suspect attempted to steal a bottle of wine from the store.  When the employee confronted them, the suspect threw the bottle at the employee.  Officer located the suspect and placed him under arrest.  The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 6:22PM the elderly victim was scammed by two suspects inside the store.  The suspects told the victim they won a large some of money and needed assistance donating it.  They asked the victim to donate some also, so the victim withdrew cash from their bank and gave it to the suspects.  The suspects fled the area with the victim’s money.
At 8:49PM two suspects filled two bags worth of merchandise and fled the store with the items after their check was declined.

At 8:06PM after leaving the ATM machine, the suspect took the victims wallet and fled in a vehicle. Attempted Carjacking - 6050 El Cerrito Plaza-IFO Barnes and Noble: At 9:03PM while the victim was seated in her parked vehicle, the suspect vehicle blocked her in.  The suspect then tried to open the victim’s door handled but it was locked.  The suspect then fled the area.

At 8:29PM, three suspects were confronted by Security guards when they stole items from the store.  The suspects threatened to kill and shoot the security guards.  One suspect pulled out a taser and threatened them with it.  The security guards pepper sprayed the suspect, who then fled towards the Del Norte BART station.  Two of the three suspects were caught by responding officers, and one fled the area.  One suspect had a warrant for robbery. The suspects were booked at ECPD and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:16PM the suspect stole merchandise and fled in a vehicle.

At 2:52AM officers were sent to the report of a fire.  They found several bushes on fire.  THe El Cerrito Fire Department resopondedd and extniguished the fires.  It is unknown who set the bushes on fire.
At 6:30PM an officer reports that the license plate from the victim's vehicle was stolen between September 12th and 15th.

CrimeType: Possession of a Controlled Substance/ Warrant Arres
Location: San Pablo Ave at Blake St
Description: At 8:42 PM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver of the vehicle for an outstanding warrant out of Orange County. During a search incident to arrest, the driver was found to be in possession of methamphetamine and a glass pipe. The driver was issued a citation on the new charges and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility for the warrant.
An officer reports an unknown suspect stole several items from a display and fled the store. The theft occurred between 4:00-4:10 PM.
At 10:29 AM the victim reported that during a party on Saturday 9/16 an acquaintance inappropriately touched them.
After investigating the auto burglary on the 500 block of Elm St., Officers noticed another auto burglary.  The suspect broke the window of the vehicle and rummaged through the vehicle.

At 2:19 PM the victim was shopping when the suspect touched them inappropriately.  Officers detained the suspect, but the victim refused to place them under citizen’s arrest.  The suspect was released.
At 3:15 AM the victim noticed a suspect inside of their vehicle.  Nothing was taken from the vehicle.
At 7:18 PM the victim reports that their vehicle was burglarized. The suspect used the garage door opener in the victim’s car to enter the garage and stole additional items.
At 7:03 PM A witness reported a suspect sprayed graffiti on the back wall of the theater.
At 4:46 PM the victim reports that an unknown suspect entered their unlocked vehicle and stole items.
At 10:29 AM the suspect contacted the victim via their cellphone.  The suspect gained remote access of the victim’s cellphone and wired money from the victim’s bank account to theirs.
At 9:08 AM an officer located an unoccupied reported stolen vehicle.  The vehicle was impounded  and the victim was notified.
At 6:26 PM the victim reports a suspect broke the window to their vehicle and damaged the ignition in an attempt to steal.
At 7:54 AM the victim reports that someone entered their vehicle and stole miscellaneous items.
An officer reports the theft of a 2003 Chevy Astro van between midnight and 11 AM on 9/21.
At 2:42 PM a suspect threw items at a Ross employee because she was upset over the process to complete a return.

At 11:08 AM an officer attempted a traffic stop on a vehicle but it failed to yield.  Due to public safety concerns the officer did not pursue, and the vehicle was last seen southbound past Columbia Avenue.  At 11:34 AM the female passenger of the vehicle called 911 to report the driver had fled the vehicle and left her behind on Huntington Ave just west of San Pablo Ave, after he had circled back to the area.  The driver was not located.

At 1:57 PM an officer reports the arrest of a woman after she stole items, then threatened Loss Prevention with pepper spray when they attempted to stop her.  The woman was placed under arrest, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 6:00 PM officers were dispatched to Starbucks in regards to a male subject cussing, throwing rocks and spray painting on the order board sign. He was last seen going towards the Home Depot. Officers located the subject and detained him. During an infield show-up the male was positively identified as the suspect and placed under Citizen’s Arrest. During a records check officers discovered the suspect had an outstanding warrant. After booking, the suspect was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:20 AM an officer arrested a subject on two no bail warrants.  The subject was arrested, booked at ECPD, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 11:01 AM an officer reports the theft of the rear license plate from a vehicle between 8:00 PM on 9/23 and 9:30 AM on 9/24.
At 12:09PM the suspect stole merchandise and fled.
At 10:58AM the victim reports that their front license plate from their vehicle was stolen.

CrimeType: Stolen Vehicle and Possession of Fentanyl
Description: At 1:47PM an officer located a stolen occupied vehicle parked behind the gas station.  The driver and passenger were arrested.  The driver was in possession of 5.2 grams of fentanyl.  The passenger was released at the scene.  The driver was booked at ECPD and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Location: San Mateo St. at Central Ave.
Description: At 2:08M the El Cerrito PD Flock System (Automated License Plate Reader) alerted to a stolen vehicle traveling southbound on San Pablo Ave from Potrero Ave.  Officers located the vehicle speeding away westbound on El Dorado Ave towards San Mateo St.  The vehicle was located parked unoccupied on San Mateo Ave at Central Ave. and the driver of the vehicle was not located.  The vehicle was recovered, and the owner was notified. Grand Theft  – 1300 block of Rifle Range Rd. At 3:18PM AT&T reported that approximately 30 feet of copper wire had been stolen from the telephone pole wires.

At 5:42PM the suspect showed up to his ex-girlfriends residence intoxicated and demanding to see his child.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the police department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:51AM the victim reported their rear license plate from their vehicle was stolen.
At 7:28AM the suspect entered the victim’s backyard and stole a bike.  The victim chased after the suspect and retrieved his bike however the suspect got away.
At 8:04AM the reporting party discovered the front glass door to the business was shattered.
At 6:08AM the victim reports that their driver’s side window on their vehicle was shattered, and their purse was stolen.

At 9:30AM the Santa Rita Jail advised El Cerrito PD that a suspect that had a warrant for a prior stolen vehicle case in El Cerrito was being held there.  An El Cerrito Police Officer transported the suspect from Santa Rita Jail to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:45PM The suspect threw an object and shattered the front window of a residence. The suspect was arrested, booked at ECPD and released to a family member.

At 1:59PM a suspect approached the victim, pointed a firearm at them, and ripped the victim’s watch off their wrist.  The suspect fired the handgun during the Robbery however the victim was not struck by the bullet.  The suspect fled the area in a vehicle.
At 11:19AM the victim reports their front license plate to their vehicle was stolen.
At 11:45 PM an officer reports a suspect attempted to steal the victim’s vehicle but was unsuccessful and stole property from inside the vehicle.
At 10:50 AM an officer reports a front license plate was stolen from the victim’s vehicle.
Between 7:30 PM on 09/28/23 and 7:30 AM on 09/29/23, a victim reports their vehicle was broken into and items were taken from their vehicle.
At 6:58 AM an officer reports a window of the business was broken.  A suspect entered and stole merchandise and then fled.

At 12:10 AM an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested a passenger for an outstanding warrant. After the passenger was booked at ECPD, she was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:07 AM an officer reports the victim’s vehicle was taken from outside their residence sometime overnight.
At 7:00 AM an officer reports a suspect attempted to steal the victim’s vehicle from outside their residence.

CrimeType: DUI/ False ID to an Officer/ Warrant
Description: At 11:52 PM an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for driving under the influence. During the investigation, the driver gave the officer a false name. Upon learning of the driver’s true identity, a records check was conducted, and the officer discovered they had six outstanding warrants. After booking, the driver was issued a citation for the DUI and was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility for the warrants.

CrimeType: Warrants/Disobey an Officer/Battery/Possession of a Controlled Substanc
Description: At 8:04 AM an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During a records check the officer discovered the driver had three outstanding warrants for their arrest. During a search incident to arrest the driver was found to be in possession of fentanyl. The driver was placed under arrest and transported to ECPD for booking. During booking the driver said they were ill and requested medical personnel. While officers and medical personnel were providing the driver with medical treatment, they began kicking at officers. The driver was transported to a local hospital for further medical treatment. While at the hospital the driver attempted several times to bite a nurse who was providing them with medical treatment. Once medically cleared, the driver was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Hit and Run/ Recovered Stolen Motorcycl
Description: At 6:15 PM an officer reports the victim was driving northbound on Arlington Blvd. when a motorcycle was traveling in the southbound lane. As the rider was making a turn around the curve, he veered into the northbound lane and collided with the victim. After briefly speaking with the victim, the rider pushed the motorcycle southbound on Arlington Blvd. As officers were responding to the scene, another caller advised the motorcycle was left in front of his residence blocking the driveway. An officer conducted a records check of the Vehicle identification number and discovered the motorcycle was reported stolen to Oakland PD on 9/7/2023.
At 3:10 PM the victim reports that their vehicle was stolen.
At 1:45 PM the victim reports that their catalytic converter was stolen from their vehicle.
At 6:44 PM the victim reports that a suspect shattered the driver’s side window to their vehicle, rummaged through it but did not steal anything.  Occurred between 10/1/23 – 10/3/23.
At 8:57 PM the victim reports their rear license plate was stolen from their vehicle while at the restaurant.
At 7:35 AM an officer located a reported stolen vehicle.  The vehicle was recovered, and the owner was notified.

CrimeType: Criminal Threats/Assault with a Deadly Weapon
Description: At 8:35 AM the victim reports that two months ago, on separate occasions, his former landlord pointed a gun at him up and held a knife to his throat, threatening to kill him.
At 11:43 AM the victim reported their vehicle was stolen last night between 8PM to 1AM.

At 2:07 PM officers were dispatched to the report of a subject passed out inside of a vehicle.  The suspect was in possession of 7.2 grams of crack cocaine.  The suspect was transported to the hospital for a possible overdose.

At 2:54 AM officers were dispatched to a welfare check of a subject loitering in front of the business.  The subject had an outstanding burglary warrant for his arrest.  The subject was arrested and later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:51 PM the suspect entered the store, stole merchandise, and fled in a vehicle towards Carlson Blvd.

At 1:06 AM officers were dispatched to the area in regards to a suspicious person. Officers located the subject wandering in the area and made contact with them. A records check was conducted on the subject and officers discovered he had an outstanding warrant out of Alameda County. The subject was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:17 AM Officers report a suspect broke the front door with a rock.

At 1:13 PM Officers were dispatched to Atlas liquors in response to two males fighting.  Officers arrived and contacted a male physically fighting with another male inside of the store.  It was discovered one of the males was the protected party in a restraining order with the other male as the restrained.  The restrained party was heavily intoxicated, was placed under arrest, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:01 AM Officers report an office window was broken.

Location: San Pablo Ave at Bart Path
Description: At 4:34 PM a victim reported a suspect approached them while holding a metal pole and demanded property.  When the victim did not provide anything, the suspect struck them twice with a two foot long metal pole.
At 7:13 AM Officers report the victim’s vehicle was vandalized causing approximately $16,000 in damages.
At 5:16 PM the victim reported their rear license plate was stolen off their vehicle between 4:10 PM and 4:30 PM.
At 11:41 PM an officer reports the victim was confronted by two suspects who demanded their property. When the victim refused to give up any property the suspects began to beat them.
At 12:29 AM an officer reports three victims were confronted by an armed suspect who demanded their property.  The suspect stole property and fled the scene.

At 7:11 PM a victim advised a group of 10- 15 juveniles to not block the sidewalk with their bicycles. Two of the juveniles confronted the victim and assaulted him with a metal tool and fled the scene.
At 3:32 PM the victim reports their vehicle was stolen from in front of their residence.
The victim reports that between 11PM on 10/06/23 to 8:30AM on 10/07/23 a suspect stole three tires and rims off their vehicle.
At 4:53 AM four suspects entered through the front door of Classy Nail Salon’s business and stole an ATM and the cash register.

At 10:20AM officers placed a subject under citizen’s arrest after he refused to leave the restaurant. The male would not provide officers with his true identifying information.  After being transported to the Martinez County Jail, the subject’s true identity was revealed.

CrimeType: Burglary/Unauthorized Use of Credit Card/Identity Theft
Description: At 2:05PM the victim’s backpack was stolen from their locked locker.  Staff later found the backpack, but the victim’s purse, keys, wallet containing miscellaneous cards were stolen.   The victim’s credit card was used in El Cerrito and at another store in Richmond.
At 10:18 AM an officer contacted a subject and found a Methamphetamine pipe on their person.  The subject was issued a citation and released from the scene.

Location: San Pablo Ave at Eastshore Blvd.
Description: At 2:58 PM an officer made a traffic stop and arrested the driver for a warrant.  The driver was booked at the police department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:57 PM a suspect stole merchandise and fled the scene.

At 8:37 PM officers conducted a traffic stop.  The driver had an outstanding misdemeanor warrant for their arrest from Orange County.  The driver was issued a citation for the warrant and released at the scene.
On October 9th at 12:30 PM the victim was shopping at Safeway when her wallet was stolen from inside her purse.
At 11:12 AM the victim reports that the sunroof on their vehicle was shattered and miscellaneous items were stolen from inside.
At 1:27 PM an officer arrested a subject for an arrest warrant.  The subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:14 PM officers responded to a shoplifting that had just occurred.  Officers detained a subject who was identified by store employees and placed under citizens’ arrest.  The subject is also on active felony probation out of Contra Costa County.  The subject was later transported to county jail in Martinez.
At 3:16 PM the suspect stole miscellaneous items, pushed their way past an employee and fled the store.
At 3:54 PM an officer contacted the subject and discovered they had an outstanding warrant for their arrest.  The subject was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 10:50 AM the suspect threatened to kill the victim, while reaching into his waistband.   The suspect was contacted a short distance away and placed under arrest.  No weapon was located.  The suspect was booked at the police department and released on a citation.
