At 8:30 AM AT&T reports that approximately 75 ft of copper wire was stolen from the power lines.

At 6:17 PM an officer reports a male attempted to leave the store with a cart full of merchandise and was contacted by security. The male brandished a knife and threatened the security officer while leaving the store with merchandise.
An officer reports an unknown suspect attempted to steal the victim’s vehicle. When unsuccessful, the suspect stole property.
The suspect stole approximately 1000 feet of copper wire cable.  The incident occurred from October 9th to October 13th.
At 5:33 AM an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.

An officer reports that between the hours of 2300 hrs. on 10/13/23 and 1200 hrs. on 10/14/23, the victim’s garage was opened via garage door opener and an electric bicycle was stolen from inside of the garage.  The suspect gained access to the opener via an unlocked vehicle in the driveway.

At 8:34 PM an officer reports a suspect attempted to leave the store with merchandise when employees confronted him. During a struggle over the handbasket, the suspect pulled out a knife and threatened employees. The employees retained the merchandise and the suspect fled.
At 10:00 AM the victim noticed the rear driver side window was shattered and the steering column was damaged.
At 4:43 PM the victim reported approximately 50 ft of copper wire was stolen between October 11th at 5PM to October 15th at 12:00PM
At 6:30 AM a suspect shattered the window of the victim’s vehicle and stole items.

Sometime between October 15th at 11 PM to October 16th at 8 AM, the suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle, stole items and opened the victims garage door with the garage door opener. The suspect entered the garage and stole a custom bike.

At 7:19 PM an officer received a Flock notification (Automated License Plate Reader camera) for a stolen vehicle. Officers circulated the area and located the stolen vehicle occupied. Officers conducted an enforcement stop and took the driver into custody. During a records check, it was discovered the driver had an outstanding warrant. After booking the driver was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:18 PM an officer reports an unknown suspect broke into the victim’s vehicle and stole property.
At 7:19 PM an officer reports a suspect stole the victims vehicle.
At 10:02 PM an officer reports a suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.

At 11:34 PM, a San Pablo PD officer pursued a stolen vehicle into El Cerrito but terminated the pursuit after the speeds reached over 100MPH. ECPD officers circulated the area and located the vehicle unoccupied and recovered it.
At 12:30 AM an officer reported two suspects broke into the victim’s vehicle and stole property.

Location: Frannie Express Hawaiian BBQ 11775 San Pablo Ave
Description: At 9:01AM the owner reported that the front door was pried open, and the suspects stole cash from the register.

Location: El Mono Restaurant 11720 San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 5:42AM officers responded to a burglary alarm at the business and noticed the front door was pried open. No one was located inside, and the cash register was stolen.

At 6:01AM a reporting party saw the front glass door shattered to the business.  Four suspects were seen fleeing from inside the business and fled in a dark-colored vehicle.  Several bottles of alcohol were stolen.
At 10:49AM the victim reports an unknown suspect withdrew money from his account.  A fraudulent check was also made with the victims account number and deposited in another account.

At 2:56PM officers arrested a subject after he was placed under citizen’s arrest for stealing shoes, and had a warrant for theft out of Fairfield PD.  The subject was booked police station and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:57PM officers arrested a subject for driving while under the influence of alcohol.  The subject was booked at the police station and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 12:17AM, officers conducted a suspicious vehicle check of a car running and partially blocking the roadway.  The driver was asleep in the driver’s seat and admitted to consuming alcohol.  The driver was arrested and later cited out to a friend at ECPD.
At 8:29AM the owner reports that the front door the business was pried open.  The suspects damaged the coin dispenser and stole the coins ATM kiosk that contained cash.
At 9:29PM the victim reported that the rear window of his vehicle had been shattered.

At 2:47AM, officers were dispatched to a reported sexual battery.  During the investigation, it was determined a verbal argument took place between a husband and wife.  During the argument, the husband attempted to punch the wife but missed.  The husband pushed a shopping cart at a male friend of the wife, striking him.  The subject was placed under citizen’s arrest, transported back to ECPD, and later released on a citation.
At 3:20AM, officers were flagged down in the parking lot of the police department.  The subject had a misdemeanor warrant for his arrest.  The subject was released on a citation.
At 1PM an unwanted subject returned and refused to leave.  The subject was arrested but transported to the hospital for an unknown medical condition.

Location: Portola Dr and San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 7:17AM an officer arrested a subject for possession of drug paraphernalia.  The subject was released on a citation.

At 1:54AM, officers were dispatched to a reported battery.  The suspect was arrested after threatening to kill his brother and then assaulting him.  The suspect was later transported to the county jail in Martinez.
At 7:38AM an officer reported that six vehicles had their rear drivers’ side windows broken out.
At 9:38PM the victim reports that a window to their vehicle was shattered.
At 9:07AM the victim reports their vehicle's windows were shattered.
At 2:38AM, officers located an unoccupied stolen vehicle reported to Vallejo PD.  The vehicle was recovered and towed.
At 12:23 PM, two female teens stole wigs.  When the business owner tried to take the wigs as the teens walked toward the door, both suspects yanked the wigs from her hands.
An unidentified suspect shattered the window of the victim’s vehicle while the victim was in the store shopping, sometime between 3:00-3:45 PM.
A 2013 white Kia Optima was stolen between 12:00 on 10/15 and 7:00 PM on 10/19.
At 1:21 PM, an officer reports the arrest of a man on four arrest warrants to county jail.
A 2003 GMC Yukon was stolen sometime between noon on 10/20 and 6:00 AM on 10/21.
At 1:55 AM, an officer was flagged down by the victim who stated an employee of the business pushed him.
At 1:12 PM, a woman was robbed of cash by use of a simulated weapon.
At 9:04 PM, an officer reported unknown suspects threw rocks and eggs at the victim’s house.
At 10:43 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.
At 11:07 AM the reporting called police after he found that over the weekend the suspect entered the school and spray painted graffiti in the classrooms.

Location: Home Depot parking lot 11939 San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 10:50 AM the victim was standing in the parking lot when the suspect drove by him and brandished a firearm at the victim.  The suspect was placed under citizen’s arrest. Officers were unable to locate the firearm.  The suspect was booked at the police department and released on a citation.

At 3:49 PM several juveniles and an adult assaulted another juvenile.  The suspects fled prior to police arrival.  The victim was transported to the hospital by ambulance for their injuries, and their parents were notified.

At 7:17 PM officers were dispatched to a battery.  Upon further investigation, the subject attempted to steal items from the business and was confronted by security.  The subject attempted to fight with security and flee the store.  The subject was detained, arrested and later transported to county jail.
At 10:20 PM the victim reported leaving his wallet at the business.  The suspect found the wallet and attempted to make several online purchases, but it was declined.
At 12:49 PM the suspect shattered the front glass door with a large cinder block, and fled the area on foot.
At 9:15 AM an officer recovered an unoccupied stolen vehicle out of Santa Clara PD.  The vehicle was towed and the owner was notified.
At 7:56 AM the victim discovered that their driver side window was shattered, and tools were stolen.

At 5:27 PM officers were dispatched to a vandalism.  Upon further investigation, the subject attempted to steal items from the business and was confronted by employees.  The subject dropped the items and hit employees.  The subject was detained, arrested and later transported to county jail.
At 6:25 PM the suspect was captured on video surveillance stealing packages from the victim’s residence.  The suspect fled the area prior to police arrival.
At 10:05 AM the suspect got into an argument with the victim and spat in the victim’s face.  The suspect fled in their vehicle prior to police arrival.
At 11:52 AM the victim reported that someone stole their vehicle.
At 7:49 AM the reporting party advised that someone stole their vehicle sometime last night.
At 10:03 AM the victim reported that their vehicle’s rear driver side window was shattered.
At 12:02PM, a suspect placed a skimming device on one of the ATM’s.
At 3:15 PM the suspect entered the victim’s residence through an unsecured door and stole miscellaneous items.
At 4:34 PM the victim reported that their vehicle was stolen.
At 4:34 AM the victim reports 15k worth of items were taken from their vehicle sometime overnight.
At 11:20 AM the victim’s side window on their vehicle was shattered.
At 12:58 PM the suspect wrote graffiti with a permanent marker on the victim’s driveway.

At 8:23 AM a CSO reports the theft of a vehicle sometime after 1815 hours on 10/26. Recovered Stolen Vehicle / Felony Evading / Parole Violation / Warrants /  San Pablo Ave at Fairmount Ave: At 9:29 and 9:30 AM, Flock (automated License Plate Reader cameras) hits were received for a stolen vehicle last seen southbound on Ashbury Ave at Central Ave.  The vehicle was located at San Pablo Ave and Fairmount Ave and a traffic stop was initiated on San Pablo Ave at Moeser Lane. The vehicle failed to yield and a vehicle pursuit ensued, eventually ending when the suspect vehicle lost control and came to rest against a sound wall on SR37 in Vallejo.  The driver fled on foot and was apprehended by a police K-9. The driver was arrested and they were transported to the hospital to be treated for superficial dog bite wounds.  They were later transported to county jail.
At 1:28 PM an officer reports the theft of a vehicle taken over the previous 10 minutes.

At 8:40 PM an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the employees’ locker room and stole an employee backpack containing her ATM card. The suspect later attempted to use the card at the Berkeley BART station.

At 1:59 PM a customer was denied entry to the business because he did not have an ID.  The man became irate and challenged the security guards to a fight.  He then picked up a rock about 7 inches across and threw it at the security guard, striking him on the wrist and causing a contusion and abrasions.

At 11:17 PM, officers were dispatched to the area for a collision. Upon arrival officers located a driver who had collided into four parked vehicles. During the investigation officers determined the driver was under the influence of an alcoholic beverage. The driver was arrested and transported to the ECPD for booking. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a relative.

At 6:59 PM an officer reports an employee requested a customer show him her identification card before being admitted into the business. When the customer refused, the employee pushed the person out of the business and the customer spat in the employee’s face.

Location: San Pablo Ave. and Tehama Ave
Description: At 2:22 AM, officers conducted a traffic enforcement stop and noticed the driver had objective signs of being under the influence. Officers conducted a DUI investigation and arrested the driver for driving under the influence of an alcoholic beverage. The driver was transported to the ECPD for booking. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a family member.
At 10:17 AM an unidentified suspect “poked” the victim with a screw causing no injury, then threw an empty detergent bottle at the victim, striking her on the leg and again causing no injury.
At 10:49 AM an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole items from the victim’s vehicle between midnight and 10:45 AM.
At 8:38 AM an officer reports the theft of a vehicle from the car lot just after 5:00 AM.
At 6:42 AM an officer reports the theft of a vehicle sometime overnight.  The vehicle was recovered by Kensington PD in the early morning hours after a brief pursuit and collision.
At 4:24 PM the victim was tricked into giving the suspect a large sum of money under false pretenses.

At 10:38 AM an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of an unwanted person by Plaza security for trespassing.  The man had been camped out in the plaza nightly, and refused several orders by security to leave the property.  He was becoming more aggressive with security each morning, and began threatening to hit the security guard if not left alone.  The man was field cited and escorted from the property with an admonishment to not return.

Business: East Bay Korean Christian Reformed Church
Description: At 11:09 AM the reporting party found the suspect inside the churches locked shed.  The suspect fled westbound on Potrero Ave. prior to police arrival.  Part of the children’s play structure was damaged as well.
At 3:41 PM three suspects entered the store and stole merchandise and fled.

Location: Portola Dr at San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 8:19PM, officers located a felony vehicle after receiving a flock hit.  The vehicle was listed as a Felony vehicle out of Gilroy PD.  Officers attempted a traffic stop on the vehicle and it fled.  A pursuit was not initiated due to safety concerns.

CrimeType: Possession of Controlled Substances, Possession of Drug Paraphernalia and Warrant Arres
Description: At 11:43 AM an officer contacted a suspicious person that was possibly associated with a burglary.  The subject was found to have a warrant for his arrest out of South San Francisco for drug related charges.  The subject was arrested, and during a search of his person officers located methamphetamines and drug paraphernalia.  The subject was booked at the police department and released on a citation on all his charges.

At 7:03 AM officers responded to a report of a vehicle driving recklessly in the area.  Upon arrival it was discovered the suspect had crashed into another vehicle.  The driver fled the scene and parked around the corner.  The driver was found and later arrested for drunk driving and hit & run.  The driver was later transported to county jail.

At 6:54 PM officers responded to a report of vandalism.  The suspect and victim were in a verbal argument when the suspect hit the victim’s truck causing damage.  The case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office.
At 4:48 PM citizens reported an internet outage in the area.  AT&T arrived and found that 30 feet of wire had been cut from the utility pole and stolen.

At 7:06 PM officers responded to a report of a theft.  Two suspects stole items from the business and when confronted by staff, one of the suspects brandished a taser.  Both suspects fled prior to police arrival.

At 3:53 PM an officer was dispatched to a lobby detail regarding a Home Invasion Robbery that occurred about half an hour prior.  The victim’s cell phone was among the property taken, and he was not able to call 911, so he came to the PD lobby to make the report. Possession of a Stolen Vehicle/Felon in Possession of a Loaded Firearm/Evading: At 3:56 AM an officer received a Flock notification of a stolen vehicle. The officer circulated the area and located the occupied vehicle in the Safeway parking lot. The driver noticed the officer and quickly sped away. Officers pursued the vehicle onto eastbound I-80. The vehicle exited at San Pablo Dam Rd. and continued to evade officers at a high rate of speed. While near the Orinda city limit line the suspect vehicle was involved in a solo vehicle collision. Officers detained the suspect as she exited the vehicle.  During a search of the vehicle, a loaded firearm commonly known as a “ghost gun” was located inside the vehicle. Officers conducted a records check and discovered the suspect was currently on felony probation out of Alameda County for grand theft. Emergency medical services responded to the scene to provide medical aid to the suspect and eventually transported her to a local hospital for treatment. The suspect was released from police custody as they were admitted to a hospital for injuries sustained during the vehicle collision and this case will be forwarded to the Contra Costa County DA’s Office for review.

At 7:39 AM a suspect was arrested for vandalism after they threw rocks at their mother’s house window when she wouldn’t let them in, causing chips in the surface of the window glass.  He then turned his attention to a neighbor’s car, kicking dents in the doors and smashing the windshield and rear window with rocks.  The subject was located by other officers and placed under arrest and transported to county jail.
The victim reports their vehicle was stolen sometime overnight.
The victim reported that their vehicle was stolen sometime overnight.
At 9:21 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle. The theft occurred on 11/04 @ 5:00 AM.
At 1:30PM an ATM technician found a skimming device in one of the ATM machines.

At 12:06PM the suspect stole miscellaneous items from Lucky and punched an employee in the head while exiting the store.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Petty Theft/Possession of a Controlled Substance/Possession of Drug Paraphernalia/ Warrant Arrest
Description: At 2:11PM a witness saw the suspect take the victim’s purse and walk towards Del Norte BART.  The suspect was detained at the BART station and had a theft warrant for his arrest.  The suspect was booked at ECPD and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 11:13pm, officers were dispatched to three subjects tampering with the exterior surveillance cameras.  The suspects were last seen riding bicycles to the rear of Ross.  Officers detained three possible suspects and identified them.
At 1:21PM suspects broke into a vehicle and stole miscellaneous items.  The suspects fled in a vehicle.
At 1:33PM three suspects entered Chevron and stole merchandise from behind the counter. One of the suspects punched the store clerk several times in the face. The suspects fled in a vehicle.

At 5:59AM an unknown suspect sent school administrators an email stating that at a bomb will go off at the school.  School was cancelled for the day.  BART PD Bomb dogs searched the school, and no suspicious devices or bombs were located.
At 1:23AM, officers located a vehicle with a shattered window and ignition damage.  The owner was contacted and the attempt to steal the vehicle occurred overnight between 11/5 – 11/6.
At 5:55AM a suspect stole miscellaneous items and fled on foot towards BART
At 1:01PM an unknown suspect pried open the bicycle storage unit and stole a bicycle.
At 7:12PM, a suspect walked down the street and smashed the windows of three vehicles with a metal pipe.  The suspect fled the area prior to police arrival.

At 1:49PM a store employee confronted the suspect who was stealing merchandise.  The suspect threatened to shoot the employee, but no weapons were seen.  The suspect fled the store on foot with unpaid merchandise.
At 3:57PM officers arrested a suspect for stealing items from the store.  The suspect was also in possession of a meth pipe.  The suspect was given a citation and released.
At 9:57AM the victim’s vehicle was stolen during the night.  Officers learned that it was found abandoned in the City of Richmond early this morning.
At 9:00PM, officers were dispatched to a glass break alarm at the business.  Upon arrival, officers found a broken window.  Suspect fled prior to police arrival.
At 6:30PM the victim reports an unknown suspect stole the front license plate of their vehicle.

At 8:27AM the reporting party reports that an unknown suspect shattered a glass window to the business with a rock. Vandalism – 11100 San Pablo Ave #105 (Café 9!ne) At 8:53AM the victim reports that an unknown suspect shattered the rear glass window to the business with a brick.
At 8:52AM the victim reports that an unknown suspect shattered the rear windshield to their vehicle.
At 9:08 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s property that was stored in a gym bag.

At 9:21 hours, an officer reports an unidentified suspect broke a window of a red Hyundai Elantra sometime overnight. Vandalism/Battery on an Officer/Drunk in Public/ Threat to an Officer - San Pablo/Cutting: At 11:17 PM, officers responded to the area for an unwanted subject at a nearby business. Upon arrival an officer noticed a male was kicking the door of an AC Transit bus. The officer detained the subject and was advised by the bus driver the subject had shattered the windows to the main doors of the bus by kicking it several times. The subject was transported to the ECPD for booking. Upon arrival at booking, the subject spat at an officer and made threats to harm the officer. The subject also refused to identify himself to officers. The subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:18 AM, an officer reports the arrest of a suspect for possession of counterfeit currency and possession of methamphetamine.  Booked at ECPD and cited.
At 4:59 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed out a glass door with a rock.

At 6:34 PM, officers responded to a collision. Upon arrival, officers discovered a driver had collided into two parked vehicles. Officers smelled an odor of an alcoholic beverage coming from the driver and conducted a DUI investigation. Officers determined the driver was under the influence and placed the driver under arrest. The driver was transported to the ECPD for booking. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.

At 10:32 PM, an officer reports the suspect attempted to order food from the business but was short on funds. The suspect became upset when he was denied service and started a disturbance. When an employee attempted to call 911 the suspect took the employee’s cellphone. Another employee was able to retrieve the phone back.
At 11:20 AM, an officer reports the front license plate to the victim’s vehicle was stolen overnight.
At 2:04 AM, an officer reports unknown suspect(s) shot BB’s at the business causing damage to the windows.
At 12:59 PM, an unidentified suspect stole merchandise from the store. He pushed the manger away, after the manager attempted to block him from leaving, and fled on foot.
At 12:56 PM, an unidentified suspect threw a chunk of concrete through the right rear window of a vehicle.

At 10:03 AM, a Community Service Officer reports an unidentified suspect threw a large chunk of concrete through the front window of the shop sometime overnight.  The entire front window shattered, but the hole was not large enough for entry.

At 8:10 AM, a Community Service Officer recovered a vehicle reported stolen to Berkeley PD on 11/6/23. Recovered stolen vehicle / Possess Stolen Property / Evading / Unlicensed Driver / Resist Officer – Arlington Blvd / Thors Bay: At 8:55 AM, offiecrs were made aware of a vehicle that was reported stolen to the Oakland PD and the owner had tracked it to this location.  An officer located the occupied vehicle parked on Arlington Blvd across from Arlington Park and watched it while waiting for additional units.  The vehicle fled, and a short pursuit ensued, ending at the dead end of King Ct.  The passenger was taken into custody as she attempted to walk away from the vehicle.  The driver fled into the Hillside Natural Area.  He was later apprehended as he exited the natural area onto city streets, via several residential yards and fences.  Both juveniles were booked at ECPD and then released to parents.

At 12:07 AM, an officer reports the victim was walking in the area when the suspect approached him from behind and took his backpack. The victim chased the suspect a short distance and stopped when the suspect threatened to fight him. The suspect then entered a nearby home. Officers contacted the occupants of the home and located a subject matching the suspect’s description. An infield show-up was conducted and the suspect was positively identified. The victim’s property was located at the scene and returned to the victim. After the suspect was booked at the ECPD he was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:02 PM, officers were dispatched to a robbery.  The suspect stole items from the store and threatened to assault staff with a crutch.  The suspect was detained in a nearby business, positively identified and arrested.  The suspect was later transported to county jail.

At 3:20 PM El Cerrito Police Officers assisted Contra Costa County District Attorney Investigators with the arrest of a suspect that was wanted for assault with a deadly weapon and domestic violence.  The suspect suffered an unknown medical condition while being transported to the county jail.  The suspect was then transported to a local hospital via ambulance, with the D.A. Investigators.
At 7:33 AM school administrators received an anonymous threatening email.  The school was closed for today and school staff notified parents.
At 9:25 AM an officer reports the victim’s vehicle was stolen overnight.
At 12:30 AM, officers conducted a pedestrian stop on a bicyclist.  The subject had an outstanding warrant.  The subject was arrested and later transported to county jail.
At 3:01 PM the suspect stole merchandise and fled northbound through the parking lot.
At 12:36 PM the suspect stole a bottle of wine and threatened to hit the security guard with it as they left the store.

CrimeType: Vandalism/Possession of a Controlled Substance/Possession of Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 8:29 AM a suspect was arrested for spraying graffiti along the BART path.  The suspect was in possession of fentanyl and a meth pipe. The suspect was booked at ECPD and released on a citation.

CrimeType: Stolen Vehicle / Drugs / Drug Paraphernalia / Felon in Possession of Pepper Spray
Description: At 3:41 AM, an officer located a parked stolen vehicle in the parking lot.  The driver was asleep in the driver’s seat.  The driver was taken into custody without incident.  During a search, the subject was in possession of several baggies of fentanyl and drug paraphernalia.  The subject was a convicted felon and in possession of pepper spray.  The subject was later transported to county jail on all charges.

At 6:57 PM, officers responded to a subject chasing another person.  Both were detained and one subject was arrested on a felony warrant.  During booking, the subject was found in possession of drugs and drug paraphernalia.  The subject was cited on the drug charges and transported to county jail on the warrant.
At 7:53 AM an officer recovered a parked unoccupied stolen vehicle. The vehicle was towed, and the owner was contacted.
At 6:04 AM school administrators received an anonymous email threat.  School remained open and ECPD officers provided extra patrol during the day.
At 6:24 AM, two suspects stole a pair of camping chairs and fled in a vehicle prior to police arrival.

At 8:30 PM, officers were dispatched to an unwanted subject at the business.  The suspect was harassing customers and refusing to leave.  The suspect then assaulted the victim and in self-defense, the victim punched the suspect.  The suspect was transported to the hospital and the case will be forwarded to the DA’s office.

At 2:55 PM, officers responded to a robbery at the business.  The subject stole items and pushed his way past an employee during the theft.  The subject then went into CVS and Lucky and stole more items from those businesses.  The subject was detained at the BART station and attempted to flee after being placed in handcuffs.  The subject was later transported to county jail.
At 4:31 PM, an officer reports that the victim’s vehicle was stolen sometime overnight.

CrimeType: Indecent Exposure / Trespassing / False Information to Peace Officer
Description: At 8:08 AM, officers were dispatched to an unwanted subject inside the business.  The subject refused to leave, then exposed himself and fondled his genitalia in front of an employee.  The subject provided a false name to officers.  The subject was placed under citizen’s arrest and transported to county jail.
A vehicle that had been parked at this location was stolen sometime overnight.  CHP pursued the vehicle and towed it in the early morning hours, and it was released to the owner and towed home today.
An unidentified suspect broke into the victim’s padlocked locker in the women’s locker room while she was working out, and stole her backpack and wallet.  Occurred between 3:45-5:30 AM.
An officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle around 10:58 PM.

CrimeType: Resist Arrest/ Possession of Burglary Tools
Description: At 8:28 PM, officers were dispatched to a trespassing detail. Upon arrival officers located the subject inside a vacant apartment. During a records check officers discovered the subject had two outstanding warrants and was currently on active probation out of Alameda County. As officers were attempting to take the subject into custody, she resisted. Officers located a burglary tool during a search incident to arrest. The subject was transported to the ECPD for booking. The subject was issued a citation on the new charges and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility for the warrants.
A vehicle was stolen sometime overnight.  At 12:38 PM, an officer located the vehicle on El Dorado at Carlson unoccupied and missing both plates.
At 7:28 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the store and filled two tote bags full of cosmetics and fled the store.

At 11:35 PM, officers were dispatched to an unwanted subject. Upon arrival officers contacted the subject and he was intoxicated. The subject was advised to leave the business and he agreed to leave in an Uber. An hour later the subject returned and still appeared to be intoxicated. The subject was placed under arrest and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 2:24 PM officers were dispatched to a report of two juveniles who broke into a vehicle and tampered with the ignition.  An officer located the juveniles nearby and detained them.  An officer contacted the victim and determined nobody actually saw the juveniles in the vehicle, but saw them running away from the vehicle with its window shattered.  The detained juveniles claimed there was a third subject with them who tried to steal the car, and they ran away as soon as they realized what was going on.
At 11:55 AM an officer recovered a stolen vehicle reported to Oakland PD on 11/10/2023.

At 7:28 AM a stolen vehicle hit was received on the Flock camera near Ashbury Ave at Central Ave.  The vehicle was stolen out of Antioch on 10/18/2023. At 7:34 AM an officer located the vehicle as it turned from Ashbury on to Moeser.  A traffic stop was attempted on Richmond St at Schmidt Ln, but the vehicle failed to yield and a pursuit was initiated.  The pursuit went on to westbound I-80 and into Oakland.  Officers terminated the pursuit when the suspect vehicle drove at unsafe speeds on Oakland city streets.

At 7:17 PM the victim reports she was in a verbal argument with a friend.  When she attempted to call 911, the suspect took the phone from the victim.  The victim got her phone back from the suspect and went inside her residence.  The suspect fled prior to police arrival.
At approximately 1:51 PM Officers report a suspect entered the store with two other females and grabbed handfuls of clothing and fled.
At approximately 2:17 PM Officers report a suspect broke the rear passenger side window of the victim’s vehicle.  The suspect did not take anything from inside of the vehicle.

At approximately 10:05 AM Officers report a male was captured on surveillance cameras taking a bicycle and stroller from a shared garage area.  A suspect was contacted on the BART Path with the items.  The suspect was placed under arrest, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to MDF on the charges.
At 5:06 AM officers were dispatched to a shoplifting in progress.  The suspect concealed items in a purse and fled the store prior to police arrival.

On 11/05/23 at approximately 3:00 PM a suspect purchased a vehicle from Honda.  Today, the Manager of Honda received a call from Alameda County alerting him that the vehicle had been purchased using a fraudulent ID.

At 11:02 PM officers conducted a suspicious vehicle check.  The driver was arrested for drug paraphernalia and no license.  The driver was released at the scene on a citation and a licensed driver took possession of the vehicle.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence / Warrant Arrest
Description: At 1:55 AM officers conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle.  A DUI investigation was conducted, and the driver was determined to be under the influence.  The driver also had a DUI warrant out of Marin County.  The driver was arrested and cited for the DUI charges in El Cerrito and transported to county jail on the warrant. Warrant Arrest / Recovered Stolen Property – I-80 westbound / Central Ave offramp. At 4:32 AM officers conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for one lost or stolen plate.  The driver had an outstanding misdemeanor warrant for his arrest.  The stolen license plate was recovered, and the driver was field cited on the warrant and released at the scene.
At approximately 4:39 PM officers report three suspects entered the store and stole merchandise and then fled the area.
At approximately 10:49 AM officers report the victim discovered a suspect attempted to withdraw money out of his Charles Schwab account.
At approximately 10:52 AM officers report the front driver’s side window of the victim’s vehicle was broken.
At approximately 1:36 PM Officers report the victim noticed a check was missing from their mail.
At approximately 10:41 AM officers report the suspect assaulted the victim by grabbing and pulling on their arm.
At approximately 3:06 PM officers report three suspects entered the store, stole merchandise, and fled the scene.

At approximately 11:56 AM officers report three suspects entered the store and stole misc. items.  Two female suspects were detained at their vehicle while a third suspect ran south through the parking lot.  The driver and the passenger were placed under arrest.  They were cited and released from the scene.
At approximately 8:45 AM officers recovered a stolen company vehicle after it was located in Oakland by an employee and driven back to El Cerrito.
At approximately 12:42 PM officers report the rear license plate was stolen off the victim’s vehicle.

At approximately 4:13 PM officers were dispatched to Safeway in response to a Robbery.  Officers arrived and placed a suspect under arrest after he attempted to exit the store with bags of items and attempted to strike security with a frying pan twice.  The suspect was placed under arrest and transported to Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:06 PM the suspect entered the complex and stole mail.  The suspect fled prior to police arrival.
An officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle. A check of the Flock cameras showed the vehicle leaving the city at 6:51 PM.
At 8:31 AM an officer recovered a vehicle reported stolen to Richmond PD on 11/24, after the victim located his own vehicle.  Released to owner at scene.
At 2:44 AM an officer reports the suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and located the garage door opener. The suspect rummaged through several boxes in the garage but did not take anything.
At 6:48 PM an officer reports three subjects worked together and stole merchandise.

Location: San Pablo Ave/ El Dorado St
Description: Around 12:37 AM an officer noticed a vehicle travelling on the wrong side of the road. The officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on the vehicle.  During the initial contact, the officer noticed an odor of an alcohol beverage coming from the driver. The officer conducted a DUI investigation and determined the driver was under the influence. The driver was placed under arrest and transported to the ECPD for booking. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a relative.
At 10:22 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect entered an unlocked vehicle sometime overnight and stole property from inside.

At 2:37 PM four suspects stole merchandise and fled through the south emergency exit door. Stolen Vehicle- 5600 Block of Ludwig: At 7:34 PM an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.
An unidentified suspect entered the secured apartment building, then entered a locked storage area by unknown means around 3:00 AM, as recorded on surveillance video, and stole property.
At 6:23 PM an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the license plate from the victim’s vehicle.

At 7:14 AM an officer reports an unknown suspect filled up a duffle bag with a large quantity of merchandise and fled the store without paying. Attempt Residential Burglary: At 4:14 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect shattered a window of the victim’s residence and pulled on it. The suspect did not make entry into the residence.
A vehicle was stolen by an unidentified suspect sometime overnight.
At 10:45 PM an officer reports the victim was contacted by a subject she believed was her friend via Facebook to send them money. The victim later discovered account did not belong to her friend.

At 8:44 AM school staff heard a voice on their handheld school radios that said “There’s going to be a bomb”.  Officers assisted with a walk through of the campus at the request of staff, and school administrators placed the campus on a “soft lockdown”.  Administrators elected to keep the campus open and notify parents of the lockdown.
An unidentified suspect entered an unlocked vehicle and stole property between 2:00 PM on 11/23 and 3:00 PM on 11/26.
At 1:54 PM five unidentified suspects entered Footlocker together.  Two of the suspects collected merchandise, then all five left together.  The suspects stole the clothing and fled.

CrimeType: Battery on an Officer/Disobeying an Office
Description: At 7:59 PM an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on a vehicle after observing a vehicle code violation. The officer issued the driver a citation, but she refused to sign the citation. A sergeant responded to the scene and explained the process to the driver and gave her an opportunity to sign the citation. The driver continued to refuse to sign the citation. The driver was advised she would be taken into custody and asked to step out of the vehicle. The driver refused to exit the vehicle. As officers were physically escorting the driver out of the vehicle, she threatened to kick one of the officers and began flailing her arms and legs. During the process, officers suffered minor injuries. The driver was transported to the ECPD for booking. After booking the driver was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
A CSO took a lobby report for the victim, who was swindled out of money by an online dating app cryptocurrency scheme.

CrimeType: Criminal Threats / False Info to Police

The suspect then provided a false name to the police.  The suspect was arrested on both charges and brought back to ECPD.  The suspect was cited and released from ECPD.

At 9:41 AM the victim reports that an unknown suspect entered their unlocked vehicle and damaged the steering column in an attempt to steal the vehicle.
At 6:47 PM officers responded to a collision.  One of the drivers had a misdemeanor warrant for his arrest.  The driver was arrested and transported to county jail.

At 11:51 PM officers conducted a pedestrian stop on a bicyclist.  The subject had a misdemeanor warrant for his arrest.  The subject was issued a citation and release at the scene with a new court date.

At 1:06 PM a suspect was arrested after he was found squatting in a vacant house.  There was mail found in the house, that did not belong to the suspect.  The suspect also had a warrant for his arrest for burglary from Davis PD.  The suspect was booked at the police station and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Location: Waldo Ave and San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 3:30PM the suspect engaged in conversation with the victim, hugged her and took a piece of the victim’s jewelry.

Location: I-80 Eastbound offramp at Potrero Ave.
Description: At 12:09 AM officers located a stolen vehicle on the offramp.  The vehicle was reported stolen out of Vallejo PD in an armed carjacking.

The suspects stole the victim’s wallet and fled the scene. Possession of Narcotics – Plaza BART station. At 9:45 PM officers conducted a pedestrian stop on a subject suspected of stealing items from Ross.  During a probation search of the subject, he was found in possession of suspected methamphetamine.  The subject was issued a citation in the field and released.

An unidentified suspect burglarized a vehicle parked at this location sometime overnight. Burglary – 1400 block of Vista Rd: An unidentified suspect unsuccessfully attempted to force entry to the residence at approximately 4:30 AM.  The suspect then entered an unlocked vehicle and stole items from an unlocked shed.
The victim reported to an officer their was stolen sometime overnight.

Location: San Mateo Ave/ El Dorado Ave
Description: At 6:53 PM, an officer located the victim’s stolen vehicle.

At 11:57AM, an El Cerrito Police Sergeant located an unoccupied vehicle that was reported stolen to BART PD on November 25th.  The vehicle was towed and registered owner of was contacted.

At 1:34PM a Community Service Officer recovered an unoccupied stolen vehicle that was reported to Pinole PD. The vehicle was released to the owner on scene.
At 7:58PM, the victim reported their motorcycle stolen sometime between 12/1/23 at 5:00PM and 12/2/23 at 4:30PM.

The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:49AM the owner reported that sometime last night the suspect shattered the glass door and stole cash and electronics from the register.

At 11:50AM the victim reported their vehicle was stolen sometime last night.

At 8:16AM the victim reported their rear driver side window was shattered last night.

At 2:34PM the victim reports their vehicle was stolen sometime last night.
At 7:02 PM, an officer reports the suspect removed an iPhone and 3 iPads from the displays and fled the business.

At 9:05 PM, an officer was patrolling the area and noticed a window of the victim’s vehicle had been smashed out. Upon further investigation, the officer discovered the unknown suspect attempted to steal the vehicle.

Description: At 1:51 PM, an officer and El Cerrito Fire report an unidentified suspect placed a mop and bucket under the front of a grey Honda in the rear lot and lit it on fire, causing damage to the vehicle.

At 6:16 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.
At 2:59 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.

At 6:38AM the victim reported their vehicle was stolen sometime last night.

The vehicle had stolen license plates attached and is associated with auto burglaries in Redwood City and package theft in Albany.

It was determined that the suspects had stolen merchandise from Marshall's.

The subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:41 PM, an officer reports the suspect filled up a garbage bag with merchandise and ran out of the store without paying.

At 8:19 PM, an officer reports two masked subjects entered the business and told the employee to get back and the employee feared for their safety. The subjects stole merchandise and fled in a vehicle.
At 8:26 AM, officers report a suspect stole miscellaneous items from the store and fled.
At 6:51 PM, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a suspect for shoplifting. The subject was cited and released.
At 9:27 AM, officers report a suspect stole miscellaneous items and fled.

At 1:13 PM, officers report the victim noticed her vehicle had a broken window.  Upon further investigation, the victim noticed the ignition had been damaged.  The crime occurred between 01/20/2024 – 01/24/2024.

Location: San Pablo Ave. at Waldo Ave.
Description: At 11:38 PM, an officer responded to a vehicle accident. The officer conducted a DUI investigation and determined the driver was intoxicated. The driver of the vehicle was placed under arrest for DUI and booked at the El Cerrito PD. After booking, the driver was released to a sober friend.
At 10:37 PM, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a suspect who brandished a weapon. The subject was later transported to Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:45 AM, an unknown suspect shattered a window on the victim’s vehicle.
At 6:39 PM, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a suspect for shoplifting. The subject was cited and released.
At 1:36 PM, officers reported the victim parked a vehicle in front of their house.  The victim later discovered miscellaneous items were taken from inside the vehicle.

At 3:26 PM, officers responded to a report of roommates in a physical fight. When officers arrived, they found one person with multiple injuries. The victim was transported to the hospital. The subject was booked to the El Cerrito PD and later transported to Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:28 AM, an officer responded to a report of a subject that was told to leave the plaza. The subject pulled out a weapon and threatened the victim with it. Subject then fled the plaza before police arrival.
At 9:53 AM, an officer responded to the report of a suspect who entered the store, selected merchandise, and fled without paying.
At 8:55 AM, an officer reports the victim was scammed out of money by telephone.
At 3:13 PM, an officer reports two suspects forced entry into a home around 8:26 PM on 1/24/2024 and stole property.
At 5:56 AM, three suspects shattered the front door glass and entered the store and stole items.  Suspects fled prior to police arrival.

Location: San Pablo Ave. at Fresno Ave.
Description: At 11:43 PM, an officer noticed a subject that appeared intoxicated with several empty beer bottles around him. After further investigation, the officer determined the subject was unable to care for himself and placed him under arrest. The subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility after booking.
At 6:37 AM, an officer recovered a vehicle that was reported stolen to San Leandro PD on 1/21/2024.
At 3:49 PM, a suspect stole entered the store, selected merchandise, and fled without paying.

CrimeType: Robbery/ Warrant Arrest/ Possession of a Controlled Substance
Description: At 9:44 PM, an officer reports the security guards recognized the suspect from prior thefts. The suspect attempted to leave the store with merchandise he had concealed inside his clothing. When the security guards attempted to detain the suspect, he elbowed one of the guards. The other guard sprayed the suspect with pepper spray. Officers located the suspect in the parking lot in distress from the pepper spray. ECFD and AMR were summoned to provide medical treatment to the suspect. AMR transported the suspect to a local hospital for further treatment. While at the hospital, the suspect was found to be in possession of unpaid merchandise and suspected heroin. During a records check, officers discovered the suspect had an active warrant. After being medically cleared, the suspect was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 8:36 PM, an officer reports an employee witnessed the suspect conceal merchandise inside his jacket and attempted to leave the store. The employe attempted to stop the suspect from leaving and the suspect pushed the employee and made threats to the employee.

At 10:04 PM, officers responded to an armed robbery in the parking lot.  The victims were loading their groceries when the suspect approached them and demanded their belongings.  The suspect fled the scene prior to police arrival.
At 2:30 PM, a AT&T worker reports that a suspect cut copper wire from the poles located adjacent to the freeway.
At 11:59 PM, the victim reported their locker broken into and car keys were stolen.  When the victim walked out, they noticed their vehicle had been stolen.

CrimeType: Attempted Commercial Burglary, False Identification, Possession of Drug Paraphernalia, and a Warrant Arrest
Description: At 2:27 PM, a suspect attempted to rip open the doors to a closed business.  The suspect went to an adjacent business and attempted to pry open an office door.  The suspect was detained and positively identified by witnesses. The suspect was in possession of a meth pipe, lied about his name and was in violation with the terms of his probation.  The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito PD and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:01 PM, the victim reported their locker was broken into on 1/29/2024. The victim’s belongings were stolen, and credit cards were used in Richmond.
At 7:00 PM, the victim reported their vehicle stolen from the parking lot.

CrimeType: Forgery, False Identification, False Personation, Possession of a Controlled Substance
Description: At 4:50 PM, a suspect used a fake driver license and attempted to cash a fraudulent check.  The suspect was in possession of other fraudulent items, altered identifications, and methamphetamine.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito PD and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 1:31 AM, officers responded to the report of a vandalism.  The suspect shattered a window to the business and was located nearby by officers. The suspect was transported to the hospital for injuries sustained during the broken window.  The case will be forwarded to the district attorney’s office for prosecution.

At 2:46 AM, while on routine patrol, an officer noticed a suspicious vehicle. The officers investigated the vehicle and found out it was parked in front of the owner’s house. The officer contacted the owner, when the owner examined their vehicle, they realized that someone had stolen property from the vehicle.
At 10:03 AM, the victim reported their vehicle stolen last night after 5 PM.

CrimeType: Grand Theft / Theft of Access Card
Description: At 6:15 PM, the victim reported their jacket and wallet was stolen from school today.

CrimeType: Failure to Comply with a Lawful Order/ Stolen Vehicle
Location: San Pablo Ave. at Madison Ave.
Description: At 4:43 PM, officers attempted to stop a reported stolen vehicle, however the vehicle fled northbound on San Pablo Ave. A pursuit was not initiated due to concerns for public safety.
At 10:58 AM, an officer located a stolen vehicle that was parked along the roadway unoccupied.  The vehicle was recovered and later released to the registered owner.
At 11:53 AM, the victim reported that their vehicle was stolen last night.

At 2:12 PM, an officer was called to the store to assist the fire department with an unresponsive person in the dressing room. When the officer arrived, they noticed drug paraphernalia around the subject. The subject woke up and was in no need of medical attention. The subject was placed under arrest for possession of drug paraphernalia, issued a citation, and released.
At 4:43 PM, the victim reports that their vehicle was stolen last night.
At 7:19 PM, the victim returned from work to find his car stolen.
At 12:43 PM, the suspect entered the business, grabbed a handful of merchandise, and fled.
At 2:16 PM, six suspects loaded bags of merchandise and fled without paying.
At 10:31 AM, a community service officer impounded an abandoned vehicle that they later learned was stolen out of San Francisco.
At 12:04 PM, an officer reports a vehicle was stolen between 4:00 PM on 1/25/2024 and noon on 2/2/2024.

At 3:40 PM, a Sergeant was flagged down regarding a shoplift from Marshall’s and detained the suspect.  An officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of the suspect for shoplifting.  The suspect had a no bail warrant for his arrest and was later booked at the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 3:46 PM, while speaking with the witness for the above case, an officer was flagged down for another shoplift / robbery.  The officer stopped the suspect and placed them under arrest. The suspect was later booked at the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:09 PM, a suspect stole merchandise and fled without paying.

At 8:30 PM, an officer was patrolling in the area when he was flagged down by employee for a report of a theft in progress. The employees pointed out the suspect and the officer detained them. The suspect was placed under Citizen’s Arrest and transported to the El Cerrito PD for booking. After booking, the suspect was issued a citation.

Location: San Pablo Ave. at Manila Ave.
Description: At 3:30 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence. The driver was transported to the El Cerrito PD for booking. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.
At 11:21 AM, an officer reports the theft of a rear license plate sometime between 2/2/2024 and 2/4/2024.
At 5:18 PM, an officer reports four suspects shattered out a window on the victim’s vehicle but did not make entry.
At 6:04 PM, an officer reports a suspect threw a rock through the window of the victim’s vehicle to gain entry. While in the vehicle, the suspect damaged the steering column.

Location: San Pablo Ave. at Blake St.
Description: At 1:27 PM, an officer arrested a person during a pedestrian stop. The person had a citable warrant and was cited and released.
At 2:26 PM, an officer reports criminal threats were exchanged between two employees.

At 11:28 PM, an officer reports a suspect shattered glass to the front door of the business and threw several pieces of the broken glass at the employees before fleeing. Officers located the suspect several blocks away. The person was placed under arrest, booked at El Cerrito PD and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 11:38 PM, an officer reports the victim heard the alarm of her vehicle sounding. The victim went to investigate and discovered the suspect attempted to steal the vehicle.

At 2:02 AM, an officer reports two subjects were fighting inside the business. Upon arrival both subjects had separated. During the investigation the officer determined it was mutual combat and both parties declined to pursue charges.

CrimeType: Possession of a Fictitious Check/Attempting to Pass a Fictitious Check/Providing False Identification/False Personation/Possession of Stolen Property/Conspiracy
Description: At 4:22 PM, a suspect attempted to cash an altered check using suspect’s ID and was arrested inside of the bank. A second suspect was arrested in a vehicle which was parked in the bank parking lot. Their vehicle contained more fraudulent items.  Both suspects were booked at the El Cerrito PD and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:51 PM, an officer contacted a subject in front of the McDonald’s restaurant who had two citable warrants for trespassing.  The subject was cited and released.
At 3:17 PM, the victim reported that someone stole their vehicle sometime during the day.
At 2:18 PM, a suspect damaged a window to the victim’s vehicle. The suspect fled in a red vehicle.

At 10:25 PM, officers responded to an alarm at the business. The suspect robbed the business, took merchandise, and forced the clerk into a backroom.  The suspect fled in a vehicle prior to police arrival.
At 11:15 AM, an officer recovered a reported stolen vehicle found parked on the sidewalk.  The vehicle was recovered, and the registered owner was notified.

At 6:39 AM, the owner of the building saw unknown subjects walking inside via video surveillance cameras hours before calling officers. Officers arrived and located an open window at the rear of the building. Officers searched the interior of the building but did not locate anyone inside.

At 1:20 PM, after asking the suspect to leave the premises, the suspect threated to kill a Plaza employee. Officers contacted the suspect and learned the suspect also had a warrant for their arrest.  The suspect resisted arrest and attempted to fight with officers.  The suspect was taken into custody and transported directly to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:00 AM, the victim reported that their vehicle was stolen last night.

At 7:42 PM, an officer reports the suspect entered the business via rear door and locked himself in the restroom. When asked to leave, the suspect threatened an employee and assaulted a customer. Responding officers located the suspect and took the suspect into custody. After booking, the suspect was released on a citation.

At 1:15 PM, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a juvenile after they refused to leave the beauty shop. The juvenile started throwing the victim’s property around inside the shop.  Juvenile was released to caseworker on a juvenile affidavit.
At 1:51 PM, two suspects shoved a victim and took their skateboard.

Location: San Pablo Ave at Stockton Ave.
Description: At 4:27 PM, officers responded to a Flock hit for a stolen vehicle entering the city. Officers located the vehicle which fled. The pursuit was terminated due to the suspect driving with complete disregard for other motorists’ safety.
An officer reports the suspect stole the victim’s vehicle on 2/7/2024 at 5:08 PM. The incident was captured on surveillance video.

At 2:32 AM, officers were dispatched to a hit and run collision in the area. Officers located the suspect vehicle and contacted the driver. During the DUI Investigation, officers determined the driver was under the influence of an alcoholic beverage. After the driver was booked at the El Cerrito PD, the driver was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:28 PM, an officer reports the theft of a rear license plate.
At 4:30 PM, the victim reports their parked vehicle had been taken.

At 9:52 PM, the suspect entered the store and filled a trash bag with merchandise.  After leaving the store, the suspect pushed the security guard.  The suspect entered a vehicle and fled prior to police arrival.

At 11:59 AM, the victim reports they were contacted by a suspect who made demands and threats of violence to the victim and victim’s family if the victim did not comply.

CrimeType: Brandishing Weapon / Criminal Threats / Petty Theft
Description: At 9:03 PM, the suspect entered the business, walked behind the counter and stole food.  The suspect ran outside and to a vehicle.  As the employee followed the suspect, the driver pointed a handgun at the employee.  The suspects fled in the vehicle prior to police arrival.

At 1:17 PM, the victim reports a suspect entered the residence through an unsecured window, went inside the residence.  The suspect took items and left through the front door of the residence, leaving the front door open.
At 7:01 PM, two suspects entered the business and shattered the glass to a display case, stealing merchandise.  Both suspects fled prior to police arrival.
At 10:23 AM, the victim reports a window of their vehicle was shattered between 11:00 PM on 02/12/2024 and 7:30 AM on 02/13/2024.
At 10:01 AM, the victim reports their license plates were stolen between 12/21/2023 at 12:00 PM and 12/22/2023 at an unspecified time.
At 1:29 PM, the victim reports they noticed their vehicle doors unlocked and items missing.

CrimeType: Stolen Vehicle / Attempt Assault on Peace Officer / Burglary / Conspiracy / Evading / Obstruction / Unlicensed Driver
Description: At 8:01 PM, officers responded to a Flock hit of a stolen vehicle.  Officers located the vehicle as a suspect was attempting to burglarize a vehicle.  The vehicle fled the scene and was stopped after entering a dead-end street.  The suspect fled on foot and officers were able to catch and arrest the driver. The driver was later transported to juvenile hall.

At 11:21 PM, officers conducted a traffic stop and the driver was arrested on an outstanding warrant. The driver was also in possession of narcotics and drug paraphernalia. The driver was cited on the drug charges and transported to Martinez Detention Facility on the warrant.
At 10:26 PM, the victim’s vehicle was stolen on the evening of 02/14/2024.

At 7:29 PM, the suspect entered the business and attempted to leave the store without paying.  The suspect was stopped by loss prevention and placed under citizens’ arrest.  The suspect was issued a citation and released at the scene.
At 7:17 AM, the victim’s stolen vehicle was found unoccupied on the 1700 block of Arlington Blvd. The vehicle was recovered and returned to the owner.
At 8:07 PM, the victim reports their vehicle was stolen between 2:30 PM and 7:20 PM.
At 7:18 AM, the suspect threw a cup of water at the victim after threatening the victim.  The suspect fled prior to police arrival.

At 2:17 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence. The driver was transported to the El Cerrito PD for booking. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober family member.

At 9:47 AM, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a suspect after the suspect became upset with bank staff and began hitting staff and customers. The suspect was placed under arrest, cited, and released at the scene.

CrimeType: Possess Stolen Vehicle / Unlicensed Driver / Hit and Run / Resist Officer / Recovered Stolen Vehicle
Description: At 8:42 AM, a suspect was taken into custody after the suspect’s vehicle collided with several parked cars, trees, signs, and a house. The suspect exited the vehicle and fled on foot into the high school.  Based on the outstanding description provided by callers, officers detained the man in the rear alley of the high school. The man was later released at the hospital for unrelated medical treatment. The case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office.
At 12:01 AM, an officer reports the suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.
At 9:31 PM, an officer reports the suspect stole the rear license plate from the victim’s vehicle.

At 10:33 PM, officers patrolling in the area encountered an occupied suspicious vehicle. During the investigation, an officer located a bag of suspected methamphetamine. The subject was arrested, cited, and released.
At 8:35 AM, an officer reports a vehicle was stolen from the street sometime overnight.  The vehicle was recovered in Antioch later in the day.
At 6:59 AM, an officer reports a suspect shattered the window of the victim’s vehicle sometime overnight.
At 8:36 PM, an officer reports six juveniles entered the store and stole merchandise.

At 1:23 AM, officers were dispatched to a suspicious circumstance. The reporting party advised two subjects were possibly breaking into a donation bin. Officers arrived on scene and located one of the subjects. During the investigation an officer located a glass pipe in the possession of the subject. The subject was arrested, cited, and released.
At 6:22 PM, the victim reported a suspect threw a rock through the front window of their residence.
At 9:40 AM, the victim purchased a guitar from the suspect online.  The suspect never sent the victim the guitar and blocked all contact from the victim.
At 4:26 PM, a suspect entered the store and stole merchandise. .

At 10:13 AM, as the victim walked into the store, the suspect struck the victim unprovoked.  The victim was transported to the hospital. The suspect fled towards the Del Norte BART station prior to police arrival.
At 8:57 AM, a suspect entered the store and stole merchandise.
At 9:09 AM, the victim reports that their rear license plate was stolen off their vehicle.

At 12:10 AM, officers responded to a prowler in the backyard of the residence.  Upon arrival the suspect was gone.  The suspect attempted to kick in the front door and appeared to have been scared off by the owner.

CrimeType: Brandishing a Weapon, Assault with a Deadly Weapon, Criminal Threats, Concealing a Knife
Description: At 8:21 AM, an employee asked the female suspect to exit the men’s restroom.  The suspect pulled a knife out, pointed at the employee, threatened to harm the employee and began walking towards them.  The employee immediately called 911.   Officers arrived, arrested the suspect, and located the knife concealed on the suspect’s person.  The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 11:47 AM, the victim reports that the rear window to their vehicle was shattered and miscellaneous items were stolen.
At 8:20 AM, an officer located an unoccupied vehicle parked in the area. The vehicle was recovered, and the owner was contacted.

CrimeType: Possession of a Controlled Substance, Drug Paraphernalia, and Burglary Tools
Description: At 8:15 AM, the suspect fell asleep in their vehicle while at the gas pump.  The suspect was in possession of a glass pipe and methamphetamines.  During an investigation, officers located shaved keys and burglary tools in the vehicle.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released on a citation.

At 2:36 AM, an officer was flagged down regarding a suspicious person. An officer contacted the subject and determined the subject had just driven to the location and was under the influence. The subject was placed under arrest and transported to the El Cerrito Police station for booking. After booking, the subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 12:06 AM, an officer noticed a FLOCK activation for a stolen vehicle. The officer circulated the area and located the stolen vehicle. Officers attempted to conduct a felony vehicle stop and the driver led officers on a pursuit into Oakland. While in pursuit, officers lost sight of the vehicle.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence / Hit and Run / Resist Arrest
Location: San Pablo Ave / Van Fleet Ave.
Description: At 1:21 PM, officers were dispatched to an injury collision involving two vehicles. Upon arrival, a vehicle was unoccupied blocking the roadway with the driver walking away. The driver was later detained and found during investigation to have an odor of an alcoholic beverage. The suspect fled on foot from the scene and a brief foot pursuit ensued. The suspect was taken into custody, transported to Contra Costa Regional Medical Center for medical clearance and later cited.
At 12:32 PM, an officer reports a suspect punched the door locks and shattered the windows of the victim’s pickup truck sometime overnight.

CrimeType: Possession of Stolen Vehicle / Recovered Stolen Vehicle
Description: At 5:20 PM, officers were dispatched to a disturbance over a resident parking space. When officers arrived, officers found the vehicle to be stolen out of Las Vegas.  An officer located the suspect in the Harbor Freight parking lot. The suspect was taken into custody, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:21 AM, a victim reports that their wallet was stolen from a locker and their credit cards were used.
At 3:58 PM, six subjects entered the store wearing ski masks, filled bags with store merchandise, and exited the store without paying.  One of the suspects pushed a store employee onto the ground.
At 1:25 PM, the victim reports a suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and stole miscellaneous items.  The suspect fled the area in another vehicle.
At 11:29 AM, a suspect stole merchandise and fled prior to police arrival.
The victim reports that on February 25th between 8:54 AM and 2:06 PM, a suspect damaged the door handle to their side garage door.
At 5:07 PM, the suspect vehicle ran over the victim’s foot while the victim was walking in the crosswalk. The suspect did not stop and fled.

At 2:25 PM, a suspect was refused business by the restaurant manager. During investigation, the suspect was under the influence of alcohol and refused to cooperate with officers.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Stolen Vehicle/Burglary Tools/ Possess Controlled Substance
Description: At 10:20 AM, the city’s license plate reader system (FLOCK) alerted officers to a stolen vehicle traveling in El Cerrito. Officers located the vehicle parked unoccupied in the Safeway parking lot.  As soon as the suspect returned to the vehicle, the suspect was taken into custody.  The juvenile suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released to Children and Family Services.
At 11:59 AM, a former employee entered the store, walked up to the manager and threatened the manager.

At 10:59 AM, the victim was riding their electric scooter when they collided with a child. The child’s mother got upset and struck the victim several times.  The victim fled the area and called the police an hour later.

At 9:21 AM, an officer learned that the victim was driving their vehicle with a different license plate attached. The license plate attached was from a reported stolen vehicle.  The officer collected the license plate.
At 2:35 PM, an officer took a report of theft of personal property between 11:00 AM and 2:00 PM.
At 8:23 AM, an officer arrested a subject after the subject was involved in a collision. The subject was taken to Kaiser Hospital and cited.

At 9:57 AM, an officer reports a suspect punched the door locks to enter the victim’s vehicle sometime overnight. The suspect damaged the ignition in an apparent unsuccessful attempt to steal the vehicle.
At 5:12 PM, an officer reports a vehicle was stolen sometime overnight. The vehicle hit on FLOCK system between 3:10 AM and 3:20 AM.
At 11:47 AM, an officer took a report of the theft of a license plate sometime overnight.
At 12:29 PM, an officer reports a suspect shattered the windows of the victim’s vehicle on 03/02/2024 at 11:00 PM.

CrimeType: Warrant Arrest / Possession of a Controlled Substance
Description: At 1:20 AM, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop and discovered the subject had an active warrant. The officer conducted a search incident to arrest and located methamphetamine and a glass smoking pipe. The subject was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:29 PM, an officer reports the suspect took merchandise and fled the store prior to police arrival.

At 11:15 PM, an officer reports two parties got into a verbal argument. During the argument one of the suspects pushed the victim several times before threatening the victim. The suspect was placed under Citizen’s Arrest, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:41 PM, an officer reports the suspect stole the victim’s vehicle. Officers later located the vehicle. The vehicle was recovered from the scene and the owner was contacted.
At 9:11PM, two suspects entered the business and attempted to steal merchandise. Security was able to retrieve the items back. The suspects fled the scene prior to police arrival.
At 9:45 PM, a subject was arrested on an outstanding misdemeanor warrant.  The subject was arrested, cited, and released at the scene.
At 10:14 PM, officers were dispatched to a disturbance between two parties.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:56 PM, the victim reported the theft of their vehicle.
At 9:55 AM, a reporting party stated that one of their employees stole merchandise from the store.

Location: San Pablo Ave. / Van Fleet Ave.
Description: At 10:17 AM, a police officer located an unoccupied vehicle that was reported stolen to Richmond PD on 02/26/2024.  The vehicle was recovered from the scene and the owner was contacted.

At 9:43 AM, the city’s License Plate Reader Cameras located a stolen vehicle traveling in El Cerrito.  Officers attempted to stop the vehicle but discontinued pursuing the vehicle as it continued to drive recklessly.

At 2:45 PM, the victim reported their vehicle stolen after the victim’s backpack containing their keys had been stolen from an unlocked locker. An officer later located the unoccupied vehicle in the Safeway parking lot. The vehicle was recovered from the scene and the owner was contacted.
At 11:28 AM, an officer located an unoccupied stolen vehicle that was reported to Oakland PD on 03/04/2024.  The vehicle was recovered, and the registered owner was contacted.
At 3:56 PM, an officer located an unoccupied stolen vehicle that was reported to Richmond PD on 02/28/2024.  The vehicle was recovered, and the owner of the vehicle was contacted.
At 7:45 AM, the victim reported their vehicle was stolen sometime last night.
At 7:21PM, the victim reported being assaulted by a neighbor.

CrimeType: Sending Harmful Matter to a Minor / Extortion
Description: At 5:14 PM, the victim contacted police after sending explicit photos over social media.  The suspect attempted to extort the victim for more photos.
At 2:50 PM, the suspect took a vehicle for a test drive. When the suspect failed to return the car, the dealership called police to report the vehicle stolen.
At 12:28 AM, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop and discovered the subject had an outstanding warrant. The subject was arrested, issued a citation, and released at the scene.
At 12:51 PM, an officer reports four apartment mailboxes were vandalized.
At 3:56 PM, four suspects entered Sketchers and worked in concert to steal merchandise without paying.

At 8:56 PM, officers were dispatched to a theft. Officers located the suspect at the Del Norte BART station. The suspect was positively identified during an infield show-up and placed under Citizen’s Arrest. During a search incident to arrest, the suspect was found to be in possession of the stolen merchandise.  The suspect was transported to the El Cerrito Police Department. After booking, transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:01 AM, an officer located an unoccupied stolen vehicle that was parked along the curb.  The vehicle was recovered, and the owner was contacted.
At 2:55 PM, a subject was arrested for having a local warrant. The subject was arrested, cited, and released.
On March 11, between 7:52 PM and 7:58 PM, the suspect attempted to syphon fuel and damaged the security devices on the gas pump handles.
Between 6:00 AM and 3:00 PM, the victim’s vehicle was stolen.
At 9:06 PM, a subject was found in possession of drug paraphernalia. The subject was arrested, cited, and released on a citation.
Between 3:30 AM to 6:15 AM, a suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and stole miscellaneous items.
From March 12, at 3:30 PM to March 13, at 8:30AM, a suspect entered the victims unlocked vehicle and stole miscellaneous items.
Sometime last night to 3:00 PM today, a suspect entered the victims unlocked vehicle and stole miscellaneous items.
Sometime around 1:30 AM, the victim found their vehicle had been stolen.
At 10:37 AM, a suspect stole a cellphone from the display shelf before fleeing.
At 4:30 AM, a suspect shattered a window to the victim’s vehicle and stole items.

At 1:48 AM, officers responded to the area for a suspicious vehicle. Officers located the vehicle involved in a single vehicle collision. Officers located the driver passed out in the driver’s seat of the vehicle. After a DUI investigation, officers determined the driver was under the influence. The driver was placed under arrest and transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking. After booking the suspect was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.
At 4:17 PM, a business employee reported that the suspect spray-painted graffiti along the exterior north wall of the business.
At 1:33 PM, a suspect attempted to steal from the store. The suspect was found to have a warrant for their arrest. The suspect was arrested, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
The victim reported their vehicle was stolen from their driveway between 10 AM -12 PM.
The victim reported their vehicle was stolen sometime between March 18th and March 19th.

At 12:45 PM, officers detained a suspect who was caught shoplifting. The suspect gave officers several different names and dates of birth.  After obtaining the suspect’s true identity, officers learned the suspect had seven warrants for their arrest.  The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
From March 17th - March 18th, a suspect stole reflectors along the victims fence.
Between 1:00 PM - 1:28 PM, the victim’s rear windshield was damaged.
At 10:32 AM, the victim’s rear license plate was stolen off their vehicle.
At 10:48 AM, the victim reported that their vehicle was stolen.
At 5:14 PM, officers arrested one adult and two teenage juveniles for stealing merchandise.  The suspects were arrested, cited, and released at the scene.
At 8:08 PM, two suspects entered the business and stole merchandise. Both suspects fled the store prior to police arrival.

At 3:48 PM, officers located an occupied stolen vehicle that was reported stolen to Oakland PD. Officers contacted the driver of the vehicle and found the driver to be the legal owner of the vehicle. The driver told officers they had forgotten to contact Oakland PD after they found their vehicle.
At 6:59 AM, the victim was beaten by two suspects after intervening in a dispute between them and an employee. The suspects fled prior to police arrival.
At 3:56 PM, the store called to report a theft that occurred the previous day.  Four suspects worked in concert to steal merchandise.
At 9:26 PM, an officer reports a suspect left the store with unpaid merchandise.

At 3:43 AM, officers received a Flock notification for a stolen vehicle. An officer located the vehicle and attempted to conduct an enforcement stop. The suspect instead sped away and committed several vehicle code violations. Officers terminated their attempts to stop the vehicle due to safety concerns.
At 11:11 PM, a resident reports a suspect fired a gun into the air.

At 10:59 PM, an officer reports the suspect was asked to leave the business by security guards. In the process, the suspect attempted to leave the store with merchandise while throwing a chair and pushing a security guard. Officers located the suspect and detained them. During a records check, officers discovered the suspect had an outstanding warrant out of San Francisco County. After the suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, the suspect was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence / Hit and Run
