The suspect was placed under arrest and transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking. After booking, the suspect was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.

After the DUI investigation, officers determined the driver was under the influence. The driver was placed under arrest and transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking. After booking, the driver was released on a citation.

Officers located the suspect as he was trying to leave the area. The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and later released on a citation.

CrimeType: Arrest for Possession of a Stolen Vehicle

During the investigation, the suspect admitted to consuming alcohol to officers and showed objective signs of being under the influence.  The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.  The child was released to family members.

A DUI Investigation was initiated, and the driver was found to be heavily intoxicated. The suspect was then arrested and booked in the El Cerrito Police Department. During the booking process, the suspect attempted to hit an officer and spat on another officer.  The suspect was later transported to Martinez Detention Facility.

The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and cited.

Upon further investigation, it was determined a second cellphone in the possession of the suspect was taken from the same business two days prior. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

The manager reported to officers that this was the fifth time within the week that the subject had falsely pulled the fire alarm. The manager wanted the subject placed under citizen’s arrest. Officers accepted the manager’s citizen arrest and took the subject into custody. The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:22 PM, officers arrested a suspect for vandalizing three vehicles at the business. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
Between the hours of 12:45 PM - 4:45 PM, a suspect burglarized a residence.
At 3:56 PM, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a suspect caught shoplifting. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released on a citation.
At 3:38 PM, the victim reports the theft of their license plate.
At 8:24 AM, a victim reports the theft of their catalytic converter sometime overnight.

CrimeType: Robbery / Brandish a Firearm / Resisting a Peace Officer
Description: At 3:38 hours, a suspect was arrested for armed robbery. The suspect fled from police and was later located. The suspect failed to comply with police orders and was apprehended by a police K9 unit. The suspect was medically treated and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Resisting Arrest / Possession of Drug Paraphernalia / Theft or Misappropriation of Lost Property

The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:03 PM, a victim reports a suspect attempted to steal their motorcycle.

CrimeType: Robbery / Battery on a Peace Officer / Warrant
Description: At 2:00 PM, a suspect robbed the store of merchandise and was placed under arrest. Further investigation found the suspect to have a warrant for their arrest. While escorting the suspect to the police patrol vehicle, the suspect kicked the officer. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 3:38 AM, officers conducted an traffic enforcement stop. During the investigation, officers found the driver to be under the influence of alcohol. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation to a sober friend.

CrimeType: Robbery / Assault with a Deadly Weapon
Description: At 6:33 AM, a victim reports they were robbed of their property. The suspects also assaulted the victim with a firearm and fled the area prior to police arrival.

Officers were able to later identify the subject and found the subject to have a probation violation and outstanding felony warrants for their arrest. Further investigation into the suspect’s vehicle led officers to locate evidence of narcotic sales and a loaded and concealed handgun under the seat. The passenger of the vehicle was also arrested for evidence of narcotic sales. Officers also found the passenger to have a warrant for a probation violation. Both subjects were arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:48 PM, an officer reports a suspect stole merchandise. The suspect was located and placed under citizen’s arrest. The suspect was arrested and released on scene with a citation.

At 7:15 PM, while on the above call for service, officers located and arrested two suspects for shoplifting. One of the suspects was found to have multiple out of county warrants and drug paraphernalia on their persons. Both suspects were arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Officers canvased the area and located the suspect at the El Cerrito Plaza BART station and was found to be in possession of the stolen merchandise. At 3:28 PM, the victim reports their vehicle was stolen between the hours of 4:50 AM - 3:00 PM.

The suspect refused to comply with officers orders and struck a police officer. The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 10:34 AM, a victim reports a cable wire was cut and copper inside stolen.
At 11:52 AM, the victim reported that the suspect opened a life insurance policy in the victim’s name.
At 4:07 PM, the victim reports their vehicle was stolen from their driveway sometime between 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM.

Officers determined the subject was intoxicated and placed the suspect under arrest. The subject was booked at the EL Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:32 AM, the victim reports their vehicle was stolen.
An officer reports a suspect entered the victim’s residence through a back sliding door and moved property within the home.

At 11:56 PM, an officer reports when the victim left the business with their food, the suspect followed the victim out and assaulted them. Officers arrived on scene and accepted the victim’s citizen’s arrest. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released on a citation.

At 9:48 AM, employees reported a suspect was found inside an apartment.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence / Unlicensed Driver
Description: At 1:08 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the DUI investigation, the driver was found to be unlicensed and under the influence of alcohol. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and released with a citation to a sober friend.

At 12:48 AM, an officer reports the victim’s ex-significant other entered the victim’s residence through an open window. The suspect fled the scene prior to officers’ arrival.
At 8:17 AM, multiple victims reported their windows to their vehicles were shattered sometime overnight.

At 1:17 AM, officers responded to the area for a suspect shooting at vehicle. During the investigation, officers discovered the suspect and several victims were at a local bar when a verbal altercation ensued. All parties were asked to leave and went to a nearby parking lot where they continued their verbal altercation. As the victims got into their vehicles, the suspect shot at them using a firearm. There were no injuries reported after the investigation concluded. The suspect was arrested, and the case was forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office.

Location: Van Fleet Ave. / San Luis Ave.
Description: While searching for the suspect in the above case, an officer located the suspect and conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the DUI investigation, the suspect was found to be under the influence of alcohol. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 6:55 PM, a victim reported their property was left at the business while shopping at another business. Upon return, the victim discovered a suspect had stolen their property.
At 11:14 AM, a victim reported their license plate was stolen.

At 9:08 PM, officers responded to a battery between roommates.  During the altercations, one of the roommates threatened to kill the other. The report will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s office.

Location: San Pablo Ave. / El Dorado St.
Description: At 2:42 AM, officers responded to an injury accident involving two vehicles.  The driver collided into a parked vehicle and was transported to a local hospital for minor injuries.  The driver was arrested on suspicion of driving while intoxicated and released on a citation to the hospital.

At 7:52 AM, an officer located the victim’s stolen vehicle parked unoccupied.  The owner of the vehicle was called and the vehicle was released to the owner on scene.

A records search of the suspect found the subject had a warrant for their arrest. The subject was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Location: San Pablo Ave. / El Dorado St.
Description: At 6:42 PM, officers responded to a report of a battery and criminal threats.  The report will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s office for review.

A records check of the subject found the subject was also on active probation. The subject was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 6:18 PM, a victim reports a suspect hit the victim in the head with their elbow. The case was forwarded to the District Attorney’s office.

While officers were responding, dispatch advised one of the drivers was yelling at other patrons and hitting their vehicles. Upon arrival, officers conducted a DUI investigation. During the investigation, officers found the suspect to be under the influence of alcohol. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and released on a citation to a sober friend.
At 6:14 PM, an officer reports a suspect threw a brick through the victim’s vehicle window. The suspect fled prior to police arrival.
At 12:10 PM, an officer reports a suspect sold the victim a fake phone.
At 10:35 PM, officers recovered a stolen truck reported to Berkeley PD.  The truck was released to the owner on scene.

CrimeType: Possession of Marijuana for Sales / Possession of Marijuana Over an Ounce / Possession of Prescribed Narcotics without a Prescription
Description: At 10:46 PM, officers conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the investigation, officers located drug paraphernalia on the passenger. The passenger was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and released on a citation.

The suspect grabbed some of the cash from the victim and fled the scene. At 4:17 PM, employees recognized the suspect when they returned to the bank and called police. Officers arrived on scene and detained the suspect. The suspect was positively identified by the victim during an in-field identification. Officers conducted a records check and found the suspect was on parole with an active warrant for their arrest. The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Officer contacted the suspect and the employee placed the suspect under Citizen’s Arrest. During a search incident to arrest, the suspect was found to be in possession of drug paraphernalia. The suspect was transported directly to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:53 AM, a suspect entered the closed unsecured business and stole a contractor’s property.
At 9:12 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole a license plate from the victim’s vehicle.

The suspect attempted to flee from officers and a short foot pursuit ensued. The suspect was detained by officers and placed under arrest. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

The detained suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Due to the driver’s erratic behavior, the officer elected not to pursue the vehicle. Minutes later the officer located the vehicle abandoned near the city limits. The officer processed the vehicle for evidence and released the vehicle.

The suspect failed to obey the officer’s commands and attempted to flee. After a short foot pursuit, the suspect was arrested. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Officers found the suspect had symptoms of being under the influence of alcohol.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Description: At 12:28 PM, officers located an occupied stolen vehicle traveling in the city.  Officers conducted traffic enforcement stop of the vehicle and arrested the driver.  The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.  The owner of the vehicle was contacted and the vehicle was released on scene.

Upon arrival, the officer determined the subject was intoxicated and refused medical assistance. The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

The driver was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility and the juveniles were released to the custody of a relative.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence / Unlicensed Driver / Hit and Run

Witnesses provided officers with a description of the suspect. Officers circulated the area and located the registered owner of the suspect vehicle who appeared intoxicated. The suspect was positively identified during an infield investigation by witnesses. Officers conducted a DUI investigation and found the suspect to be under the influence of alcohol. The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation to a sober friend.

The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

After a short foot pursuit, the subject was detained. During a search incident to arrest, the subject was found to be in possession of a loaded firearm. Officers conducted a records check and discovered the subject was a convicted felon. The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 11:42 PM, two subjects were in a physical altercation.  Both subjects refused to press charges.  The report will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office.

The suspects were booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. The vehicle was released to the owner on scene.

During a search incident to arrest, the driver was found to be in possession of brass knuckles. The driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation to a sober friend.

CrimeType: Warrant Arrest / False Info to Officer / Possess Controlled Substance / Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 4:11 PM, an officer made a traffic enforcement stop. During the investigation, the passenger of the vehicle initially provided a false name but was eventually identified and found to have a warrant for their arrest. During a consent search of the vehicle, the officer located paraphernalia and a large amount of methamphetamine. Both the driver and passenger claimed joint ownership of all of the above.  The driver was cited and later released.  The passenger was cited, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martienz Detention Facility for the warrant.

At 1:59 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the DUI investigation, officers found the driver to be driving under the influence. The driver was arrested, booked at El Cerrito Police Department, and released with a citation to a sober relative.

At 5:30 AM, officers responded to the business for an unwanted subject. Officers discovered the subject had returned to the business for the third time after being asked to leave by employees prior. The subject was placed under Citizen’s Arrest, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:49 PM, a victim reports their license plate was stolen sometime overnight.
At 6:47 PM, an officer reports a suspect took items and left the store with the unpaid merchandise.
At 4:20 PM, a victim reports while inside shopping, their electronic bicycle was stolen from in front of CVS.

At 3:56 PM, officers were dispatched to a report of six suspects stealing items from the shelves at Walgreens. Officers were notified of a suspect on the BART platforms. BART officers detained the suspect and was positively identified during an in-field show up. Further investigation found the suspect to be on probation. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. Merchandise belonging to Walgreens was recovered and returned.

At 6:15 PM, an officer reports a known subject was intoxicated and yelling at customers in front of the business. An officer contacted the subject and requested an ambulance to transfer the subject to the hospital for medical treatment. This case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office for review.

CrimeType: Organized Retail Theft Operation / Warrant / Possession of Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 12:00 PM - 6:00 PM, El Cerrito Police conducted an organized retail theft operation to combat theft in the plaza. Officers thwarted five thefts in progress and arrested nine thieves coming out of stores. During the investigation, officers found two suspects to have outstanding warrants, while also in possession of drug paraphernalia. Officers arrested a group of suspects who attempted to flee in their vehicle. The vehicle was impounded, and the suspects were booked at the El Cerrito Police Department before some of them being transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:11 AM, an officer reports suspects broke into two parked vehicles and stole miscellaneous items.  The suspects fled prior to police arrival.

At 9:31 AM, Officers received a call of the suspect causing a disturbance inside Burger King. The suspect refused to leave the business, resisted arrest, and was later taken into custody. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:38 PM, the victim reports that during an argument, the suspect punched the victim in the face and fled.
At 3:31 PM, the victim reports that their catalytic converter was stolen sometime last night.
At 12:36 PM, an officer reports that a suspect stole miscellaneous items. The suspect fled prior to police arrival.
At 11:09 AM, the victim reported that the suspect stole copper wire.

At 8:36 PM, an officer reports employees at the business heard loud noises coming from the roof area. Employees went outside to investigate the noise and located a subject on the roof. The subject fled the scene when employees attempted to detain the subject.

At 4:21 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop.  During the DUI investigation, officers found the driver to be driving under the influence. The driver was arrested, booked at El Cerrito Police Department, and released with a citation to a sober friend.
At 1:24 PM, an officer reports a suspect vandalized the victim’s car while it was parked in the apartment parking lot sometime overnight.

CrimeType: Warrant Arrest / Possession of a Controlled Substance
Description: At 7:02 PM, officers were dispatched to the business for a suspicious person. Upon arrival, officers contacted the subject and discovered the subject had two outstanding warrants for their arrest. During a search incident to arrest, the subject was found to be in possession of suspected methamphetamine. The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:12 PM, an officer reports a suspect stole the victim’s property from an unsecured locker.

At 9:23 PM, officers responded to the business after witnesses called and advised a subject was inside the business inappropriately touching themselves. Upon officers arrival, the suspect was found inside the business still inappropriately touching themseleves. The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and released with a citation. The case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office.

CrimeType: Shoplift / Probation Violation / Drug Paraphernalia / Arrest Warrants
Description: At 9:45 AM, an officer reports the arrest of a suspect for stealing merchandise. During a search incident to arrest, the suspect was found to be in possession of drug paraphernalia. During the investigation, officers found the suspect was on probation with three warrants for their arrest. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:33 AM, an officer reports housemates argued and fought. The case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office.
At 6:58 PM, an officer reports a suspect broke the lock off the victim’s detached storage shed and appeared to have slept inside. The suspect was not on scene during officers arrival.

At 5:29 AM, an officer reports the suspect and victim were in a verbal argument. During the argument, the suspect shattered the screen of the victim’s electronics and stole their wallet. The suspect fled prior to police arrival.

At 9:39 PM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the investigation, officers found the driver held an outstanding warrant. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 3:43 PM, officers located a subject found unresponsive in their vehicle while blocking traffic on San Pablo Ave. When officers and medical made entry into the vehicle, the subject showed signs of consuming a controlled substance.  The subject admitted to being under the influence of narcotics and was transported to the hospital with a citation.
At 11:33 AM, an officer reports a suspect shattered a window to the victim’s residence, entered, and stole property. The suspect fled prior to police arrival.
At 8:45 AM, the victim reports between 12/14/2024 -12/16/204, a suspect shattered a window to the victim’s vehicle and stole miscellaneous items.

CrimeType: Shoplifting / Warrant / Possession of Drug Paraphernalia and Controlled Substances
Description: At 8:44 AM, officers report a suspect stole merchandise from the store and fled. Officers detained the suspect a few blocks away and was positively identified. During a search incident to arrest, the suspect was found to be in possession of drug paraphernalia and controlled substances. Further investigation found the suspect also had a warrant for their arrest. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation.

At 6:36 PM, officers responded to a shoplifting at the business. Officers located and arrested the suspect a few blocks away.  The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation.
At 4:02 PM, an officer located a parked unoccupied stolen vehicle out of Oakland PD.  The vehicle was released to the owner on scene.
At 3:08 PM, the victim reported a suspect damaged their ignition in an attempt to steal their vehicle. The suspect fled prior to police arrival.

At 3:48 PM, the victim reports that while they were in their vehicle, the suspect opened the door, threw them out and stole their vehicle. Another suspect then stole the victim’s purse before fleeing the scene.
At 11:28 PM, the victim’s ex-significant other threatened and stole items from their vehicle.  The suspect fled prior to police arrival.
At 9:40 AM, an employee reports that the suspect sent a fraudulent prescription order to the pharmacy.
At 6:06 PM, the victim sent the suspect money for an online purchase and never received the item.

At 7:28 PM, an officer located and arrested a suspect for stealing packages from the porch of a residence.  The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:28 AM, an officer took a telephone report from the victim who said an acquaintance robbed them of a jacket shortly after midnight on 12/06/2024.
At 1:38 PM, an officer reports the theft of a vehicle that occurred on 12/18/2024.
At 8:36PM, an officer reports suspect(s) egged the victim’s house.

CrimeType: Shoplifting / Felony Theft Repeat Offender / Battery / Probation Violation
Description: At 1:55 PM, an officer arrested a suspect for shoplifting and for spitting on the security guard as they left the store.  Further investigation found the suspect was also on probation. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 11:55 AM, an officer reports a suspect broke into the victim’s vehicle and stole items.
At 4:19 PM, an officer reports theft of license plates.
At 11:30 AM, an officer reports a suspect used counterfeit bills to purchase merchandise.
At 4:44 PM, an officer reports a suspect shattered the passenger window of the victim’s vehicle and stole the victim’s purse off the front seat.

At 1:55 AM, officers responded to the area for an injury traffic collision. During the investigation, officers learned the driver of the vehicle collided with a pedestrian who was walking on the roadway. While speaking to the driver, officers noticed the driver appeared to be intoxicated and conducted a DUI investigation. During the investigation, officers found the driver to be under the influence. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility. The pedestrian was transported to a local hospital by medics for injuries.

At 12:43 AM, officers responded to an audible alarm with a shattered window to the business. With the assistance of a Richmond Police Canine Unit, officers searched the building and did not locate any suspects inside. An employee responded to the scene and conducted a quick inventory and advised property was missing.
At 4:24 PM, an officer reports a suspect pulled the hair of one of the store employees after the employee accused the suspect of shoplifting.
At 1:13 PM, an officer reports a suspect posed as the owner of a vacant lot in attempt to list the property for sale with a local realtor. The suspect fled prior to police arrival.
At 7:37 PM, an officer reports three suspects had fled the store with merchandise, prior to police arrival.

CrimeType: Possession of a Controlled Substance / Probation Violation
Description: At 10:21 AM, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop. During an investigation, officers found the subject was on probation with an outstanding arrest warrant. During a search incident to arrest, officers located a controlled substance on the subject.  The suspect was cited in the field and released.
At 7:25 AM, an officer recovered a vehicle that was reported stolen to Oakland PD on 12/21/2024. The owner of the vehicle was contacted, and the vehicle was released on scene.

CrimeType: Public Intoxication / Open Container / Probation Violation
Description: At 9:31 AM, an officer responded to a report of a person passed out on the sidewalk with an almost empty pint of an alcoholic beverage next to them. The suspect was unable to care for themselves and found to be on probation. The subject was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention.

At 5:00 PM, an officer responded to a report of a person passed out on the sidewalk. Officers located the subject and found the subject was too intoxicated to care for themselves. The subject was transported to the hospital by medics. This case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s office.

CrimeType: Sex Offender Fail to Register / Possession of a Controlled Substance
Description: At 8:09 PM, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop. Further investigation found the pedestrian was a registered sex offender that was out of compliance. During the search incident to arrest, the pedestrian was found to be in possession of narcotics and drug paraphernalia. The pedestrian was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation.

At 12:22 PM, an officer reports a suspect scanned and paid for one item at self-check but placed numerous items inside of their grocery bag. When confronted by store security, the suspect struck the security guard. The suspect was arrested, booked and interviewed at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 1:10 AM, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop and discovered the subject had an outstanding warrant. The subject was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:04 PM, a victim reported that while walking, a vehicle stopped and shot at them with an airsoft weapon.  The suspects fled prior to police arrival.

At 11:45 PM, officers responded to an overturned vehicle collision. Officers arrived on scene and found the driver of the vehicle had collided with a parked vehicle. During a DUI investigation, an officer found the driver to be under the influence. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation to a sober friend.
At 2:15 AM, a victim reports that while walking in the area, a vehicle stopped and shot at them with an airsoft weapon.  The suspects fled prior to police arrival.

At 11:20 PM, officers responded to a subject trying to open the front door of a residence. The subject was not known to the homeowners and was found to be highly intoxicated. The subject was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 5:42 PM, an officer reports a suspect took items from the store without paying and fled. Officers located and arrested the suspect. During a search incident to arrest, officers found the suspect to be in possession of drug paraphernalia. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:23 PM, an officer reports a suspect grabbed the victim’s purse from and fled the area.
At 7:57 AM, the victim reports that their vehicle was stolen sometime last night.

At 5:08 PM, an officer reports a suspect stole alcohol from the store and fled. Officers located and positively identified the suspect. The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Shooting into an Unoccupied Vehicle / Vandalism
Description: At 8:23 AM, the victim reports that at some time last night, a suspect damaged their vehicle with an object and used a weapon to shoot into her vehicle.
At 10:30 PM, an officer reported that after ordering food, the suspect displayed a firearm at the cashier and demanded the money from the register. The suspect then fled prior to police arrival.
At 8:09 PM, an officer reports a suspect entered the business and stole items.  The suspect fled prior to police arrival.
At 6:04 PM, an officer reports that a suspect entered the business and stole items. The suspect fled prior to police arrival.

At 2:19 PM, an officer reports store employees witnessed the suspect stealing items from the store. When employees contacted the suspect, the suspect threatened to harm them. The suspect then fled from the store prior to police arrival.
At 1:00 PM, the victim reported that the suspect attempted to purchase a vehicle in their name.
At 10:30 PM, a suspect stole the purse off the victim and fled the area prior to police arrival.

At 12:32 AM, officers responded to the business for an unwanted subject. Upon arrival, officers discovered the suspect was intoxicated and was asked to leave by the bar staff. The suspect refused to leave and bit a member of the staff. The suspect was placed under Citizen’s Arrest, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and released with citation to a sober family member.
At 12:03 AM, an officer reports a suspect shot the victim using an airsoft weapon.
At 8:55 PM, an officer reports the victim found the subject inside their unlocked vehicle. The subject was placed under Citizen’s Arrest and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:52 AM, an officer reports a suspect pretending to be a corporate employee tricked an employee into depositing store money into a fraudulent account.
At 2:05 PM, store staff attempted to stop a suspect from stealing until the suspect brandished a firearm at security. The suspect fled the area prior to police arrival.
At 1:25 PM, two suspects stole two boxes full of alcohol beverages. The suspects fled prior to police arrival.
At 4:24 PM, an officer reports a suspect stole items from the store and fled prior to police arrival.

At 6:18 PM, officers responded to a report of vandalism in progress. The suspect was positively identified and arrested. The suspect was booked into the El Cerrito Police Department and later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:24 PM, the victim reported receiving threatening text messages.

At 12:46 AM, officers conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the investigation, officers found the driver had an outstanding warrant for their arrest. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 5:23 PM, an officer reports a suspect stole items from the store and fled prior to police arrival.
At 4:57 PM, an officer reports a suspect stole items from the store and fled prior to police arrival.
At 1:03 PM, the victim reported their vehicle being stolen.
At 2:21 PM, the victim reported their vehicle being stolen.

At 6:15 PM, officers responded to a report of a subject unable to move. Upon arrival, the officer discovered the subject was intoxicated and unable to care for themselves. The subject was transported to a local hospital by medics. The case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office for review.
At 3:55 PM, a subject turned themselves in on an outstanding warrant from San Jose PD. The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:31 AM, an officer reports their vehicle was taken overnight.

At 1:03 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the DUI investigation, officers found the driver to be under the influence. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and released with a citation to a sober family member.

At 10:49 PM, an officer reports two acquaintances of the victim attempted to leave the victim’s residence with the victim’s property. When the victim confronted them, the suspects used force and took the items and fled the area.

At 2:23 PM, then again at 4:07 PM, officers report that a known suspect stole items from the store and fled. Officers located and arrested the suspect. The suspect was positively identified and transported directly to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 12:46 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During a DUI investigation, officers found the driver to be under the influence. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation to a sober friend.
At 8:18 PM, an officer reports a suspect stole the victim’s cell phone while the victim was shopping.

At 9:06 AM, officers responded to a report of a theft in progress. Officers arrived on scene and detained the suspect who stole merchandise. The suspect was positively identified and placed under Citizen’s Arrest. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 10:34 AM, officers responded to a report of a combative suspect who stole items from the store. Officers arrived on scene and assisted the employees in placing the suspect under arrest. The suspect was booked into the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 4:40 AM, officers responded to a burglary alarm from the business. The suspect had stolen items and fled the business prior to police arrival.

At 1:13 AM, officers conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the investigation, officers found the driver had an outstanding warrant for their arrest. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martienz Detention Facility.
At 9:21 PM, the suspect entered the business and stole items. The suspect fled prior to police arrival.

At 9:46 PM, the subject entered the business which they had already been banned from.  Officers arrived on scene and the subject was placed under arrest. The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:41 AM, a victim reports the theft of their rear license plate sometime overnight.

At 8:37 PM, an officer reports the suspect was upset over a transaction with an employee and began damaging merchandise. Officers located and arrested the suspect. The suspect was cited and released on scene.

At 9:26 AM, the victim reports two suspects were interrupted by the victim while cutting the catalytic converter out of the victim’s vehicle. One of the suspects raised the front of their shirt to display a handgun at the victim before fleeing into a nearby waiting vehicle.

At 1:45 PM, an officer accepted the citizen’s arrest of a suspect who stole merchandise.  The suspect was booked into the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 5:48 AM, an officer reported a theft of a vehicle taken between 11:00 AM on 01/24/2025 and 4:00 AM on 01/25/2025.
At 9:47 AM, an officer located an unoccupied stolen vehicle.

At 2:12 PM, an officer accepted the Citizen’s Arrest of a suspect who stole merchandise.  The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:06 AM, an officer reported a theft of a vehicle taken sometime between 01/20/2025 and 01/25/2025.
At 3:53 PM, an officer reports a suspect stole merchandise and fled prior to police arrival.

At 11:07 AM, an officer accepted the Citizen’s Arrest of a suspect who stole merchandise. The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 1:04 AM, officers noticed a suspicious vehicle and contacted the driver. During the investigation, officers located suspected methamphetamine and drug paraphernalia in the driver’s possession. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and released with a citation.
At 9:52 AM, a suspect threatened staff with a weapon before stealing items from the business.

CrimeType: Warrant Arrest / Possession of Narcotics / Paraphernalia
Description: At 1:50 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During an investigation, officers found the driver to have an outstanding warrant. During a search incident to arrest, the driver was found to be in possession of suspected methamphetamine and drug paraphernalia. The driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation.
At 9:59 PM, an officer reports a suspect had entered and rummaged through the victim’s vehicle. The victim reports no items were taken.
On 01/25/2025 between the hours of 2:00 PM to 3:00 PM, the victim reports they were standing at a bus stop when a suspect stole their purse. The suspect fled the area prior to police arrival.
At 12:00 PM, a suspect stole the victim’s bicycle, placed it inside of a vehicle, and fled the area prior to police arrival.
At 10:15 AM, the victim reports their vehicle was stolen sometime last night.
At 2:42 PM, an officer reports a suspect stole items and was stopped by security.  The suspect fought store security and fled the area prior to police arrival.
At 4:07 PM, an officer reports a suspect stole items and fled the area prior to police arrival.

At 6:06 PM, a victim reports their stolen bicycle GPS was located at a nearby motel. Officers responded to the area and located the stolen bicycle inside one of the occupied rooms. The case was forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office.
At 7:55 AM, an officer reports a suspect stole a vehicle battery sometime between 01/28/2025 and 01/31/2025.
At 4:21 AM, an officer reports a suspect threw a rock through a window on the north side of the business to gain entry. The suspect stole property and fled the scene prior to police arrival.

At 8:09 PM, an officer reports a suspect attempted to leave the store with the unpaid merchandise when the suspect was stopped by a store employee. The suspect became upset and started throwing items at the employee. The suspect then threw a rock at the windows and fled the area prior to police arrival.

CrimeType: Theft with Priors / Possession of Ammo by Felon / Possession of Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 8:31 PM, an officer was at the business conducting follow-up of a previous another incident when the officer was flagged down by an employee for a theft in progress. As the suspect was attempting to leave the store with concealed unpaid merchandise, the officer stopped the suspect. During the investigation, officers confirmed the crime had taken place. During a search incident to arrest, the suspect was found to be in possession of drug paraphernalia and live ammo. Further investigation found the suspect had two prior convictions for theft. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 5:13 AM, a victim reported their vehicle being stolen.

At 11:07 AM, officers located a suspect drinking alcohol in public and was visibly intoxicated. The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:10 PM, a suspect was placed under Citizen’s Arrest after stealing items from the store. The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and released with a citation.
At 8:06 PM, the victim received a threatening phone call.

At 10:15 AM, the victim reports they were assaulted by the suspect while both parties were inside the business.  The suspect fled prior to police arrival. Battery – Albany Middle School (Cougar Field): At 6:14 PM, the victim was assaulted by a suspect.  The suspect fled prior to police arrival.

CrimeType: Shoplifting / Petty Theft with Priors / Fraud / Possession of Narcotics / Probation Violation / Warrant Arrest
Description: At 9:45 PM, officers responded to a theft in progress.  Two suspects fled the store and were later detained at the Plaza BART Station. One suspect was positively identified and placed under arrest.  During a search incident to arrest, the suspect was found to be in possession of fraudulent items and narcotics. Further investigation found the suspect was on probation and had a warrant for their arrest. Both suspects were booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 9:27 PM, officers responded to the business where two suspects stole a portable generator. The following day, officers received information about the generator’s location in a nearby city. Officers responded to the area, located, and processed the generator for evidence. The generator was then returned to the owner.
At 11:42 AM, an officer reports a bicycle was stolen sometime between 02/03/2025 and 02/05/2025.
At 7:46 PM, an officer reports a suspect fled the store with unpaid merchandise, prior to police arrival.
At 12:54 PM, an officer reports theft of property from inside a parked vehicle.
At 11:07 PM, an officer reports three suspects consumed food and ran out of business without paying.
At 3:38 PM, a victim reported four juveniles attempted to steal their vehicle but were interrupted by a neighbor when the vehicle’s alarm sounded.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence / Suspended License
Description: At 2:02 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During a DUI investigation, officers found the driver to be under the influence. Officers conducted a records check of the driver and discovered the driver had a suspended license. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and released with a citation to a sober friend.
At 2:30 PM, the victim reports vehicle was stolen sometime last night.
At 3:07 AM, officers responded to an alarm call and detained a subject who was attempting to steal items. The subject was identified and released from the scene.

At 2:39 AM, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop. During the investigation, officers found the subject to have an outstanding warrant. During a search incident to arrest, the subject was found to be in possession of drug paraphernalia.  The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation.
At 7:19 PM, the suspect entered the business and stole an item. The suspect fled prior to police arrival.
At 4:47 AM, an officer located cut power lines and stolen copper from a power pole.
At 6:24 PM, the victim reported a suspect damaged property inside the complex.

At 9:14 PM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the investigation, officers found the driver to have an outstanding warrant.  The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and later released with a citation.
At 13:57 PM, an officer reports a suspect exposed themselves and inappropriately touched themselves. The suspect then fled the store with unpaid merchandise prior to police arrival.

At 1:43 PM, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop. During an investigation, officers found the subject to have two outstanding warrants. The subject was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Possession of a Stolen Vehicle / Fleeing from a Peace Officer / Hit and Run
Description: At 9:47 PM, officers received a FLOCK alert advising that a stolen vehicle had entered into city limits. An officer located the vehicle and the driver of the vehicle sped away from the officer. The officer pursued the vehicle for approximately three blocks where the suspect vehicle collided into a parked vehicle. The suspect exited the vehicle and attempted to run from the officers but was detained and taken into custody. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and issued a Juvenile Citation before being released to parents.
At 1:38 PM, an officer reports theft of copper from a power pole.
At 5:51 PM, an officer reports a suspect exited the store with unpaid merchandise, prior to police arrival.

At 11:52 PM, an officer reported suspects had entered the victim’s home through an unlocked door. The suspects woke up the victims and demanded money before fleeing the scene prior to officers’ arrival.

At 9:01 AM, officers accepted the Citizen’s Arrest of a suspect who brandished a replica firearm at another person. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation.
At 5:37 AM, an officer reports a suspect threw a large chunk of concrete through the side window of the business, shattering the glass.

At 3:13 PM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During a DUI investigation, officers found the suspect to be under the influence and far too intoxicated to stand on their own. The driver was transported to a local hospital by AMR and given a citation.

At 1:26 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the investigation, officers found the driver had an outstanding warrant for their arrest. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Warrant Arrest / Possession of Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 4:09 AM, officers conducted a pedestrian stop. A records check of the subject found the subject to have an outstanding warrant. During a search incident to arrest, the subject was found in possession of narcotics. The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Possession of Drug Paraphernalia / Probation Violation
Description: At 12:52 AM, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop. A records check of the subject found the subject to be on both probation and parole. Officers conducted a probation search of the subject and located drug paraphernalia on their persons. The subject was arrested and while en-route to the police department, had a medical episode. AMR responded and provided medical treatment. The subject was issued with a citation and transported to a local hospital.
At 7:01 PM, a suspect entered the store, selected items, and fled the store prior to police arrival.
At 7:27 PM, a victim reports a suspect stole their package off their porch.

At 6:03 PM, officers responded to a shoplift in progress. Officers located and arrested the suspect. A records check found the suspect to have several warrants for their arrest. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:47 PM, an officer was notified of copper wires that were stolen.

At 3:08 PM, a victim reports being in a road-rage argument where an occupant of the suspect’s vehicle brandished a firearm at the victim and threatened to shoot.  The victim fled the area and prior to notifying the police.

CrimeType: Warrant Arrest / Parole Violation / False Identification to a Peace Officer /  Possession of Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 2:02 PM, an officer found a suspicious vehicle and learned the vehicle was wanted by the Alameda County Sheriff’s Office for evading police. Officers conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the investigation, officers discovered two of the occupants had warrants for their arrest out of Alameda County, while the third occupant lied about their name. Officers discovered the identity of the occupant and found the subject was a parolee-at-large. During a search incident to arrest, officers found that suspect to be in possession of drug paraphernalia. All three suspects were booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.  The vehicle was impounded on scene.
At 7:45 PM, the victim reports two suspects spray painted another building located on the property.  The suspects fled prior to police arrival.

At 5:45 AM, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop. During the investigation, officers found the subject had a warrant for their arrest. The subject was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:48 AM, a victim reports a suspect shattered the front passenger window of the victim’s vehicle and stole property.  The suspect fled prior to police arrival.

At 6:30 PM, officers responded to a road-rage incident where the victim reported they were being followed. While driving to the police department, the suspect swerved and attempted to hit the victim’s vehicle. After arriving at the back lot of the police department, the suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

The suspect was transported to the Police Department for booking and released with a citation.

CrimeType: Possess Stolen Vehicle / Recovered Stolen Vehicle
Description: At 3:13 PM, a stolen vehicle hit on Flock ALPR cameras in the city.  A sergeant located the vehicle in the O’Reilly’s parking lot and the driver was arrested.   To county jail.

CrimeType: Robbery / Assault Against Peace Officers / Resist Arrest / Possess Ghost Gun
Description: At 3:54 PM, a Lieutenant witnessed a disturbance between the Stiiizy security guard and another subject that started inside the store and spilled out into the street.  Officers arrived at Stiiizzy and checked in with employees, and learned the suspect had asked to see a cannabis product, then snatched the package off the counter when the employee put it down.  At the same time, the suspect put down a note telling the employee to be quiet and not say anything.  The suspect started toward the door, and told the security guard he would kill him if he tried to intervene. An officer located the suspect near San Pablo Avenue and Potrero avenue.  The suspect refused to stop and continued walking away from officers then turned and brandished an umbrella toward an officer before continuing to run across San Pablo Avenue.  The suspect ran in to the doorway of a business, where he turned to face officers and raised an aerosol can and a lighter toward them.  An officer deployed his Taser at the suspect, incapacitating him, and he was taken into custody and put in a Wrap restraint.  A search of the suspect’s property incident to arrest revealed an un-serialized self-manufactured firearm.  The suspect was medically cleared at the hospital then transported to the county jail.
At 9:58 AM, a suspect stole merchandise.

At 9:21 PM, a subject was arrested on an outstanding citable warrant. The subject was transported to the Police Department where the subject was booked, cited, and released from the Police Department.
At 8:50 PM, an officer took a report of a theft of merchandise from the business.
At 2:18 PM, an officer took a report of a package theft over the past week.

At 1:01 PM, officers were dispatched to a hit and run collision at Fairmount and San Pablo Ave.  An officer reports approximately 75 bicyclists blocked traffic in the intersection during a “memorial ride”.  A driver became impatient and honked his horn while creeping forward and caused damage to a bicycle.  A member of the group climbed on the vehicle, and the driver fled out of fear for his safety.  He drove directly to the El Cerrito Police Department to give a statement.

The subject was taken into custody, transported to Kaiser for medical clearance, then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility for booking. The subject was taken into custody and directly transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

The subject was placed under arrest, booked at ECPD, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

During the investigation, the driver was determined to be under the influence of alcohol and placed under arrest. Description: At 8:09 AM, the victim reports their vehicle was stolen between 07/22/25 at 7:00 PM and 07/23/25 at 8:05 AM.

After the subject was taken into custody, she was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
