At 1:20 PM, an officer reports a theft of a vehicle between 2:00 AM – 10:00 AM this morning.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence/Hit and Ru
Description: At 9:31 PM, officers responded to a report of a vehicle that collided into a house. Upon arrival officers contacted the driver and determined that she was under the influence of alcohol. The driver was placed under arrest and transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking. While investigating this incident officers discovered the driver had collided with a parked vehicle several blocks away prior to this incident. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.

At 11:00 AM, an officer arrested a suspect on a no cite Marin County traffic warrant during a traffic stop.  The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 6:20 AM, officers responded to an alarm at the business and found the front door shattered and property stolen.
At 8:09 AM, Officers arrested a suspect after they set a fire on the side of the business which burned the wall.  The suspect was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:40 PM, the victim reports the theft of personal items were taken after being left in a cart.
At 1:51 PM, the victim reports a suspect entered their residence and destroyed items.
At 6:45 AM, Officers located a stolen vehicle to the rear of the above-mentioned location.
At 1:26 PM, the victim reports a suspect entered their residence and took several items. Vandalism – 700 Block of Colusa: At 9:21 am, the victim reports their vehicle was vandalized.

At 9:44 PM, officers responded to a shoplift at the business.  The subject refused officers’ commands and once detained, struggled with the officer.  The subject was highly intoxicated and had stolen from the store.  The subject was transported to the hospital for medical treatment and later released on a citation.
At 7:16 PM, Officers placed an individual under arrest after it was discovered they had a warrant, the suspect was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Possession of stolen property / Identity theft / probation violation / possession of drug paraphernalia / felon in possession of stun gun and pepper spray / warrant
Description: At 4:16 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop on a vehicle. The driver and passenger were found to be in possession of stolen property, and drug paraphernalia. Both occupants were determined to be convicted felons and in possession of a stun gun and pepper spray.  The driver was found to be in possession of the victims identifying information. The passenger was found to have a misdemeanor warrant.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence / No License
Location: San Pablo Ave / San Diego street
Description: At 1:37 AM, officers conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver for driving under the influence and no license.  The subject was later released on a citation.
At 10:38 PM, Officers recovered a previously stolen vehicle.
At 4:23 PM, the victim reports their vehicle was stolen.
At 11:19 AM, Officers were alerted to a felony wanted vehicle in city limits.  The vehicle was impounded at the request of the originating agency.
At 11:53 AM, Officers recovered a previously stolen vehicle.
At 10:56 AM, the victim reports their vehicle was stolen.
At 3:40 PM, an officer arrested a suspect on a no cite warrant during a ped stop.  The subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 4:40 PM, Flock hit on a stolen vehicle entering the city.  An officer located the vehicle on San Pablo Ave at Manila Ave and made a felony stop as the driver attempted to turn around on Kearney St to the rear of the police department.  The driver was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detecntion Facility.
At 7:30 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked locker and removed property.

CrimeType: Grand Theft from Person / Unlawful Use of Access Card
Description: At 4:27 PM, an officer reports the victim’s ATM card was exchanged by a suspect who was using the next ATM, while the victim was distracted by a second suspect.  The suspects then withdrew funds using the stolen card.
At 10:27 PM, an officer contacted a subject who had an outstanding warrant. The subject was taken into custody and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:29 AM, an officer reports a vehicle window was shattered overnight, between 8:30 PM and 7:00 AM.

CrimeType: Possession of Drug Paraphernalia/ Destruction of Evidenc
Description: At 4:10 AM, an officer noticed a subject walking in front of the police station smoking a glass pipe. The officer detained the subject to place him under arrest. While under arrest the subject stepped on the glass pipe in an attempt to destroy the evidence. Officers conducted a records check and discovered the subject had a probation violation warrant for his arrest. The subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 1:32 AM, an officer noticed a vehicle traveling at a high rate of speed without any headlights on and failed to stop for several red lights and stop signs.  The officer caught up with the vehicle and conducted a traffic enforcement stop. Officers determined the driver was under the influence of alcohol and placed them under arrest. The driver was transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.

At 5:17 PM, an unidentified suspect attempted to sell an item to the music store.  The manager recognized the item as a rental from the same store and called the renter, who was not sure how the clarinet left their possession.

At 9:43 PM, officers were dispatched to the business for two suspects who exited the store with unpaid merchandise. An officer circulating the area located one of the suspects at the El Cerrito Plaza BART station and detained him. The suspect was positively identified during an infield show up and placed under Citizen’s Arrest. Officers conducted a records check of the suspect and discovered he had an outstanding warrant and four prior theft convictions. After booking, the suspect was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 4:19 PM, an officer took a lobby report of a Battery that occurred on Saturday.
At 11:28 PM, officers conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver on several outstanding warrants.  The subject was later transported to Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:37 PM an officer reports the suspect stole a catalytic converter.
At 4:06 PM an officer accepted the citizens arrest of a subject who displayed lewd conduct in the parking lot. Subject was transported to Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:01 PM, officers responded to a Battery where the suspect had already fled the scene.

CrimeType: Drug Paraphernalia / Drug Possession / Probation Violation
Description: At 11:56 PM, officers conducted a bicycle stop and after conducting a probation search of the subject officers found drug paraphernalia and narcotics in their possession.  The subject was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facilty.

At 11:52 AM, an officer reports the suspect shattered the victims’ driver window and stole property. Warrant Arrest / Possession of Drug Paraphernalia: At 3:02 PM, an officer contacted a subject who had an outstanding warrant. A search was conducted and the officer located drug paraphernalia. The subject was transported to Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:21 PM, two unidentified suspects stole merchandise.

At 8:05 PM, an officer reports the victim received notification that her security cameras had been activated. The victim reviewed the security footage and discovered a known suspect was walking around her property.
An officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s residence via an unsecured window and stole property. The incident occurred between 1:40 PM and 5:15 PM.
At 11:08 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect rummaged through the victim’s unlocked vehicle between 8:30 PM on 12/11 and 8:30 AM on 12/12.
At 6:45 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect concealed items underneath their clothing and fled the store without paying.
At 9:43 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect entered an unlocked vehicle and stole property sometime overnight.
At 11:34 AM, an unidentified suspect shoved the victim after cutting in line, then fled on a bicycle.

At 11:18 PM, employees called to report an unknown suspect fled the business with unpaid merchandise. An officer located the suspect at the El Cerrito Plaza BART station and detained him. The suspect was positively identified after officers conducted an infield show-up with an employee.
At 5:31 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed a window on the victim’s vehicle and stole property.

CrimeType: Organized Retail Theft with Priors/Possession of a Controlled Substance/ Outstanding Warrant
Description: At 9:12 PM, an officer was at the business conducting a security check when employees notified him of two suspects inside the store who were in the process of stealing merchandise. As additional officers were responding to the area the two suspects exited an emergency door and attempted to flee with the unpaid merchandise. Officers detained both suspects and they were placed under Citizen’s Arrest. During a search incident to arrest one of the suspects was found to be in possession of a controlled substance. Officers discovered the suspect had two prior drug possession convictions and three outstanding warrants. During a records check for the second suspect, officers discovered he had two prior theft convictions. Boths suspects were transported to the Martinez Detention Facility after booking.
At 8:36 AM, and officer reports a vehicle was burglarized sometime overnight and property was taken.

At 4:37 AM, an officer reports the victim reviewed his security camera video and discovered three subjects wearing masks and gloves walking up to his house at 1:13 AM. The suspects were scared off by the victim’s motion sensor lights.
At 3:02 AM, officers responded to a theft of a vehicle that just occurred.  The suspects fled prior to police arrival and could not be located.
At 3:44 PM, an officer reports an unknown subject vandalized a street sign at an unknown time.
At 2:19 AM, officer located an unreported stolen vehicle that was abandoned.
At 8:35 PM, the victim was assaulted from behind by an unknown suspect.  The suspect fled the area prior to police arrival.
At 11:18 AM, officers contacted and arrested a subject for an outstanding warrant. The subject was later transported to Martinez Detention Facility.
At 11:54 PM, officers located an unoccupied stolen vehicle.  Officers contacted the victim and the victim came and took possession of their vehicle.
At 1:24 PM, the victim reports their vehicle was stolen sometime overnight.
At 2:08 PM, during a call for service, officers located a wanted subject. The subject was booked at El Cerrito Police Department and released with a new court date.
At 7:42 PM, the victim reported the theft of personal identifying information by an unknown suspect.

CrimeType: Felon in Possession of Firearm / Possession of Concealed Firearm / Felon in Possession of Ammunition / Possession of Narcotics and Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 12:33 AM, officers contacted a subject and observed drug paraphernalia in plain view.  Upon a search incident to arrest, the subject was found in possession of a firearm, ammunition, and narcotics. The subject was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:38 AM, an officer reports the suspect stole merchandise and fled prior to police arrival.

Location: San Pablo Ave / El Dorado Ave
Description: At 3:23 AM, officers conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence.  The driver was later released on a citation to a sober friend.
At 8:57 PM, officers conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver on an outstanding warrant.  The subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:49 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim's residence over that last few days and property within the residence had been moved.

At 11:19 AM, during a suspicious vehicle check and later during a pedestrian stop an officer arrested a subject for being in possession of a concealed firearm inside the vehicle. The subject was booked at ECPD and later released on a citation.
At 10:02 AM, an officer reports the suspect forced entry to the building, ransacked and vandalized the business.
At 12:58 PM, an officer reports the suspect entered a construction area and cut numerous copper pipes.
At 3:38 PM, the victim reports their motorcycle was stolen sometime overnight.

At 10:26 PM, officers were dispatched to a subject standing in the roadway and lighting something on fire.  Officers arrived on scene and detained a subject.  The subject was on parole, and during a search was found in possession of narcotics.  The subject was arrested and a parole hold was obtained.  The subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Shoplifting / Theft with Priors / Probation Violation
Description: At 11:05 PM, officers responded to a shoplift in progress.  Officers arrived on scene a detained a subject in the parking lot.  The subject was identified and placed under citizen’s arrest.  The subject was on probation and had several prior convictions for theft related crimes.  The subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 5:06 AM, officers responded to a theft of a catalytic converter.  The suspects fled prior to police arrival.

At 12:55 AM, an officer conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver for suspicion of driving under the influence. The driver was transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking. After booking the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.

At 8:18 AM, an officer reports unidentified suspect(s) cut the chain link fence on the greenway side to make entry to the yard, then broke in to a travel trailer in the yard and stole property.  Occurred overnight between 12/18 and 12/19.
At 3:18 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect pushed the victim off his scooter and into the bushes, then brandished a knife.  The victim got up and fled the area.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence and Possession of a Controlled Substance
Description: At 4:34 AM, officers responded to the area for a solo rollover collision. Upon arrival, officers located the driver along with a passenger. The driver had collided into a sidewalk causing the vehicle to overturn. Officers conducted a DUI investigation and placed the driver under arrest for suspicion of driving under the influence. During a search incident to arrest the driver was found to be in possession of fentanyl. The driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:22 PM, an officer took a report of stolen license plates taken between 12/16 and 12/20.
At 8:38 PM, an officer reports a known suspect to the victim made verbal threats to harm the victim.

CrimeType: Robbery / Public Intoxication / Battery on Peace Officer / Warrant Arrest
Description: At 12:17 PM, officers responded to a report of an UNWANT at Lucky.  While enroute, the detail escalated to a robbery.  The suspect struck one customer, then shoved another and stole property before fleeing. An officer located a person who matched the suspect description on Central Ave near San Mateo Ave and detained him.  The suspect was highly intoxicated, matched the description exactly, was positively identified during an in-field show up and was placed under arrest.  During the struggle to get him in the patrol car, the suspect kicked an officer.  The suspect also had an arrest warrant out of Berkeley PD.  Booked at the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 8:01 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle. The theft occurred between noon and 7:00 PM.

At 2:34 AM, officers responded to the business for a disturbance. While officers were on scene two subjects began fighting each other. Officers separated the two parties and discovered they both were intoxicated. The subjects were arrested and transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking. After booking the subjects were issued citations and released to a sober friend.
At 8:20 AM, an officer reports theft of a vehicle overnight, between 10:00 PM – 8:00 AM.
At 9:19 AM, an officer returned found property to a resident and learned the property was taken from her unlocked car on 12/15, on San Pablo Ave near Del Norte BART.
At 12:05 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole cash from a purse that was left hanging in a bathroom stall.
At 4:19 PM, during an retail theft operation, an officer arrested a suspect for stealing merchandise.  The suspect had 3 prior convictions and qualified for 666.1PC.  To Martinez Detention Facility.

At 2:29 PM, an officer reports the victim(s) vehicles were locked/secured and parked in front of their residence. The victim(s) noticed headlights of a vehicle, looked outside, and saw two unknown suspects possibly tampering with their vehicles. The victim(s) exited their house to confront the unknown suspects who ran to a waiting vehicle which fled the area.

At 9:48 PM, officers conducted a security check of a vacant property. Officers found an open window to the property and located a suspect who was inside.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 8:32 PM, officers responded to an alarm and discovered the front door of the business was unsecured with no signs of forced entry. Officers conducted a search of the business and located two juveniles carrying merchandise. The juveniles were detained and released to their parents.

At 8:11 PM an officer reports the victim left her purse in the restroom of the business. When the victim noticed her purse was missing, she returned to the store and retrieved it from Customer Service. The victim discovered property was missing from her purse.
At 8:24 AM, while assisting on the above burglary, an officer reports a suspect shattered the left rear window of a vehicle to gain entry and attempted to steal the vehicle.
At 9:53 PM, officers reported two suspects had broken into five vehicles in the parking lot and stole miscellaneous items. The suspects fled prior to police arrival.

Location: 1000 El Cerrito Plaza / 9950 San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 11:02 AM, a victim reported the suspect stole and used one of the victim’s access cards at 1:14 PM on 12/04/2024.

At 2:03 AM, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop and discovered the pedestrian had an outstanding warrant. The officer also discovered the pedestrian had been reported as a missing person. During a search incident to arrest the pedestrian was found to be in possession of suspected methamphetamine and drug paraphrenia. The susbject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Warrant Arrest / Burglary Tools / Unlicensed Driver / Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 8:31 AM, an officer responded to a traffic collision. During the investigation, officers found the suspect was unlicensed, on probation, and had an outstanding warrant for their arrest. During a probation search of the vehicle, officers also located burglary tools and drug paraphernalia. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility with the new charges.
At 9:04 PM, officers conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver on two outstanding warrants.  The subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 5:05 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the business and attempted to steal merchandise. The merchandise was recovered by employees, and the suspect fled the area.

At 1:04 AM, officers conducted a traffic enforcement stop. Further investigation found the driver to have two outstanding warrants. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence / Hit & Run / Suspended License / Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 8:15 PM, officers responded to a collision involving a vehicle into a house.  As officers arrived on scene, the suspect vehicle drove away.  Officers conducted a traffic enforcement stop and detained the driver. Further investigation found the driver to be under the influence with a suspended license. During the search incident to arrest, officers found drug paraphernalia in the driver’s possession.  The driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation to a sober friend.

At 12:34 PM, officers conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the investigation, officers found the driver had an outstanding juvenile warrant. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to Juvenile Hall.

At 1:48 AM, officers responded to a physical family fight with the suspect armed with a weapon. Officers arrived on scene and arrested the suspect. The suspect was then transported to hospital by medics and released to the custody of the hospital.
At 5:43 AM, a victim reports that their vehicle was stolen, along with their wallet that was inside the vehicle.
At 7:21 PM, officers located and recovered a stolen vehicle. During an inventory search, officers found burglary tools inside.
At 1:51 AM, officers conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver for driving under the influence.  The driver was later released on a citation to a sober friend.

At 2:35 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect cut the lock cable that was securing the victim’s electric trike and carried the trike up a small set of stairs before they were scared off by the victim’s dogs.

At 6:09 PM, officers were alerted to a felony vehicle traveling through El Cerrito by Flock Safety cameras. Officers located the vehicle and contacted a subject who was associated with the vehicle. The subject was detained and later released from the scene. The vehicle was impounded at the request of the investigating police agency.

At 8:04 AM, a suspect was arrested after he threatened to shoot employees of Starbucks while reaching into his coat and backpack, but no weapon was seen.  Booked at ECPD then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:05 PM, an officer took a phone report of a brandishing incident that took place three weeks ago.
At 8:09 AM, an officer reports a suspect shattered the front window to the vacant building sometime overnight.

At 4:33 PM, officers reported a suspect had taken mail from mailboxes before entering the backyard cottage of a residence. Officers arrived on scene and located the suspect coming from out of the backyard. The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 4:57 PM, an officer reports theft of a vehicle between 12/19 and today.
At 9:44 PM, an officer reports two suspects stole items from the store. The suspects fled prior to police arrival.
At 12:20 AM, an officer located a stolen vehicle using the AXON Automated License Plate Reader system.

CrimeType: Hit and Run / Unlicensed Driver / Obstructing / Public Intoxication
Description: At 6:07 AM, officers responded to the report of a collision between a vehicle and a city light pole. Officers arrived on scene and located the driver and passenger. The driver did not have a valid license and refused to identify themselves. The passenger was extremely intoxicated and belligerent, unable to adequately care for themself, and also refused to identify themselves. Both the driver and passenger were positively identified by witnesses and arrested. The driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation. The passenger was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:03 AM, an officer reports a suspect shattered a window to the business.

CrimeType: Possession of Drug Paraphernalia / Resisting, Delaying a Peace Officer
Description: At 3:18 AM, an officer reports that the suspect was located outside of Ross when officers received a front door entry alarm activation. The suspect was detained and was in possession of drug paraphernalia. The suspect refused to identify themself and was placed under arrest. The suspect later provided identification and was cited and released from the scene. Vehicle Theft – 1600 block of Elm St: At 10:06 AM, an officer reports theft of a vehicle between midnight and 10:00 AM.
At 4:54 PM, an officer reported a car window was shattered with no loss taken.
At 2:55 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole property from the victim.

At 12:31 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop. During the investigation, officers found the driver had an outstanding warrant out of Marin County. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released with a citation.

At 11:37 PM, an officer received a FLOCK Safety notification for a reported stolen vehicle out of Pinole. Officers circulated the area and located the vehicle unoccupied. The owner of the vehicle was contacted, and the vehicle was released on scene.
At 3:28 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole property from the victim as they were shopping in the store.
At 2:40 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the front license plate of the victim’s vehicle.

CrimeType: Possession of Drug Paraphernalia/ Parole Hold
Description: At 1:59 PM, officers responded to Safeway for an unwanted subject. Officers contacted the subject and discovered he was a parolee at large with an outstanding warrant. Officers took him into custody for the warrant. During a search incident to arrest officers located a glass pipe commonly used for smoking narcotics. After booking, the subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:39 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole copper wire from a telephone pole.

At 8:59 AM, officers responded to a severely intoxicated person lying on the ground. The subject was transported to the hospital by medics. This case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s office.
At 8:02 PM, an officer reports two suspects stole items from the store. The suspects fled prior to police arrival.

At 2:26 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect ordered food and consumed it. The suspect attempted to leave the business without paying and was confronted by an employee and was punched by the suspect.
At 6:42 AM, officers responded to the report of a stolen vehicle.
At 1:38 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for an outstanding warrant. The driver was issued a citation at the scene and released.
At 8:45 AM, officers responded to a theft from an unlocked vehicle.

At 4:24 PM, officers responded to a report of a theft. Officers located and positively identified the suspect. The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:47 PM, officers responded to a theft. The suspect fled the area prior to police arrival.
At 3:55 PM, an officer received a Flock notification for a stolen vehicle in the area. The officer located the vehicle parked unoccupied. The vehicle was recovered from the scene.
At 1:52 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s property from in front of the business.

At 9:01 PM, officers responded to the business after the suspect threw beer at the victims. Officers contacted the suspect at the scene and determined he was under the influence of alcohol. The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:45 PM, an officer reports and unknown suspect shattered the windows of the victim’s vehicle.

At 12:13 AM, officers responded to the area for a solo vehicle collision. During the investigation officers determined the driver was under the influence of alcohol and placed them under arrest. The driver was transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.
At 3:50 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect damaged the victim’s gate.
At 12:46 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole a package from the victim’s porch.
At 11:58 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s unlocked vehicle and stole property.
At 6:23 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s vehicle.
At 4:03 PM, an officer reports two suspects brandished a firearm at the victim’s and demanded their property.
At 10:57 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect smashed out a window of the victim’s vehicle to gain entry. The suspect then rummaged through vehicle but did not take anything.

At 8:15 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect pulled out mace and a steak knife and threatened employees when confronted for shoplifting.  All merchandise was recovered before the suspect fled to a waiting vehicle.

At 7:43 PM, an officer reports two unknown suspects attempted to leave the store with unpaid merchandise and was confronted by store employees. One of the suspects threw a bag of merchandise back causing damage and fled with another bag.
At 9:28 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect fled the store after concealing various merchandise into bags.
At 2:47 PM, an officer reports two juveniles ordered and consumed food, then left without paying.  The juveniles were located and detained. Both juveniles were turned over to adult relatives.
At 3:57 PM, an officer reports a known suspect came to the residence in violation of a restraining order, and stole property.
At 6:39 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect got into an argument with the victim. During the altercation the suspect punched the victim.
At 2:51 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole a rolling cart off the porch of the residence.
At 10:04 AM, an officer reports a suspect broke the handle on a washing machine.
An officer reports multiple suspects entered the victims residence during the day and removed an unknown amount of property at this time.

At 8:53 PM, two suspects ordered food from the business.  After receiving the food, they fled without paying.  When the business owner attempted to stop the suspects the business owner was assaulted by one of the suspects.  The suspects fled in a vehicle prior to police arrival.
At 3:22 PM, an officer reports the victims license plate was stolen between 12/31/25 and 01/07/26.
At 4:47 AM, an unknown subject set fire to a porta potty.

At 3:15 PM, an officer reports the suspect damaged property inside the business. Officers detained the suspect and accepted the citizen’s arrest. The suspect was booked at El Cerrito Police Department and released on a citation.
At 5:45 PM, an officer reports the victims locker was broken into and property was stolen. The victim later recovered some of the stolen property in Richmond.

At 4:45 PM, an officer reports the victim witnessed a fight and an attempted stabbing with a knife. After the suspect attempted to stab the first victim, the suspect put the knife away and then kicked the second victim (the reporting party). The suspect left prior to officers arrival. While on scene officers were unable to locate the victim of the attempted stabbing. The officer spoke with the victim who got kicked and the victim did not desire prosecution for the battery.

CrimeType: Obstruction and Delaying a Peace Officer
Description: At 3:32 AM, officers conducted a pedestrian stop on a subject.  The subject refused to identify themselves and was placed under arrest.  The subject refused to sign the citation and was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility Martinez.
At 11:00 AM, an officer reports an unknown subject vandalized a City of El Cerrito trailer within the last several days.
At 09:08 AM, an officer reports a suspect vandalized the victims vehicle after a road rage incident. The suspect fled prior to police arrival.
At 12:59 PM, an officer reports the suspect shattered all of the vehicle’s tires, smashed all of the vehicles windows and stole property from the vehicle.
At 2:49 PM, an officer reports the suspect entered and ransacked the victims vehicle. The suspect stole property and no forced entry was located.
At 6:41 PM, the victim reported a package stolen.
At 12:56 AM, an officer reports a witness noticed the suspect inside the victim’s vehicle and confronted him. After a brief verbal altercation, the suspect fled the scene.
At 7:00 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect used an unknown object to damage the victim’s vehicle.

At 1:37 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for driving under the influence and being unlicensed. The driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.

At 8:48 PM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for driving under the influence. The driver was transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.

At 2:27 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence. The driver was transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.

CrimeType: Theft with Priors/ Possession of Drug Paraphernalia/Warran
Description: At 7:19 PM, an officer was in the area for an unrelated incident when he noticed the suspect exited the store with an armful of merchandise. The officer contacted employees who confirmed the suspect had exited the business without paying for the merchandise. The officer caught up to the suspect and detained him. The suspect was placed under citizen’s arrest. During a search incident to arrest the suspect was found to be in possession of a glass pipe commonly used for smoking methamphetamine. During a records check the officer discovered the suspect had an outstanding warrant and five prior theft convictions. After the suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, he was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 6:00PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole property from the victim’s unlocked vehicle.
At 8:55 AM, an officer reports the victims vehicle was stolen and property was stolen.
At 12:44 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect threw a rock at one of the windows of the business causing damage.
At 2:17 PM, an officer reports a vehicles license plates were stolen.
At 2:07 PM, an officer reports property was stolen from inside of the unlocked vehicle.

CrimeType: Possession of Burglary tools/Possession of controlled substances
Description: At 2:05 PM, an officer conducted a suspicious vehicle check and contacted one of the occupants. The officer conducted a search of the subject and they were placed under arrest for being in possession of a controlled substance and burglary tools. The subject was booked at ECPD and later released on a citation.

At 5:35 PM, an officer reported an Autozone loss prevention officer conducted a theft investigation into one of its employees. The investigation revealed the employee had embezzled money from the business for the last several months. Autozone reported a loss of over ten thousand dollars’ worth of merchandise. The suspect was arrested and booked at ECPD. The suspect was later transported to MDF.
At 9:40 AM, an officer reports the suspect forced entry into the residence and ransacked it.
At 11:06 AM, an officer reports the suspect caused damage to the business window overnight.

The suspect had four prior theft convictions, which qualified him for felony charges.  They were transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Officers responded to the location and took custody of the subject and transported him to the Martinez Detention Facility.

A stolen vehicle hit on Flock entering the city at 7:27 AM, Central Ave/San Mateo St.  After several additional Flock hits, at 8:02 hours, officers located the vehicle parked on S.53rd St at Potrero Avenue, occupied.  A brief pursuit ensued, westbound on Potrero and northbound on Carlson, but the pursuit was canceled when the vehicle was headed for a construction zone at high speed.  Richmond Police later recovered the vehicle unoccupied in Point Richmond and it was returned to its owner.

At 6:02 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect stole the victim’s property.

At 1:30 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for driving under the influence. The driver was transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.

The victim called the police to report the incident and advised the vehicle was equipped with a tracking device. Officers responded to the area in Richmond and located the vehicle. The suspect fled the area at a high rate of speed when they saw the officers. Officers pursued the vehicle onto the freeway and lost sight of the of it. The victim continued tracking the vehicle and it was located in the City of Richmond abandoned. The victim responded to the scene and took possession of his vehicle.

At 1:04 AM, officers arrested a subject on an outstanding warrant.  The subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Shoplifting, False Identification, Possession of a controlled substance, and Warrant

The suspect was issued a citation on the new charges and transported to county hospital for a previous medical issue. After receiving medical clearance, the suspect was booked in to the Martinez Detention Facility for the outstanding warrant.

At 1:39 AM, an officer located the parolee and detained him. The officer confirmed the warrant and transported the parolee to the Martinez Detention Facility.

Description: At 5:01 AM, an officer located a suspect inside of the victim’s vehicle. When contacted the suspect resisted arrest and fled from the officer. After an investigation the driver was arrested for Driving Under the Influence and transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking. CrimeType: Vehicle Theft/Evading Officers/Hit and Run/Possession of a Stolen Vehicle
Location: San Pablo Avenue at Macdonald Avenue
Description: At 2:14 PM, Officers were alerted by Flock cameras to a stolen vehicle in the area.  Officers located the vehicle and attempted to conduct a traffic enforcement stop.  The vehicle fled, collided with a vehicle, continued driving and then collided with a fire hydrant.  The driver and two passengers fled.  Officer pursued the occupants and caught them both.  Both occupants were placed under arrest and transported to the El Cerrito Police  Department.  Since they were juveniles, they were released from the El Cerrito Police Department to their guardians.
At 4:32 PM, the victim reports their storage locker was broken into and items were taken.
At 4:04 PM, the victim reports a subject gained access to their personal identifying information and used it in an attempt to obtain financial gain.

CrimeType: Shoplifting/Provide False Information to Police/Warrant Arrest
Description: At 12:19 PM, Officers were dispatched to a report of shoplifting.  The subject was contacted and provided a false name to Officers.  The officers figured out the subject's real name and a records check of the individual revealed they had an active warrant for their arrest.  They were placed under arrest, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 4:31 PM, Officers contacted an individual who had an outstanding arrest warrant.  They were arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:17 PM, the victim reports an individual gained access to their personal identifying information and used it for financial gain.
At 10:00 AM, an individual was arrested for an outstanding warrant and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Warrant Arrest/Possession of Narcotics and Drug Paraphernalia/Misappropriation of Lost Property/Probation Violation
Description: At 8:00 PM, officers conducted a pedestrian stop and arrested the subject on a felony warrant.  The subject was also in possession of narcotics, drug paraphernalia, and debit card not in their name.  The subject was also on probation.  The subject was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility on all charges.
At 10:00 AM an individual was arrested for an outstanding warrant and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:10 PM, the victim reports two individuals conspired to steal an item from the establishment.
At 10:12 AM, the victim reports their vehicle was broken into and items were taken.

CrimeType: Possession of Narcotics and Drug Paraphernalia
Description: At 2:52 AM, officers conducted a pedestrian stop and arrested a subject for possession of narcotics and drug paraphernalia.  The subject was later released on a citation.
At 9:16 PM, an officer reports an unknown driver damaged one of the gas nozzle.
At 6:54 PM, an officer took a report of the suspect making threats to harm the victim and their family via telephone.

CrimeType: Trespass/Battery on a Police Officer/Resisting Arrest/Possession of Drug Paraphrenia
Description: At 7:41 PM, officers responded to the school for an unwanted subject. Officers contacted the subject and asked him to leave per the request of an employee or be placed under citizen’s arrest. The subject refused to leave and disobeyed officers’ orders to leave the school campus. As officers attempted to take the subject into custody, he slapped the arm of one of the officers and continued to physically resist. Officers eventually took the subject into custody. During a search incident to arrest, the subject was found to be in possession of a glass pipe used to injest narcotics. After booking, the subject was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 7:59 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect painted over the address numbers on the victim’s curb.
At 1:13 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.
At 3:31 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.
At 2:02 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.
At 3:51 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence. After booking, the driver was issued a citation and released to a sober friend.

At 9:25 PM, an officer reports the victim was pushed during a verbal argument with a known suspect. The suspect fled the scene prior to officers’ arrival. The suspect was contacted via telephone and declined to give a statement about the incident. The case will be forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office for review.
At 2:05 PM, the victim reports an item stolen from their mail room.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence of Alcohol and Drugs/Driving on Suspended License/Possess Narcotic Equipment
Description: At 11:44 AM, Officers were dispatched to Walgreens in response to a non-injury collision.  The driver was under the influence of drugs and alcohol, was driving on a suspended license and was in possession of narcotics equipment. The driver was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department and released to a sober relative.
At 2:48 PM, Officers were alerted to a stolen vehicle via Flock cameras.  The vehicle was located running and unoccupied in front of DDs Discounts. The vehicle was recovered.

At 1:13 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under Influence. The driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, then released to a sober relative on a citation.

CrimeType: Fail To Register as Sex Offende
Description: At 12:42 AM, an officer conducted an enforcement stop on a bicyclist and discovered the subject was a sex offender that had not recently registered as required. The subject was placed under arrest. During a search incident to arrest, the subject was found to be in possession of suspected methamphetamine and two glass smoking pipes. The subject was booked the the El Cerrito Police Department and then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:17 AM, the victim reports a suspect entered the store, took items, and left without paying for them.

At 4:10 AM, an officer conducted a pedestrian stop and arrested the subject for two outstanding warrants. The subject was transported to the El Cerrito Police Department for booking and released on a citation.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence/Hit and Run
Description: At 4:09 PM, a sergeant heard the impact of a collision and saw the suspect vehicle fleeing the scene. The sergeant caught up with the vehicle several blocks away and conducted a traffic enforcement stop. The driver appeared intoxicated. Officers conducted a DUI investigation and arrested the driver for DUI and hit and run. After the driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, they were issued a citation and released to a sober friend.

At 5:47 AM, officers arrested a suspect after they threw full champagne bottles at a security guard after being confronted for shoplifting.  The suspect also caused extensive property damage when she threw items around in the store.  The suspect was placed under arrest and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 8:07 AM, an officer noticed an unsecured door to the vacant building and conducted a security check. The officer contacted the owner who responded later in the day and discovered unknown suspects had stole property from the building.

At 11:43 PM, an officer located a subject that was intoxicated and unable to care for thenself. The subject was taken into custody and booked at the El Cerrito Police Department. After booking, the subject was issued a citation and released to a sober relative.
At 9:04 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect fled the business with unpaid merchandise.

Location: Portola Drive and San Pablo Ave.
Description: At 12:10 PM, an officer located a subject who had an outstanding arrest warrant.  The suspect was arrested, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, cited, and released.

At 5:20 AM, officers conducted a pedestrian stop and arrested the subject on a misdemeanor warrant.  The subject was placed under arrest, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 2:11 AM, officers conducted a pedestrian stop and arrested a subject with two misdemeanor warrants for their arrest.  The subject was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 7:44 PM, officers responded to a disturbance in front of the business.  Officers located a subject who was highly intoxicated.  The subject was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:56 PM, the victim reports their vehicle was broken into and items were stolen.
At 7:15 AM, Officers report a window of the building was intentionally broken. Stolen Vehicle Recovery – 1300 block of Rifle Range Rd: At 2:08 PM, Officers report the recovery of a stolen tractor.

At 10:02 AM, Officers were dispatched to a residence in response to multiple activations of a residential alarm.  Upon arrival, Officers located a damaged, unsecured door and ensured there were no suspects inside of the residence.  With the assistance of the homeowner, Officers conducted an investigation, locating video footage from a neighboring residence. Using FLOCK camera systems, a suspect vehicle was identified.
At 4:52 AM, officers conducted a pedestrian stop and arrested a subject for a misdemeanor warrant.  The subject was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 10:59 PM, officers received a Flock notification of a stolen vehicle entering the city.  Officers located the vehicle unoccupied a short time later and recovered the vehicle.
At 2:05 PM, the victim reports their personal identification was used for financial gain.

CrimeType: Robbery/Assault on Peace Officer/Probation Violation/Obstructing Peace Officer/Warrant Arrest
Description: At 7:07 PM, officers responded to the report of shoplifting.  The suspect was located nearby and attempted to walk away from officers.  After being stopped, the suspect threw a punch at an officer.  During the investigation the suspect threatened violence towards the employees during the theft.  The suspect was on active felony probation and also had a felony warrant for their arrest.  The suspect was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Driving Under the Influence/Possession of a Concealed Firearm/Felon in Possession of Ammunition/Possession of Narcotics while armed with a firearm/Sales and Transportation of Narcotics
Description: At 12:18 AM, officers conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence. During a search of the vehicle, a loaded firearm and a large quantity of narcotics were located.  The driver was a convicted felon.  The driver was placed under arrest, booked the the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:34 PM, the victim reports their vehicle was stolen.
At 2:15 PM, an officer reports the victim was robbed of his watch at school.
At 7:10 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s locked vehicle and stole property.

At 7:00 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect attempted to hit the victim with his fist. The victim stated the suspect was the same suspect who battered another victim several days ago. The suspect also matched the description of the suspect from a battery that was reported earlier in the day. The suspect fled the scene prior to officers’ arrival. At 7:51 PM, an officer on patrol located a subject who matched the description of the suspect and detained them. An infield show-up was conducted and the victim identified the subject as the person who assaulted them. After the suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, they were transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 11:41 PM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence and suspended license. The driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, then issued a citation, and released to a sober friend.
At 9:01 AM, an officer reports the victim was sitting at the bus stop waiting for a bus when the unidentified suspect walked up and then punched the victim.

At 2:02 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence. The driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, then issued a citation, and released to a sober relative.
At 1:32 PM, an officer located and arrested a person on three outstanding arrest warrants.

Location: San Pablo Ave at Wall Ave
Description: At 3:08 PM, an officer was flagged down for a disturbance and accepted the citizen’s arrest of a suspect after he punched the victim unprovoked.  The suspect was the same as the previous incidents in the area and was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 4:23 AM, an officer reported the suspect forced entry into an electrical room of the business and lit a fire.

At 2:52 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence. The driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, then issued a citation and released to a sober relative.
At 2:40 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect tampered with the electrical panel on the side of the building and stole the fuse box.  Occurred between 4:00 PM on 5/9 and 2:40 PM on 5/10.

At 3:07 PM, an officer arrested a suspect on a warrant.  During a search incident to arrest, a small useable amount of methamphetamine was located on his person.  The suspect was issued a citation for the for the warrant and possession of narcotics.
At 9:10 AM, an officer reports unidentified suspects stole the catalytic converter from a vehicle several hours prior.
At 9:43 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect tore the Ring doorbell off the wall and threw it in the trash between 5/8 and 5/11.
At 7:58 PM, the victim reported the suspect stole jewelry from her person earlier in the day.

At 5:30 PM, the suspect entered the store and stole a pair of shoes.  The suspect was located in the area and arrested.  The suspect had several prior convictions for theft.  The suspect was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:44 PM, the victim reported being sexually assaulted and the victim of indecent exposure 8 years ago.

At 12:35 PM, Officers placed a suspect under arrest after they entered the store and began consuming merchandise.  When confronted, the suspect attempted to retrieve the items from security by force.  The suspect was placed under arrest and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:01 PM, the suspect used a rock and shattered two windows in two different businesses.  The suspect fled prior to police arrival.

Location: Blake St at the BART Path
Description: At 10:34 AM, the victim reports the theft of copper wire.

CrimeType: Transportation and Possession for Sale of Narcotics/Possession of Prescription Medication/Suspended Driver’s License
Location: San Pablo Ave at Portola Dr.
Description: At 2:26 AM, officers conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver for transportation and sales of narcotics.  The suspect was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:16 PM, the victim was inside the business when an unknown suspect punched the victim and fled the area prior to police arrival.

CrimeType: Throwing Items From a Moving Vehicle and Battery
Location: Central Ave at San Pablo Ave
Description: At 3:00 PM, the victim reports while driving, another driver threw an item from a vehicle.  The item struck the passenger in the face.
At 1:20 PM, the victim reports a suspect entered the business, broke into a closet and took items from inside.

Location: San Pablo Ave at Knott Ave
Description: At 6:30 PM, the victim reported they had been assaulted the previous day by an unknown suspect.
At 6:49 PM, officers conducted a pedestrian stop and arrested the suspect on an outstanding misdemeanor warrant.  The suspect was later transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 6:53 PM, officers responded to a theft that just occurred.  Two suspects requested an item from behind the counter.  Afterwards one of the suspects distracted the employee and the other suspect fled the store with the item.  The suspects fled the scene in a vehicle prior to police arrival.

CrimeType: Possess Controlled Substance and Paraphernalia/Provide False info to Police Officer
Description: At 10:28 AM, an officer responded to a disturbance and arrested a suspect for possession of a controlled substance which was in plain view.  The suspect provided a false name.  The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department then released on a citation.
At 9:01 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole food items and pushed a cashier aside to commit the theft.

Location: San Pablo Ave at Cutting Blvd
Description: At 6:33 PM, officers received a FLOCK notification of a stolen vehicle in the area of San Pablo Ave and Cutting Blvd. An officer located the vehicle occupied in the Walgreens parking lot. Officers conducted a felony vehicle enforcement stop and detained the driver and passenger. The passenger was found to be in possession of suspected methamphetamine. During a criminal history check of the passenger, he was found to have at least two prior convictions for possession of narcotics. After both parties were booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, they were transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

At 2:00 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence. The driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, issued a citation, and released to a sober friend.
At 12:32 PM, an officer reports theft of an ebike.
At 9:28 AM, the victim reports they were threatened by another individual causing them to be in fear for their safety.

At 5:05 PM, Officers were dispatched to the business in response to a suspect waving a knife, threatening employees and breaking items inside of the establishment.  The suspect was contacted, placed under arrest, booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:39 hrs, the victim reports an individual passed fraudulent paperwork to complete a purchase.
At 1:50 PM, the victim reports the theft of several items.
At 7:01 PM, the victim reported their vehicle was stolen.
At 8:02 PM, officers responded to a battery between two subjects.  Neither party wanted to pursue criminal charges.
At 1:11 PM, the victim reports they were assaulted by a customer.
At 5:10 PM, the victim reports their property was stolen from in front of the establishment.

At 7:28 AM, Officers conducted a traffic enforcement stop and placed the driver under arrest for an outstanding warrant.  The driver was placed under arrest, transported to the El Cerrito Police Department, and issued a citation and released. .
At 6:51 AM, the victim reports their vehicle was broken into and items were taken.
At 11:58 PM, officers conducted a traffic stop and arrested the driver on suspicion of Driving Under the Influence.  The driver was later released on a citation to a sober friend.
At 8:38 PM, officers responded to a theft from the business.  The suspect fled prior to police arrival.

At 5:32 PM, an officer reports two unidentified suspects attempted to purchase product from See’s, but their check was declined.  The suspects decided to take the product anyway and left toward CVS.  A passerby followed them toward CVS and took the See’s product back, then returned it to the store.  The suspects returned to See’s demanding the recovered stolen property be returned to them, but the employees refused.  The passerby started pushing the suspects out of the store, when one suspect pepper sprayed him.  Both suspects then fled toward CVS again.  The pepper spray victim left the scene prior to police arrival and was not identified.

At 12:17 AM, while officers were investigating the above incident they saw a collision occurr nearby. Officers contacted one of the drivers and determined they were under the influence and placed them under arrest. After the driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, they were issued a citation, and released to a sober friend.

At 12:07 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence. After the driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, they were issued a citation, and released to a sober friend.
At 5:00 PM, an officer reports a theft of a bicycle that stolen while its owner was working out.
At 6:46 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect damaged a window to the victim’s house.
At 3:46 AM, an officer reports an unknown suspect entered the victim’s vehicle and stole property.
At 9:49 PM, an officer reports an unknown suspect called the victim posing as a health worker and obtained the victim’s personal identifying information.

CrimeType: Warrant Arrest/Possess Controlled Substance/Shoplift/Petty Theft with Priors
Description: At 3:58 PM, an officer arrested a suspect on a Placer County warrant during a stop for drinking in public. During a search incident to arrest, a small useable amount of methamphetamine was discovered in the suspect’s pocket.  The suspect had a bag in his possession with Ross tags still on it.  The bag was stuffed full of clothing, all with Ross tags.  A check with Ross security revealed the suspect had been noticed committing the theft, but Ross did not call the police.  Since the suspect was identified and in custody however, Ross desired prosecution and signed a citizen’s arrest.  The suspect had two prior qualifying theft convictions.  The suspect was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 3:43 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect stole items and fled.
At 8:44 AM, an officer took a telephone report of a lost wallet. The victim got notifications that their card had been used at Safeway in El Cerrito.
At 3:22 PM, the victim reports their property was damaged.

At 12:30 AM, officers were dispatched to the area for a subject walking in and out of traffic. Upon arrival officers located the subject who appeared to be intoxicated and could not care for himself. The subject was placed under arrest and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:57 AM, the victim reported their vehicle had just been stolen.
At 8:23 PM, officers responded to a reported sexual assault.  The suspect was arrested and transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Assaulting a Peace Officer/Evading the Police/Evading the Police Wrong Way/Driving Under the Influence/Hit and Run/Obstructing a Peace Officer
Description: At 1:57 AM, officers received a flock alert of a felony vehicle. Officers located the vehicle and attempted a traffic stop. The vehicle fled from officers and drove into a dead end. The suspect rammed a police vehicle and parked vehicle to escape. Officers pursued the vehicle throughout Richmond, San Pablo, and El Cerrito. The suspect drove into another dead end and officers blocked the vehicle with their patrol vehicles. The suspect rammed a police vehicle a second time. The suspect was arrested and transported to Martinez Detention Facility.
At 1:14 AM, officers responded to a prowling call.  Two suspects were seen on the victim’s property. The suspects fled prior to police arrival.

At 1:06 PM, Officers were dispatched to the area for a collision. The driver of the vehicle was discovered to be driving while intoxicated.  The driver was placed under arrest, transported to the El Cerrito Police Department. The driver was then released with a citation to a sober friend.
At 11:46 AM, the victim reports their property was damaged.

CrimeType: Robbery/Assault with a Deadly Weapon, Mayhem/Obstruction
Location: BART path north of Wall Ave
Description: At 1:33 PM, Officers were dispatched to a report of a robbery in which the victim was struck multiple times with a wrench. The suspect then took possession of items carried by the victim. The suspect was located on the BART Path, identified, and placed under arrest. Due to medical complaints, the suspect was transported to a local Hospital, while still in police custody.  The victim was also transported to a separate hospital to have their injuries assessed. Officers transported the suspect to the Martinez Detention Facility.

CrimeType: Obstruction/Petty Theft with Priors/Assault on a Police Officer
Description: At 9:22 AM, an Officer was flagged down regarding an individual inside of the establishment who was causing a disturbance and eating items they had not paid for. When the Officer attempted to contact the individual, they struck the Officer. The Officer then took the individual to the ground where they were placed under arrest. The individual was transported to the El Cerrito Police Department where it was discovered the individual had several convictions for theft. The individual was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 9:39 PM, officers responded to a vandalism.  The suspects fled prior to police arrival.

At 8:15 PM, officers were at the business for an unrelated incident when officers recognized a subject in the area with an active arrest warrant. Officers contacted the subject and took them into custody for the warrant. During a search incident to arrest officers located a switchblade in the subject’s possession. The subject was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, then transported to the Martinez Detention Facility.
At 12:12 AM, the victim reported their vehicle was stolen.
At 3:22 PM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect broke a floodlight on the house, and tore a rear windshield wiper off the victim’s vehicle.
At 9:51 AM, an officer reports the theft of a vehicle between midnight and 9:30 AM.
At 8:29 AM, an officer reports theft of a vehicle from the street between 9:30 PM on 5/28 and 8:30 AM on 5/29.
At 9:04 AM, an officer reports an unidentified suspect broke the door handle and window of the victim vehicle between 10:00 PM on 5/29 and 8:00 AM on 5/30.

At 7:47 AM, an officer was flagged down by the shop owner as a suspect ran from the store with food items. The officer deatined the suspect and accepted the citizen’s arrest for the theft. The suspect refused to identify themself and had to be brought back to the El Cerrito Police Department for fingerprinting.  After their right hand was fingerprinted, the suspect balled his left fist and refused to allow his left hand to be fingerprinted.  The suspect was transported to the Martinez Detention Facility as a John Doe on his refusal to identify. After the suspect was identified, a review of his criminal history revealed he had at least eight prior theft convictions.

At 5:58 AM, an officer conducted a traffic enforcement stop and arrested the driver for Driving Under the Influence. After the driver was booked at the El Cerrito Police Department, they were issued a citation and released to a sober friend.
